EDCS-00380787.text_edition: Genio Luci R B Ursinus servus votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-00380787.translation: To the Genius of Lucius R. B., Ursinus, his slave, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380787.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from a slave, Ursinus, to the Genius (protective spirit) of his master, Lucius R. B., fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-00380788.text_edition: Atestas Attonis filius Lutussa Bittonis filia mater Bucca Gannici filia uxor Matugenta filia
EDCS-00380788.translation: Atestas, son of Atto; Lutussa, daughter of Bitto, his mother; Bucca, daughter of Gannicus, his wife; Matugenta, his daughter.
EDCS-00380788.summary: This inscription appears to be a tombstone or memorial, listing the family members (father, mother, wife, daughter) of an unnamed individual, likely the deceased.

EDCS-00380789.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae pro salute Imperatoris [Commodi] Augusti Pii qui templum vii sic conlapsum impendio suo restituerunt et mortalitatis causa convenerunt Marullo et Aeliano consulibus VI Kalendas Iulias  Iulius Secundinus pater obitus Trebius Zoticus pater Rufius Severinus Iulius Optatus Septimius Mercator Septimius Marcus Tertinius Tertinus Mamilius Dionysius Sabinius Hermaiscus obitus Sentius Hermes Priscius Oppidanus Varius Fortis Titius Ruso Annius Syrillio Lydacius Charito Baienius Axio obitus Rufius Fuscus Marius Achilleus Claudius Quintilianus Iulius Carpus Publilius Moderatus Mamilius Crescentinus obitus Varius Secundus Marius Eutyches obitus Atticius Sextus pater  Atticius Tacitus Annius Calocaerus Claudius Mercuralis Brittannius Syrus Mercatorius Crispinus Lydacius Ingenuus Claudius Quintianus Cornelius Maturus Cornelius Florentinus Trebius Alfius pater Valerius Hermophilus Baienius Homuncio Licinius Marcianus Iulius Philostratus Volusius Atticus Aelius Successus Marcius Marcianus Claudius Felix Aelius Marcianus Varius Sabinus Caius Baebius Cupitus Auconius Eucarpus Aelius Fuscus  Marius Eutychus Baebius Bassianus Quintus Baienius Ingenuus Caius Flavius Nectareus pater Publius Aelius Fuscianus Marcus Marius Zosimus Titus Auconius Callimorfus Lucius Fac Onesimus Titus Mestrius Respectus Quintus Septimius Speratus Lucius Albius Aristo Aulus Tappetius Chionius Lutuccius Maro Marcus Marius Severianus pater Caius Iulius Maternus Lucius Candidius Aper Caius Priscius Crescentinus Quintus Iulius Adrastus Trosius Tertullinus Aelius Seccundianus sic Calendinus Successi Antonius Atticianus Claudius Primitivus  Veponius Chrestus Auconius Boniatus Tiberius Claudius Curio Gaius Iulius Secundinus Claudius Tiberius Dignus Lucius Valerius Valerianus Gaius Baebius Ursulus Gaius Marius Tertullinus Bellicius Senecio Summianius Maximus Publius Aelius Cupitus Asconius Severinus Gemellius Gemellianus Marcus Ulpius Atticus Aelius Celerinus Marcus Ulpius Atticianus Speratus servus Lucius Vibius Iuvenis Helvius Varronianus Lucius Vibius Aeliomarus Rufius Saturninus Marcus Mansuetius Mansuetus Marcus Bellicius Reditus Tiberius Claudius Optatus Gaius Secundius Reditus Successius Florianus Lucius Quartinius Quartus  Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus ob dedicationem templi tabulam aeream donum dedit et camaram picturis exornavit
EDCS-00380789.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, for the well-being of the Emperor Commodus Augustus Pius, who restored this temple, which had collapsed, at their own expense, and convened for the sake of mortality, on the 6th Kalends of July, during the consulship of Marullus and Aelianus. Julius Secundinus (father, deceased), Trebius Zoticus (father), Rufius Severinus, Julius Optatus, Septimius Mercator, Septimius Marcus, Tertinius Tertinus, Mamilius Dionysius, Sabinius Hermaiscus (deceased), Sentius Hermes, Priscius Oppidanus, Varius Fortis, Titius Ruso, Annius Syrillio, Lydacius Charito, Baienius Axio (deceased), Rufius Fuscus, Marius Achilleus, Claudius Quintilianus, Julius Carpus, Publilius Moderatus, Mamilius Crescentinus (deceased), Varius Secundus, Marius Eutyches (deceased), Atticius Sextus (father), Atticius Tacitus, Annius Calocaerus, Claudius Mercuralis, Brittannius Syrus, Mercatorius Crispinus, Lydacius Ingenuus, Claudius Quintianus, Cornelius Maturus, Cornelius Florentinus, Trebius Alfius (father), Valerius Hermophilus, Baienius Homuncio, Licinius Marcianus, Julius Philostratus, Volusius Atticus, Aelius Successus, Marcius Marcianus, Claudius Felix, Aelius Marcianus, Varius Sabinus, Gaius Baebius Cupitus, Auconius Eucarpus, Aelius Fuscus, Marius Eutychus, Baebius Bassianus, Quintus Baienius Ingenuus, Gaius Flavius Nectareus (father), Publius Aelius Fuscianus, Marcus Marius Zosimus, Titus Auconius Callimorfus, Lucius Fac Onesimus, Titus Mestrius Respectus, Quintus Septimius Speratus, Lucius Albius Aristo, Aulus Tappetius Chionius, Lutuccius Maro, Marcus Marius Severianus (father), Gaius Julius Maternus, Lucius Candidius Aper, Gaius Priscius Crescentinus, Quintus Julius Adrastus, Trosius Tertullinus, Aelius Secundianus, Calendinus Successus, Antonius Atticianus, Claudius Primitivus, Veponius Chrestus, Auconius Boniatus, Tiberius Claudius Curio, Gaius Julius Secundinus, Claudius Tiberius Dignus, Lucius Valerius Valerianus, Gaius Baebius Ursulus, Gaius Marius Tertullinus, Bellicius Senecio, Summianius Maximus, Publius Aelius Cupitus, Asconius Severinus, Gemellius Gemellianus, Marcus Ulpius Atticus, Aelius Celerinus, Marcus Ulpius Atticianus, Speratus (slave), Lucius Vibius Iuvenis, Helvius Varronianus, Lucius Vibius Aeliomarus, Rufius Saturninus, Marcus Mansuetius Mansuetus, Marcus Bellicius Reditus, Tiberius Claudius Optatus, Gaius Secundius Reditus, Successius Florianus, Lucius Quartinius Quartus, Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus donated a bronze tablet and decorated the vault with paintings upon the dedication of the temple.
EDCS-00380789.summary: This lengthy inscription details the restoration of a Mithraic temple for the health of Emperor Commodus in 180 AD, listing numerous members of the collegium (religious association) who participated and noting that Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus dedicated a bronze tablet and decorated the vault.

EDCS-00380790.text_edition: IL dae Iuni sorori filii fecerunt
EDCS-00380790.translation: To the divine Iuno, his sister, his sons made this.
EDCS-00380790.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription made by sons in honor of their divine sister, Juno (likely a deceased relative deified or associated with the goddess).

EDCS-03000731.summary: This inscription records the name and title of Licinius Rufinus, who served as an imperial legate, likely part of a larger monument or official document.

EDCS-03300721.text_edition: Cai Caesaris Augusti Germanici Imperatoris ex Noricis
EDCS-03300721.translation: Of Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Imperator, from Noricum.
EDCS-03300721.summary: This inscription identifies the Emperor Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus (Caligula) and indicates an origin or connection to the province of Noricum, likely part of a dedicatory or honorific monument.

EDCS-03300725.text_edition: Iovi -ONN-C --I--- Onirus et Aelia Copia ex imperio
EDCS-03300725.translation: To Jupiter [---], Onirus and Aelia Copia [made this] by command.
EDCS-03300725.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Onirus and Aelia Copia, indicating they fulfilled a divine command.

EDCS-03300727.text_edition: Sextus Calventius Ingenuus vivus fecit sibi et Mattiae Meltimae coniugi annorum XL et Cupito filio annorum X et Quintiliano filio annorum VII
EDCS-03300727.translation: Sextus Calventius Ingenuus, alive, made this for himself and for Mattia Meltima, his wife, 40 years old, and for Cupitus, his son, 10 years old, and for Quintilianus, his son, 7 years old.
EDCS-03300727.summary: This is a funerary inscription prepared by Sextus Calventius Ingenuus during his lifetime for himself, his wife Mattia Meltima, and his sons Cupitus and Quintilianus, indicating their ages at death.

EDCS-03300729.text_edition: Resimarus Tocionis sic filius nnorum XXXX hic situs est Vercaius et Vibius filii vivi fecerunt
EDCS-03300729.translation: Resimarus, son of Tocio, 40 years old, is laid here. Vercaius and Vibius, his sons, made this while alive.
EDCS-03300729.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Resimarus, son of Tocio, aged 40, erected by his sons Vercaius and Vibius during their lifetimes.

EDCS-03300731.text_edition: Primus Secundi libertus vivus fecit sibi et Boioniae Surae coniugi annorum LX Censorinae sorori annorum L
EDCS-03300731.translation: Primus, freedman of Secundus, made this while alive for himself and for Boionia Sura, his wife, 60 years old, and for Censorina, his sister, 50 years old.
EDCS-03300731.summary: This is a funerary inscription prepared by Primus, a freedman of Secundus, during his lifetime for himself, his wife Boionia Sura, and his sister Censorina, indicating their ages.

EDCS-03300732.text_edition: Quintus Ennius Quinti filius C vivus fecit sibi et Enniae Quinti libertae Secundae uxori optimae piissimae annorum LX et suis
EDCS-03300732.translation: Quintus Ennius, son of Quintus, of the C tribe, made this for himself while alive, and for Ennia Secunda, freedwoman of Quintus, his excellent and most devoted wife, aged 60, and for his dependents.
EDCS-03300732.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Quintus Ennius for himself, his freedwoman wife, and his dependents, likely dating to the early Roman Empire.

EDCS-03300733.translation: To Bonillo, son of Togio, aged 70.
EDCS-03300733.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bonillo, son of Togio, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-03300734.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibio Marcello annorum  C
EDCS-03300734.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Vibius Marcellus, aged 100.
EDCS-03300734.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Vibius Marcellus, who lived to the remarkable age of 100.

EDCS-03300735.text_edition: Silvanus Sabini filius annorum LXX filius fecit hic situs est Aleto Batavi filius annorum LXX Bonio Silvani filius annorum LX Spectata PVIIE filia annorum LX Boniatus Bonionis annorum L
EDCS-03300735.translation: Silvanus, son of Sabinus, aged 70, his son made this. Here lies Aletus, son of Batavus, aged 70. Bonius, son of Silvanus, aged 60. Spectata, daughter of PVIIE, aged 60. Boniatus, son of Bonio, aged 50.
EDCS-03300735.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely from a shared tomb, recording the names and ages of several individuals, with one son making the dedication for his father.

EDCS-03300739.text_edition: Rustico Secundi filio annorum LX et Gaixuni coniugi OF
EDCS-03300739.translation: To Rusticus, son of Secundus, aged 60, and to his wife Gaixuna, OF.
EDCS-03300739.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rusticus, son of Secundus, aged 60, and his wife Gaixuna, likely dedicated by their family or community.

EDCS-03700652.text_edition: Dis Manibus Secundino Sexti Claudi Alpini @(obito) annorum LX Ursa coniux Ursa con(iux) viva fecit et Secundina filia
EDCS-03700652.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Secundinus, deceased, aged 60, of Sextus Claudius Alpinus. Ursa, his wife, made this while alive, and their daughter Secundina.
EDCS-03700652.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Secundinus, who died at age 60, made by his wife Ursa while she was still alive, and their daughter Secundina.

EDCS-03700653.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Congonneti filius Troucillus mil(es) coh(ortis)? Mont(anorum) primae? stipendiorum  hic situs est
EDCS-03700653.translation: Tiberius Iulius Troucillus, son of Congonnetus, a soldier of the first Cohort of Montani, of [---] years of service, lies here.
EDCS-03700653.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Iulius Troucillus, a soldier of the Cohort of Montani, recording his military service and place of burial.

EDCS-03700654.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Bellicius Victor IIvir decurio civitatis Celeienssium sic Ursina liberta patrono optimo et bene merito @(obito) annorum LXXX fecit
EDCS-03700654.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Bellicius Victor, duumvir and decurion of the city of Celeia. Thus Ursina, his freedwoman, made this for her excellent and well-deserving patron, deceased, aged 80.
EDCS-03700654.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Bellicius Victor, a prominent citizen of Celeia, dedicated by his freedwoman Ursina to her deceased patron, who lived to be 80.

EDCS-04900768.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary or dedicatory, mentioning the names Anna Ma and Aelius Fuscus.

EDCS-04900769.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae sacrum Vibenus votum libens solvit
EDCS-04900769.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta. Vibenus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-04900769.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Victoria Augusta by Vibenus, who willingly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-04900774.translation: Optata, a freedwoman of Tertius, three years old.
EDCS-04900774.summary: This is an epitaph for Optata, a freedwoman who died at the age of three.

EDCS-04900776.translation: The 16th day before the Kalends of September, Isauricus.
EDCS-04900776.summary: This inscription records a date, August 17th, possibly related to an event or the lifespan of Isauricus.

EDCS-04900780.translation: Vibenus, son of [---]enus, and [---]nucco, son of [---] Quartus.
EDCS-04900780.summary: This inscription, likely a funerary or dedicatory text, mentions two individuals, Vibenus and [---]nucco, specifying their patronymics.

EDCS-04900781.text_edition: Publio Giamillio Primigenio annorum LXX et Censorinae coniugi annorum L
EDCS-04900781.translation: To Publius Giamillius Primigenius, 70 years old, and to Censorina, his wife, 50 years old.
EDCS-04900781.summary: This is an epitaph for Publius Giamillius Primigenius, aged 70, and his wife Censorina, aged 50.

EDCS-04900783.summary: This is a dedication made by Quadratius Vitalis to the goddess Epona.

EDCS-04900784.text_edition: Caio Talamerio Sabino AO SRIA NA O
EDCS-04900784.translation: To Gaius Talamerius Sabinus. [Remaining text is unclear].
EDCS-04900784.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Gaius Talamerius Sabinus, though its exact purpose is unclear due to illegible text.

EDCS-04900786.text_edition: Satuco Tutionis fili- annorum IX et Bacaucuni Baustionis F et Sammo eto annorum XXV
EDCS-04900786.translation: To Satucus, son of Tutio, nine years old, and to Bacaucunus, son of Baustio, and to Sammo [---], twenty-five years old.
EDCS-04900786.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Satucus, who died at age nine, Bacaucunus, and Sammo, who died at age twenty-five.

EDCS-04900791.text_edition: Macrino AcGili#Acili#AGIL Censorini servo Satulla soror fecit
EDCS-04900791.translation: To Macrinus Acilius, a slave of Censorinus, his sister Satulla made this.
EDCS-04900791.summary: This is an epitaph for Macrinus Acilius, a slave, erected by his sister Satulla.

EDCS-04900793.text_edition: Titus Flavius Euphrosynus vivus fecit sibi et libertis libertabusque hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-04900793.translation: Titus Flavius Euphrosynus, while living, made this monument for himself and for his freedmen and freedwomen; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-04900793.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Titus Flavius Euphrosynus during his lifetime for himself and his freed dependents, explicitly excluding inheritance.

EDCS-04900794.summary: This inscription identifies a woman named Vettia Prima, specifying her father as Gaius, likely part of a funerary or familial record.

EDCS-04900799.text_edition: Vervicio Nemetilli filio et niciae Varconis filiae et verae Vervici filiae pater vivus fecit etto Vervici filio
EDCS-04900799.translation: To Vervicius, son of Nemetilla, and to Nicia, daughter of Varco, and to Vera, daughter of Vervicius, their father made this while living, and to Vervicius, his son.
EDCS-04900799.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a father making a memorial for himself and his children while still alive, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-04900800.text_edition: Vegeto Secundi filio et Saturninae Gemelli filiae coniugi
EDCS-04900800.translation: To Vegetus, son of Secundus, and to Saturnina, daughter of Gemellus, his wife.
EDCS-04900800.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Vegetus and his wife Saturnina, likely set up by surviving family members in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-04900801.text_edition: Velina Severus ma de pecunia sua
EDCS-04900801.translation: Velina Severus, from his own money.
EDCS-04900801.summary: This brief inscription records Velina Severus funding something from his own money, likely a public work, dedication, or tomb, in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-04900804.text_edition: Volkano sic Augusto sacrum Publius Sextius V
EDCS-04900804.translation: Sacred to Volkanus Augustus. Publius Sextius V[---].
EDCS-04900804.summary: This is a votive dedication to Volkanus Augustus, offered by Publius Sextius, likely a private individual fulfilling a vow, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-04900805.text_edition: Attius Avitus sibi et Maximillae M uxori optimae
EDCS-04900805.translation: Attius Avitus for himself and for Maximilla, his most excellent wife.
EDCS-04900805.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Attius Avitus for himself and his beloved wife Maximilla, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-04900810.text_edition: Deo Invicto Soli maritimi votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-04900810.translation: To the Unconquered Sun god, seafarers gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-04900810.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Unconquered Sun god, dedicated by a group of seafarers in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period, perhaps the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-04900814.text_edition: Lucius Egnatius Secundus testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-04900814.translation: Lucius Egnatius Secundus ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-04900814.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Lucius Egnatius Secundus arranged for his memorial to be constructed through his will, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-04900815.text_edition: Marcus? Claudius Marci filius Claudia Rufus IIvir iure dicundo sibi et Corneliae Cai filiae Crispinae uxori optimae et  Claudio  filio annorum XVIII
EDCS-04900815.translation: Marcus? Claudius, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, Rufus, duovir for administering justice, for himself and for Cornelia Crispina, daughter of Gaius, his most excellent wife, and for Claudius, his son, aged 18 years.
EDCS-04900815.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a prominent local magistrate, Marcus Claudius Rufus, who was a duovir, and his family, including his wife Cornelia Crispina and his 18-year-old son, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-04900816.translation: To Magius Dato, and to Aelia Ingenua, while living.
EDCS-04900816.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Magius Dato and Aelia Ingenua prepared their tomb while still alive, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05200537.text_edition: Domestico Sexti Curi Bassi liberto et Pontiae Secundae uxori vivae filii vivi fecerunt et Avito fratri nnorum X Pontio Secundo annorum L
EDCS-05200537.translation: To Domesticus, freedman of Sextus Curius Bassus, and to Pontia Secunda, his living wife, their living sons made this, and to Avitus, their brother, aged 10 years, and Pontius Secundus, aged 50 years.
EDCS-05200537.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman, Domesticus, his wife Pontia Secunda, and two other family members, Avitus and Pontius Secundus, erected by their sons while still alive, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-05200538.text_edition: tus nis filius et a Elvissi f(ilia)? vivi fecerunt sibisibi Vero filio et Verino  nepoti
EDCS-05200538.translation: Tusnis, son, and A. Elvissi, daughter (?), made this while alive for themselves, for their son Verus, and for their grandson Verinus.
EDCS-05200538.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Tusnis and A. Elvissi for themselves and their descendants while they were still living.

EDCS-05200539.text_edition: Sentio Nigro decurioni alae Petrianae civium Romanorum torquatae decurioni Virunensium Sentia Nigrina filia fecit
EDCS-05200539.translation: Sentia Nigrina, daughter, made this for Sentius Niger, decurion of the Ala Petriana of Roman citizens, decorated with torques, decurion of the Virunenses.
EDCS-05200539.summary: This inscription records a monument dedicated by Sentia Nigrina to her father, Sentius Niger, a distinguished Roman cavalry officer and municipal decurion.

EDCS-05200540.text_edition: Aelius Maximus vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Serotinae uxori dignissimae
EDCS-05200540.translation: Aelius Maximus made this while alive for himself and for Julia Serotina, his most worthy wife.
EDCS-05200540.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Aelius Maximus for himself and his wife, Julia Serotina, during his lifetime.

EDCS-05200541.text_edition: Aulus Terentius Felix et Fusca Suri filia vivi fecerunt sibi et
EDCS-05200541.translation: Aulus Terentius Felix and Fusca, daughter of Surus, made this while alive for themselves and...
EDCS-05200541.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription records a monument made by Aulus Terentius Felix and Fusca for themselves while still living.

EDCS-05200543.text_edition: Iuvenalis et Bausu B H servi vivi fecerunt sibi et Ursulo filio annorum XXV
EDCS-05200543.translation: Juvenalis and Bausu B.H., slaves, made this while alive for themselves and for their son Ursulus, aged 25 years.
EDCS-05200543.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Juvenalis and Bausu, who were slaves, for themselves and their 25-year-old son, Ursulus, during their lifetimes.

EDCS-05200544.text_edition: Bonia Vivani sibi et Mulioni coniugi defuncto nnorum LXX Talio Urbici sibi
EDCS-05200544.translation: Bonia Vivani (made this) for herself and for her deceased husband Mulio, aged 70 years; Talio Urbici (made this) for himself.
EDCS-05200544.summary: This funerary inscription records monuments made by Bonia Vivani for herself and her deceased husband Mulio, and by Talio Urbici for himself.

EDCS-05200545.text_edition: Proclo Sexti filio annorum LXX et Mentitae Vindicis filiae coniugi
EDCS-05200545.translation: To Proclus, son of Sextus, aged 70 years, and to Mentita, daughter of Vindex, his wife.
EDCS-05200545.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication to Proclus, son of Sextus, aged 70, and his wife, Mentita, daughter of Vindex.

EDCS-05200546.translation: To Mascul[us], son of Ni[---], aged 5, and...
EDCS-05200546.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription records a dedication to Mascul[us], son of Ni[---], who died at age 5.

EDCS-05200547.summary: This very fragmentary inscription mentions a person named Quadratus, possibly indicating a profession or status and parts of a name.

EDCS-05200549.text_edition: Boionius Primus Firmino fratri carissimo et coniugi eius fecit
EDCS-05200549.translation: Boionius Primus made this for Firminus, his dearest brother, and for his wife.
EDCS-05200549.summary: This inscription records a monument made by Boionius Primus for his dearest brother Firminus and Firminus's wife.

EDCS-05200550.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Ulpius Nativos sic decurio alae I
EDCS-05200550.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, Ulpius Nativos, decurion of the First Wing, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05200550.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Ulpius Nativos, a decurion of an ala, likely a military unit, dating to the early-mid 3rd century AD.

EDCS-06100473.translation: To Ursio, son of Marcianus, 50 years old.
EDCS-06100473.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ursio, son of Marcianus, who died at the age of 50, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-06100477.text_edition: Iuliae Titi gena et  Avitae Martiali  Aeliae Rufillae  Rufiae Mart Ael Mai
EDCS-06100477.translation: To Iulia Titi Gena, and Avita Martialis, Aelia Rufilla, Rufia Martiana, Aelia Maior.
EDCS-06100477.summary: This inscription lists several women's names, likely on a tombstone or monument commemorating a family, possibly dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-06100495.text_edition: et Potentinia CL coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Aureliae Antoniae filiae vivivae
EDCS-06100495.translation: And Potentinia C. L., his wife, made this in their lifetime for themselves and for Aurelia Antonia, their daughter, who is alive.
EDCS-06100495.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Potentinia C. L. and her husband while they were alive, for themselves and their living daughter Aurelia Antonia, indicating a family tomb, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-07000801.text_edition: Lolliae Atticae @(obitae) annis XII Lollia Onesime filiae pientissimae fecit
EDCS-07000801.translation: Lollia Onesime made this for her most devoted daughter Lollia Attica, who died at 12 years of age.
EDCS-07000801.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Lollia Onesime for her daughter Lollia Attica, who passed away at the age of twelve, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07400585.text_edition: Tito Vario Titi filio Cla(udia) Cel(eia) Prisco procuratori provinciarum  Mauretaniae Tingitanae Daciae inferioris praefecto classis Britannicae praefecto alae I Ulpiae contariorum miliariae praefecto alae I Taurianorum torquatae victricis praefecto alae I Hispanorum Campagonum civium Romanorum tribuno legionis  praefecto cohortis I Lusitanorum equitatae praesidi optimo decuriones alaries cohortales centuriones et officiales provecti ab eo
EDCS-07400585.translation: To Titus Varius, son of Titus, of the Claudia tribe, Ce[r]el[is] Priscus, procurator of the provinces of Mauretania Tingitana and Dacia Inferior, prefect of the British fleet, prefect of the First Ulpian cohort of contarii, a thousand strong, prefect of the First Taurian cohort, decorated with torques, victorious, prefect of the First cohort of Spanish Campagoni, Roman citizens, tribune of the legion, prefect of the First cohort of Lusitanian cavalry; to an excellent governor, the decurions, alares, cohortales, centurions, and officials promoted by him, [dedicated this].
EDCS-07400585.summary: This is a comprehensive inscription honoring Titus Varius Priscus, detailing his extensive military and administrative career, set up by those he promoted, likely dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07400586.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Opelli Severi Macrini Pii Felicis Augusti et Marci Opelli Antonini Diadumeniani nobilissimi Caesaris Aurelius Aquila et Aurelius Bassus et Varinus sacerdotes templum vi ignis cunsumptum
EDCS-07400586.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, for the health of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, noblest Caesar, Aurelius Aquila, Aurelius Bassus, and Varinus, priests, [rebuilt] the temple consumed by the force of fire.
EDCS-07400586.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of a temple dedicated to Jupiter Dolichenus, which had been destroyed by fire, by three priests (Aurelius Aquila, Aurelius Bassus, and Varinus), for the well-being of Emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, thus dating it to 217-218 AD.

EDCS-07400588.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XI consul II pro consule pater patriae et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix tribunicia potestate III pro consule et Publius Septimius Geta Caesar miliaria vetustate conlabsa sic restituerunt curante Marco Iuventio Suro Proculo legato pro praetore a Celeie sic milia passuum III
EDCS-07400588.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 9th time, hailed as imperator for the 11th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, father of his country, and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, proconsul, and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, restored the milestone, which had collapsed due to old age, under the care of Marcus Iuventius Surus Proculus, legate pro praetore, at the third mile from Celeia.
EDCS-07400588.summary: This inscription commemorates the restoration of a milestone, which had fallen due to age, by Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, under the supervision of Marcus Iuventius Surus Proculus, dating it specifically to 201 AD.

EDCS-07400589.text_edition: Acutus C S servus vivus fecit sibi et Verecundae nnorum  filio Supero annorum IX et Domestica filia  Casto C V servo annorum
EDCS-07400589.translation: Acutus, a slave of C. S., made this in his lifetime for himself and for Verecunda, and for his son Superus, 9 years old, and for his daughter Domestica, [and] for Castus, a slave of C. V., [who was] X years old.
EDCS-07400589.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Acutus, a slave, during his lifetime for himself and his family, including his children Superus and Domestica, and another slave named Castus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07600337.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Sabinius Aelio Moderatus Cai filius beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07600337.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Sabinius Aelius Moderatus, son of Gaius, beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italic Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600337.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest, made by Gaius Sabinius Aelius Moderatus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Italica, indicating a fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-07600343.text_edition: Vibe sic sacrum Mosiqu sic Qupiti sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-07600343.translation: To Vibe, thus sacred, and to Mosicus, thus to Jupiter, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600343.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Vibe, Mosicus, and Jupiter.

EDCS-07600344.text_edition: Privato Turturi et Secundinae Fannionis Plus filius vivus sibi et parentibus
EDCS-07600344.translation: For Privatus Turtur and Secundina, daughter of Fannio, Plus, their son, while living, made this for himself and his parents.
EDCS-07600344.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Plus, while alive, dedicated a monument for himself and his parents, Privatus Turtur and Secundina.

EDCS-07600345.text_edition: Valentis filio et Bononiae Cattunis filiae coniugi filii fecerunt
EDCS-07600345.translation: They made this for their son Valentis and for Bononia Cattunis, their son's wife, their daughter-in-law.
EDCS-07600345.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by parents for their son Valentis and their daughter-in-law Bononia Cattunis.

EDCS-07600346.text_edition: Caius Attius Cai filius Sedatus sibi et Domitiae Palumbi filiae uxori karissimae et Attio Vibio filio et Attiae Vibiae nepti et Titiae Successae nurui pientissimae
EDCS-07600346.translation: Gaius Attius Sedatus, son of Gaius, for himself and for Domitia Palumbi, daughter of Palumbus, his dearest wife, and for Attius Vibius, his son, and for Attia Vibia, his granddaughter, and for Titia Successa, his most devoted daughter-in-law.
EDCS-07600346.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Attius Sedatus for himself and various members of his family, including his wife, son, granddaughter, and daughter-in-law.

EDCS-08400430.text_edition: A Iucundus et Vibenus Finiti filii fratres votum solverunt
EDCS-08400430.translation: A. Jucundus and Vibenus, sons of Finitus, brothers, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-08400430.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by two brothers, A. Jucundus and Vibenus, sons of Finitus.

EDCS-08600546.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Marina ex voto posuit
EDCS-08600546.translation: Marina placed this to Juno Regina, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08600546.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marina to Juno Regina, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08600547.text_edition: Iovi Dolicheno Aelius Secundianus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08600547.translation: Aelius Secundianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Dolichenus.
EDCS-08600547.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aelius Secundianus to Jupiter Dolichenus.

EDCS-08600548.text_edition: Dolicheno  Iovi Optimo Maximo Successus Potentis votum reddidit libens laetus merito
EDCS-08600548.translation: Successus, son of Potens, willingly, happily, and deservedly rendered his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.
EDCS-08600548.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Successus, son of Potens, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.

EDCS-08600549.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Duligeno sic  votum solvit laetus merito Facundus
EDCS-08600549.translation: Facundus happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.
EDCS-08600549.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Facundus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.

EDCS-08600550.text_edition: Matugena Iovi Dulicheno sic votum solvit laeta libens  Matugena Iunoni votum solvit libens laeta  merito
EDCS-08600550.translation: Matugena happily and willingly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Dolichenus. Matugena willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Juno.
EDCS-08600550.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of vows by Matugena to both Jupiter Dolichenus and Juno.

EDCS-08600551.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Mer solvit votum libens laetus merito
EDCS-08600551.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aurelius Mer willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08600551.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Aurelius Mer to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08600552.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dulicheno sic Vera votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08600552.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, of Doliche, Vera thus willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-08600552.summary: This inscription is a votive offering made by Vera to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, indicating devotion to theichean Jupiter.

EDCS-08600553.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dulicheno sic Apollonius Runna votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-08600553.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, of Doliche, Apollonius Runna thus willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08600553.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication by Apollonius Runna to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, a Syrian deity popular in the Roman Empire.

EDCS-08600554.text_edition: Dulicheno sic palmam scri=IR(ipsi)#scri(ipsi)#SCIR libens laetus merito
EDCS-08600554.translation: To Doliche, I thus inscribed a palm willingly, joyfully, and deservedly.
EDCS-08600554.summary: This inscription, dedicated to Doliche, records the act of inscribing a palm, likely as a votive offering or mark of victory.

EDCS-08600555.text_edition: Dulicheno sic Veneria votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08600555.translation: To Doliche, Veneria thus willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-08600555.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Veneria to the god Doliche, a variant of Jupiter Dolichenus.

EDCS-08600556.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Surus votum solvit libens merito  MOI Surus
EDCS-08600556.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Surus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. MOI Surus.
EDCS-08600556.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Surus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, with an additional, unclear inscription by Surus himself.

EDCS-08600557.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Aestivus votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-08600557.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, of Doliche, Aestivus thus willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08600557.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Aestivus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, reflecting a common dedication to this deity.

EDCS-08600567.text_edition: Claudio Tacito et Restitutae Montani filiae uxori filii fecerunt
EDCS-08600567.translation: For Claudius Tacitus and Restituta, daughter of Montanus, wife of their son, their sons made this.
EDCS-08600567.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by sons for their father, Claudius Tacitus, and their sister-in-law, Restituta, daughter of Montanus.

EDCS-08600570.text_edition: Iomatio Velsonis filio militi cohortis IIII Tungrorum stipendiorum XXXIII Firminae filiae annorum III filii faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-08600570.translation: For Iomatius, son of Velso, soldier of the Fourth Cohort of Tungrians, with 33 years of service; for Firmina, his daughter, 3 years old; his sons arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-08600570.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Iomatius, a veteran soldier, and his young daughter Firmina, erected by his sons.

EDCS-08900508.text_edition: Successus Celeianorum nnorum XXXX hic situs est Successus Celeianorum nnorum XXV Exoratus Celeianorum annorum XXV hic situs est
EDCS-08900508.translation: Successus of the Celeiani, 40 years old, lies here. Successus of the Celeiani, 25 years old. Exoratus of the Celeiani, 25 years old, lies here.
EDCS-08900508.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument commemorating three individuals, Successus (two different individuals) and Exoratus, all identified as members of the Celeiani, specifying their ages and burial place.

EDCS-08900509.text_edition: coniugi nnorum X Lucio Fabio Fortunato filio nnorum XXXX
EDCS-08900509.translation: To my husband of ten years, Lucius Fabius Fortunatus, and to my son of forty years.
EDCS-08900509.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely dedicated by a woman to her deceased husband and son.

EDCS-08901074.summary: This inscription records a person named Us Surulus from Volubilis, possibly in connection with a quantity of rings, suggesting an inventory or offering.

EDCS-08901075.summary: This inscription lists items, 110 discs and boats, associated with Orosius of Volubilis, possibly detailing goods or a dedicatory inventory.

EDCS-08901076.text_edition: N et Mo coniux et Quin Tinianus filius annorum XXV filii f(ecerunt)
EDCS-08901076.translation: N. and Mo., his wife, and Quin Tinianus, their son, 25 years old. The sons made this.
EDCS-08901076.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by sons for their parents, N. and Mo., and their deceased brother Quin Tinianus.

EDCS-09301158.text_edition: Aelius Ingenuus obitus annorum LX et Iucunda filia annorum VI Iulia coniunx et Festo annorum XX filio fecit et Novellus filius
EDCS-09301158.translation: Aelius Ingenuus, died at 60 years old, and Iucunda, his daughter, 6 years old. Julia, his wife, and Novellus, his son, made this for Festo, their son, 20 years old.
EDCS-09301158.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julia, a wife and mother, and Novellus, a son, for Aelius Ingenuus, Iucunda, and Festo, commemorating multiple deceased family members.

EDCS-09301159.text_edition: Lupius Severis libertus obitus annorum XX Ius uxor eius marito eFt#et#FT sibi et suis viva fecit
EDCS-09301159.translation: Lupius Severis, a freedman, died at 20 years old. Ius, his wife, made this for her husband and for herself and her family while alive.
EDCS-09301159.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Ius for her deceased husband, Lupius Severis, and also prepared by her for herself and her family during her lifetime.

EDCS-09301160.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a name like Rustic[---] and identifying them as the son of someone whose name began with Au[---].

EDCS-09400579.summary: This short inscription appears to be a personal statement or attribution, perhaps marking possession or a dedication, linking Adtressa and Vibebos.

EDCS-09400582.text_edition: VS VRSIIS votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09400582.translation: VS URSIIS willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09400582.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that VS URSIIS fulfilled a vow willingly and deservedly, likely to a deity.

EDCS-09400587.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mercury by Aticus.

EDCS-09401144.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mythrae sic sacrum Maturus servus actor domini nostri
EDCS-09401144.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered God Mithras. So sacred, Maturus, slave and agent of our master, [made this].
EDCS-09401144.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mithras by Maturus, a slave and agent, suggesting a private religious act by someone of unfree status.

EDCS-09401153.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Craconi Ulpia Nundina viva fecit sibi et Ingenuus pater
EDCS-09401153.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Aurelius Craco, Ulpia Nundina, alive, made this for herself and for Ingenuus, her father.
EDCS-09401153.summary: This is a funerary inscription, set up by Ulpia Nundina while still alive, for Aurelius Craco, herself, and her father Ingenuus, likely anticipating a family burial plot.

EDCS-09401155.text_edition: Aquoni sacrum Caius Statius Masculus et Publicius Ianuarius votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-09401155.translation: Sacred to Aquo. Gaius Statius Masculus and Publicius Ianuarius willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-09401155.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication to the deity Aquo, where Gaius Statius Masculus and Publicius Ianuarius fulfilled a vow they had made.

EDCS-09401156.text_edition: Aquoni Abascantus Luci T P servus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09401156.translation: To Aquo. Abascantus, slave of Lucius T. P., willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09401156.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the deity Aquo, made by Abascantus, a slave of Lucius T. P., in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09401158.text_edition: noni annorum L ius filius fecit et AEC PI
EDCS-09401158.translation: for Noni, aged 50. His son made this, and AEC PI.
EDCS-09401158.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a person named Noni, aged 50, set up by their son, with other unclear participants.

EDCS-09500109.text_edition: Dis Manibus Voltian?  viv- fecit sibi et Ve
EDCS-09500109.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Voltian[us/a] made this while alive for himself/herself and Ve[---].
EDCS-09500109.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Voltian[us/a] during their lifetime for themselves and another individual, likely for a family tomb.

EDCS-09500110.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus)? Mansueto Covnerti et Covnert erti  carissim-- VE I
EDCS-09500110.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Mansuetus, son of Covnertus, and Covnert[us] [---] most dear [---].
EDCS-09500110.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Mansuetus, son of Covnertus, and possibly another individual, indicating a family memorial.

EDCS-09500111.text_edition: Quarti filius sibi et niae coniugi et Quarto et Boniae parentibus et Marino fratri pientissimo et Quartillae sorori karissimae et Prisc-- Lascivae fili-- et Amandae
EDCS-09500111.translation: Quartus, son of Quartus, [made this] for himself and for [Anto]nia, his wife, and for Quartus and Bonia, his parents, and for Marinus, his most devoted brother, and for Quartilla, his dearest sister, and for Prisc[a] Lasciva, his daughter, and for Amanda.
EDCS-09500111.summary: This is a comprehensive funerary monument dedicated by Quartus, son of Quartus, for himself and numerous family members including his wife, parents, siblings, and daughters, demonstrating a detailed family remembrance.

EDCS-09500112.text_edition: Vitali et Sextae et Ampliato Perennis servus pientissimus vivus fecit
EDCS-09500112.translation: Perennis, a most devoted slave, made this while alive for Vitalis and Sexta and Ampliatus.
EDCS-09500112.summary: This is a funerary monument made by a devoted slave named Perennis, while still alive, for three other individuals: Vitalis, Sexta, and Ampliatus.

EDCS-09500116.text_edition: IIRIII s et Valeri-- RecI et Varia Respecta donum dederunt  T eis ne defuncto NNMFE  nio s us  Quarti  Verinus Ingenui inus inani s us i Primitivi nus s s tus i s i i i s i  Aurelius Iulius Valentinus Primitivi Claudius Claudianus Vitalius Aprilis Iunilius Martius Genialis Ingenui Pompeius Chryseros Volusius Ursus Gemellinus Balosi Iulius Nicephor Quartus Balosi Serenianus Placidi Ursus Sabini Marius Thallus Antonius Primus Valerius Nigrinus Valerius Respectus Successus Baieni Atilius Felix Troianius Flavinus
EDCS-09500116.translation: [---] and Valerius [---] and Varia Respecta gave this gift [---] to them, not to the deceased [---] Quartus [---] Verinus, son of Ingenuus [---] Ingenuinus, son of Ingenuus [---] Aurelius Julius Valentinus, son of Primitivus, Claudius Claudianus, Vitalius Aprilis, Iunilius Martius, Genialis, son of Ingenuus, Pompeius Chryseros, Volusius Ursus, Gemellinus, son of Balosus, Julius Nicephor, Quartus, son of Balosus, Serenianus, son of Placidus, Ursus, son of Sabinus, Marius Thallus, Antonius Primus, Valerius Nigrinus, Valerius Respectus, Successus, son of Baienus, Atilius Felix, Troianius Flavinus.
EDCS-09500116.summary: This extensive inscription appears to be a list of names, likely a dedication or a record of benefactors or members of a collegium, possibly with a funerary context due to the mention of 'the deceased' but primarily listing individuals with their patronymics.

EDCS-09500126.text_edition: Matri Deum Magnae Cassius Restutus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09500126.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods, Cassius Restutus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09500126.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the Great Mother of the Gods by Cassius Restutus, likely on an altar or dedicatory plaque.

EDCS-10000404.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Sextus Valerius.
EDCS-10000404.summary: This is a simple funerary or civil inscription identifying Lucius Valerius as the son of Sextus Valerius, possibly from a tombstone or statue base.

EDCS-10700593.text_edition: sibi et Iuliae Censorinae filiae et Iulio Sabino filio et Claudio Decriano marito @(obito) annorum L decurioni ornato aput sic municipium Altinatium
EDCS-10700593.translation: For herself and her daughter Julia Censorina, and her son Julius Sabinus, and her husband Claudius Decrianus, who died at 50 years old, an honored decurion in the municipality of Altinum.
EDCS-10700593.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by an unnamed woman for herself and her family members, including her deceased husband, Claudius Decrianus, who was a decurion of Altinum.

EDCS-11300984.text_edition: Savo et Atsallutae sic Caius Iulius Iustus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-11300984.translation: To Savus and Atsalluta, Gaius Julius Justus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11300984.summary: This inscription records Gaius Julius Justus's willing and deserved fulfillment of a vow to the deities Savus and Atsalluta, likely on a votive offering.

EDCS-11300985.text_edition: Anta Ressati filia viva fecit sibi et Castori coniugi annorum LX
EDCS-11300985.translation: Anta, daughter of Ressatus, made this while living for herself and her husband Castor, who lived for 60 years.
EDCS-11300985.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Anta, daughter of Ressatus, made a tomb for herself and her deceased husband Castor during her lifetime.

EDCS-11300986.text_edition: abili annorum LXX uro filio annorum  coniugi annorum  nnrum XX
EDCS-11300986.translation: [For her son, who was] 70 years old, [and her] husband, [and ...] 20 years old.
EDCS-11300986.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription likely records a tomb dedicated to family members, including a son and a husband, with their ages partially preserved.

EDCS-11301657.text_edition: Cautopati Tiberius Dabonius Ingenuus cum suis
EDCS-11301657.translation: To Cautopates, Tiberius Dabonius Ingenuus with his family.
EDCS-11301657.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deity Cautopates by Tiberius Dabonius Ingenuus and his family, likely on a votive altar or statue base.

EDCS-11500006.text_edition: Vibebos posuerunt Serenus et Maru votum solverunt
EDCS-11500006.translation: Vibebos. Serenus and Maru placed this and fulfilled their vows.
EDCS-11500006.summary: This votive inscription records that Serenus and Maru placed a monument and fulfilled their vows to Vibebos, likely a local deity.

EDCS-11800774.text_edition: QVI Θ(εοῖς) Χθονίοις ex voto suscepto Cadius Primus Horatius Antipatrus Pataris scifos
EDCS-11800774.translation: To the Chthonic Gods, in accordance with the vow undertaken, Cadius Primus, Horatius Antipater, and Pataris [dedicated these] bowls.
EDCS-11800774.summary: This inscription records a dedication of bowls (scifos) to the Chthonic Gods by Cadius Primus, Horatius Antipater, and Pataris, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-12000826.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinu[s] Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate co(n)s(ul) II pater patriae consul proconsul et Marcus Opelliu[s] Antoninus Diadumenian[us nobilissimus] Caesar princeps iuventutis providentissimi Augusti fecerunt a Cetio milia passuum XVI
EDCS-12000826.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, father of the country, consul, proconsul, and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, princeps iuventutis, these most provident Augusti, made [this] from Cetius, 16 miles away.
EDCS-12000826.summary: This monumental inscription records a construction or repair project, such as a road or building, carried out by the emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, dating to their reign (217-218 AD), located 16 miles from Cetius.

EDCS-12000827.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Flavio Valerio Liciniano Licinio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto
EDCS-12000827.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Licinianus Licinius, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated Augustus.
EDCS-12000827.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Licinius, likely from a statue base or milestone, dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-12000831.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely part of a tombstone or a more extensive dedication, identifying the individual Secundinus and his father.

EDCS-12100877.text_edition: Publi Atti Severi Luci Pulli Daphni Marci Servili Getae
EDCS-12100877.translation: Of Publius Attius Severus, Lucius Pullus Daphnus, Marcus Servilius Geta.
EDCS-12100877.summary: This inscription lists the names of three individuals, possibly indicating co-ownership, a shared monument, or a group dedication, perhaps dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-12700112.text_edition: Nixibus sanctis  pro salute Constitutes sic
EDCS-12700112.translation: To the holy efforts, for the well-being of Constitutes, thus.
EDCS-12700112.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made to "holy efforts" (or perhaps a divine power associated with them) for the health of an individual named Constitutes, indicating a personal prayer or offering.

EDCS-13302410.translation: Harigastus, of the third vexillation of Illyrian alae (wings of cavalry).
EDCS-13302410.summary: This inscription identifies Harigastus as a soldier serving in the third vexillation of Illyrian cavalry units, providing military occupational information.

EDCS-13400135.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari domini nostro Marco Aurelio Caro Pio Felici Invicto Augusto et principi iuventutis Marco Aurelio Carino nobilissimo Caesari ab Agunto milia passuum LVII
EDCS-13400135.translation: To the Emperor Caesar our lord Marcus Aurelius Carus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated Augustus, and to the prince of youth Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar, 57 miles from Agunto.
EDCS-13400135.summary: This inscription is a milestone or honorific dedication to the Emperors Carus and Carinus, indicating a distance from the town of Agunto, dating to the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-13600390.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely from a tombstone or an honorary monument, identifying an individual named Publius Cipius Polybius.

EDCS-13600398.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae sacrum Tiberius Iulius Ursulus veteranus votum retttulit pro se et suis libens laetus merito solvit
EDCS-13600398.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, Tiberius Julius Ursulus, a veteran, fulfilled his vow for himself and his kin, willingly, joyfully, deservedly paid.
EDCS-13600398.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the god Mithras by Tiberius Julius Ursulus, a veteran, expressing gratitude and fulfillment of a vow for his family's well-being, typical of Mithraic cults.

EDCS-13700338.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Hilarinus Hilari filius votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-13700338.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Hilarinus, son of Hilarus, willingly, joyfully, deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13700338.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the god Mithras by Hilarinus, son of Hilarus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow, characteristic of Mithraic worship.

EDCS-13800200.text_edition: Philodamus Laribus dono dedit libens merito
EDCS-13800200.translation: Philodamus gave this as a gift to the Lares, willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-13800200.summary: This inscription records a gift or dedication made by Philodamus to the Lares, the Roman household gods, indicating an act of piety or a votive offering.

EDCS-13800201.summary: This is a brief annotation, possibly indicating authorship or publication by Claudianus, perhaps of a document or text.

EDCS-13800202.text_edition: O    RO   Sacrificium capricorni Caio Vibio Postumo consule pridie  NoveImbres#Novembres#NOVIMB Gallus fecit Iu |(quincuncem) vini |(trientem) crusti |(bessem) murrae   LIT QVISVO
EDCS-13800202.translation: O.R.O. A sacrifice of a goat was performed by Gallus on the day before the Kalends of November, in the consulship of Gaius Vibius Postumus. One ounce of wine, one third of a measure of crust, two-thirds of a measure of myrrh. LET WHOSOEVER...
EDCS-13800202.summary: This inscription records a sacrifice of a goat performed by Gallus, specifying the date by the consulship of Gaius Vibius Postumus (most likely 154 AD) and listing offerings of wine, crust, and myrrh, probably for a religious observance or ritual.

EDCS-13900087.text_edition: Herquli sic Augusto Aurelius Mer solvit votum libens laetus merito
EDCS-13900087.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Aurelius Mer willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-13900087.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Aurelius Mer to Hercules Augustus, expressing the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-13900440.summary: This is a simple dedicatory or memorial inscription for Publius Clodius Quirinalis.

EDCS-14400010.translation: Restitutius Tertullus made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-14400010.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Restitutius Tertullus commissioned his own tomb or monument during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400011.text_edition: Attico Tuti filio et Quartae filiae Tiberius Claudius Uppius et Muso Secci filia mater et Belatulla Atuci filia uxor vivi fecerunt et sibi
EDCS-14400011.translation: For Atticus, son of Tutus, and Quarta, his daughter, Tiberius Claudius Uppius and Muso, daughter of Seccus, his mother, and Belatulla, daughter of Atucus, his wife, made this while alive, and for themselves.
EDCS-14400011.summary: This is a family funerary monument, erected by Tiberius Claudius Uppius, his mother Muso, and his wife Belatulla for Atticus and Quarta, and also for themselves, while still living.

EDCS-14400012.text_edition: Lucio Barbio Luci filio Sperato et Barbiae Quinti filiae Augurinae Sextus Barbius Finitus filius fecit
EDCS-14400012.translation: To Lucius Barbius Speratus, son of Lucius, and to Barbia Augurina, daughter of Quintus, Sextus Barbius Finitus, their son, made this.
EDCS-14400012.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Sextus Barbius Finitus for his parents, Lucius Barbius Speratus and Barbia Augurina.

EDCS-14400013.text_edition: Masculina Masculi viva fecit sibi et Tertio Cupiti coniugi optimo
EDCS-14400013.translation: Masculina, daughter of Masculi, made this for herself while alive, and for Tertius Cupitus, her excellent husband.
EDCS-14400013.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Masculina commissioned a monument for herself during her lifetime and for her husband, Tertius Cupitus.

EDCS-14400014.text_edition: Corinthiae Kani filiae matri pientissimae Censorinus Candidi filius et sibi fecit
EDCS-14400014.translation: To Corinthia, daughter of Canus, a most devoted mother, Censorinus, son of Candidus, made this for her and for himself.
EDCS-14400014.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Censorinus for his mother, Corinthia, and also for himself, indicating it was likely a family tomb.

EDCS-14400016.text_edition: Publio Aelio Clementi veterano ex |(centurione) et Claudiae Carae uxori filii fecerunt
EDCS-14400016.translation: To Publius Aelius Clemens, a veteran ex-centurion, and to Claudia Cara, his wife, their sons made this.
EDCS-14400016.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by the sons for their parents, Publius Aelius Clemens, a veteran centurion, and his wife Claudia Cara.

EDCS-14400017.text_edition: Cornelia Quinti filia viva fecit sibi et Finito et CI
EDCS-14400017.translation: Cornelia, daughter of Quintus, made this in her lifetime for herself and Finitus and CI.
EDCS-14400017.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Cornelia for herself and two other individuals while she was still alive.

EDCS-14400019.text_edition: Titus Fannius Quartus sibi et Valeriae Iuliae uxori et Fannio Valerio filio annorum XXX
EDCS-14400019.translation: Titus Fannius Quartus [made this] for himself and Valeria Iulia, his wife, and Fannius Valerius, his son, aged 30 years.
EDCS-14400019.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Fannius Quartus, his wife, and his son, who died at 30 years old.

EDCS-14400020.text_edition: Martio vivus fecit sibi et Lupo filio karissimo
EDCS-14400020.translation: Martius, while alive, made this for himself and his dearest son Lupus.
EDCS-14400020.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Martius during his lifetime for himself and his beloved son, Lupus.

EDCS-14400021.text_edition: Fontanis Nymphis Publius Cornelius Diadumenus et Iulia Threpte votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14400021.translation: To the Fontane Nymphs, Publius Cornelius Diadumenus and Iulia Threpte willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14400021.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Publius Cornelius Diadumenus and Iulia Threpte fulfilling a vow to the Fontane Nymphs.

EDCS-14400022.translation: And Lucania Optata made this while alive.
EDCS-14400022.summary: This is likely a fragment of a funerary inscription, indicating Lucania Optata made a monument for herself while living.

EDCS-14400023.text_edition: Elvissio Bellici defuncto annorum XXX Matiu Lucreto filio et coniugi carissimo viva fecit
EDCS-14400023.translation: To Elvissius Bellicus, deceased at 30 years old, Matiu Lucretius, his son and dearest husband, made this while alive.
EDCS-14400023.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Matiu Lucretius to his deceased son and beloved husband, Elvissius Bellicus, who died at age 30.

EDCS-14400024.text_edition: Marti Augusto sacrum pro salute  Cassiani Baebiae Maturae et Gaviani et Honorati filiorum Ianuarius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400024.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus, for the well-being of Cassianus, Baebia Matura, and their sons Gavianus and Honoratus, Ianuarius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400024.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars Augustus by Ianuarius for the health of Cassianus, Baebia Matura, and their two sons.

EDCS-14400025.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ripanio Flaviano aedilicia potestate Claudi Viruni @(obito) nnorum LX et Rufia sic Primula sic coniux sic eiius Ripanius Adnamus filius parentibus faciendum curavit
EDCS-14400025.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for Ripanius Flavianus, who held aedilitian power for Claudius Virunus, who died at 60 years old. And Rufia Primula, his wife, and Ripanius Adnamus, his son, arranged for this to be made for their parents.
EDCS-14400025.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Ripanius Flavianus, a former aedile, by his wife Rufia Primula and his son Ripanius Adnamus.

EDCS-14400027.text_edition: Dis Manibus Privatus SePnecionis#Senecionis#SPNECIONIS vivus fecit et Terentiae coniugi
EDCS-14400027.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Privatus, son of Senecio, made this while alive, and for Terentia, his wife.
EDCS-14400027.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Privatus, son of Senecio, for himself and his wife Terentia during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400028.text_edition: Iulius Acceptus et Iulia Quinta Iuliae=EA#Iuliae#IULIEA Cassiae liberti vivi fecerunt sibi et Acceptae filiae annorum V
EDCS-14400028.translation: Iulius Acceptus and Iulia Quinta, freedpersons of Iulia Cassia, made this while alive for themselves and their daughter Accepta, aged 5 years.
EDCS-14400028.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Iulius Acceptus and Iulia Quinta, freedpersons of Iulia Cassia, for themselves and their 5-year-old daughter, Accepta, during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14400029.text_edition: Latobio sacrum Caius Speratius Vibius et Valeria Avita pro incolumitate filiorum suorum voto suscepto navale vetustate conlapsum restituerunt  votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14400029.translation: Sacred to Latobius. Gaius Speratius Vibius and Valeria Avita, having made a vow for the safety of their sons, restored a shrine that had collapsed from old age. They willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14400029.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Gaius Speratius Vibius and Valeria Avita fulfilling a vow to Latobius for their sons' safety by restoring a shrine, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400030.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Sabini Censorini et Liciniae Atticae patronis optimis Ianuarius et Serena liberti votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14400030.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of Sabinus Censorinus and Licinia Attica, their most excellent patrons, Ianuarius and Serena, freedpersons, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14400030.summary: This votive inscription records the freedpersons Ianuarius and Serena dedicating an offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of their patrons, Sabinus Censorinus and Licinia Attica.

EDCS-14400031.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Publi Aeli Finiti beneficiarii consularis et Tulliae Felicitati patronis merentibus Publius Aelius Surus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400031.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of Publius Aelius Finitus, beneficiary of the consular, and Tullia Felicitas, deserving patrons, Publius Aelius Surus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400031.summary: This votive inscription records Publius Aelius Surus dedicating an offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of his patrons, Publius Aelius Finitus, a consular beneficiary, and Tullia Felicitas.

EDCS-14400032.text_edition: Secundinae Florae libertae fili- fec et Sequndae sic nepoti
EDCS-14400032.translation: To Secundina Flora, freedwoman, her son made this, and to Secunda, his granddaughter.
EDCS-14400032.summary: This funerary inscription was made by a son for his freedwoman mother, Secundina Flora, and also mentions his granddaughter, Secunda.

EDCS-14400033.text_edition: Tiae Marci libertae Candalae et suis Quintus Marius Claudia Priamus vivus fecit
EDCS-14400033.translation: To Tia Candala, freedwoman of Marcus, and to his own family, Quintus Marius Claudia Priamus made this while alive.
EDCS-14400033.summary: This funerary inscription was prepared by Quintus Marius Claudia Priamus while still alive for Tia Candala, a freedwoman of Marcus, and for his own family.

EDCS-14400034.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Vibius Fortunatus
EDCS-14400034.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Expeller, Vibius Fortunatus [dedicates this].
EDCS-14400034.summary: This is a short votive inscription by Vibius Fortunatus dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Depulsor, an epithet of Jupiter as 'the Expeller' or 'Averter of Evil'.

EDCS-14400035.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque omnibus Agilis Quersoris cum suis votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-14400035.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses, Agilis Quersoris with his family willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400035.summary: This votive inscription records Agilis Quersoris and his family fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all other gods and goddesses.

EDCS-14400037.text_edition: Dis Manibus Seio Supero @(obito) annorum L Matura Cupiti marito karissimo viva fecit et sibi hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14400037.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Matura Cupiti made this while alive for her dearest husband Seius Superus, who died at 50 years old, and for herself. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14400037.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Matura Cupiti for her deceased husband, Seius Superus, who died at age 50, and also for herself, explicitly stating it is not for an heir.

EDCS-14400040.text_edition: Romano  Claudi- Virun- us fratri  defunct- annorum XX
EDCS-14400040.translation: To Romanus. Claudi- Virun-us made this for his brother, who died at 20 years old.
EDCS-14400040.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Claudi- Virun-us for his deceased brother, Romanus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-14400041.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pervinca Ex filia viva fecit sibi et Decorato coniugi carissimo optimo
EDCS-14400041.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Pervinca, daughter of Ex, made this while alive for herself and for Decoratus, her dearest and best husband.
EDCS-14400041.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Pervinca, daughter of Ex, while still alive, for herself and for her husband Decoratus.

EDCS-14400043.text_edition: Dis Manibus Probina viva fecit sibi et Iuventino coniugi karissimo
EDCS-14400043.translation: To the Divine Manes. Probina, while living, made this for herself and for Iuventinus, her dearest husband.
EDCS-14400043.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Probina, while alive, dedicated a monument for herself and her beloved husband Iuventinus.

EDCS-14400046.text_edition: Vibio Vindicis filio annorum XXX Tattu Varronis filia sibi et marito
EDCS-14400046.translation: To Vibius, son of Vindex, 30 years old. Tattu, daughter of Varro, [made this] for herself and her husband.
EDCS-14400046.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tattu, daughter of Varro, to herself and her husband Vibius, who died at 30 years old.

EDCS-14400050.text_edition: Tertius Quarti filius Secundae coniugi karissima e
EDCS-14400050.translation: Tertius, son of Quartus, [made this] for Secunda, his dearest wife.
EDCS-14400050.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tertius, son of Quartus, to his beloved wife Secunda.

EDCS-14400057.text_edition: us fec  coniugi karissimo @(obito) annorum  o interfecto a barbaris
EDCS-14400057.translation: [...]us made this for his dearest husband, who died at [---] years old, killed by barbarians.
EDCS-14400057.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated by an unnamed individual to her beloved husband, who was killed by barbarians.

EDCS-14400058.text_edition: Homuncio filius  Tertius Togionis filius et Atouta filia coniux fecerunt et Iunio eiusdem liberto
EDCS-14400058.translation: Homuncio, son of Tertius, son of Togi, and Atouta, his wife, made this, and for Iunius, his freedman.
EDCS-14400058.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Homuncio, son of Tertius, and his wife Atouta, to themselves and to Iunius, Homuncio's freedman.

EDCS-14400059.text_edition: Iunius Firmus et Tertia uxor sibi vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14400059.translation: Iunius Firmus and Tertia, his wife, made this for themselves while living.
EDCS-14400059.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Iunius Firmus and his wife Tertia dedicated a monument for themselves during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14400060.translation: Farsuleius, son of Gaius. Publius Titius.
EDCS-14400060.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a funerary marker, simply listing two names: Farsuleius, son of Gaius, and Publius Titius.

EDCS-14400061.text_edition: Diti Smertrio Augusto Marcus Claudius Rufus et Iulia Gemellina aedem fecerunt libentes merito
EDCS-14400061.translation: To Ditus Smertrius Augustus, Marcus Claudius Rufus and Iulia Gemellina willingly and deservedly built a temple.
EDCS-14400061.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording that Marcus Claudius Rufus and Iulia Gemellina built a temple for the god Ditus Smertrius Augustus.

EDCS-14400062.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primo Ursini et Seneciae coniugi eius Ursus filius parentibus fecit
EDCS-14400062.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ursus, son, made this for his parents, Primus Ursinus and Senecia, his wife.
EDCS-14400062.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ursus to his parents, Primus Ursinus and his wife Senecia.

EDCS-14400063.text_edition: Romanio Ingenuo filio annorum II hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14400063.translation: To Romanius Ingenuus, son, 2 years old. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14400063.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Romanius Ingenuus, who died at two years old, with a clause restricting inheritance of the monument.

EDCS-14400064.text_edition: Caius Valentinius Crispus vivus fecit sibi et ritiuni coniugi optimae
EDCS-14400064.translation: Gaius Valentinius Crispus, in his lifetime, made this for himself and for Ritiuni, his excellent wife.
EDCS-14400064.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Gaius Valentinius Crispus prepared a monument for himself and his wife while still alive.

EDCS-14400066.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Tacitus.

EDCS-14400068.text_edition: Iovenat Augusto Attia Ingenua votum solvit
EDCS-14400068.translation: To Iovenat Augustus, Attia Ingenua fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-14400068.summary: This is a votive inscription by Attia Ingenua to the deity Iovenat Augustus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14400072.text_edition: Dis Manibus Adiutoriae Ursi libertae obitae annorum XL et Aufidio Avito militi legionis II Italicae Adiutor et Dionysia soror fratri et sorori optimis
EDCS-14400072.translation: To the spirits of the departed: for Adiutoria, freedwoman of Ursus, who died at 40 years of age, and for Aufidius Avitus, soldier of the Second Italic Legion. Adiutor and Dionysia, sister, made this for their excellent brother and sister.
EDCS-14400072.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Adiutor and Dionysia to the spirits of their deceased sister, Adiutoria, and brother, Aufidius Avitus, a soldier of the Legio II Italica.

EDCS-14400075.text_edition: Biraconi Adnamati filio et Montiae Buccionis filiae et Masculo filio annorum XXXI
EDCS-14400075.translation: To Biraco, son of Adnamatus, and to Montia, daughter of Buccio, and to Masculus, their son, 31 years of age.
EDCS-14400075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Biraco, Montia, and their son Masculus, who died at the age of 31.

EDCS-14400076.translation: Avita, while alive, made this for herself.
EDCS-14400076.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Avita prepared her own monument during her lifetime.

EDCS-14400079.text_edition: Primi nnorum II mensium  dierum XVI Caius Antonius  Quintianus
EDCS-14400079.translation: Of Primus, 2 years, 6 months, 16 days old. Gaius Antonius Quintianus...
EDCS-14400079.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child named Primus, detailing his age at death, dedicated by Gaius Antonius Quintianus.

EDCS-14400083.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing the name Sabina.

EDCS-14400088.text_edition: Atticus Quarti et Melissa Melissonis ab haeredebus sic factum
EDCS-14400088.translation: Atticus, son of Quartus, and Melissa, daughter of Melisso. Made by the heirs in this manner.
EDCS-14400088.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atticus and Melissa, indicating that their monument was erected by their heirs.

EDCS-14400089.text_edition: tori et ae uxori to filio |(centurioni) nus
EDCS-14400089.translation: ...to... and to his wife, to his son, a centurion...
EDCS-14400089.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a centurion and his family.

EDCS-14400091.text_edition: militi cohortis  praetoriae nnorum  stipendiorum  et Veneria coniux fecerunt
EDCS-14400091.translation: To a soldier of the Praetorian Cohort, [---] years old, with [---] years of service. His wife, Veneria, made this.
EDCS-14400091.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier of the Praetorian Cohort, set up by his wife, Veneria, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400092.text_edition: Quartus Nemetai libertus et Urbana Palumbi liberta vivi fecerunt et filiae Quintae annorum XVIII dis Manibus
EDCS-14400092.translation: Quartus, freedman of Nemeta, and Urbana, freedwoman of Palumbus, made this during their lifetimes for themselves and for their daughter Quinta, 18 years old, to the spirits of the dead.
EDCS-14400092.summary: This is a funerary monument created by two freedpersons, Quartus and Urbana, for themselves and their deceased daughter Quinta, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-14400093.text_edition: Capitonio Luci Tein L et  et
EDCS-14400093.translation: To Capito, son of Lucius Teinius, L. [---], and [---].
EDCS-14400093.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Capito, likely erected by his family members, from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14400094.text_edition: Elvioni Blendonis filio et Elvissae Agisonis filiae uxori heres fecit
EDCS-14400094.translation: To Elvio, son of Blendo, and to Elvissa, daughter of Agiso, his wife, the heir made this.
EDCS-14400094.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by an heir to Elvio, son of Blendo, and his wife Elvissa, daughter of Agiso.

EDCS-14400095.text_edition: Ursuius vivus sibi fecit et iurae uxori
EDCS-14400095.translation: Ursuius made this for himself during his lifetime, and for his wife Iura.
EDCS-14400095.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Ursuius for himself while living, and for his wife Iura, indicating a shared tomb.

EDCS-14400097.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication to Vocretanus, possibly a deity or a deceased person, from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14400098.text_edition: Vocretano Augusto sacrum Iulius Primulus et Vibia Vibi votum solverunt laeti libentes merito
EDCS-14400098.translation: Sacred to Vocretanus Augustus. Iulius Primulus and Vibia, daughter of Vibius, gladly and willingly fulfilled their vow deservedly.
EDCS-14400098.summary: This is a votive inscription to the deity Vocretanus Augustus, made by Iulius Primulus and Vibia, in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400100.text_edition: Gracco servo et Iuniae ancillae coniugi carissumae sic
EDCS-14400100.translation: To Graccus, slave, and to Iunia, handmaiden, his dearest wife. So [be it].
EDCS-14400100.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a slave, Graccus, and his wife, a handmaiden named Iunia, likely erected by a fellow slave or their master.

EDCS-14400101.text_edition: Publius Cispius Publi libertus Trouca Lucius Barbius Luci libertus Anchialus sibei et sueis vivi fecerunt Lucius Cispius Luci Barbi et Publi Cispi libertus Suadrus Cispia Barbi et Cispi liberta Exapia
EDCS-14400101.translation: Publius Cispius, freedman of Publius, named Trouca, and Lucius Barbius, freedman of Lucius, named Anchialus, made this during their lifetimes for themselves and their family. Lucius Cispius, freedman of Lucius Barbius and Publius Cispius, named Suadrus. Cispia, freedwoman of Barbius and Cispius, named Exapia.
EDCS-14400101.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by two freedmen, Publius Cispius and Lucius Barbius, for themselves and their families, explicitly mentioning their freedpersons Lucius Cispius and Cispia.

EDCS-14400103.text_edition: Silvano Adiuta Valeri Attici liberta votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400103.translation: To Silvanus. Adiuta, freedwoman of Valerius Atticus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-14400103.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Silvanus, made by Adiuta, a freedwoman of Valerius Atticus, in fulfillment of a vow, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400104.text_edition: Marti Marcus Lollius Priscus et Memmia Prisca cum suis
EDCS-14400104.translation: To Mars. Marcus Lollius Priscus and Memmia Prisca with their own [family/people].
EDCS-14400104.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Mars by Marcus Lollius Priscus and Memmia Prisca, likely made with their family.

EDCS-14400105.text_edition: Sammus Goutonis vivus fecit sibi et Coptiae
EDCS-14400105.translation: Sammus, son of Gouto, made this for himself and for Coptia while alive.
EDCS-14400105.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Sammus, son of Gouto, prepared a tomb for himself and Coptia during his lifetime, characteristic of a burial monument.

EDCS-14400109.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Venimari filius Fronto miles cohortis Montanorum primae nnorum L stipendiorum XXV hic situs est Tiberius Iulius Modestus patrono et sibi vivus fecit
EDCS-14400109.translation: Tiberius Julius Fronto, son of Venimarus, soldier of the First Cohort of Montani, aged 50, with 25 years of service, is buried here. Tiberius Julius Modestus made this for his patron and for himself while alive.
EDCS-14400109.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Tiberius Julius Fronto, a veteran soldier, erected by Tiberius Julius Modestus for his patron and himself, indicating a tomb prepared during Modestus's lifetime.

EDCS-14400110.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that only partially preserves the name Successus, making its full purpose unclear.

EDCS-14400114.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, only preserving a partial name Philotimus and a few letters, making its original purpose indeterminate.

EDCS-14400116.text_edition: Valtidia Qurena sic viva fecit sibi et Valtidio Barduni coniugi karissimo et Ingenuo filio et Secunda Secundi viva fecit sibi et Covnertiano coniugi karissimo
