EDCS-14400116.translation: Valtidia Qurena made this for herself while alive, and for Valtidius Bardunus, her dearest husband, and for Ingenuus, her son. And Secunda, daughter of Secundus, made this for herself while alive and for Covnertianus, her dearest husband.
EDCS-14400116.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a family tomb, where Valtidia Qurena prepared a monument for herself, her husband, and son, and Secunda, daughter of Secundus, separately prepared one for herself and her husband, all while still living.

EDCS-14400118.text_edition: Hilara Adiutoris filia viva fecit sibi et Batasiae matri et Adiutoriri patri defuncto annorum VL
EDCS-14400118.translation: Hilara, daughter of Adiutor, made this for herself while alive, and for Batasia, her mother, and for Adiutor, her deceased father, who lived 55 years.
EDCS-14400118.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Hilara, daughter of Adiutor, prepared a tomb for herself, her mother Batasia, and her deceased father Adiutor, during her lifetime.

EDCS-14400119.translation: They paid, deservedly, for their son.
EDCS-14400119.summary: This is a short, fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary dedication, indicating parents fulfilled a duty to their son.

EDCS-14400122.text_edition: et Annetia Ursa coniunx vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14400122.translation: and Annetia Ursa, his wife, made this while alive.
EDCS-14400122.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely from a funerary monument, indicating a wife, Annetia Ursa, prepared a memorial while still living, possibly alongside her husband.

EDCS-14400123.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, made by Restitutius Provincialis for his uncle.

EDCS-14400124.text_edition: Finitus Terti filius et Ateressa Quinti filia vibi sic fecerunt Floro filio annorum XVIII et sibi
EDCS-14400124.translation: Finitus, son of Tertius, and Ateressa, daughter of Quintus, while living thus made (this monument) for their son Florus, aged 18 years, and for themselves.
EDCS-14400124.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Finitus and Ateressa for their deceased son Florus and for themselves while still living.

EDCS-14400125.text_edition: Seneca Primigeni filius et Vibena Vibeni filia uxor Ampliatus
EDCS-14400125.translation: Seneca, son of Primigenius, and Vibena, daughter of Vibenus, wife of Ampliatus.
EDCS-14400125.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records part of a dedication or epitaph for Seneca and Vibena, possibly mentioning Vibena's husband Ampliatus.

EDCS-14400126.text_edition: Lottonis filio etiulae Dab filiae ro filius et a filia filii parentibus fecerunt
EDCS-14400126.translation: To the son of Lotto and to Etiula, daughter of Dab. The son of R[---] and the daughter of A[---], sons, made (this) for their parents.
EDCS-14400126.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by children for their parents, Lottonis and Etiula Dab, with some names partially lost.

EDCS-14400127.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II consul pater patriae
EDCS-14400127.translation: Imperator Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of the country.
EDCS-14400127.summary: This inscription is a titulature, likely part of a larger monument, commemorating the Emperor Trajan Decius, detailing his official titles and powers, datable to 250 AD based on his second tribunician power.

EDCS-14400129.text_edition: Macus Titonis filius et Munita Titionis
EDCS-14400129.translation: Macus, son of Tito, and Munita, [daughter] of Titio.
EDCS-14400129.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, identifying Macus and Munita by their patronymics.

EDCS-14400131.translation: [---] sons of Vercaius, while living, made (this).
EDCS-14400131.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records that the sons of Vercaius made a monument while still alive, likely for themselves or other family members.

EDCS-14400135.text_edition: Vercaius Ittaes sic filius et Agisiae Vindili filiae et Quarto filio annorum XXV et Mosica Ituni vvivi fecerunt sibi hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur  et Vero  nnorum XV
EDCS-14400135.translation: Vercaius, son of Ittae, and Agisia, daughter of Vindilus, and their son Quartus, aged 25 years, and Mosica, [daughter] of Itunus, while living, made this monument for themselves; it shall not pass to an heir, and for Verus, aged 15 years.
EDCS-14400135.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Vercaius and Agisia for their deceased sons Quartus and Verus, as well as for themselves and Mosica, explicitly stating that it is not intended for an heir.

EDCS-14400137.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Fabiani libertus Quartus et Matrona Attae filia uxor vivi
EDCS-14400137.translation: Tiberius Iulius Quartus, freedman of Fabianus, and Matrona, daughter of Atta, his wife, while living.
EDCS-14400137.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or part of a monument set up by Tiberius Iulius Quartus and his wife Matrona while they were still alive.

EDCS-14400138.text_edition: filius Anniae Ogiae uxori Rucconi Val
EDCS-14400138.translation: Son of Annia Ogia, to his wife Ruccona Val[---].
EDCS-14400138.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication by a son of Annia Ogia to his wife Ruccona, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-14400139.text_edition: Domitius Attunis filius sibi et Mosicuni uxori et Quartae filiae pientissimae
EDCS-14400139.translation: Domitius, son of Attunus, for himself and for his wife Mosicuna and for his most devoted daughter Quarta.
EDCS-14400139.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Domitius for himself, his wife Mosicuna, and his beloved daughter Quarta.

EDCS-14400141.text_edition: Secundus Melisses sic libertus vivus fecit sibi etovixtae sorori
EDCS-14400141.translation: Secundus Melisses, freedman, made this during his lifetime for himself and for Vixta, his sister.
EDCS-14400141.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a freedman for himself and his sister, indicating their tomb was prepared while they were still alive.

EDCS-14400142.text_edition: Postumus Rufini servus  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400142.translation: Postumus, slave of Rufinus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400142.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording a slave fulfilling a vow to a deity or for a specific purpose.

EDCS-14400143.text_edition: Saxano Augusto sacrum Messor Cai Herenni Rufini servus ex vissu sic posuit
EDCS-14400143.translation: Sacred to Saxanus Augustus. Messor, slave of Gaius Herennius Rufinus, set this up by command.
EDCS-14400143.summary: This is a votive dedication to the deity Saxanus Augustus, set up by a slave on behalf of his master.

EDCS-14400144.summary: This inscription lists a series of names, possibly indicating a dedication or ownership by multiple individuals.

EDCS-14400145.text_edition: CatIia#Catia#CAIIA QuartIi#Quarti#QUARII liberta Iunonibus ancillarum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400145.translation: Catia, freedwoman of Quartius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the Junos of the slave women.
EDCS-14400145.summary: This is a votive inscription made by a freedwoman to a specific group of deities, the Junos of the slave women, after fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14400146.text_edition: Baussus Cotulonis et Caletiu Vercai vivi fecerunt sibi et Quartae filiae annorum XII
EDCS-14400146.translation: Baussus Cotulonis and Caletiu Vercai, during their lifetime, made this for themselves and for their daughter Quarta, 12 years old.
EDCS-14400146.summary: This is a funerary inscription prepared by parents for themselves and their deceased daughter, who died at the age of twelve.

EDCS-14400147.text_edition: Biraco Montiae libertus vivus fecit sibi et cae coniugi
EDCS-14400147.translation: Biraco, freedman of Montia, made this during his lifetime for himself and his wife.
EDCS-14400147.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a freedman for himself and his wife, prepared while they were still alive.

EDCS-14400149.text_edition: IIvir et praefectus iure dicundo  Litullae parentibus optimis fecit
EDCS-14400149.translation: A duumvir and prefect with judicial power made this for his excellent parents, Litulla.
EDCS-14400149.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription, likely funerary, made by a high-ranking official for his parents, including a woman named Litulla.

EDCS-14400152.text_edition: Thallus Quinti Septuei Clementis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400152.translation: Thallus, [slave or freedman] of Quintus Septueus Clemens, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400152.summary: This is a votive inscription recording an individual named Thallus, associated with Quintus Septueus Clemens, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14400160.text_edition: Aesculapio Augusto sacrum Caius Marius Lucanius Priscus decurio et IIvir iure dicundo Claudio Viruno et Cominia Quinti filia Celsinio cum Lucaniis Maximiano Aveto Spectato et Prisco filiis ex voto posuerunt libentes laeti merito
EDCS-14400160.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius Augustus. Gaius Marius Lucanius Priscus, decurion and duumvir with judicial power, along with Claudius Virunus and Cominia, daughter of Quintus Celsinius, together with their sons Lucanius Maximianus, Avetus, Spectatus, and Priscus, willingly, happily, and deservedly set this up from a vow.
EDCS-14400160.summary: This is a detailed votive dedication to Aesculapius Augustus, made by a prominent family, led by a decurion and duumvir, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14400161.text_edition: Cantius Darbius vivus fecit sibi et Risiae Terti filiae coniugi et Terentio filio et Vercillae Viai filiae
EDCS-14400161.translation: Cantius Darbius, during his lifetime, made this for himself and for Risa Terti, his wife, and for his son Terentius, and for Vercilla Viai, his daughter.
EDCS-14400161.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Cantius Darbius for himself and his family members, indicating a common practice of pre-purchasing burial plots during one's lifetime.

EDCS-14400164.text_edition: Drippio Lottae filio et Suttuni Agrippae filiae uxori Ingenuus filius vivus fecit
EDCS-14400164.translation: Ingenuus, his son, made this during his lifetime for Drippio Lotta's son and Suttuni Agrippa's daughter, his wife.
EDCS-14400164.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Ingenuus for his parents (or possibly a father and his own wife) while he was still alive, indicating filial piety and foresight in securing family burials.

EDCS-14400165.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ursino Terti? filio et Sertoriae Serotini filiae parentibus Maturus Ursini filius fecit et  coniugi karissimae et sibi
EDCS-14400165.translation: To the Divine Manes. Maturus, son of Ursinus, made this for his parents Ursinus, son of Tertius, and Sertoria, daughter of Serotinus, and for his dearest wife and himself.
EDCS-14400165.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maturus, son of Ursinus, to his deceased parents, his beloved wife, and also for himself, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-14400167.text_edition: RCV LC et Augnuni Attuci filiae
EDCS-14400167.translation: RCV LC and Augnuna, daughter of Attucus.
EDCS-14400167.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a woman named Augnuna, daughter of Attucus, with other incomplete names or abbreviations.

EDCS-14400168.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, preserving only a few kinship terms and a numeral, suggesting a family context.

EDCS-14400169.text_edition: Venimaro Secconis filio Messilla Attuci filia uxor viva fecit
EDCS-14400169.translation: Messilla, daughter of Attucus, his wife, made this during her lifetime for Venimaros, son of Secco.
EDCS-14400169.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Messilla, wife of Venimaros, for her husband while she was still alive, indicating conjugal devotion.

EDCS-14400170.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly mentioning a freedwoman named ]omata, suggesting a funerary context or a dedication.

EDCS-14400171.text_edition: Mercurio Quintus Volusius Priscus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400171.translation: To Mercury, Quintus Volusius Priscus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400171.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mercury by Quintus Volusius Priscus, fulfilling a vow, which is a common form of religious dedication.

EDCS-14400172.text_edition: Marco Aurelio Ursino veterano misso honesta missione ex legione II Italica obito nnorum LX marito optimo et Aelio Verano et Iuniae Celataes sic parentibus vivis Aelia Deivilla et Aurelii Ursina R
EDCS-14400172.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Ursinus, a veteran honorably discharged from the Second Italian Legion, who died at 60 years of age, a most excellent husband; and to Aelius Veranus and Iunia Celataes, his parents, while still living. Aelia Deivilla and Aurelii Ursina R.
EDCS-14400172.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to a veteran, Marcus Aurelius Ursinus, by his wife Aelia Deivilla and possibly children (Aurelii Ursina), also honoring his parents, Aelius Veranus and Iunia Celataes, who were still alive at the time of the inscription.

EDCS-14400175.text_edition: Augusto sacrum  Terentius Agathopus et Attius Hilarus Barbius Vitalis votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14400175.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Terentius Agathopus and Attius Hilarus Barbius Vitalis willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14400175.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the emperor Augustus by Terentius Agathopus and Attius Hilarus Barbius Vitalis, fulfilling a vow, indicating a display of loyalty or gratitude towards the imperial cult.

EDCS-14400176.text_edition: Marco Opellio Diadumeniano Cai Opelli Severi Macrini Imperatoris filio Caesari nobilissimo principi iuventutis ordo Teurniensium
EDCS-14400176.translation: To Marcus Opellius Diadumenianus, son of Emperor Gaius Opellius Severus Macrinus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth. The order of the Teurnians (set this up).
EDCS-14400176.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated by the city council of Teurnia to Diadumenianus, son of Emperor Macrinus, likely set up during his father's short reign (217-218 AD).

EDCS-14400178.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription, likely by Albanus, for someone named Abiona.

EDCS-14400181.text_edition: Carminius Secundinus tia Primitiva vivi fecerunt sibi et Carminio Felicioni et  Primitivae
EDCS-14400181.translation: Carminius Secundinus and Primitiva made this while living for themselves and for Carminius Felicio and Primitiva.
EDCS-14400181.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Carminius Secundinus and Primitiva during their lifetime for themselves and two others with the same names, likely family members.

EDCS-14400183.text_edition: O IO CIO umarae us Quordi  Aneca uxor
EDCS-14400183.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a man (possibly Iocius) and his wife Aneca.

EDCS-14400184.text_edition: Adnamatus Lali Momma Attuis filia Priamo patrono vivo fecerunt sibi et suis
EDCS-14400184.translation: Adnamatus Lali, daughter of Momma Attuis, made this while living for herself and her family, for her patron Priamo.
EDCS-14400184.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Adnamatus Lali, daughter of Momma Attuis, during her lifetime for herself and her family, dedicated to her patron Priamo.

EDCS-14400186.text_edition: Devinato Boioni Restuti defuncto annorum XXXV Candida marito optimo et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-14400186.translation: To the deceased Devinatus Boioni Restutus, who lived 35 years. Candida, while living, made this for her excellent husband and for herself.
EDCS-14400186.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Candida for her deceased husband Devinatus Boioni Restutus, who died at 35, and also for herself while still alive.

EDCS-14400187.text_edition: Vibiae F  libertae et Att P   libertae Vibiae
EDCS-14400187.translation: To Vibia F[---], freedwoman, and to Att[ius] P[---], freedman, (and) to Vibia.
EDCS-14400187.summary: This fragmentary inscription is dedicated to a freedwoman named Vibia, a freedman named Attius, and another Vibia, suggesting family or patron-freedperson relationships.

EDCS-14400189.text_edition: Ulpius Dasio veteranus ex centurione et Ulpia Nigrina coniux fecerunt
EDCS-14400189.translation: Ulpius Dasio, a veteran ex-centurion, and Ulpia Nigrina, his wife, made this.
EDCS-14400189.summary: This is a monument, likely a funerary one, set up by Ulpius Dasio, a veteran and former centurion, and his wife Ulpia Nigrina.

EDCS-14400190.text_edition: iorum Ingenuo et Marcellino filiis  Sabino filio Av
EDCS-14400190.translation: To Ingenuus and Marcellinus, sons; to Sabinus, son of Av[---].
EDCS-14400190.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, dedicated to sons named Ingenuus, Marcellinus, and Sabinus (son of Av[---]).

EDCS-14400191.text_edition: Senanto Adiatulli filio et Epantiae Iuni filiae coniugi
EDCS-14400191.translation: To Senanto, son of Adiatullus, and to Epantia, daughter of Iunius, his wife.
EDCS-14400191.summary: This is an inscription, likely funerary, dedicated to Senanto, son of Adiatullus, and his wife Epantia, daughter of Iunius.

EDCS-14400192.text_edition: Hermes Invicto Mithrae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400192.translation: Hermes willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Unconquered Mithras.
EDCS-14400192.summary: This inscription records Hermes' dedication of a vow to the god Mithras, likely on an altar or religious monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400193.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ecronius Restutus et Suceliu
EDCS-14400193.translation: To the Divine Manes, Ecronius Restutus and Suceliu.
EDCS-14400193.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Ecronius Restutus and Suceliu to the spirits of the dead, probably for a tombstone or funerary monument.

EDCS-14400194.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Trausi filius Attucius missicius cohortis I Noricorum nnorum L stipendiorum XXVIII testamento fieri iussit sibi et Primo Fusco Prisco libertis isdem liberti et heredes fecerunt
EDCS-14400194.translation: Tiberius Claudius Attucius, son of Trausus, a veteran of the First Cohort of Norici, aged 50, with 28 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will for himself and for his freedmen Primus Fuscus Priscus; the same freedmen and heirs made it.
EDCS-14400194.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument commissioned by Tiberius Claudius Attucius, a veteran, for himself and his freedmen, and subsequently erected by his freedmen and heirs, dating probably to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400196.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sic viva fecit sibi et Ursino fabro coniugi carissimo
EDCS-14400196.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sic made this while alive for herself and for Ursinus the craftsman, her dearest husband.
EDCS-14400196.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Sic during her lifetime for herself and her beloved husband Ursinus, a craftsman, indicating a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-14400198.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Crepereius Dionisius beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400198.publication: ILLPRON 00549 = Legio-II-Ital-Nk, 00013 = Leber 00004 = Gerstl 00050 = CBI 00216 = AEA 2006, +00012 = AE 1956, 00042
EDCS-14400198.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Crepereius Dionisius, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400198.summary: This inscription records Gaius Crepereius Dionisius, a consular aide, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely on a religious dedication like an altar.

EDCS-14400199.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus a Teurnia milia passuum IIII
EDCS-14400199.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, 4 miles from Teurnia.
EDCS-14400199.summary: This is a milestone marking a distance of four miles from Teurnia, dedicated to the Emperor Severus Alexander, placing its date between 222 and 235 CE.

EDCS-14400200.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli milia passuum IIII
EDCS-14400200.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, 4 miles.
EDCS-14400200.summary: This is a milestone dedicated to Emperor Gordian III, specifying his titles and a distance of four miles, dating it to his reign (238-244 CE).

EDCS-14400201.text_edition: Pusio Cai Valentinii Flavi servus vivus fecit sibi et Tertiae coniugi pientissimae et Boniatae socrae
EDCS-14400201.translation: Pusio, slave of Gaius Valentinius Flavus, made this while alive for himself and for Tertia, his most devoted wife, and for Boniata, his mother-in-law.
EDCS-14400201.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Pusio, a slave, during his lifetime for himself, his wife Tertia, and his mother-in-law Boniata.

EDCS-14400203.text_edition: P(ublius) Aelius Lucretius veteranus ex |(centurione) sibi et Butturae Cypaeri filiae uxori et Augustae filiae nnorum
EDCS-14400203.translation: Publius Aelius Lucretius, a veteran from a centurion, for himself and for Buttura, daughter of Cypaerus, his wife, and for Augusta, his daughter, of X years.
EDCS-14400203.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Publius Aelius Lucretius, a veteran, for himself, his wife Buttura, and his daughter Augusta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400205.text_edition: Bellicus Sabini Verani vivus fecit sibi et Candidae coniugi et Saturnino fratri et Sporae matri et Senoni et Sextiliae sororibus
EDCS-14400205.translation: Bellicus, son of Sabinus Veranus, made this while alive for himself and for Candida, his wife, and for Saturninus, his brother, and for Spora, his mother, and for Seno and Sextilia, his sisters.
EDCS-14400205.summary: This is a family funerary monument created by Bellicus during his lifetime for himself and several close family members: his wife, brother, mother, and sisters.

EDCS-14400209.text_edition: Pudens conductoris ferrariarum Noricarum et assessoris ferrariarrum sic servos sic et Senecia mater Aniceto filio nnrum XVIII parentes vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14400209.translation: Pudens, a manager of the Norican ironworks and an assessor of the ironworks, thus for his slaves and thus Senecia, the mother, for their son Anicetus, 18 years old, his parents made this while living.
EDCS-14400209.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb made by Pudens and Senecia for their son Anicetus while they were still alive, possibly in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14400210.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Satulius to Hercules Augustus, likely a votive offering or altar from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400212.translation: To the son of Titus [---], and the son of [---], and PA [---].
EDCS-14400212.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a son of Titus and another son, and possibly referring to family members.

EDCS-14400216.text_edition: Isidi Augustae sacrum Caius Iulius Onesimus et Pompeia Chronia et Iulia Ingenua filia votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14400216.translation: Sacred to Isis Augusta. Gaius Julius Onesimus, Pompeia Chronia, and their daughter Julia Ingenua willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14400216.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Isis Augusta by Gaius Julius Onesimus, Pompeia Chronia, and their daughter Julia Ingenua, indicating a fulfilled promise to the deity, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400224.text_edition: Vibebos Augustis sacrum Gaius Decius Successianus aedilicia potestate Claudi Viruni et Vibenia Masculina pro se et suis votum solverunt laeti libentes merito
EDCS-14400224.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Vibebos. Gaius Decius Successianus, with the aedilician power of Claudius Virunus and Vibenia Masculina, for himself and his family, happily and willingly fulfilled his vow deservedly.
EDCS-14400224.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the local deities Vibebos by Gaius Decius Successianus, alongside Claudius Virunus and Vibenia Masculina, fulfilling a vow for himself and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400225.text_edition: Murcius Sabinianus libens laetus votum solvit Vibebos
EDCS-14400225.translation: Murcius Sabinianus happily and willingly fulfilled his vow to the Vibebos.
EDCS-14400225.summary: This is a votive inscription by Murcius Sabinianus, happily fulfilling a vow to the local deities Vibebos, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400226.text_edition: Casuontano L T C N S vic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400226.translation: To Casuontanus, L.T.C.N.S. [---] willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-14400226.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to a deity named Casuontanus, with an individual identified by initials fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400227.translation: To his spouse and son, aged [---] years.
EDCS-14400227.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, dedicated to a spouse and a son of an unknown age.

EDCS-14400228.text_edition: Attucio Adnamati filio et Attuciae Veterani filiae uxori filii vivi fecerunt et Sammae sorori
EDCS-14400228.translation: To their son Attucius Adnamatus and to Attucia Veterani, daughter and wife of their son, the living parents made this, and also to their sister Samma.
EDCS-14400228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Attucius Adnamatus and his wife Attucia Veterani, made by their living parents, and also for Samma, a sister, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-14400229.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a tombstone or an ownership mark, simply stating the name Aulus Iulius Firmus.

EDCS-14400231.text_edition: Belesti Augustae sacrum Latinus Tapponi Macrini servus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400231.translation: Sacred to Belesta Augusta. Latinus, a slave of Tapponius Macrinus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400231.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Belesta Augusta by a slave named Latinus, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-14400232.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Verinus beneficiarius consularis pro se et suis votum solvit libens laetus merito  Victorino et Severo consulibus
EDCS-14400232.publication: ILLPRON 00660 = Legio-II-Ital-Nk, 00009 = CBI 00268 = Leber 00001 = Gerstl 00018 = AEA 2006, +00012
EDCS-14400232.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Publius Aelius Verinus, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family, during the consulship of Victorinus and Severus.
EDCS-14400232.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a beneficiarius consularis, Publius Aelius Verinus, made for himself and his family in 222 CE.

EDCS-14400233.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Calventinius Maternus beneficiarius Claudi Prisciani procuratoris Augusti votum solvit merito Paullo II et Aproniano II consulibus
EDCS-14400233.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Calventinius Maternus, a beneficiarius of Claudius Priscianus, procurator Augusti, deservedly fulfilled his vow, during the second consulship of Paullus and the second consulship of Apronianus.
EDCS-14400233.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Calventinius Maternus, a beneficiarius, likely made in 223 CE.

EDCS-14400236.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning the name Successus.

EDCS-14400238.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Valerio Tertullino aedili Claudia Teurnia Caius Valerius Tertullus et Rettia Iulia filio @(obito) annorum XXV et sibi vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14400238.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Gaius Valerius Tertullinus, aedile, who died at 25 years of age. Claudia Teurnia, Gaius Valerius Tertullus, and Rettia Iulia, his parents, made this while living for their son and for themselves.
EDCS-14400238.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Claudia Teurnia, Gaius Valerius Tertullus, and Rettia Iulia for their deceased son, Gaius Valerius Tertullinus, an aedile, and for themselves while still living.

EDCS-14400245.text_edition: Restitutius IIvir iure dicundi sic Claudio Viruno et Helvia Cai filia Attica pro salute Cai Restituti Ripani fili votum solverunt libentes laeti merito
EDCS-14400245.translation: Restitutius, duumvir with judicial power, thus, with Claudius Virunus and Helvia Attica, daughter of Gaius, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled a vow for the health of Gaius Restitutus Ripanus, his son.
EDCS-14400245.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Restitutius, a duumvir, along with Claudius Virunus and Helvia Attica, for the health of Restitutius' son, Gaius Restitutus Ripanus.

EDCS-14400251.text_edition: Mansueto et Cogitatae Fabiani servis  et Samtuni Mandatius Secundio fratri optimo
EDCS-14400251.translation: To Mansuetus and Cogitata, slaves of Fabianus, and to Samtunius. Mandatius Secundio [made this] for his excellent brother.
EDCS-14400251.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Mandatius Secundio to his brother, Samtunius, and to two slaves of Fabianus named Mansuetus and Cogitata.

EDCS-14400255.text_edition: Quintus Volusius Secundinus Volusio Civi et Anniae Uttilae parentibus et Volusio Accepto filio defuncto annorum XIII vivus fecit et sibi et Optatiae Tertullae coniugi
EDCS-14400255.translation: Quintus Volusius Secundinus made this while living for his parents, Volusius Civis and Annia Uttila, and for his deceased son, Volusius Acceptus, who died at 13 years of age, and for himself and his wife, Optatia Tertulla.
EDCS-14400255.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Quintus Volusius Secundinus for his parents, his deceased son Volusius Acceptus, himself, and his wife Optatia Tertulla.

EDCS-14400259.text_edition: Genio Cucullato sacrum Caius Iuventius Speratus et Cornelia Secundina votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14400259.translation: Sacred to Genius Cucullatus. Gaius Iuventius Speratus and Cornelia Secundina willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14400259.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deity Genius Cucullatus by Gaius Iuventius Speratus and Cornelia Secundina, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-14400261.text_edition: Quintus Octavius Masculus vivus fecit sibi et Macriniae Secundinae coniugi
EDCS-14400261.translation: Quintus Octavius Masculus made this while living for himself and his wife, Macrinia Secundina.
EDCS-14400261.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Quintus Octavius Masculus for himself and his wife, Macrinia Secundina, while still living.

EDCS-14400262.text_edition: Clevius Rusci filius sibi et Elvissae Maticonis filiae coniugi vivus fecit
EDCS-14400262.translation: Clevius, son of Ruscus, made this for himself and for Elvissa, daughter of Matco, his wife, while living.
EDCS-14400262.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Clevius for himself and his wife, Elvissa, during their lifetime.

EDCS-14400265.text_edition: Panenti Lavi filio Dasto Nepotis marito et sibi
EDCS-14400265.translation: To Panens, son of Lavus, husband of Dasta, daughter of Nepos, and for herself.
EDCS-14400265.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Panens, husband of Dasta, and also for Dasta herself.

EDCS-14400269.text_edition: IM RI no filio annorum XX
EDCS-14400269.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely commemorating a son who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-14400270.text_edition: Optata sibi et Buccioni filio annorum XXV
EDCS-14400270.translation: Optata for herself and for Buccio, her son, 25 years old.
EDCS-14400270.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Optata for herself and her son Buccio, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-14400271.text_edition: Spectatae Tertullina matri et  Macri filius miles cohortis II Batavorum
EDCS-14400271.translation: To Spectata, his mother, from Tertullina, and the son of Macrus, a soldier of the Second Cohort of Batavians.
EDCS-14400271.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a dedication to Spectata, a mother, by Tertullina and a soldier from the Second Cohort of Batavians.

EDCS-14400273.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Publius Aelius Senecianus et Petronia Silvana filia votum solverunt libentes laeti merito pro se et suis
EDCS-14400273.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, Publius Aelius Senecianus and his daughter Petronia Silvana willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled their vow for themselves and their family.
EDCS-14400273.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Publius Aelius Senecianus and his daughter, Petronia Silvana.

EDCS-14400274.text_edition: Saetubolo Diastulli filio et Talsae Bremiatis filiae uxsori testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-14400274.translation: He ordered this to be made by testament for Saetubolus, son of Diastullus, and Talsa, daughter of Bremiates, his wife.
EDCS-14400274.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument ordered by testament for Saetubolus and his wife Talsa.

EDCS-14400275.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Campili Veri conductoris ferrariarum Fortunatus vilicus aquam perduxit
EDCS-14400275.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of Campilus Verus, contractor of the ironworks, Fortunatus the steward led the water.
EDCS-14400275.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording the construction of an aqueduct by Fortunatus for the well-being of Campilus Verus, a contractor.

EDCS-14400276.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, possibly indicating the status of a freedperson related to someone named Cae[---].

EDCS-14400277.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Bellicianus et Postumia Septumina vivi fecerunt sibi et Aelio Urso filio obito annorum VIIII
EDCS-14400277.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Bellicianus and Postumia Septumina made this while living for themselves and for Aelius Ursus, their deceased son, who lived for 9 years.
EDCS-14400277.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, made by Aelius Bellicianus and Postumia Septumina for themselves and their deceased son, Aelius Ursus.

EDCS-14400278.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman named Iulius, likely set up by his former master.

EDCS-14400280.translation: Allo, son of Adgeleus, and Coma.
EDCS-14400280.summary: This fragment appears to be part of an inscription naming Allo, son of Adgeleus, and Coma, possibly for a dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-14400281.text_edition: Caius Sedatius Ca Catroniae Optatae et C
EDCS-14400281.translation: Gaius Sedatius [dedicates this to] Catronia Optata and G[aius].
EDCS-14400281.summary: This inscription fragment likely records Gaius Sedatius dedicating a monument to Catronia Optata and another individual, possibly for funerary purposes.

EDCS-14400282.text_edition: Atia  Ate fecit Uc filio et sibi et Matum Tat
EDCS-14400282.translation: Atia Ate made this for her son Uc and for herself, and for Matum Tat.
EDCS-14400282.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Atia Ate for herself, her son Uc, and Matum Tat, while she was still alive.

EDCS-14400283.text_edition: Quintus Valerius Valeri libertus Crispus vivus fecit sibi et Cattae matri et Fuscae uxori et Primae Crispi filiae
EDCS-14400283.translation: Quintus Valerius Crispus, freedman of Valerius, made this while living for himself and for his mother Cotta and for his wife Fusca and for his daughter Prima Crispa.
EDCS-14400283.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quintus Valerius Crispus for himself, his mother, wife, and daughter, prepared during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400285.text_edition: Restuta Solicuri viva fecit et sibi et Secundo filio et Covnerto filio et Cemenuni coniugi
EDCS-14400285.translation: Restuta Solicuri made this while living for herself and for her son Secundus and for her son Covnertus and for her husband Cemenunius.
EDCS-14400285.summary: This is a funerary inscription created by Restuta Solicuri during her lifetime for herself, her two sons Secundus and Covnertus, and her husband Cemenunius.

EDCS-14400286.text_edition: Atuconi Glabrionis filio et Rumae Rudsci filiae uxori filii fecerunt
EDCS-14400286.translation: They made this for Atuconius, son of Glabrio, and for Ruma, daughter of Rudscus, wife of their son.
EDCS-14400286.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by unnamed parents to their son Atuconius and his wife Ruma, who was the daughter of Rudscus.

EDCS-14400288.text_edition: Safrio Tamaco Quarta matIer#mater#MAIER viva fecit
EDCS-14400288.translation: Quarta, mother of Safrius Tamacus, made this while living.
EDCS-14400288.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quarta for her son Safrius Tamacus, created during her lifetime.

EDCS-14400289.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus consul III pater patriae sacerdos amplissimus et Marcus Aurelius Alexander Caesar
EDCS-14400289.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, consul for the third time, father of his country, most esteemed priest, and Marcus Aurelius Alexander Caesar.
EDCS-14400289.summary: This is an official inscription, likely part of a public monument or dedication, honoring the co-emperors Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus) and Severus Alexander, dating to Caracalla's third consulship (211 AD) or shortly thereafter.

EDCS-14400293.translation: Titus Flavius Aelianus, reciter of Homer.
EDCS-14400293.summary: This inscription identifies Titus Flavius Aelianus as a 'homerista,' indicating his profession as a reciter of Homeric epic, possibly from a professional dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-14400294.text_edition: Genio Norecorum sic Crispinius Adiutor votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400294.translation: To the Genius of the Norici, Crispinius Adiutor willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow thus.
EDCS-14400294.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Crispinius Adiutor to the Genius of the Norici, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14400295.translation: To the Genius of the Norici, Gaius Secundius.
EDCS-14400295.summary: This is a simple dedication by Gaius Secundius to the Genius of the Norici.

EDCS-14400297.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo iussus sic Felix Auggustorum libertus tabularius
EDCS-14400297.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, ordered thus, Felix, freedman of the Augusti, records clerk.
EDCS-14400297.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Felix, a freedman of the emperors and a records clerk, made as instructed.

EDCS-14400299.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Egronius Primianus et Aelia Valeria ex voto posuit sic
EDCS-14400299.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Egronius Primianus and Aelia Valeria placed this, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-14400299.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, placed by Egronius Primianus and Aelia Valeria in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14400300.text_edition: Dis Manibus era viva fecit et
EDCS-14400300.translation: To the Divine Manes, the mistress made this while alive and...
EDCS-14400300.summary: This inscription is part of a funerary monument made by a mistress for herself while still alive, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-14400311.text_edition: NFERE Ursulus filius vivus fecit sibi
EDCS-14400311.translation: [I]NFERE. Ursulus, the son, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-14400311.summary: This inscription, likely part of a tomb, records that Ursulus, the son, made it for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400316.text_edition: Quintus Curius Quinti filius Iunianus et Curia Sexti filia Quarta uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Quinto Iunio Iuniano filio nnum V
EDCS-14400316.translation: Quintus Curius Iunianus, son of Quintus, and Curia Quarta, daughter of Sextus, his wife, made this while living for themselves and for Quintus Iunius Iunianus, their son, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-14400316.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Quintus Curius Iunianus and his wife Curia Quarta for themselves and their deceased son, Quintus Iunius Iunianus, who died at age 5.

EDCS-14400317.text_edition: Dis Manibus Successae Claudi Quintiliani ancillae Vibius filius matri fecit
EDCS-14400317.translation: To the Divine Manes of Successa, slave of Claudius Quintilianus. Vibius, her son, made this for his mother.
EDCS-14400317.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes of Successa, a slave of Claudius Quintilianus, erected by her son Vibius.

EDCS-14400318.text_edition: Nardinae annorum V et Secundae annorum XV Casimae annorum XX
EDCS-14400318.translation: To Nardina, 5 years old, and Secunda, 15 years old, and Casima, 20 years old.
EDCS-14400318.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Nardina, Secunda, and Casima, listing their ages at death.

EDCS-14400319.text_edition: Quartus Caiani servus vivus fecit sibi et Sisiuni coniugi optimae et Tertullo filio annorum XXV
EDCS-14400319.translation: Quartus, slave of Caianus, made this while alive for himself, and for his excellent wife Sisiunis, and for his son Tertullus, 25 years old.
EDCS-14400319.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Quartus, a slave of Caianus, for himself, his wife Sisiunis, and his son Tertullus, who was 25 years old.

EDCS-14400320.text_edition: Excingo Magiovindi filius Excingomaro filio et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-14400320.translation: Excingo, son of Magiovindus, made this for his son Excingomarus and his family while alive.
EDCS-14400320.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Excingo, son of Magiovindus, prepared a tomb for his son Excingomarus and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400321.text_edition: Iulius Victor vivus fecit sibi et Sisiae coniugi et Restuto Persici filio annorum XV
EDCS-14400321.translation: Julius Victor made this while alive for himself, his wife Sisia, and Restutus, son of Persicus, aged 15 years.
EDCS-14400321.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Julius Victor prepared a tomb for himself, his wife Sisia, and Restutus, son of Persicus, aged 15, during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400322.text_edition: vivus fecit et sibi io filio et  Viatori nepoti
EDCS-14400322.translation: He made this while alive for himself, his son Io, and his grandson Viator.
EDCS-14400322.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a larger monument, recording that the dedicator prepared a tomb for himself, his son Io, and his grandson Viator during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400326.text_edition: Martia Teti filia annorum XXXX  Masculus Secundi filius annorum XXXXV Martia Teti filia annorum XXXX
EDCS-14400326.translation: Martia, daughter of Tetus, aged 40 years. Masculus, son of Secundus, aged 45 years. Martia, daughter of Tetus, aged 40 years.
EDCS-14400326.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing the names and ages of two individuals, Martia, daughter of Tetus, and Masculus, son of Secundus, indicating a memorial for them.

EDCS-14400330.text_edition: Cemeia Tromperi nnorum XXXX hic sita est
EDCS-14400330.translation: Cemeia, daughter of Tromperus, aged 40 years, is buried here.
EDCS-14400330.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Cemeia, daughter of Tromperus, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-14400331.text_edition: Cocinae Aiunis libertae annorum Successa LII Success-- liberta vixit annos L
EDCS-14400331.translation: For Cocina, freedwoman of Aiunis, aged [---] years. Successa, aged 52, freedwoman, lived 50 years.
EDCS-14400331.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cocina, a freedwoman of Aiunis, and Successa, a freedwoman who lived 50 years and died at age 52.

EDCS-14400333.text_edition: Blaus Camai vixit annos IXX Saurus Atvorti filius nnrum L et Blaus Cadiasi filius derus -- libertus annorum
EDCS-14400333.translation: Blaus, son of Camai, lived 19 years. Saurus, son of Atvortus, aged 50 years, and Blaus, son of Cadiasus, a freedman, aged [---] years.
EDCS-14400333.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Blaus, son of Camai (who lived 19 years), Saurus, son of Atvortus (who lived 50 years), and Blaus, son of Cadiasus, a freedman.

EDCS-14400335.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Flavius Surio annorum  Valeria Ingenua coniunx viva fecit sibi et
EDCS-14400335.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Surio, aged [---] years. His wife, Valeria Ingenua, made this while alive for herself and [him].
EDCS-14400335.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Flavius Surio, with his wife Valeria Ingenua preparing the tomb for herself and him during her lifetime.

EDCS-14400336.text_edition: Aurelius Docimus miles legionis II Italicae piae fidelis et Aurelia Matura vivi sibi fecerunt et Aurelio Claudio filio obito nnrum XIIII
EDCS-14400336.translation: Aurelius Docimus, soldier of the Second Italic Legion, Pious and Faithful, and Aurelia Matura, while alive, made this for themselves and for their deceased son Aurelius Claudius, who lived 14 years.
EDCS-14400336.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Aurelius Docimus, a soldier of Legio II Italica, and Aurelia Matura, his wife, prepared a tomb for themselves and their deceased son Aurelius Claudius, who died at age 14, during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14400337.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Quartinus miles alae primae Commagenorum obitus annorum L Dubitatia Saturnina viva fecit sibi et coniugi
EDCS-14400337.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Quartinus, soldier of the First Ala of the Commageni, deceased, aged 50 years. Dubitatia Saturnina, while alive, made this for herself and her husband.
EDCS-14400337.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Aurelius Quartinus, a soldier of the First Ala of the Commageni who died at age 50, with his wife Dubitatia Saturnina preparing the tomb for herself and her husband during her lifetime.

EDCS-14400338.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Ulpius Adiutor pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400338.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Ulpius Adiutor willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-14400338.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Ulpius Adiutor on behalf of himself and his household.

EDCS-14400340.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Pompeio Celeri militi cohortis I Flaviae Brittonum annorum XXV Maximus cohRere[s#coheres#CORERE faciendum curavit
EDCS-14400340.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Pompeius Celer, a soldier of the 1st Flavian Cohort of Britons, 25 years old. Maximus, his messmate, took care to have this made.
EDCS-14400340.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Pompeius Celer, a soldier, set up by his messmate Maximus.

EDCS-14400341.text_edition: annorum XXII et Ulpi-- Marci fili--
EDCS-14400341.translation: 22 years old, and Ulpius [---], son of Marcus [---]
EDCS-14400341.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be part of a funerary monument, recording the age of one individual and the name of another with his patronymic.

EDCS-14400342.text_edition: Cai Ausoni Sergia Silvini annorum XXX Ingenuus titulum faciendum curavit
EDCS-14400342.translation: For Caius Ausonius Sergius Silvinus, 30 years old. Ingenuus took care to have the titulus made.
EDCS-14400342.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Caius Ausonius Sergius Silvinus, commissioned by Ingenuus.

EDCS-14400343.text_edition: Genio sacrum Ulpius Liberalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400343.translation: Sacred to the Genius. Ulpius Liberalis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400343.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the Genius by Ulpius Liberalis.

EDCS-14400345.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Cupitus veteranus et Cupita coniunx vivi fecerunt sibi et Aureli-
EDCS-14400345.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Cupitus, a veteran, and Cupita, his wife, made this while living for themselves and Aurelia [---]
EDCS-14400345.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Aurelius Cupitus, a veteran, and his wife Cupita, for themselves and another individual, Aurelia, while they were still alive.

EDCS-14400347.text_edition: Troucleimarus Demari filius eques alae Thracum annorum XXXXV stipendiorum XXVI collegae pro pietate
EDCS-14400347.translation: Troucleimarus, son of Demarus, a cavalryman of the Thracian ala, 45 years old, with 26 years of service. His colleagues made this out of piety.
EDCS-14400347.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Troucleimarus, a Thracian cavalryman, erected by his colleagues due to their respect.

EDCS-14400350.text_edition: Maveta Veregundi sic filia annorum III hic sita est Romana mater posuit
EDCS-14400350.translation: Maveta, daughter of Veregundus, aged 3, lies here. Romana, her mother, placed this.
EDCS-14400350.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maveta, a three-year-old girl, set up by her mother, Romana.

EDCS-14400351.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifeIx#pontifex#TIFIX maximus tribunicia potestate II consul pater patriae proconsul et Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar princeps iuventutis ddomini indulgentissimi pontes refecerunt vias munierunt miliaria restituerunt a Cetio milia passuum XXII
EDCS-14400351.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, Consul, Father of the Country, Proconsul, and Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Princeps Iuventutis, our most indulgent lords, restored bridges, paved roads, and re-established milestones, 22 Roman miles from Cetium.
EDCS-14400351.summary: This inscription is a milestone or public works dedication, recording the restoration of bridges, roads, and milestones by the emperors Maximinus Thrax and Maximus Caesar, likely around 236 CE.

EDCS-14400355.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Aurelio Vero Augusto tribunicia potestate III consuli II cohors II Thracum equitata pia fidelis
EDCS-14400355.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, with tribunician power for the third time, Consul for the second time. The 2nd Thracian Cohort, mounted, pious, faithful.
EDCS-14400355.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus, likely a statue base, set up by the 2nd Thracian Cohort, around 163 CE.

EDCS-14400359.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Quartinus cornicen legionis II Italicae piae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Aureliae Crispinae coniugi vivae fecit et Aeliae Quartinae filliae @(obitae) annorum XIIII et filiis duobus vivis fecit
EDCS-14400359.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelius Quartinus, cornicen (horn-blower) of the Second Italian Legion, dutiful and loyal, made this during his lifetime for himself and for Aurelia Crispina, his wife, who is alive, and for Aelia Quartina, his deceased daughter, aged 14 years, and for his two sons, who are alive.
EDCS-14400359.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a legionary horn-blower, Aelius Quartinus, for himself, his living wife and sons, and his deceased daughter, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14400360.text_edition: Tertinius Terentius vivus fecit sibi et Rufiae Helpidi coniugi et Tertio nnorum CX et Respectae nnorum LXXXX parentibus Tertinia Grata filia bene merentibus scribendum curavit
EDCS-14400360.translation: Tertinius Terentius, during his lifetime, made this for himself and for Rufia Helpis, his wife, and for Tertius, aged 110, and Respecta, aged 90, his parents. Tertinia Grata, his daughter, took care of having this inscribed for her well-deserving parents.
EDCS-14400360.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Tertinius Terentius for himself, his wife, and his exceptionally long-lived parents, with the inscription supervised by his daughter, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400362.text_edition: Legionis II Italicae aras principatu suo Tabellius Victoris faciendas curavit
EDCS-14400362.translation: Tabellius Victor had altars of the Second Italian Legion made during his principate.
EDCS-14400362.summary: This inscription records the making of altars for the Second Italian Legion by Tabellius Victor during his term as princeps, likely indicating a dedication or restoration of religious structures in a military context.

EDCS-14400363.translation: Aurelius Successus, soldier of the legion.
EDCS-14400363.summary: This fragment likely comes from a funerary or dedicatory inscription mentioning a soldier named Aurelius Successus, serving in an unnamed legion.

EDCS-14400365.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Soni filius Provincialis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400365.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Tiberius Claudius, son of Sonius, Provincialis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400365.summary: This is a votive altar or plaque dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Tiberius Claudius Provincialis, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400368.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, mentioning only the nomen Aurelius.

EDCS-14400369.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, identifying the deceased by the name Mogiancus.

EDCS-14400370.text_edition: Genio Cai Domiti Montani Septani Romani praefecti alae I Pannoniorum Tampianae victricis Castricius Sabinus duplicarius alae eiusdem aram consecravit votum libens merito solvit
EDCS-14400370.translation: To the Genius of Gaius Domitius Montanus Septanus Romanus, prefect of the First Pannonian Tampiana Victorious cavalry regiment, Castricius Sabinus, duplicarius of the same regiment, consecrated this altar and willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400370.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to the Genius of a cavalry prefect by a duplicarius (junior officer) of the same unit, the First Pannonian Tampiana Victorious cavalry regiment, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400375.text_edition: Aureliae Alexandriae @(obitae) annorum XXV et Aureliae Marcellinae filiae Aurelius Eutiches sexvir Augustalis coniugi carissimae vivus
EDCS-14400375.translation: To Aurelia Alexandria, deceased, aged 25 years, and to Aurelia Marcellina, his daughter. Aurelius Eutiches, Augustalis sexvir, made this for his dearest wife during his lifetime.
EDCS-14400375.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Eutiches, a sexvir Augustalis, to his deceased wife Aurelia Alexandria and his daughter Aurelia Marcellina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400377.text_edition: IngCenua#In]genua#CENUA  anno--- L Publius I  Ovilavensium libert-?
EDCS-14400377.translation: Ingenua Cenua. In the year [---] L. Publius I. Of the Ovilavensians, freedman (?).
EDCS-14400377.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Ingenua Cenua and a Publius, potentially a freedman associated with the Ovilavensians, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-14400378.text_edition: Adiutor Seniunis vivus et Martiae coniugi eius obitae annorum IXXV
EDCS-14400378.translation: Adiutor Seniunis, while living, and to his deceased wife Martia, who lived 75 years.
EDCS-14400378.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Adiutor Seniunis to himself, while still alive, and to his deceased wife, Martia, who was 75 years old.

EDCS-14400379.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pontiae Pompeianae filiae @(obitae) annorum VII Gaius Pontius Pompeianus |(centurio) legionis II Italicae piae fidelis eFt#et#FT Servilia Vestina parentes fecerunt
EDCS-14400379.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for Pontia Pompeiana, their deceased daughter, who lived 7 years. Gaius Pontius Pompeianus, centurion of the Second Italic Legion, 'Pia Fidelis', and Servilia Vestina, her parents, made this.
EDCS-14400379.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the deceased 7-year-old daughter, Pontia Pompeiana, by her parents, Gaius Pontius Pompeianus, a centurion of Legio II Italica, and Servilia Vestina.

EDCS-14400386.translation: To the Genius of the centuria, Aurelius Surus, a soldier [dedicates this].
EDCS-14400386.summary: This is a religious dedication made by the soldier Aurelius Surus to the Genius, or guardian spirit, of his military centuria.

EDCS-14400387.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Marinus vir perfectissimus cum suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400387.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Aurelius Marinus, a most perfect man, with his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400387.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Marinus, a vir perfectissimus, and his family, to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-14400388.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae Augustae ceterisque dis deabusque Aelius Restutus vir perfectissimus agens vice praesidis votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-14400388.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Queen Juno, Minerva Augusta, and the other gods and goddesses, Aelius Restutus, a most perfect man acting in place of the governor, willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400388.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Aelius Restutus, a vir perfectissimus acting as vice-governor, to a pantheon of deities including Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva, to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-14400389.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori dis deabusque omnibus Aurelius Severus
EDCS-14400389.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Preserver, and to all gods and goddesses, Aurelius Severus [dedicates this].
EDCS-14400389.summary: This is a religious dedication made by Aurelius Severus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator and all other gods and goddesses.

EDCS-14400390.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo tutori Marius Messorianus miles legionis pro se et omnibus suis  votum solvit laetus libens merito
EDCS-14400390.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Protector, Marius Messorianus, a soldier of the legion, willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and all his family.
EDCS-14400390.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Tutor by Marius Messorianus, a legionary soldier, for himself and his family.

EDCS-14400394.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely made for the health of an individual named Attia and possibly her family.

EDCS-14400398.text_edition: Claudio Augusto  Domitio Celeri  Respectio Vitali Aurelio Apolausto Aurelio Onesimo Sattio Marciano Claudio Iuliano       A M A I
EDCS-14400398.translation: To Claudius Augustus, Domitius Celer, Respectius Vitalis, Aurelius Apolaustus, Aurelius Onesimus, Sattius Marcianus, Claudius Iulianus. A. M. A. I.
EDCS-14400398.summary: This inscription appears to list several names, including an emperor Claudius Augustus and various individuals, possibly commemorating an event or dedication involving these people; the final letters 'A.M.A.I.' are an uncertain abbreviation.

EDCS-14400399.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Capitonio Urso @(obito) annorum XII Aurelius Capitonius candidatus legionis II Italicae
EDCS-14400399.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Capitonius Ursus, who died at 12 years of age. Aurelius Capitonius, a candidate of the Second Italic Legion [set this up].
EDCS-14400399.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Capitonius, a candidate of Legio II Italica, to Capitonius Ursus, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-14400401.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, preserving only the name Julia.

EDCS-14400405.text_edition: Crispiae coniugi obitae annorum XL Saecularis Sabiniae Dignae servus vivus fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-14400405.translation: To Crispiae, my deceased wife, 40 years old. Saecularis, the slave of Sabinia Dignae, made this while living for himself and his family.
EDCS-14400405.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Saecularis, a slave, dedicating a tombstone to his deceased wife Crispia, and also reserving space for himself and his family.

EDCS-14400408.text_edition: Herculi sacrum votum pro Quinto et suis Hermes posuit
EDCS-14400408.translation: Hermes placed this sacred vow for Hercules, for Quintus and his family.
EDCS-14400408.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Hermes dedicating an offering to Hercules on behalf of Quintus and his family.

EDCS-14400409.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae Verbicius vilicus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400409.translation: Verbicius, the bailiff, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Victoria Augusta.
EDCS-14400409.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Verbicius, a bailiff, fulfilling a vow to the goddess Victoria Augusta.

EDCS-14400410.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Florinus ex voto pro se et suiis sic libens laetus merito
EDCS-14400410.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Julius Florinus, from a vow for himself and his family, thus willingly, joyfully, and deservedly.
EDCS-14400410.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Julius Florinus dedicating an offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus as a fulfillment of a vow for himself and his family.

EDCS-14400413.text_edition: Marco Sacconio Parato annorum LX patrono optimo et Marco Antonio Clementi annorum LV coniugi karissimo et Rucconi filio annorum III Sacconia Docilis fecit sibi vivae et Vitali et Festo filiis vivis
EDCS-14400413.translation: Sacconia Docilis made this for herself, while living, and for her living sons Vitalis and Festus, in memory of Marcus Sacconius Paratus, 60 years old, an excellent patron, and Marcus Antonius Clemens, 55 years old, a dearest husband, and Rucconus, their son, 3 years old.
EDCS-14400413.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Sacconia Docilis dedicating a tombstone for herself and her living sons, and in memory of her deceased patron Marcus Sacconius Paratus, her dearest husband Marcus Antonius Clemens, and their son Rucconus.

EDCS-14400414.text_edition: Ulpia Pacata viva fecit sibi et Iulio Successo coniugi @(obito) annorum LV et Ulpiis Optato filio @(obito) annorum XXX et Cogitato filio @(obito) annorum XXV
EDCS-14400414.translation: Ulpia Pacata, while living, made this for herself and for Julius Successus, her deceased husband, 55 years old, and for her deceased sons Ulpius Optatus, 30 years old, and Cogitatus, 25 years old.
EDCS-14400414.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Ulpia Pacata dedicating a tombstone for herself while alive, and in memory of her deceased husband Julius Successus and her deceased sons Ulpius Optatus and Cogitatus.

EDCS-14400415.text_edition: Dis Manibus Chartius Tagaduni eques alae Augustae singularis natione Tunger annorum XXXV Valens Ianuari heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-14400415.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Chartius Tagaduni, a cavalryman of the Ala Augusta Singularis, of Tungrian nationality, 35 years old. Valens Ianuarius, his heir, had this made.
EDCS-14400415.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Chartius Tagaduni, a Tungrian cavalryman, erected by his heir Valens Ianuarius.

EDCS-14400418.translation: Tus, duumvir with judicial power of the colony.
EDCS-14400418.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a duumvir named Tus holding judicial power in a Roman colony, likely part of a public monument or honorary inscription.

EDCS-14400423.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vicciae Gai filiae Severae obitae annorum XXXII coniugi karissimae Caius Iulius Pontianus et sibi et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-14400423.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Viccia Severa, daughter of Gaius, 32 years old, a dearest wife. Gaius Julius Pontianus, while living, made this for her and for himself and his family.
EDCS-14400423.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Gaius Julius Pontianus dedicating a tombstone to his deceased wife Viccia Severa, and also reserving space for himself and his family.

EDCS-14400428.text_edition: Marci fili--    IIvir iure dicundo   ri
EDCS-14400428.translation: Son of Marcus, duumvir with judicial power.
EDCS-14400428.summary: This fragment likely records a public office held by the son of Marcus, specifically that of a duumvir exercising judicial authority.

EDCS-14400430.text_edition: ina ellius Candidus [] [] Primus posuerunt libentes laeti merito  dono dederunt die Nonis Septembribus
EDCS-14400430.translation: Ina Ellius Candidus and Primus placed this, willingly, joyfully, deservedly, and gave it as a gift on the Nones of September.
EDCS-14400430.summary: This inscription records a dedication or offering made by Ina Ellius Candidus and Primus, given willingly and joyfully, dated to the Nones of September.

EDCS-14400431.text_edition: Genio domini Paratus di Rufi servus  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400431.translation: To the Genius of the master, Paratus, slave of Rufus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400431.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Paratus, a slave of Rufus, to the Genius of his master, fulfilling a vow willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-14400432.text_edition: Volusiae Cai filiae Saturninae nnorum XVI Volusia Latina soror et Caius Vitorius Cupitus vir sibi et Cleuvoni Veitoris filio patri Voltisemae Bussumari filiae matri Vitorio Latino filio Vitoriae Secundinae filiae vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14400432.translation: For Volusia Saturnina, daughter of Gaius, 16 years old, Volusia Latina, her sister, and Gaius Vitorius Cupitus, her husband, made this for themselves and for Cleuvo, son of Vitor, their father; for Voltisema, daughter of Bussumarus, their mother; for Vitorius Latinus, their son; for Vitoria Secundina, their daughter, while living.
EDCS-14400432.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Volusia Latina and Gaius Vitorius Cupitus for Volusia Saturnina (aged 16), and also for themselves and other family members, including their parents, son, and daughter, while they were still alive.

EDCS-14400434.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription preserving part of the name Decimus Opetrius.

EDCS-14400437.text_edition: Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari ab Agunto milia passuum IIII
EDCS-14400437.translation: To Marcus Iulius Philippus, the most noble Caesar, 4 miles from Agunto.
EDCS-14400437.summary: This inscription is a milestone or honorary dedication to Marcus Iulius Philippus, identified as the most noble Caesar, located four miles from Agunto, likely from the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14400438.text_edition: Dacus Coponi Luciani servus sibi et Matronae coniugi obitae nnum XXXX et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-14400438.translation: Dacus, slave of Coponius Lucianus, made this for himself and for Matrona, his departed wife, who lived 40 years, and for his own family, while living.
EDCS-14400438.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Dacus, a slave of Coponius Lucianus, for himself, his deceased wife Matrona (who lived 40 years), and his family, prepared during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400439.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et dis deabusque omnibus Turbonius Fuscinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito dominis nnostris Severo III et Antonino consulibus
EDCS-14400439.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses, Turbonius Fuscinus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italic Legion, pious and faithful, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family, in the consulship of our masters Severus (for the third time) and Antoninus.
EDCS-14400439.summary: This is a votive inscription made by Turbonius Fuscinus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Italica, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses, fulfilling a vow for himself and his family, dated to the consulship of Severus (III) and Antoninus (likely 222 AD).

EDCS-14400440.text_edition: Quintus Sabinius Euprepes vivus fecit sibi et Sabiniae Tertullae uxori optimae obitae nnorum XXXV
EDCS-14400440.translation: Quintus Sabinius Euprepes made this while living for himself and for Sabinia Tertulla, his excellent wife, who died at 35 years of age.
EDCS-14400440.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Quintus Sabinius Euprepes for himself and for his deceased wife, Sabinia Tertulla, who died at the age of 35, prepared during his lifetime.

EDCS-14400443.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae pro salute Luci Pomponi Aquilini Potentis Florentina nutrix votum solvit libens laeta merito
EDCS-14400443.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, for the health of Lucius Pomponius Aquilinus Potens, Florentina, his nurse, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-14400443.summary: This is a votive offering to Fortuna Augusta by Florentina, the nurse of Lucius Pomponius Aquilinus Potens, made for his health, fulfilling her vow willingly, joyfully, and deservedly.

EDCS-14400444.text_edition: Cocceio Cupito militi legionis II Italicae adiutori principis consularis @(obito) nnorum XXII stipendiorum VII Cocceius Proculus beneficiarius consularis fratri fecit
EDCS-14400444.translation: To Cocceius Cupitus, soldier of the Second Italic Legion, assistant to the consular governor, who died at 22 years of age and 7 years of service. Cocceius Proculus, consular beneficiarius, made this for his brother.
EDCS-14400444.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cocceius Proculus to his brother, Cocceius Cupitus, a soldier of the Legio II Italica who died at a young age.

EDCS-14400446.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Cupitia, likely a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-14400447.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae pro salute Luci Albi Attici Crius curante Cattio Seio legato  Deo Invicto Mithrae pro salute Luci AlIbi#Albi#AIBI Attici Crius rius curante Cattio Seio legato
EDCS-14400447.translation: To the Invincible God Mithras, for the well-being of Lucius Albius Atticus Crius, with the care of Cattius Seius, legate.
EDCS-14400447.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras for the health of Lucius Albius Atticus Crius, sponsored by Cattius Seius.

EDCS-14400449.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto patri patriae a Teurnia milia passuum XLV
EDCS-14400449.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, father of the fatherland. From Teurnia, 45 miles.
EDCS-14400449.summary: This is a milestone or dedicatory inscription honoring Emperor Philip the Arab, indicating a distance of 45 miles from Teurnia.

EDCS-14400451.text_edition: Lucius Vedius Optatus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400451.translation: Lucius Vedius Optatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400451.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Lucius Vedius Optatus's fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14400453.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate III consul II pater patriae proconsul et Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar princeps iuventutis domini indulgentissimi pontes refecerunt et vias munierunt et miliaria restituerunt mille passuum I
EDCS-14400453.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, in his third year of tribunician power, twice consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, princeps iuventutis, most indulgent lords, rebuilt bridges, paved roads, and restored milestones. One mile.
EDCS-14400453.summary: This is a milestone or public works inscription commemorating the repair of bridges, roads, and milestones by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and Maximus Caesar, likely dating to 237/238 CE.

EDCS-14400454.text_edition: Marco Haterio Luci filio Claudia Summo decurioni municipii Iuvavi IIviro iure dicundo plebes sic oppidana optimo civi ob annonam relevatam
EDCS-14400454.translation: To Marcus Haterius Summus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, decurion of the municipality of Iuvavum, duovir for dispensing justice. The townspeople to an excellent citizen for relieving the grain supply.
EDCS-14400454.summary: This is a public honorific inscription dedicated by the townspeople of Iuvavum to Marcus Haterius Summus for his beneficence in relieving the grain supply, likely a statue base.

EDCS-14400457.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument, mentioning Seccius Victor.

EDCS-14400459.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Of Candidus.
EDCS-14400459.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Candidus, under the traditional formula for the Manes.

EDCS-14400460.text_edition: Profuturo vestigiatori Lolli Honorati obito nnorum XXX Barb et rina parentes et coniux vivi posuerunt
EDCS-14400460.translation: To Profuturus, investigator of Lollius Honoratus, who died at 30 years of age. Barb and Rina, his parents and wife, erected this while living.
EDCS-14400460.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Profuturus, an 'investigator' of Lollius Honoratus, erected by his parents and wife during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14400461.text_edition: Vindicio Verecundi vivus fecit sibi et Secundae coniugi et suis
EDCS-14400461.translation: Vindicius, son of Verecundus, made this while living, for himself and his wife Secunda and his family.
EDCS-14400461.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Vindicius, son of Verecundus, prepared a tomb for himself, his wife Secunda, and his family while still alive, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400462.text_edition: io Ingenuo et  filiis viva fecit Aurelia
EDCS-14400462.translation: Aurelia, while living, made this for Ingenuus and her sons.
EDCS-14400462.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Aurelia, while still alive, prepared a tomb for Ingenuus and her sons, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400465.text_edition: viva fecit sibi et marito et privigno obito annorum XV
EDCS-14400465.translation: While living, she made this for herself and her husband and her stepson, who died at fifteen years of age.
EDCS-14400465.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that a woman, while still alive, prepared a tomb for herself, her husband, and her stepson who died at the age of fifteen, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400468.text_edition: Ottus Devilli filius Bouda Momuci filia coniunx vivi filii fecerunt
EDCS-14400468.translation: Ottus, son of Devillus, and Bouda, daughter of Momucus, his wife, made this while their sons were living.
EDCS-14400468.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Ottus, son of Devillus, and his wife Bouda, daughter of Momucus, made this monument while their sons were still alive, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400470.text_edition: Dis Manibus Moderatiae Ursulae -- @(obitae) annorum XLVIIII coniugi carissimae pientissimae et opsequentissimae sic Cupitius Cupitanus decurio civitatis et sibi et Cupitanus filius fecerunt
EDCS-14400470.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Moderatia Ursula, who died at 49 years of age, his dearest, most devoted, and most compliant wife. Cupitius Cupitanus, decurion of the city, and Cupitanus, his son, made this for her and for himself.
EDCS-14400470.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cupitius Cupitanus, a city decurion, and his son Cupitanus, to Moderatia Ursula, his beloved wife who died at 49, and also for himself, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400471.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning 'Nus Titia Valentina' and possibly a continuation, making its full purpose unclear, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400472.text_edition: Dis Manibus Successus Summi et Accepta Accepti filia coniux eius vivi fecerunt sibi et Citato filio @(obito) annorum II
EDCS-14400472.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Successus, son of Summus, and Accepta, daughter of Acceptus, his wife, made this while living, for themselves and for their son Citatus, who died at two years of age.
EDCS-14400472.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Successus, son of Summus, and his wife Accepta, daughter of Acceptus, prepared a tomb for themselves and their son Citatus, who died at the age of two, while still alive, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400474.text_edition: Nimphis sic Geminis sacrum Gaius Annius Iuvenalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400474.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs, the Gemini. Gaius Annius Iuvenalis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400474.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs (specifically the Gemini) by Gaius Annius Iuvenalis, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400476.translation: For Titius Vibianus, his son, aged 15 years.
EDCS-14400476.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titius Vibianus, who died at the age of 15, likely placed by a parent, from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400478.text_edition: Avita Ingenui filia annorum et Avito filio annorum Crispinus vivus posuit
EDCS-14400478.translation: Crispinus, while living, placed this for Avita, daughter of Ingenuus, of [---] years, and for Avitus, his son, of [---] years.
EDCS-14400478.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Crispinus, while still alive, erected a monument for Avita, daughter of Ingenuus, and his son Avitus, whose ages are not fully preserved, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14400482.text_edition: Cupitus Cammi Sabini servus fecit sibi et Pri Vibiae coniugi annorum XXV et Samudae Cupiti filiae annorum II
EDCS-14400482.translation: Cupitus, the slave of Cammus Sabinus, made this for himself and for his wife, Pri Vibia, aged 25 years, and for his daughter, Samuda Cupiti, aged 2 years.
EDCS-14400482.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by a slave for himself, his wife, and his daughter, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400483.text_edition: Secundo Tecci filio et Tamisietae Avetae filiae et Avito filio annorum XXV heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-14400483.translation: To Secundus, son of Teccus, and to Tamisieta, daughter of Aveta, and to Avitus, their son, aged 25 years, the heir had this made.
EDCS-14400483.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Secundus, Tamisieta, and their son Avitus, commissioned by an unnamed heir.

EDCS-14400484.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a veteran named Covnertus.

EDCS-14400485.text_edition: filio annorum XV Respectus frater fecit
