EDCS-14400485.translation: To his son, aged 15 years, his brother Respectus made this.
EDCS-14400485.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a brother named Respectus for his fifteen-year-old son.

EDCS-14400487.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Pio Felici invicto Augusto pontifici maximo patri patriae tribunicia potestate consuli procon suli ordo Solvensium
EDCS-14400487.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius Pius Felix, unconquered Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Father of the Country, with tribunician power, consul, proconsul, the council of the Solvensians [dedicated this].
EDCS-14400487.summary: This is a public dedication, likely for a statue base, honoring Emperor Claudius Gothicus (Marcus Aurelius Claudius) by the local council of Solva during his reign (268-270 AD).

EDCS-14400488.text_edition: Caio Memmio Cavarino et Donniae Vennonis filiae matri et patri filii fecerunt
EDCS-14400488.translation: To Gaius Memmius Cavarinus and Donnia, daughter of Venno, his mother and father, their sons made this.
EDCS-14400488.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by sons to their parents, Gaius Memmius Cavarinus and Donnia.

EDCS-14400489.text_edition: Honoratus annorum LXX et Materna coniux Finitus filius et Vibena coniux
EDCS-14400489.translation: Honoratus, aged 70 years, and Materna his wife, Finitus his son, and Vibena his wife.
EDCS-14400489.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Honoratus and Materna, also mentioning their son Finitus and his wife Vibena, likely a family tomb.

EDCS-14400491.text_edition: Sammus Masculi filius annorum  et Adnamiano Siricci sic filio annorum
EDCS-14400491.translation: Sammus, son of Masculus, aged [---] years, and Adnamianus, son of Siriccus, aged [---] years.
EDCS-14400491.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sammus and Adnamianus, whose ages are illegible, likely a shared or family monument.

EDCS-14400492.text_edition: Itto Expediti et Nigella Cotelei et Covrunae filiae
EDCS-14400492.translation: Itto, [daughter] of Expeditus, and Nigella, [daughter] of Cotelus, and Covruna, their daughter.
EDCS-14400492.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Itto, Nigella, and their daughter Covruna, likely a family monument.

EDCS-14400493.text_edition: VitIali#Vitali#VIIALI Ursini Pinitu Satullaes sic liberta marito optimo et sibi fecit
EDCS-14400493.translation: To Vitalis Ursini, Pinitu Satulla, his freedwoman, made this for her excellent husband and for herself.
EDCS-14400493.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by a freedwoman named Pinitu Satulla for her husband Vitalis Ursini and for herself, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400494.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Vera viva fecit sibi et S Secundino coniugCi#coniugi#CONIUCI carissimo @(obito) annorum L
EDCS-14400494.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Vera, in her lifetime, made this for herself and for S. Secundinus, her dearest husband, who died at the age of 50.
EDCS-14400494.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb made by Aelia Vera for herself and her deceased husband, S. Secundinus, who died at age 50, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400495.text_edition: Tertius Vallauni vivus fecit sibi et Deivillae Duni filiae uxori karissimae
EDCS-14400495.translation: Tertius Vallauni, in his lifetime, made this for himself and for Deivilla Duni, his dearest wife.
EDCS-14400495.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tomb made by Tertius Vallauni for himself and his beloved wife, Deivilla Duni, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400496.text_edition: Tertulliano II semis  Dis Manibus Restutus Dubitani et coniugem sic Meduni Rep et  VAM sima XXX
EDCS-14400496.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Tertullianus II, one half [part]. Restutus Dubitani and his wife [made this] thus for Meduni Rep. and VAM sima, 30 [years old].
EDCS-14400496.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for multiple individuals, including Tertullianus and Meduni Rep., erected by Restutus Dubitani and his wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400497.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Uxlemitano  Aurelius Celsinus ex voto pro Marcellino filio milite legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit laetus libens merito
EDCS-14400497.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Uxlemitanus. Aurelius Celsinus, in accordance with a vow made for his son Marcellinus, a soldier of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, gladly, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400497.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Uxlemitanus, made by Aurelius Celsinus in fulfillment of a vow for his son Marcellinus, a soldier of the Legio XIIII Gemina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400499.text_edition: Niger Hilari vivus fecit sibi et Moniae coniugi et Respecto Terti filio annorum XXVI
EDCS-14400499.translation: Niger Hilarius, in his lifetime, made this for himself and for Monia, his wife, and for Respectus, son of Tertius, aged 26 years.
EDCS-14400499.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb made by Niger Hilarius for himself, his wife Monia, and his son Respectus, who died at age 26, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400500.text_edition: Dis Manibus positum Sexto Sacretio Prisco Quirina Solva genero pientissimo annorum XXVI et Capitoniae Iuliae filiae pientissimae annorum XX Iustinus Similis et Capitonia Ingenuina coniux
EDCS-14400500.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Placed for Sextus Sacretius Priscus, a most devoted son-in-law, aged 26 years, by Quirina Solva. And for Capitonia Iulia, a most devoted daughter, aged 20 years, by Iustinus Similis and Capitonia Ingenuina, his wife.
EDCS-14400500.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, commemorating Sextus Sacretius Priscus (aged 26) and Capitonia Iulia (aged 20), erected by their respective family members, Quirina Solva, Iustinus Similis, and Capitonia Ingenuina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400503.text_edition: Tito Turbonio Blasto annorum LX et Novello filio annorum XV Tito Turbonio Callisto annorum LXXXX et Turboniae Fuscae annorum LXV parentibus pientissimis
EDCS-14400503.translation: To Titus Turbonius Blastus, aged 60 years, and Novellus, his son, aged 15 years; to Titus Turbonius Callistus, aged 90 years, and Turbonia Fusca, aged 65 years, most devoted parents.
EDCS-14400503.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating multiple family members: Titus Turbonius Blastus, his son Novellus, Titus Turbonius Callistus, and Turbonia Fusca, honored as most devoted parents, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400507.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sabinio Exuperanti et Sabinia Orea coniugi carissimo fecit vivBa#viva#VIBA annoruO(m)#annoru(m)#ANNORO LXX obito
EDCS-14400507.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Sabinius Exuperans and Sabinia Orea, her dearest husband. She, in her lifetime, made this at the age of 70 years, having died.
EDCS-14400507.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Sabinius Exuperans and his wife Sabinia Orea, erected by Sabinia Orea in her lifetime, who died at age 70, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400508.text_edition: uxori suai sic et Ingenuai sic filiae
EDCS-14400508.translation: To his wife thus and to his daughter Ingenua thus.
EDCS-14400508.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, dedicated to a wife and daughter named Ingenua, whose names are preserved, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400509.text_edition: Primianus Accepti vivus fecitI#fecit#FECII sibi etI#et#EI Septumeae sic coniugi annorum
EDCS-14400509.translation: Primianus Acceptus, in his lifetime, made this for himself and for Septumea, his wife, aged [---] years.
EDCS-14400509.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb made by Primianus Acceptus for himself and his wife Septumea, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14400510.text_edition: Sammus Terti et Valentina  parentes vivi fecerunt sibi et
EDCS-14400510.translation: Sammus, son of Tertius, and Valentina, his parents, made this for themselves while alive, and for...
EDCS-14400510.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Sammus and Valentina for themselves while still living, likely for their eventual burial.

EDCS-14400512.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate imperator X consul III pater patriae proconsul dominus indulgentissimus a Solva milia passuum XL
EDCS-14400512.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the tenth time, Consul for the third time, Father of the Country, Proconsul, most indulgent lord, 40 Roman miles from Solva.
EDCS-14400512.summary: This inscription records the full titulature and honors of Emperor Severus Alexander, indicating a milestone or similar public dedication 40 miles from Solva.

EDCS-14400514.summary: This fragment likely dedicates a monument or object to Covertus, son of Arbitto.

EDCS-14400515.text_edition: Dis Manibus perpetuae securitati Longinus et Lupa liberti Proculines sic vivi fecerunt sibi et Lucio filio pientissimo @(obito) annorum XX
EDCS-14400515.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for perpetual security. Longinus and Lupa, freedpersons of Proculinus, made this for themselves while alive, and for their most dutiful son Lucius, who died at 20 years of age.
EDCS-14400515.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by the freedpersons Longinus and Lupa for themselves and their deceased son Lucius.

EDCS-14400517.text_edition: Censorinus Adiutoris et Iantumara Finiti vivi fecerunt sibi et Ursino filio militi in cohorte Breucorum annorum XXV  et Censorino filio annorum XXX
EDCS-14400517.translation: Censorinus, son of Adiutor, and Iantumara, daughter of Finitus, made this while alive for themselves, and for their son Ursinus, a soldier in the cohort of the Breuci, who died at 25 years of age, and for their son Censorinus, who died at 30 years of age.
EDCS-14400517.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Censorinus and Iantumara for themselves while living, and for their two deceased sons, Ursinus and Censorinus.

EDCS-14400518.text_edition: Caio Annaeo Cai liberto Spectato et Iuliae Terentinae uxori
EDCS-14400518.translation: To Gaius Annaeus Spectatus, freedman of Gaius, and to Julia Terentina, his wife.
EDCS-14400518.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Gaius Annaeus Spectatus, a freedman, and his wife, Julia Terentina, likely on a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-14400519.text_edition: Adiutor Terti filius vivus fecit sibi et Avetae Quarti filiae coniugii suae
EDCS-14400519.translation: Adiutor, son of Tertius, made this while alive for himself and for Aveta, daughter of Quartus, his spouse.
EDCS-14400519.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Adiutor for himself and his wife Aveta while they were still living.

EDCS-14400520.text_edition: Sabinus Esulli filius vivos sic fecit sibi et uxori Tuioni Saxionis filiae et Noibioni filio annorum X
EDCS-14400520.translation: Sabinus, son of Esullus, made this while alive for himself and for his wife Tuionia, daughter of Saxio, and for their son Noibio, 10 years old.
EDCS-14400520.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Sabinus for himself, his wife Tuionia, and their deceased son Noibio.

EDCS-14400521.text_edition: Sammus Ucci fecit vivus sibi et  filio anni I
EDCS-14400521.translation: Sammus, son of Uccus, made this while alive for himself and for his son, aged 1 year.
EDCS-14400521.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Sammus for himself and his deceased one-year-old son while Sammus was still alive.

EDCS-14400524.text_edition: Domino nostro Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari Pio Felici Invicto Augusto
EDCS-14400524.translation: To our lord Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus.
EDCS-14400524.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely on a statue base or public monument, probably dating to his reign (244-249 AD).

EDCS-14400525.text_edition: Adiutor Cupiti libertus annorum  fecit sibi et Suttae matri
EDCS-14400525.translation: Adiutor, freedman of Cupitus, aged [---] years, made this for himself and for Sutta, his mother.
EDCS-14400525.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a freedman named Adiutor who constructed a monument for himself and his mother, Sutta.

EDCS-14400526.text_edition: Autoscutto Montissi filio et Septimae Ressimari filiae uxori filii fecerunt
EDCS-14400526.translation: They made this for Autoscutto, son of Montissus, and for Septima, daughter of Ressimarus, wife of their son.
EDCS-14400526.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by unidentified parents for their son, Autoscutto, and his wife, Septima.

EDCS-14400530.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Aureliis Flavii Severus et Senator et Bellianus et Severianus iuvenior sic ex voto solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14400530.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Expeller, Aurelii Flavius Severus and Senator and Bellianus and Severianus the Younger, thus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14400530.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by four Aurelii to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Depulsor, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14400531.text_edition: Genio Attiorum Marciani et Vitelliani Marcellus libertus  Genio Attiorum Marciani et Vitelliani Marcellus libertus
EDCS-14400531.translation: To the Genius of the Attii Marcianus and Vitellianus, Marcellus the freedman [---] To the Genius of the Attii Marcianus and Vitellianus, Marcellus the freedman.
EDCS-14400531.summary: This is a duplicated votive inscription dedicated by Marcellus, a freedman, to the Genius of two individuals, Attius Marcianus and Attius Vitellianus.

EDCS-14400541.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Hostilio Tertino annorum XL et Valeriae Sedatae coniugi Hostilii Tertullinus et Tertullus parentibus optimis filii fecerunt
EDCS-14400541.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: To Hostilius Tertinus, aged 40 years, and to Valeria Sedata, wife of Hostilius Tertullinus, his sons Tertullinus and Tertullus made this for their excellent parents.
EDCS-14400541.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by two sons, Tertullinus and Tertullus, for their deceased parents, Hostilius Tertinus and Valeria Sedata.

EDCS-14400542.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning a woman named Fuccia Secundina, the full context of which is lost.

EDCS-14400543.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae Kanius Tertullinus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400543.translation: To Nemesis Augusta, Kanius Tertullinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400543.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Kanius Tertullinus to Nemesis Augusta, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14400544.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Quadrubiis sic Augustis Gabinius Quintus pro salute sua suorum votum solvit libens laetus meritu sic
EDCS-14400544.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Quadrubius, thus, to the Augusti, Gabinius Quintus willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of his family, thus.
EDCS-14400544.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Gabinius Quintus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Quadrubius and the Augusti, made for his and his family's well-being, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14400545.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque omnibus Alupio sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400545.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to all gods and goddesses, Alupius thus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400545.summary: This is a broad votive inscription dedicated by Alupius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14400547.text_edition: Ursus pro se suisque votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400547.translation: Ursus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-14400547.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Ursus, made for his own and his family's benefit, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14400548.text_edition: Dis Manibus Donatius Surus et Aurelia Zosima vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14400548.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Donatius Surus and Aurelia Zosima made this while alive.
EDCS-14400548.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Donatius Surus and Aurelia Zosima prepared their own tomb or monument during their lifetime.

EDCS-14400549.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Bottius Aventinus et Valerius Ursinus
EDCS-14400549.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Bottius Aventinus and Valerius Ursinus [made this].
EDCS-14400549.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Bottius Aventinus and Valerius Ursinus, likely for a religious offering or monument.

EDCS-14400550.text_edition: Maximus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400550.translation: Maximus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-14400550.summary: This inscription records that a man named Maximus fulfilled a vow, likely to a deity, on behalf of himself and his household.

EDCS-14400553.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio huius loci Olympius Augusti nostri vilicus stationis Escensis aedem et aram dedicavit
EDCS-14400553.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of this place, Olympius, our Augustus's bailiff of the Escensis station, dedicated this shrine and altar.
EDCS-14400553.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a shrine and altar by Olympius, an imperial bailiff, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local Genius at the Escensis station, indicating a religious offering by an imperial servant.

EDCS-14400554.text_edition: Dis Manibus Terentinus Taurionis filius e ac obitus annorum XXX et Veria Cosivia mater obita annorum LXV Aurelius Taurio coniugi et filio et Flavina filia eius vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14400554.publication: ILLPRON 01509 = Schillinger 00220 = NIBayern 00013 = AGN-1, 00121 = AE 1973, 00381 = AE 1978, 00593
EDCS-14400554.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Terentinus, son of Taurio, who died at 30 years of age, and Veria Cosivia, his mother, who died at 65 years of age. Aurelius Taurio made this for his wife and son, and Flavina, his daughter, made this while alive.
EDCS-14400554.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Terentinus and Veria Cosivia, erected by Aurelius Taurio for his wife and son, and by their daughter Flavina, while they were still living.

EDCS-14400559.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcellinus [lies here].
EDCS-14400559.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of an individual named Marcellinus.

EDCS-14400561.text_edition: XXXVI Maurus servus Matuli Iuvenis coniugi karissimae vivus fecit
EDCS-14400561.translation: 36. Maurus, a slave of Matulus Iuvenis, made this for his dearest wife while alive.
EDCS-14400561.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Maurus, a slave of Matulus Iuvenis, for his beloved wife, indicating he prepared it during his lifetime; the number 36 might refer to her age or another measure.

EDCS-14400564.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aviliae Ledae defunctae annorum XXXV Mutilius Fortunatus et Avilius Gratus alumni fecerunt
EDCS-14400564.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Avilia Leda, who died at 35 years of age. Mutilius Fortunatus and Avilius Gratus, her foster children, made this.
EDCS-14400564.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Avilia Leda, who died at 35, by her foster children, Mutilius Fortunatus and Avilius Gratus.

EDCS-14400565.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hermianus Augustorum nnostrorum scrutator stationis Bilachiniensis et Leontia coniux fecerunt Caprae filiae karissimae obitae nnorum V mensium XI dierum XIII
EDCS-14400565.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Hermianus, a customs inspector of our Augusti at the Bilachiniensis station, and Leontia, his wife, made this for their dearest daughter Capra, who died at 5 years, 11 months, and 13 days of age.
EDCS-14400565.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Hermianus, an imperial customs inspector, and his wife Leontia, for their beloved young daughter, Capra.

EDCS-14400566.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XII consul II pater patriae proconsul et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus tribunicia potestate IIII proconsul et Publius Septimius [Geta] miliaria vetustate collapsa restituerunt curam agente Marco Iuventio Suro Proclo legato pro praetore ab Agunto milia passuum LXVII
EDCS-14400566.translation: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, acclaimed imperator 12 times, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country, proconsul; and Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, holding tribunician power for the 4th time, proconsul; and Publius Septimius Geta restored the milestones collapsed from age. Marcus Iuventius Surus Proclus, legate pro praetore, supervised the work, at 67 miles from Agunto.
EDCS-14400566.summary: This inscription records the restoration of aged milestones by the emperors Lucius Septimius Severus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), and Publius Septimius Geta, under the supervision of legate Marcus Iuventius Surus Proclus, located 67 miles from Agunto, dating between 209 and 211 AD.

EDCS-14400568.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XII consul II pater patriae proconsul et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus tribunicia potestate IIII proconsul [et Publius Septimius Geta] [nobilissimus Caesar] miliaria vetustate conlabsa sic restituerunt curante Marco Iuventio Suro Proculo legato pro praetore ab Agunto milia passuum XXXXV
EDCS-14400568.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, victorious in Arabia and Adiabene, greatest victor in Parthia, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, acclaimed imperator twelve times, consul twice, father of his country, proconsul, and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, proconsul, and Publius Septimius Geta, most noble Caesar, restored milestones that had collapsed due to age, under the supervision of Marcus Iuventius Suro Proculus, legate pro praetore, from Aguntum, 45 miles.
EDCS-14400568.summary: This inscription records the restoration of milestones by Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, under the supervision of a provincial legate, likely dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14400570.text_edition: Termunibus sic Augustis Quintus Proculeius Finitus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400570.translation: To the revered boundary stones, Quintus Proculeius Finitus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400570.summary: This inscription is a votive offering made by Quintus Proculeius Finitus to sacred boundary stones.

EDCS-14400571.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning parts of names like Aurelius and Opt[atus/ima].

EDCS-14400572.text_edition: In memoriam - decurionis Claudiae Celeiae  Quintus Terentius
EDCS-14400572.translation: In memory of [---] decurion of Claudia Celeia, Quintus Terentius [set this up].
EDCS-14400572.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Quintus Terentius in memory of an unnamed decurion of Claudia Celeia.

EDCS-14400573.text_edition: Germani v(ivus)? f(ecit)? sibi et uriae osiae Secundinae  libert- et estiae Firmina onius Firmus  XXXV hic sit- est
EDCS-14400573.translation: Germani, while living, made this for himself and for Uria Osia Secundina, freedwoman, and for Estia Firmina. [---] One Firmus. He was buried here for 35 years.
EDCS-14400573.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Germani during his lifetime for himself and several other individuals, including freedwomen, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-14400578.text_edition: Sedato Augusto et cultoribus eius Lucius Aufillenius Crispinus siginum et aram donum dedit
EDCS-14400578.translation: To the revered Sedatus and his worshippers, Lucius Aufillenius Crispinus gave a standard and an altar as a gift.
EDCS-14400578.summary: This inscription records a gift of a standard and an altar from Lucius Aufillenius Crispinus to the god Sedatus and his devotees.

EDCS-14400580.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Gaiano cives sic Surus sic decurioni Italicae @(obito) annorum L et Aurelio Maximo nepoti @(obito) annorum XXXV Caius Gaianus et Aurelius Granus faciendum cQuraverunt#curaverunt#QURAERUN
EDCS-14400580.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aurelius Gaianus, a citizen of Surus, a decurion of Italica, who died at 50 years old, and for Aurelius Maximus, his grandson, who died at 35 years old, Caius Gaianus and Aurelius Granus had this made.
EDCS-14400580.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Aurelius Gaianus, a decurion of Italica, and his grandson Aurelius Maximus, erected by Caius Gaianus and Aurelius Granus.

EDCS-14400582.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nundinus Respecti vivus fecit sibi et Secundinae Secundini filiae annorum XXX et Firminam filiam annorum XL et Iunius Lucillianus annorum LX
EDCS-14400582.translation: To the Divine Manes. Nundinus, son of Respectus, made this while living for himself and for Secundina, daughter of Secundinus, 30 years old, and for Firmina, his daughter, 40 years old, and for Iunius Lucillianus, 60 years old.
EDCS-14400582.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Nundinus, son of Respectus, during his lifetime for himself and several family members, including his daughters and Iunius Lucillianus.

EDCS-14400583.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primus Finiti filius vivus sibi fecit et coniugi suae posuit
EDCS-14400583.translation: To the Divine Manes. Primus, son of Finitus, made this for himself while living, and placed it for his wife.
EDCS-14400583.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Primus, son of Finitus, during his lifetime for himself and his wife.

EDCS-14400584.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  et dis deabusque omnibus Victorius Crispinus liberatus periculis  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400584.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses, Victorius Crispinus, freed from dangers, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400584.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Victorius Crispinus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all deities in gratitude for deliverance from dangers.

EDCS-14400585.text_edition: In memoriam Luci Matti Publi fili Claudia Probi decurionis Celeiae Publius Mattius Luci filius Bellicus IIvir iure dicundo posuit  In memoriam Luci Matti Publi fili Claudia Probi decurionis Celeiae Publius Mattius Luci filius Bellicus IIvir iure dicundo posuit
EDCS-14400585.publication: ILLPRON 01607 = AGN-1, 00093a = ILJug-02, 01183a = EQNoricum-C, 00031 = AE 1980, 00668 = RICeleia 00068
EDCS-14400585.translation: In memory of Lucius Mattius, son of Publius, of the Claudia tribe, Probus, decurion of Celeia, Publius Mattius, son of Lucius, Bellicus, duumvir with judicial power, placed this. In memory of Lucius Mattius, son of Publius, of the Claudia tribe, Probus, decurion of Celeia, Publius Mattius, son of Lucius, Bellicus, duumvir with judicial power, placed this.
EDCS-14400585.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument placed by Publius Mattius Bellicus, a duumvir, in memory of Lucius Mattius Probus, a decurion of Celeia.

EDCS-14400588.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly recording a freedman of Quintus.

EDCS-14400589.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto divi Antonini filio divi Severi nepoti consuli II patri patriae
EDCS-14400589.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Severus, consul for the second time, father of the country.
EDCS-14400589.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla, listing his titles and lineage, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-14400590.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis
EDCS-14400590.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power.
EDCS-14400590.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Claudius Gothicus, listing his imperial titles, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-14400591.text_edition: MaX @(obit-) annorum  et Ma infelicissim eorum AMTIA
EDCS-14400591.translation: Max, who died at the age of years, and the most unfortunate Ma, their AMTIA.
EDCS-14400591.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, mentioning individuals named Max and Ma, and possibly an age or a relationship.

EDCS-14400592.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, possibly relating to an acceptance or reception, with a partial name including Gaius and Sextus.

EDCS-14400593.text_edition: Stritto Brem filius vivus fecit et sibi Camona Attonis filia annorum LX et Ressatus annorum XXX et Rumo annorum XX hic siti sunt Darbosa posuit
EDCS-14400593.translation: Stritto, son of Brem, made this while living, for himself and for Camona, daughter of Atto, aged 60, and Ressatus, aged 30, and Rumo, aged 20; they are buried here. Darbosa placed this.
EDCS-14400593.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Stritto for himself and his family members Camona, Ressatus, and Rumo, with Darbosa as the dedicator of the stone.

EDCS-14400594.text_edition: Ateloudo Sabini filio nnorum C et Boniatae Tutor(is) filiae uxori annorum Atin
EDCS-14400594.translation: To Ateloudo, son of Sabinus, aged 100, and to Boniata, daughter of Tutor, his wife, aged Atin.
EDCS-14400594.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Ateloudo, son of Sabinus, aged 100, and his wife Boniata, daughter of Tutor, with her age partially illegible.

EDCS-14400596.text_edition: A  Et Maximo filio annorum XXX  Dis Manibus Aulo Vedernae Maximo harispici sic annorum LXXV et Masculiniae Respectae coniugi annorum XL Aulus Vederna Maximinus parentibus et Vedernae Maximinae sorori annorum XXII et Tucciae Secundinae coniugi annorum XXX  pientissimae
EDCS-14400596.translation: A. And to Maximus, his son, aged 30. To the Spirits of the Dead, to Aulus Vederna Maximus, a soothsayer, aged 75, and to Masculinia Respecta, his wife, aged 40. Aulus Vederna Maximinus [made this] for his most devoted parents and for Vederna Maximina, his sister, aged 22, and for Tuccia Secundina, his wife, aged 30.
EDCS-14400596.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Aulus Vederna Maximinus to his parents, Aulus Vederna Maximus (a soothsayer) and Masculinia Respecta, as well as to his sister Vederna Maximina and his wife Tuccia Secundina.

EDCS-14400597.text_edition: Mogio Tritouti libertus vivus fecit sibi et suis Dubnia Mogionis filia annorum XX hic sita est Vetra Mogionis nnum
EDCS-14400597.translation: Mogio, freedman of Tritoutus, made this while living, for himself and his family. Dubnia, daughter of Mogio, aged 20, is buried here. Vetra, daughter of Mogio, for years.
EDCS-14400597.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Mogio, a freedman, for himself and his family, specifically mentioning his deceased daughter Dubnia and another daughter Vetra.

EDCS-14400598.text_edition: Romani filius sibi et Antonio Iudino filio annorum VI
EDCS-14400598.translation: Romani, son of Romani, for himself and for his son Antonius Judinus, who lived six years.
EDCS-14400598.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by a father for himself and his young deceased son.

EDCS-14400600.text_edition: Boniatus Bardonis vivus fecit sibi et Mogiae Terti annorum L Septimius Secundinus miles legionis II Italicae stupendiorum sic XV annorum XL Aurelia Ursa annorum XXX
EDCS-14400600.translation: Boniatus, son of Bardo, made this while living, for himself and for Mogia Tertia, 50 years old. Septimius Secundinus, soldier of the Second Italic Legion, of 15 years' stipends, 40 years old. Aurelia Ursa, 30 years old.
EDCS-14400600.summary: This inscription records a tomb made by Boniatus while still alive for himself and several other individuals, likely family members or comrades, including a soldier and two women of stated ages.

EDCS-14400601.text_edition: Silvanus Maximi filius annorum LXX testamento fieri iussit Bella viva fecit coniugi et filio
EDCS-14400601.translation: Silvanus, son of Maximus, 70 years old, ordered this to be made by his will. Bella, while living, made this for her husband and son.
EDCS-14400601.summary: This funerary inscription records the testamentary wishes of Silvanus and a separate dedication by Bella for her husband and son, suggesting a family tomb or shared monument.

EDCS-14400603.text_edition: Noibio Elgiati filius nnorum C hic est Courtula Adbugiouni filia nnorum LXX hic est Iunius gi filius NOVTVISVI
EDCS-14400603.translation: Noibius, son of Elgiatus, 100 years old, rests here. Courtula, daughter of Adbugiounus, 70 years old, rests here. Junius, son of gi, NOVTVISVI.
EDCS-14400603.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Noibius and Courtula, specifying their ages and burial place, with an additional, partially illegible entry for Junius.

EDCS-14400604.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Defensori sacrum Aurelius Coriphus pro se et suis ex visu votum solvit libens merito Sabino et Venusto consulibus
EDCS-14400604.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Defensor. Aurelius Coriphus, for himself and his family, gladly and deservedly fulfilled a vow based on a vision, when Sabinus and Venustus were consuls.
EDCS-14400604.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Defensor by Aurelius Coriphus for his family, fulfilling a vow made after a vision and dated to a specific consulship (likely 240 CE).

EDCS-14400605.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Aurelius Coriphus Barthiae filius pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400605.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Coriphus, son of Barthias, for himself and his family, gladly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-14400605.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Coriphus, son of Barthias, on behalf of himself and his household.

EDCS-14400608.text_edition: Atecurus Docnimari fE(ilius)#f(ilius)#E annorum XLI Sexta Mittonis fE(ilia)#f(ilia)#E
EDCS-14400608.translation: Atecurus, son of Docnimarus, 41 years old. Sexta, daughter of Mitto.
EDCS-14400608.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription for Atecurus, listing his age and filiation, and also mentioning Sexta, daughter of Mitto, likely another occupant of the tomb.

EDCS-14400610.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely identifying a freedman associated with someone named Asclepias.

EDCS-14400611.text_edition: Marco Ulpio Catullino qui vixit nnos XLV Marcus Ulpius Severianus et Ulpia Severiana filii et Iulia Ianuaria uxsor heredes faciendum curaverunt secundum voluntatem testamenti
EDCS-14400611.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Catullinus, who lived 45 years. Marcus Ulpius Severianus and Ulpia Severiana, his children, and Iulia Ianuaria, his wife, as heirs, took care to have this made according to the will's intent.
EDCS-14400611.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Catullinus, erected by his children and wife acting as his heirs, in accordance with his will.

EDCS-14400612.text_edition: Herculi sacrum Gaius Iulius Eptino votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400612.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Gaius Iulius Eptino gladly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-14400612.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules by Gaius Iulius Eptino, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14400622.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Peculiaris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400622.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Julius Peculiaris willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400622.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Julius Peculiaris, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14400625.text_edition: Atemeriae Atecnati filiae uxori annorum XXXX
EDCS-14400625.translation: To Atemeria Atecnati, daughter, wife, aged 40 years.
EDCS-14400625.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Atemeria Atecnati, noting her familial relationships and age.

EDCS-14400627.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Culminali Valerius Ingenuus pro se et suis votum solvit merito libens
EDCS-14400627.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminalis, Valerius Ingenuus, for himself and his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400627.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminalis by Valerius Ingenuus on behalf of himself and his family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14400628.text_edition: Quintus Ennius Liberalis et Ennia Oppidana fecerunt sibi et Kalendinae filiae annorum XVII et Vitulo filio annorum XXX
EDCS-14400628.translation: Quintus Ennius Liberalis and Ennia Oppidana made this for themselves and for their daughter Kalendina, aged 17 years, and for their son Vitulus, aged 30 years.
EDCS-14400628.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Quintus Ennius Liberalis and Ennia Oppidana for themselves and their two children, Kalendina and Vitulus.

EDCS-14400630.text_edition: Caiae Septimiae Cai filiae Iustae annorum LV uxori Spectati Prisciani  Caio Spectatio Cai filio Claudia Prisciano IIviro iure dicundo annorum V Caius Spectatius Finitus IIvir iure dicundo Claudiae Celeiae pater infelicissimus fecit  MATV ON
EDCS-14400630.publication: ILLPRON 01863 = ILLPRON 01864 = ILJug-01, 00371 = ILJug-01, 00372 = EQNoricum-C, 00034 = AE 1961, 00216a = RICeleia 00413 = RICeleia 00414
EDCS-14400630.translation: To Gaia Septimia, daughter of Gaius, Justa, aged 55 years, wife of Spectatius Priscianus. To Gaius Spectatius, son of Gaius, Claudia Priscianus, duumvir with judicial power, aged 5 years. Gaius Spectatius Finitus, duumvir with judicial power, to Claudia Celeia, made this, a most unhappy father. MATV ON.
EDCS-14400630.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Spectatius Finitus for his wife, Gaia Septimia Justa, and his son, Gaius Spectatius Claudia Priscianus, also mentioning a dedication to Claudia Celeia.

EDCS-14400631.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Spectatius Avitus annorum LXXX  Aurelia Severina   Caius Spectatius Secundianus vivus fecit sibi et Tutoriae Avitae coniugi carissimae annorum LV et Caio Spectatio Cervae filio annorum XXVIII et Caio Rusticio Tutori nepoti annorum XII et Rusticium sic Albinum! filium! XXX  Spectatiae Severinae annorum XXV
EDCS-14400631.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Spectatius Avitus, aged 80 years. Aurelia Severina. Gaius Spectatius Secundianus, while living, made this for himself and for his dearest wife Tutoria Avita, aged 55 years, and for his son Gaius Spectatius Cervae, aged 28 years, and for his grandson Gaius Rusticius Tutor, aged 12 years, and Rusticius, likewise Albinus, his son, aged 30. To Spectatia Severina, aged 25 years.
EDCS-14400631.summary: This inscription is a comprehensive funerary monument erected by Gaius Spectatius Secundianus, while still alive, for himself and multiple family members across several generations, including his wife, son, grandson, and Spectatia Severina.

EDCS-14400632.text_edition: Dis Manibus Statutius Secundianus et Cerva coniunx filio Statutio Secundo obito annorum IIII
EDCS-14400632.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Statutius Secundianus and Cerva his wife, for their son Statutius Secundus, who died aged 4 years.
EDCS-14400632.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Statutius Secundianus and his wife Cerva for their young son, Statutius Secundus, who died at the age of four.

EDCS-14400633.text_edition: Caius Vindonius Successus aedilis Claudiae Celeiae fecit sibi et Iuliae Sexti filiae Ingenuae uxori fidelissimae annorum L  Dis Manibus
EDCS-14400633.publication: ILLPRON 01867 = ILLPRON 01872 = ILJug-01, 00375 =  = ILJug-01, 00377 = EQNoricum-C, 00035 = AE 1958, 00249 = AE 1985, 00702 = RICeleia 00415
EDCS-14400633.translation: Gaius Vindonius Successus, aedile, to Claudia Celeia, made this for himself and for Julia, daughter of Sextus, Ingenua, his most faithful wife, aged 50 years. To the Spirits of the Departed.
EDCS-14400633.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Gaius Vindonius Successus, an aedile, for himself and his faithful wife, Julia Ingenua, also including a dedication to Claudia Celeia.

EDCS-14400634.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a name, possibly part of a funerary or dedicatory monument for Rusticius Tertullianus.

EDCS-14400639.text_edition: Publius Aelius Iunius vivus fecit sibi et Nemetae Nigellionis filiae coniugi et Vibio filio annorum XVII
EDCS-14400639.translation: Publius Aelius Junius, while living, made this for himself and for Nemeta, daughter of Nigellio, his wife, and for his son Vibius, aged 17 years.
EDCS-14400639.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Publius Aelius Junius while alive for himself, his wife Nemeta, and his son Vibius.

EDCS-14400640.text_edition: Adsallutae sacrum Lucius Servilius Eutyches cum suis gubernatoribus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400640.translation: Sacred to Adsalluta, Lucius Servilius Eutyches with his helmsmen willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-14400640.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deity Adsalluta by Lucius Servilius Eutyches and his helmsmen, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14400641.text_edition: Savo et Adsallutae Caius Memmius votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-14400641.translation: To Savo and Adsalluta, Gaius Memmius willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-14400641.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deities Savo and Adsalluta by Gaius Memmius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14400642.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II pater patriae consul pro consule et Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus nobilissimus Caesar princebs sic iuventutis providentissimi Augusti fecerunt a Celeia milia passuum III
EDCS-14400642.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, father of the country, consul, proconsul, and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, princeps of the youth, the most provident Augusti, made this three miles from Celeia.
EDCS-14400642.summary: This milestone or public work inscription records its construction by the emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, likely in 217-218 AD, indicating a distance from Celeia.

EDCS-14400643.text_edition: Imperator Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul IIII III
EDCS-14400643.translation: Imperator Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul for the fourth time, III.
EDCS-14400643.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication or milestone recording the titles of Emperor Trajan, likely from around 101 AD or later in his reign.

EDCS-14400645.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVI consul III pater patriae proconsul III
EDCS-14400645.translation: Imperator Caesar, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixteenth time, consul for the third time, father of the country, proconsul for the third time.
EDCS-14400645.summary: This inscription is a dedication or public work recording the full titles of Emperor Hadrian, dating to approximately 132 AD, indicating his sixteenth year of tribunician power.

EDCS-14400646.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pater patriae pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate imperator II consul III III
EDCS-14400646.translation: Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, imperator for the second time, consul for the third time, III.
EDCS-14400646.summary: This inscription records the titles of Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely dating to early in his reign (140-144 AD), as indicated by his third consulship.

EDCS-14400652.text_edition: Inisus  fecit sibi et  et Ursi
EDCS-14400652.translation: Inisus made this for himself and [---] and Ursi.
EDCS-14400652.summary: This fragmentary tombstone or funerary monument records Inisus making it for himself and others, including Ursi.

EDCS-14400653.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae pro salute Marci Aureli Feliciani et Marci Aureli Felicissimi filii eius et Aureli Feliciani iunioris Philumenus amicus eorundem ex voto posuit
EDCS-14400653.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, for the well-being of Marcus Aurelius Felicianus and his son Marcus Aurelius Felicissimus and Aurelius Felicianus junior, their friend Philumenus placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-14400653.summary: This inscription is a dedicatory altar to Mithras, set up by Philumenus for the health and well-being of Marcus Aurelius Felicianus and his family, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14400654.text_edition: Bardo Primi filius vivus fecit sibi et
EDCS-14400654.translation: Bardo, son of Primus, made this while living for himself and [---].
EDCS-14400654.summary: This funerary monument records Bardo, son of Primus, preparing his own tomb during his lifetime for himself and other unnamed individuals.

EDCS-14400659.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to the god Mars Augustus, made by Quintus Art[---].

EDCS-14400660.publication: ILLPRON 01910 = AIJ 00008 = RIS 00379 = EQNoricum-C, 00036 = AGN-3, 00008a = RICeleia 00380
EDCS-14400660.translation: Claudia Celeia's heirs made this while she was alive.
EDCS-14400660.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that the heirs of Claudia Celeia prepared her monument during her lifetime, suggesting it's a pre-prepared tombstone.

EDCS-14400662.text_edition: Cominius Tedsicnati filius vivus fecit sibi et suis Successae Adiutoris filiae coniugi annorum L
EDCS-14400662.translation: Cominius, son of Tedsicnatus, made this while alive for himself and his family, and for Successa, daughter of Adiutor, his wife, 50 years old.
EDCS-14400662.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Cominius, son of Tedsicnatus, dedicates a monument for himself and his family, specifically mentioning his 50-year-old wife Successa, daughter of Adiutor.

EDCS-14400664.text_edition: Egetumario Marci filio annorum LXXII hic situs est
EDCS-14400664.translation: Egetumario, son of Marcus, 72 years old, lies here.
EDCS-14400664.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Egetumario, son of Marcus, who lived to be 72 years old.

EDCS-14400665.text_edition: Tatulo Tricci filius vivus fecit sibi et A Macerinae coniugi et Titus A Celemes nnorum XXI
EDCS-14400665.translation: Tatulo, son of Triccus, made this while alive for himself and for A. Macerina, his wife, and Titus A. Celemes, 21 years old.
EDCS-14400665.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Tatulo, son of Triccus, who dedicates a monument for himself, his wife A. Macerina, and Titus A. Celemes, aged 21.

EDCS-14400666.text_edition: Avitianus us Avitiani nus Secundini a annorum S S  niani annorum  VAV
EDCS-14400666.translation: Avitianus, [son/slave] of Avitianus, [son/slave] of Secundinus, aged [---], [---] nianus, aged [---].
EDCS-14400666.summary: This appears to be a fragmented funerary inscription for multiple individuals, including an Avitianus and a Nianus, with their ages partially preserved.

EDCS-14400667.translation: We, Serenus and Marullus, placed this for Hercules.
EDCS-14400667.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Hercules, placed by Serenus and Marullus.

EDCS-14400668.text_edition: Maximus Finiti annorum LX Latinus LX
EDCS-14400668.translation: Maximus, son of Finitus, 60 years old. Latinus, 60 years old.
EDCS-14400668.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Maximus, son of Finitus, and Latinus, both recorded as being 60 years old.

EDCS-14400669.text_edition: D(is) Manibus Acceptus Senilis vivus fecit sibi Secundinaastiva Restitut?
EDCS-14400669.translation: To the Divine Manes. Acceptus Senilis made this while alive for himself and Secundinaastiva Restitut[a]?
EDCS-14400669.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Manes, indicating Acceptus Senilis prepared a tomb for himself and possibly for a woman named Secundinaastiva Restituta while still alive.

EDCS-14400670.text_edition: vixit XXX Secund vivus fecit titulum Ursinae  Ingenuus  ITVS XXX  SV XVI LIV LIV
EDCS-14400670.translation: He/she lived 30 years. Secundus/a made this titulus while alive for Ursina Ingenua. [---] ITVS 30 [---] SV 16 LIV LIV.
EDCS-14400670.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Ursina Ingenua, who lived 30 years, placed by Secundus/a during their lifetime, with additional incomplete information about other individuals or dates.

EDCS-14400673.text_edition: Herculi titulum posuit Celerinus libens visu monitus
EDCS-14400673.translation: Celerinus willingly placed this titulus for Hercules, having been warned by a vision.
EDCS-14400673.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Celerinus to Hercules, placed willingly after a divine warning or vision.

EDCS-14400675.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication, likely to Lucius Venonius Rufus, possibly for a monument or offering.

EDCS-14400676.text_edition: Iano Gemino Augusto sacrum Restitutus Secundi cultor eius donum dedit
EDCS-14400676.translation: Sacred to Janus Geminus Augustus, Restitutus, his worshiper, son of Secundus, gave this gift.
EDCS-14400676.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Janus Geminus Augustus by Restitutus, a worshiper and son of Secundus, who gave a gift.

EDCS-14400678.text_edition: Dis Manibus Proculeiae Boniatae annorum LX ENII
EDCS-14400678.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Proculeia Bonita, aged 60 years.
EDCS-14400678.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Proculeia Bonita, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-14400679.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Cerialis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14400679.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Julius Cerialis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400679.summary: This inscription records Gaius Julius Cerialis fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-14400680.text_edition: Dis Manibus Celsilla Maximi annorum LXXV Variniana annorum LXX
EDCS-14400680.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Celsilla, daughter of Maximus, aged 75 years, and Variniana, aged 70 years.
EDCS-14400680.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Celsilla, daughter of Maximus, and Variniana, who lived to be 75 and 70 years old, respectively.

EDCS-14500001.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aquilini Caesaris nostri annorum XXXXVI Iulia Stratonice coniugi pientissimo
EDCS-14500001.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Aquilinus, our Caesar, aged 46 years. Julia Stratonice to her most devoted husband.
EDCS-14500001.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julia Stratonice to her most devoted husband, Aquilinus, referred to as 'our Caesar', who lived for 46 years.

EDCS-14500002.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Venustae nnrum XXII dedicatus Utilio
EDCS-14500002.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Julia Venusta, aged 22 years, dedicated by Utilius.
EDCS-14500002.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Utilius to Julia Venusta, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-14500003.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Mutilius Chrestus vivus fecit sibi et Florentiniae Secundinae coniugi rarissimae nnorum XXV et Mutiliae Fortunatae matri nnorum LXX et Mutiliae Crispinae nepti nnorum IIII
EDCS-14500003.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Mutilius Chrestus, while living, made this for himself and for Florentinia Secundina, his most rare wife, aged 25 years, and for Mutilia Fortunata, his mother, aged 70 years, and for Mutilia Crispina, his granddaughter, aged 4 years.
EDCS-14500003.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Quintus Mutilius Chrestus, while still alive, prepared a monument for himself, his wife Florentinia Secundina, his mother Mutilia Fortunata, and his granddaughter Mutilia Crispina.

EDCS-14500004.text_edition: Secundinus Secundi et Bruttia Kalandina sic coniux et sic sibi vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500004.translation: Secundinus, son of Secundus, and Bruttia Kalandina, thus husband and thus wife, while living, made this for themselves.
EDCS-14500004.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Secundinus, son of Secundus, and Bruttia Kalandina, a married couple, prepared a monument for themselves while still alive.

EDCS-14500006.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Hermes vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Eutychiae coniugi karissimae!
EDCS-14500006.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Julius Hermes, while living, made this for himself and for Julia Eutychia, his dearest wife!
EDCS-14500006.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Julius Hermes, while still alive, prepared a monument for himself and his dearest wife, Julia Eutychia.

EDCS-14500007.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Gaius Camerius Iuvenalis et Lucania Decorata aedem resstitueruntt
EDCS-14500007.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Gaius Camerius Iuvenalis and Lucania Decorata restored the temple.
EDCS-14500007.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple dedicated to Hercules Augustus by Gaius Camerius Iuvenalis and Lucania Decorata.

EDCS-14500008.text_edition: Acuto IIII aedilicia potestate IIII iure dicundo prae=NIfecto#praefecto#PRNIF iure dicundo Quintus C Lupus fratri pientissimo
EDCS-14500008.translation: To Acutus, four times holding aedilician power, four times holding the right to pronounce judgment, prefect for pronouncing judgment. Quintus C. Lupus [set this up] for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-14500008.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Quintus C. Lupus for his brother Acutus, who held multiple civic offices including aedilician power and the right to pronounce judgment.

EDCS-14500009.text_edition: Dis Manibus Amando Titi Iuli Saturnini servo |contrascriptori Maturus et Mercator vilici bene merenti
EDCS-14500009.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Amandus, slave of Titus Iulius Saturninus, a counter-scribe. Maturus and Mercator, estate managers, [set this up] for the well-deserving.
EDCS-14500009.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the estate managers Maturus and Mercator to Amandus, a slave and counter-scribe of Titus Iulius Saturninus.

EDCS-14500010.text_edition: Genio Augusti sacrum Gaius! Iulius Verecundus et Gaius! Iulius Mercator et Gaia! Iulia Iuliana et Gaius! Iulius Secundus
EDCS-14500010.translation: Sacred to the Genius of Augustus. Gaius Iulius Verecundus and Gaius Iulius Mercator and Gaia Iulia Iuliana and Gaius Iulius Secundus [set this up].
EDCS-14500010.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of Augustus by Gaius Iulius Verecundus, Gaius Iulius Mercator, Gaia Iulia Iuliana, and Gaius Iulius Secundus.

EDCS-14500011.text_edition: Caius Antistius Celer votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500011.translation: Gaius Antistius Celer willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500011.summary: This inscription records Gaius Antistius Celer fulfilling a vow he had made.

EDCS-14500013.text_edition: Caius Antestius Ambudsuilus aedilis Teurniae S  vivus fecit sibi et suis et Atestati Bricconis filio patri Devvae Atiougonis filiae matri Caius Antestius Cai filius Lutumarus Marcus Antestius Cai filius Vogitoutus Antestia Cai filia Banona
EDCS-14500013.translation: Gaius Antestius Ambudsuilus, aedile of Teurnia, made this while living for himself and his family, and for Atestatus, son of Bricco, his father; Devva, daughter of Atiougo, his mother; Gaius Antestius, son of Gaius, Lutumarus; Marcus Antestius, son of Gaius, Vogitoutus; Antestia, daughter of Gaius, Banona.
EDCS-14500013.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Gaius Antestius Ambudsuilus, an aedile of Teurnia, for himself, his parents, and his children while he was still alive.

EDCS-14500014.text_edition: Nonia Enobugi uxor defuncta annorum  Enobux Gnauo et Adnamius P
EDCS-14500014.translation: Nonia, wife of Enobugus, deceased, of [---] years. Enobux, son of Gnauo, and Adnamius P. [set this up].
EDCS-14500014.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Nonia, wife of Enobugus, set up by Enobux and Adnamius P.

EDCS-14500015.text_edition: Herculi Invicto sacrum Gaius Donnicius Rufinus et Valeria Attica cum suis templum vetustate conlabsum sic restituerunt ex voto
EDCS-14500015.translation: Sacred to Hercules Invictus. Gaius Donnicius Rufinus and Valeria Attica with their household restored this temple, collapsed by age, as a result of a vow.
EDCS-14500015.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an ancient temple dedicated to Hercules Invictus by Gaius Donnicius Rufinus and Valeria Attica and their household, as fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14500016.text_edition: Caio Rhesio Dextro quaestori Redsomarus Brito et Litugena patri viv- fecerunt
EDCS-14500016.translation: To Gaius Rhesius Dexter, quaestor. Redsomarus Brito and Litugena made this for their father while living.
EDCS-14500016.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Redsomarus Brito and Litugena for their father, Gaius Rhesius Dexter, a quaestor, while he was still alive.

EDCS-14500017.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Quordnius followed by a sequence of letters that are likely abbreviations or a personal name in a local language.

EDCS-14500018.text_edition: Sabiniae Severinae libertae Iucundae annorum XL Caius Sabinius Plocamus coniugi pientissimae et sibi et Graecinae et Aprili et Rufo filiis vivus fecit
EDCS-14500018.translation: To Sabinia Severina, freedwoman of Jucunda, aged 40. Gaius Sabinius Plocamus made this while living for his most devoted wife, and for himself, and for his children Graecina, Aprilis, and Rufus.
EDCS-14500018.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a husband's dedication of a tomb for his freedwoman wife, himself, and their children, made during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500019.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Lampridius Faustinus Iuniae Veronillae coniugi de se optime meritae et Lampridiae Faustinae filiae annorum XXII faciendum curavit in hoc heredem monumentum sequetur
EDCS-14500019.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Lampridius Faustinus had this made for Iunia Veronilla, his wife, who deserved the best from him, and for Lampridia Faustina, his daughter, aged 22. In this monument, his heir will follow.
EDCS-14500019.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to a deceased wife and daughter, with a provision for the dedicator's heir to be interred there later.

EDCS-14500020.text_edition: Ientumaro Adnami et Secundinae coniugi et Vitali genero Ientumarus vivus fecit et Restitutae filiae
EDCS-14500020.translation: To Ientumarus, son of Adnamus, and to his wife Secundina, and to his son-in-law Vitalis, Ientumarus made this while living, and for his daughter Restituta.
EDCS-14500020.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Ientumarus's dedication of a tomb for himself and his family members (father, wife, son-in-law, and daughter) during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500021.text_edition: Ategnatae Amuronis filiae optimae coniugi Ateboduus Vercombogi filius vivus fecit et sibi et suis
EDCS-14500021.translation: To Ategnata, daughter of Amuro, his excellent wife, Ateboduus, son of Vercombus, made this while living, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-14500021.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a husband's dedication of a tomb for his deceased wife, himself, and his family, made during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500022.text_edition: Cupitianus Cupitinaes sic filius Cupitinae et Asellioni parentibus optimis et sibi vivus fecit et Saturnino avo militi defunctis
EDCS-14500022.translation: Cupitianus, son of Cupitina, made this while living for Cupitina and Asellio, his excellent parents, and for himself, and for his grandfather Saturninus, a deceased soldier.
EDCS-14500022.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Cupitianus's dedication of a tomb for his deceased parents, his deceased soldier grandfather, and himself, made during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500024.text_edition: Constanti Vottici Provincialis nnum XVIII Votticius Cupitus et Votticia Ategenta parentes vivi fecerunt et sibi
EDCS-14500024.translation: For Constantis, son of Votticus Provincialis, aged 18 years, his parents Votticius Cupitus and Votticia Ategenta made this while living, and for themselves.
EDCS-14500024.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication of a tomb by parents for their deceased son and for themselves during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14500025.text_edition: Cauti Lucius Albius Atticus et Caius Albius Avitus
EDCS-14500025.translation: To Cautus, Lucius Albius Atticus and Gaius Albius Avitus.
EDCS-14500025.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cautus by Lucius Albius Atticus and Gaius Albius Avitus, likely indicating a familial or patronal relationship.

EDCS-14500027.text_edition: Syrascus Valerianus Eutyches Albii Maximi  Nemesi Augustae
EDCS-14500027.translation: Syrascus Valerianus Eutyches, freedman of Albius Maximus, [dedicates this] to Nemesis Augusta.
EDCS-14500027.summary: This is a votive inscription where a freedman, Syrascus Valerianus Eutyches, dedicates something to the goddess Nemesis Augusta, indicating a religious devotion.

EDCS-14500028.text_edition: Sanctius Herennius Epictetus INEO gratias agit
EDCS-14500028.translation: Sanctius Herennius Epictetus gives thanks to INEO.
EDCS-14500028.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Sanctius Herennius Epictetus's expression of gratitude to a deity or entity known as INEO, whose identity is not fully explicit here.

EDCS-14500029.translation: He willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Lutianus.
EDCS-14500029.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to an individual or deity named Lutianus, indicating a religious or personal obligation.

EDCS-14500030.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Afinio Gallo Veldumniano Volusiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto ordo Teurniensium devotus numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-14500030.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Veldumnianus Volusianus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated Augustus, the council of Teurnia, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-14500030.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the council of Teurnia to Emperor Volusianus, likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500032.text_edition: Atitoni Attalonis et Lediae Casilli filiae Atismerius
EDCS-14500032.translation: Atismerius [made this] for Atito, son of Attalo, and Ledia Casillius, daughter of Atismere.
EDCS-14500032.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Atismerius for Atito, son of Attalo, and Ledia Casillius, likely a memorial to his family members.

EDCS-14500033.text_edition: Ingenuo Optati et Exepiae parentibus Optatus et sibi et Celerinae coniugi vivus fecit
EDCS-14500033.translation: Optatus made this while living for Ingenuus Optatus and Exepia, his parents, and for himself and Celerina, his wife.
EDCS-14500033.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Optatus during his lifetime for his parents, himself, and his wife Celerina.

EDCS-14500034.text_edition: Lucio Iunio Luci filio Vegeto annorum  Tettia  Secunda  coniunx
EDCS-14500034.translation: To Lucius Iunius Vegetus, son of Lucius, [who lived] for [an unknown number of] years, Tettia Secunda, his wife [made this].
EDCS-14500034.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tettia Secunda to her husband, Lucius Iunius Vegetus.

EDCS-14500035.text_edition: Caius Lollius Trophimus et Lollia Probata vivi fecerunt sibi et Florentinae Bruti pientissimae obitae annorum XXVIII
EDCS-14500035.translation: Gaius Lollius Trophimus and Lollia Probata, while living, made this for themselves and for Florentina Bruti, a most devoted woman who died at 28 years of age.
EDCS-14500035.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument created by Gaius Lollius Trophimus and Lollia Probata for themselves and their very devoted relative, Florentina Bruti.

EDCS-14500037.text_edition: Dis Manibus Onotnius ac Rufinae karissimae coniugi @(obitae) nnorum XXXV sibi suis vivus fecit
EDCS-14500037.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Onotnius made this while living for himself and his family, and for Rufina, his dearest wife, who died at 35 years of age.
EDCS-14500037.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Onotnius during his lifetime for himself, his family, and his beloved wife Rufina.

EDCS-14500041.text_edition: Invicto Deo Mithrae pro salute Marci P Potentis Ursulus libertus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500041.translation: To the Undefeated God Mithras, for the well-being of Marcus P. Potens, Ursulus, his freedman, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500041.summary: This inscription is a dedicatory offering made by Ursulus, a freedman, to the god Mithras for the health of his patron, Marcus P. Potens.

EDCS-14500042.text_edition: Tinco Redsati filius et BanoAna#Banona#BANANA Venimari filia uxor vivi fecerunt Ambidrabo sic filio equiti auxsiliario sic annorum XX hic situs est
EDCS-14500042.translation: Tinco, son of Redsatus, and Banona, daughter of Venimarus, his wife, made this while living for Ambidrabus, their son, an auxiliary cavalryman, who lived for 20 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-14500042.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Tinco and Banona during their lifetime for their son, Ambidrabus, an auxiliary cavalryman who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-14500044.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caiantiae Sumariae coniugi optimae obitae nnorum LXXX Caiantius Primitivus vivus fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500044.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caiantius Primitivus made this while living for Caiantia Sumaria, his excellent wife who died at 80 years of age, and for himself.
EDCS-14500044.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Caiantius Primitivus during his lifetime for his excellent wife Caiantia Sumaria and for himself.

EDCS-14500045.text_edition: Seccioni Tottiionis filio et Acceptae Montani filiae uxsori sic fili-- viv-- fec---
EDCS-14500045.translation: To Seccio, son of Tottio, and to Accepta, daughter of Montanus, his wife, [their] children made this while living.
EDCS-14500045.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by the children of Seccio, son of Tottio, and Accepta, daughter of Montanus, for their parents during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500046.text_edition: AVIVL EA MS et Venae filiae
EDCS-14500046.translation: To AVIUL EA MS and to his daughter Vena.
EDCS-14500046.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a tombstone or monument, dedicated to AVIUL EA MS and his daughter Vena, by an unstated dedicant.

EDCS-14500047.text_edition: Noviae Spuri filiae Maximae et Noviae Spuri filiae Proculae Lucius Regontius Natalis et Lucius Regontius Luci filius Iustus vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500047.translation: Lucius Regontius Natalis and Lucius Regontius, son of Lucius, Iustus, while alive, made this for Novia Maxima, daughter of Spurius, and for Novia Procula, daughter of Spurius.
EDCS-14500047.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Regontius Natalis and Lucius Regontius Iustus for Novia Maxima and Novia Procula, indicating a family burial plot prepared during the dedicants' lifetimes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500049.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Vibius Fortunatus cum suis urbanis iussit libens merito
EDCS-14500049.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Depulsor (Averter of Evil), Vibius Fortunatus with his household members, willingly and deservedly, ordered this.
EDCS-14500049.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Depulsor by Vibius Fortunatus and his household, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14500050.text_edition: Saturninus Severi vivus fecit sibi et Kaniae Ursulae coniugi carissimae Accepto filio annorum VIIII Masculo filio annorum XIII
EDCS-14500050.translation: Saturninus, son of Severus, while alive, made this for himself and for Kania Ursula, his dearest wife, and for their son Acceptus, nine years old, and for their son Masculinus, thirteen years old.
EDCS-14500050.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Saturninus Severi for himself, his beloved wife Kania Ursula, and their two young sons, Acceptus and Masculinus, indicating a family tomb prepared during the father's lifetime, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500051.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibianus vivus fecit sibi et L Quientillae @(obitae) annorum XXXV
EDCS-14500051.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Vibianus, while alive, made this for himself and for Lucius Quientilla, deceased at 35 years of age.
EDCS-14500051.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, made by Vibianus for himself and for Lucius Quientilla, who died at 35 years old, indicating a shared burial plot prepared by Vibianus during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500052.text_edition: Vegetoni et Itucae Civilis servis Longinus filius fecit
EDCS-14500052.translation: To Vegetonus and Ituca, slaves of Civilis, Longinus their son made this.
EDCS-14500052.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two slaves, Vegetonus and Ituca, dedicated by their son Longinus, which offers a glimpse into the family structures of enslaved individuals in the Roman world.

EDCS-14500053.text_edition: Calendinus Ategnati filius et Secunda Senicionis filia coius sic carissuma sic filii fecerum sic
EDCS-14500053.translation: Calendinus, son of Ategnatus, and Secunda, daughter of Senicio, his dearest wife, and their children, made this.
EDCS-14500053.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Calendinus and Secunda for themselves and their children, indicating a family tomb, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500054.text_edition: Phileto Motri filius et Severa Calvi filia vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500054.translation: Philetus, son of Motrus, and Severa, daughter of Calvus, while alive, made this.
EDCS-14500054.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Philetus and Severa for themselves during their lifetimes, indicating a shared tomb, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500055.text_edition: Iunonibus  vetustate conlaps restituit Aus Castruci  libens votum solvit
EDCS-14500055.translation: To the Junones, collapsed due to old age, Aus. Castruci restored this, willingly fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-14500055.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording the restoration of a shrine or monument to the Junones by Aus. Castruci, who willingly fulfilled a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500056.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sillvia Vindilla et Silvio VindillIo#Vindillo#VINDILIO et Secundae parentibus viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500056.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Sillvia Vindilla, while alive, made this for her parents, Silvius Vindilius and Secunda, and for herself.
EDCS-14500056.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, made by Sillvia Vindilla for her parents, Silvius Vindilius and Secunda, and for herself, indicating a family tomb prepared during her lifetime, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500057.text_edition: erno Secundi filio Secundinus filius vivus fecit et Boniatae uxori
EDCS-14500057.translation: Secundinus, son of Secundus, made this while alive for his son ---ernus, son of Secundus, and for his wife Boniata.
EDCS-14500057.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Secundinus while still alive for his son and wife, likely a family tomb.

EDCS-14500058.text_edition: Publius Petronius A Sincoria  vivi fecerunt sibi et
EDCS-14500058.translation: Publius Petronius A. Sincoria made this while alive for themselves and...
EDCS-14500058.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Publius Petronius and A. Sincoria for themselves, likely a family monument, with the remainder of the text missing.

EDCS-14500059.text_edition: Pessae filiae S cius et mmi filia uxor ae filiae nnorum
EDCS-14500059.translation: For his daughter Pessa, S. Cius and his daughter Mmi, his wife, to his daughter, aged [---] years.
EDCS-14500059.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription for a daughter named Pessa, likely erected by her father S. Cius and possibly other family members, with details on her age partially lost.

EDCS-14500060.text_edition: Titus Valentinius Titi libertus Firmus et Valentinia Prisci liberta Sucela vivi fecerunt sibi et Firmino Prisci servo filio annorum XVIII
EDCS-14500060.translation: Titus Valentinius Firmus, freedman of Titus, and Valentinia Sucela, freedwoman of Priscus, made this while alive for themselves and for Firminus, slave of Priscus, their son, aged 18 years.
EDCS-14500060.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by two freedpeople, Titus Valentinius Firmus and Valentinia Sucela, for themselves and their 18-year-old son, Firminus, who was still a slave.

EDCS-14500061.text_edition: Deo omnipotenti Mithrae Invicto RSVus beneficiarius
EDCS-14500061.translation: To the omnipotent, unconquered god Mithras. R.S.V.us, a beneficiarius.
EDCS-14500061.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by a beneficiarius named R.S.V.us, likely associated with a Mithraic sanctuary.

EDCS-14500063.text_edition: Belesti Augustae Titus Tapponius Macrinus et Iulia Sexti filia Cara cum suis votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14500063.translation: To Belestis Augusta, Titus Tapponius Macrinus and Iulia Cara, daughter of Sextus, with their family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14500063.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Belestis Augusta by Titus Tapponius Macrinus and Iulia Cara and their family, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14500064.text_edition: Belino Augusto sacrum Caius Marius Severus donum dedit
EDCS-14500064.translation: Sacred to Belinus Augustus, Gaius Marius Severus gave this gift.
EDCS-14500064.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Belinus Augustus, with Gaius Marius Severus as the donor.

EDCS-14500065.text_edition: Dis deabusque omnibus sacrum Marcus Ulpius Servatus et Peccia Primitiva ex visu pro et suis omnibus  posuerunt
EDCS-14500065.translation: Sacred to all gods and goddesses. Marcus Ulpius Servatus and Peccia Primitiva, in accordance with a vision, placed this for themselves and all their kin.
EDCS-14500065.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to all gods and goddesses by Marcus Ulpius Servatus and Peccia Primitiva, who placed it for themselves and their family after receiving a vision.

EDCS-14500066.text_edition: Eponae Augustae sacrum Gaius Licinius Civilis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500066.translation: Sacred to Epona Augusta, Gaius Licinius Civilis, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500066.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Epona Augusta by Gaius Licinius Civilis, a consular beneficiarius, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14500073.text_edition: Genio Augusti sacrum iuventutis Manliensium gentiles qui consistunt in Manlia in hoc donum dedit Campanius Acutus  HS nummum C
EDCS-14500073.translation: Sacred to the Genius of Augustus. The gentile youth of the Manlienses, who reside in Manlia, gave this gift. Campanius Acutus gave 100 sesterces.
EDCS-14500073.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Genius of Augustus, given by the youth of Manlia, with Campanius Acutus specifically donating 100 sesterces to the monument.

EDCS-14500075.text_edition: Genio Noricorum Mascillius Mascillinus et Secundinius Vibianus votum solverunt libentes laeti merito
EDCS-14500075.translation: To the Genius of the Norici, Mascillius Mascillinus and Secundinius Vibianus willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14500075.summary: This inscription records a dedication made to the Genius of the Norici by two individuals, Mascillius Mascillinus and Secundinius Vibianus, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14500076.text_edition: Genio Mercuri Auggustorum liberti tabularii Aurelius Fortunatus libertus
EDCS-14500076.translation: To the Genius of Mercury, Aurelius Fortunatus, freedman of the Augusti and a freedman clerk, made this dedication.
EDCS-14500076.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of Mercury by Aurelius Fortunatus, who served as a freedman clerk to the emperors.

EDCS-14500085.text_edition: Iovi Depulsori et Nymphis Quintus Luccius Maximus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500085.translation: To Jupiter Depulsor and the Nymphs, Quintus Luccius Maximus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500085.summary: This inscription commemorates Quintus Luccius Maximus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Depulsor and the Nymphs.

EDCS-14500086.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Caius Masculinius Masculus |(centurio) legionis I adiutricis frumentarius
EDCS-14500086.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Caius Masculinius Masculus, centurion of the First Adjutrix Legion, a frumentarius (grain supply officer).
EDCS-14500086.summary: This is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Masculinius Masculus, a centurion and frumentarius of Legio I Adiutrix.

EDCS-14500087.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Trebius Marci filius Palatina Alfius equo publico praefectus iure dicundo Aquileiae conductor ferrariarum Noricarum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500087.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Trebius Alfius, son of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, of equestrian rank, prefect for the administration of justice at Aquileia, and contractor of the Norican ironworks, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500087.summary: This inscription records Marcus Trebius Alfius, an equestrian official and contractor, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500089.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus et Rutenia Severina pro se et suis votum dederunt libentes merito
