EDCS-14500089.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Averter of Evil, Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus and Rutenia Severina willingly and deservedly dedicated this vow for themselves and their family.
EDCS-14500089.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus and Rutenia Severina dedicating a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Depulsor for the well-being of themselves and their household.

EDCS-14500090.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Severinius Silvinus et Aurelius Boniatus milites legionis II Italicae Severianae votum solverunt libentes laeti merito
EDCS-14500090.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Dolichenus, Severinius Silvinus and Aurelius Boniatus, soldiers of the Second Italic Severian Legion, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14500090.summary: This inscription records two soldiers, Severinius Silvinus and Aurelius Boniatus of Legio II Italica Severiana, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.

EDCS-14500094.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae pro salute Antoni Severini Helvius Acceptinus ex voto posuit
EDCS-14500094.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, for the health of Antonius Severinus, Helvius Acceptinus placed this from a vow.
EDCS-14500094.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mithras by Helvius Acceptinus for the health of Antonius Severinus, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14500095.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae templum vetustate conlabsum sic quot sic fuit per annos amplius L desertum Aurelius Hermodorus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Norici mediterranei a novo restitui fecit quot sic aedificatum est divo Maximiano VIII et Maximino iterum Auggustis consulibus Quartinio Ursiniano curante
EDCS-14500095.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, the temple, having collapsed from old age and having been deserted for more than fifty years as it was, Aurelius Hermodorus, a man of perfectissimus rank, governor of the province of Noricum Mediterraneum, ordered it to be restored anew as it was built; in the eighth consulship of the deified Maximian and the second consulship of Maximian Augusti, with Quartinius Ursinianus supervising.
EDCS-14500095.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an ancient Mithraic temple by Governor Aurelius Hermodorus, dating to 304 AD, under the consulships of Maximian and Maximinus, and supervised by Quartinius Ursinianus.

EDCS-14500096.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Diadumenus Nicolai Augusti dispensatoris arcarius regni Norici donum dedit
EDCS-14500096.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Diadumenus, treasurer of the Norican kingdom for the imperial dispenser Nicolaus, gave this gift.
EDCS-14500096.summary: This inscription records a gift to Mithras by Diadumenus, who served as a treasurer in Noricum for an imperial dispenser named Nicolaus.

EDCS-14500098.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae in honorem domus divinae Eppius Ariminensis filius
EDCS-14500098.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, in honor of the divine house, Eppius Ariminensis, son, [made this offering].
EDCS-14500098.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mithras, made by Eppius Ariminensis, likely on behalf of the imperial household.

EDCS-14500099.text_edition: Pro salute Augusti in honorem domus divinae Soli Invicto Mythrae sic Hilarus Augusti libertus tabularius procuratoris regni Norici et Epictetus arkarius sic Augusti nostri templum vetustate conlapsum sumptu suo cum pictura refecerunt Imperatore domino nostro Gordiano Augusto et Aviola consulibus sacerdote Licinio Marcello patre sac(rorum)  dedicatum VIII Kalendas Iulias Quinto Vi
EDCS-14500099.translation: For the health of Augustus, in honor of the divine house, to the Unconquered Sun Mithras: Hilarus, freedman of Augustus, tabularius of the procurator of the Norican kingdom, and Epictetus, arcaricus of our Augustus, restored this temple, which had collapsed from old age, at their own expense, including the painting, in the consulship of our lord Emperor Gordian Augustus and Aviola. Dedicated on the 8th Kalends of July by Quintus Vi[---], with Licinius Marcellus, father of the sacred rites, as priest.
EDCS-14500099.summary: This inscription commemorates the restoration of a temple to Sol Invictus Mithras by two imperial freedmen, Hilarus and Epictetus, in 240 AD, during the consulship of Gordian and Aviola.

EDCS-14500100.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto Iuventinus leo votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-14500100.translation: To the Unconquered Sun god, Iuventinus, a 'lion' (rank in Mithraism), willingly, gladly, deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500100.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Sol Invictus by Iuventinus, indicating his rank as a 'lion' within the Mithraic cult.

EDCS-14500101.text_edition: Invicto patrio Ulbius Gaianus praefectus vehiculorum
EDCS-14500101.translation: To the Unconquered ancestral god, Ulbius Gaianus, prefect of vehicles, [made this offering].
EDCS-14500101.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an 'Unconquered ancestral god' by Ulbius Gaianus, an official in charge of transportation.

EDCS-14500102.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Ulpius Valerius speculator legionis primae Noricorum votum a patre suscepto solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-14500102.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, Ulpius Valerius, a scout of the First Norican Legion, willingly, gladly, deservedly fulfilled a vow undertaken by his father.
EDCS-14500102.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Ulpius Valerius, a scout from the Legio I Noricorum, to Mithras on behalf of a vow his father had made.

EDCS-14500103.text_edition: Deo Invicto MiEthras#Mithras#METRAS Ursinus donum posuiEt#posuit#POSUET
EDCS-14500103.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, Ursinus placed this gift.
EDCS-14500103.summary: This inscription records a simple dedication or gift to Mithras by Ursinus.

EDCS-14500104.text_edition: Nemnic Lucius Barbius Luci libertus Pilocles sic donum dedit
EDCS-14500104.translation: Nemnic. Lucius Barbius Pilocles, freedman of Lucius, gave this gift.
EDCS-14500104.summary: This inscription records a gift made by Lucius Barbius Pilocles, a freedman, perhaps dedicated to a local deity or person named Nemnic.

EDCS-14500105.text_edition: Noreiae Augustae sacrum Quintus Fabius Modestus domo Roma decurio alae I Augustae Thracum phialam argenteam pondo II |(quadrantem) emblemata Noreiae aurea uncias duas donum dedit
EDCS-14500105.translation: Sacred to Noreia Augusta, Quintus Fabius Modestus, from Rome, decurion of the First Augustan Thracian cavalry wing, gave a silver bowl weighing two and one-quarter pounds, with golden Noreian emblems weighing two ounces, as a gift.
EDCS-14500105.summary: This inscription records a valuable votive offering of a silver bowl with golden emblems to the goddess Noreia Augusta by Quintus Fabius Modestus, a Roman decurion of a cavalry unit.

EDCS-14500107.text_edition: Noreiae  Chrysanthus Cypaeri Tiberi Claudi Caesaris Augusti servi vicarius votum solvit
EDCS-14500107.translation: To Noreia, Chrysanthus, vicarius of Cypaerus, slave of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500107.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the goddess Noreia by Chrysanthus, a slave who held an administrative position as a 'vicarius' to another slave of the emperor.

EDCS-14500108.text_edition: Isidi Noreiae votum solvit libens merito pro salute Quinti Septuei Clementis conductoris ferrariarum Noricarum Pannoniarum Dalmatarum et Tiberi Claudi Heraclae et Cnaei Octavi Secundi procuratorum ferrariarum Quintus Septueius Valens procurator ferrariarum
EDCS-14500108.translation: To Isis Noreia, Quintus Septueius Valens, procurator of the iron mines, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the health of Quintus Septueius Clemens, contractor of the Norican, Pannonian, and Dalmatian iron mines, and of Tiberius Claudius Heraclas and Gnaeus Octavius Secundus, procurators of the iron mines.
EDCS-14500108.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Isis Noreia by Quintus Septueius Valens, a procurator of iron mines, for the well-being of a contractor and other procurators in the extensive mining regions of Noricum, Pannonia, and Dalmatia.

EDCS-14500109.text_edition: Noreiae Isidi E Aulus Trebonius  procurator Augusti provinciae Norici
EDCS-14500109.translation: To Noreia and Isis, Aulus Trebonius, procurator Augusti of the province Noricum, [dedicates this].
EDCS-14500109.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aulus Trebonius, a procurator of the imperial province Noricum, to the goddesses Noreia and Isis.

EDCS-14500110.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae sacrum Marcus Bellicius Saturninus tribunus cohortis I Flaviae Brittonum et Finitia Verbicia cum Bellicia Saturnina et Bellicio Finitiano filiis votum susceptum solverunt laeti libentes merito Paterno et Archesilao consulibus
EDCS-14500110.translation: To Victoria Augusta, sacred. Marcus Bellicius Saturninus, tribune of the First Flavian Cohort of Britons, and Finitia Verbicia, with their children Bellicia Saturnina and Bellicius Finitianus, happily and willingly fulfilled their vow, as deserved, during the consulship of Paternus and Archesilaus.
EDCS-14500110.summary: This is a votive inscription to Victoria Augusta, made by Marcus Bellicius Saturninus, a military tribune, and his family, fulfilling a vow during the consulship of Paternus and Archesilaus.

EDCS-14500112.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae sacrum Flavius Tacitus summus curator alae Augustae - Aelius Martius summus curator cohortis I Aeliae Brittonum pro se suisque omnibus votum solverunt laeti libentes merito Kalendis Februariis Pio et Pontiano consulibus
EDCS-14500112.translation: To Victoria Augusta, sacred. Flavius Tacitus, chief curator of the Augustan cavalry unit, and Aelius Martius, chief curator of the First Aelian Cohort of Britons, happily and willingly fulfilled their vow for themselves and all their kin, as deserved, on the Kalends of February, during the consulship of Pius and Pontianus.
EDCS-14500112.summary: This is a votive inscription to Victoria Augusta, made by two chief curators of military units, Flavius Tacitus and Aelius Martius, on behalf of themselves and their families, dated to the Kalends of February during the consulship of Pius and Pontianus.

EDCS-14500113.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae pro salute Luci Lydaci Honorati Lucius Lydacius Ingenuus IIvir iure dicundo et Rufia Severa parentes votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14500113.translation: To Victoria Augusta, for the health of Lucius Lydacius Honoratus. His parents, Lucius Lydacius Ingenuus, duumvir with judicial power, and Rufia Severa, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14500113.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Victoria Augusta for the health of Lucius Lydacius Honoratus, made by his parents, Lucius Lydacius Ingenuus, a duumvir, and Rufia Severa.

EDCS-14500114.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae Lucius Lydacius Ingenuus IIvir iure dicundo adlectus sacerdos et flamen
EDCS-14500114.translation: To Victoria Augusta. Lucius Lydacius Ingenuus, duumvir with judicial power, chosen as priest and flamen.
EDCS-14500114.summary: This inscription to Victoria Augusta records the civic and religious offices held by Lucius Lydacius Ingenuus, a duumvir, priest, and flamen.

EDCS-14500115.text_edition: Aulus Poblicius Decimi libertus Antiochus Tiberius Barbius Quinti Publi libertus Tiber  Marcus Gallicinus Vindili filius Lucius Barbius Luci libertus Philotaerus procurator Craxsantus sic Barbi Publi servus
EDCS-14500115.translation: Aulus Poblicius Antiochus, freedman of Decimus. Tiberius Barbius [---], freedman of Quintus Publius. Marcus Gallicinus, son of Vindilus. Lucius Barbius Philotaerus, freedman of Lucius. Craxsantus, procurator, thus Barbius, slave of Publius.
EDCS-14500115.summary: This inscription lists a series of names, primarily freedmen and their patrons, along with a procurator and a slave, likely recording a group involved in some shared activity or association.

EDCS-14500117.text_edition: DOMINI SAR PCHIS CENCURA FRanCII C SVRVSA lius Secundinus s Secundus  Rusticus  Victor tus  A S D
EDCS-14500117.translation: Of the lord SAR PCHIS CENCURA FRANCII C SURVSAlius Secundinus, Secundus, Rusticus, Victor, [---]tus. A S D.
EDCS-14500117.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to list several names, possibly individuals under a 'lord' or associated with a specific group or location, with many parts illegible.

EDCS-14500118.text_edition: beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Augusti nostri ut vovit dedicavit Pio et Pontiano consulibus VIIII Kalendas Iulias
EDCS-14500118.translation: A beneficiarius consularis of the Legio II Italica Pia Fidelis Augusti Nostri, dedicated this as he vowed, during the consulship of Pius and Pontianus, on the ninth day before the Kalends of July.
EDCS-14500118.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a beneficiarius consularis of the Legio II Italica in fulfillment of a vow, dated to the ninth day before the Kalends of July during the consulship of Pius and Pontianus.

EDCS-14500119.translation: They built a temple in honor of Abascantus / Pascanius. Aquilinus [---].
EDCS-14500119.summary: This inscription states that a temple was built in honor of a deity or person named Abascantus or Pascanius, with Aquilinus possibly being involved in its construction.

EDCS-14500121.text_edition: Aiecii Secundina pro se et suis votum solvit libens laeta merito
EDCS-14500121.translation: Aiecia Secundina happily and willingly fulfilled her vow for herself and her kin, as deserved.
EDCS-14500121.summary: This is a votive inscription made by Aiecia Secundina, fulfilling a vow on behalf of herself and her family.

EDCS-14500122.text_edition: Privatus Cai A servus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500122.translation: Privatus, slave of Gaius A., willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500122.summary: This inscription records a vow made and fulfilled by Privatus, a slave of Gaius A., likely to a deity, during the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500123.text_edition: Neroni Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico Re
EDCS-14500123.translation: To Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Re[...]
EDCS-14500123.summary: This is a fragment of a monumental dedication to the Emperor Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, likely from the mid-1st century CE.

EDCS-14500124.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Tutoris libertus Calventius et Iulia Tiberi liberta Novella uxor fecerunt sibi et Tiberio Iulio Ingenuo filio militi cohortis XII urbanae
EDCS-14500124.translation: Tiberius Julius Calventius, freedman of Tutor, and Julia Novella, freedwoman of Tiberius, his wife, made this for themselves and for Tiberius Julius Ingenuus, their son, a soldier of the 12th urban cohort.
EDCS-14500124.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a freedman and freedwoman couple for themselves and their son, a soldier in the urban cohorts, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500126.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Aurelio Auggustorum liberto Burrano procuratori XX hereditatium praefecto  Orte us
EDCS-14500126.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Lucius Aurelius Burranus, freedman of the Augusti, procurator of the 20th inheritance tax, prefect of Orte[...]
EDCS-14500126.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a high-ranking imperial freedman, Lucius Aurelius Burranus, who held several important administrative positions, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500127.text_edition: Dis Manibus Eutycheti Augusti nostri dispensatori procuratoris regni Norici Claudia Domna coniugi et Faustina et Romulus filii et vikarii sic eius bene merenti fecerunt
EDCS-14500127.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Eutyches, dispenser of our Augustus, procurator of the kingdom of Noricum. Claudia Domna, his wife, and Faustina and Romulus, his children and his assistants, made this for him, well-deserving.
EDCS-14500127.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Claudia Domna and her children to her husband, Eutyches, an imperial administrator in Noricum, and his assistants, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500129.text_edition: Titus Accius Marcus et Saturnina Saturnini filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Accio Maximo filio pientissimo militi legionis II Italicae frumentario annorum XXII
EDCS-14500129.translation: Titus Accius Marcus and Saturnina, daughter of Saturninus, while living, made this for themselves and for Accius Maximus, their most dutiful son, a soldier of the II Italian Legion, a grain distributor, aged 22 years.
EDCS-14500129.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Titus Accius Marcus and Saturnina while still living, for themselves and for their deceased son, Accius Maximus, a soldier and grain distributor, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-14500130.text_edition: Tito Aelio Suttiho veterano et Flaviae Acconi Fulvia Mat coniugi karissimo et matri pientissimae et sibi fecit
EDCS-14500130.translation: To Titus Aelius Suttius, veteran, and to Flavia Acconi Fulvia Mat[...], his dearest wife and most dutiful mother, and for herself, she made this.
EDCS-14500130.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavia Acconi Fulvia Mat[...] to her veteran husband, Titus Aelius Suttius, and to herself, also mentioning her role as a devoted mother, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500131.text_edition: Aggaeo hexarcho alae celerum viro sagittandi peritissimo vi militum interempto Monna marito amantissimo
EDCS-14500131.translation: To Aggaeus, hexarch of the cavalry wing of the Swift, a man most skilled in archery, killed by the force of soldiers. Monna made this for her most loving husband.
EDCS-14500131.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Monna to her husband, Aggaeus, a skilled archer and hexarch of a cavalry unit, who was killed in battle, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500132.text_edition: Quintus Aurelius Macer veteranus ex beneficiario vivus fecit sibi et Ulpiae Romuli filiae Oecciae uxori annorum XXXII
EDCS-14500132.translation: Quintus Aurelius Macer, a veteran who was formerly a beneficiarius, made this while living for himself and for Ulpia Oeccia, daughter of Romulus, his wife, aged 32 years.
EDCS-14500132.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Quintus Aurelius Macer, a veteran, for himself and his wife, Ulpia Oeccia, while he was still alive, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500133.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Potitus veteranus alae Augustae vexellarius sic et Surilla Triti filia coniugi karissimae et sibi vivus fecit
EDCS-14500133.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Potitus, veteran vexillarius of the Augustan cavalry wing, made this while living for Surilla, daughter of Tritus, his dearest wife, and for himself.
EDCS-14500133.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Potitus, a veteran standard-bearer, for his beloved wife, Surilla, and for himself, while still alive, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500134.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Secundiano duplicario legionis II Italicae militi annorum XXX im sic bello desideratus sic Aurelius Senilis patrono fecit
EDCS-14500134.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aurelius Secundianus, duplicarius of the Second Italic Legion, a soldier of 30 years, who was lost in war. Aurelius Senilis made this for his patron.
EDCS-14500134.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Senilis to his patron Aurelius Secundianus, a soldier of the Legio II Italica who died in war, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500135.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Secundinus miles legionis II Italicae strator consularis vivus fecit sibi et Arriae Valentinae coniugi karissimae et Valeriae Tutae conobrinae liberi scriben(dum) curaverunt
EDCS-14500135.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius Secundinus, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion, a consular groom, made this for himself while living, and for Arria Valentina, his dearest wife, and for Valeria Tuta, his cousin. His children took care of the inscription.
EDCS-14500135.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Marcus Aurelius Secundinus, a soldier and consular groom of the Legio II Italica, for himself, his wife, and his cousin, with his children overseeing its creation, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500136.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Tertius et Aurelius Tertianus milites legionis II Italicae @(biti) annorum XL
EDCS-14500136.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Tertius and Aurelius Tertianus, soldiers of the Second Italic Legion, who died at 40 years of age.
EDCS-14500136.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two soldiers, Aurelius Tertius and Aurelius Tertianus, both from the Legio II Italica, who died at the age of 40, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500137.text_edition: Dis Manibus Baebius Secundus et Accepta Cupiti vivi fecerunt sibi et Cassius Ingenuus gener sibi fecit et Baebiae Secundae coniugi karissimae mensium VIII obitae nnorum XVI  et Baebio Accepto militi legionis II Italicae piae fidelis @(obito) annorum XXXIII
EDCS-14500137.translation: To the Divine Manes. Baebius Secundus and Accepta Cupiti made this for themselves while living, and Cassius Ingenuus, their son-in-law, made this for himself, and for Baebia Secunda, his dearest wife, who died at 16 years and 8 months of age, and for Baebius Acceptus, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion, pious and loyal, who died at 33 years of age.
EDCS-14500137.summary: This is a family funerary inscription, set up by Baebius Secundus and Accepta Cupiti for themselves and their son-in-law Cassius Ingenuus, also commemorating their daughter Baebia Secunda and their son Baebius Acceptus, a soldier of the Legio II Italica, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500138.text_edition: Caio Cantonio Ucco aedili et Iuliae Bardi filiae Eliomarae uxori et Caio Cantonio Cai filio Condiano et Tiberio Cantonio Cai filio Montano militi cohortis I  stipendiorum V et Lucio Cantonio Mu militi cohortis XI praetoriae et Iuliae C vivi fecerunt sibi et suis
EDCS-14500138.translation: While living, they made this for Gaius Cantonius Uccus, aedile, and Iulia Bardi filia Eliomara, his wife, and Gaius Cantonius Condianus, son of Gaius, and Tiberius Cantonius Montanus, son of Gaius, a soldier of the First Cohort with 5 years of service, and Lucius Cantonius Mu, a soldier of the Eleventh Praetorian Cohort, and Iulia C., for themselves and their family.
EDCS-14500138.summary: This is a family funerary monument set up while living, honoring Gaius Cantonius Uccus (an aedile), his wife Iulia Eliomara, and their sons Gaius Cantonius Condianus, Tiberius Cantonius Montanus (a soldier of Cohors I), and Lucius Cantonius Mu (a soldier of Cohors XI Praetoria), and Iulia C., likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500139.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Ingenuo militi cohortis I Asturum Bellicius Statutus decurio alae I Thracum
EDCS-14500139.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Ingenuus, a soldier of the First Cohort of Astures. Bellicius Statutus, decurion of the First Thracian Ala.
EDCS-14500139.summary: This is an inscription dedicated to Tiberius Claudius Ingenuus, a soldier of Cohors I Asturum, by Bellicius Statutus, a decurion of Ala I Thracum, suggesting a military context and potential camaraderie, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500140.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Tertius et Pomponia Procula Tiberio Claudio Cupito filio militi legionis XXI vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500140.translation: Tiberius Claudius Tertius and Pomponia Procula made this for their son Tiberius Claudius Cupitus, a soldier of the Twenty-First Legion, while living.
EDCS-14500140.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Tiberius Claudius Tertius and Pomponia Procula for their son, Tiberius Claudius Cupitus, a soldier of the Legio XXI, during their lifetimes, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500141.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Egronio S et Atticiae Att Egronius Valentinus  et sibi et Lucre coniugi karissimae et Egronio Marci filio  fratri militi legionis II Italicae
EDCS-14500141.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Egronius S. and Atticia Att. Egronius Valentinus, and for himself, and for Lucre, his dearest wife, and for Egronius, son of Marcus, his brother, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion.
EDCS-14500141.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, honoring Marcus Egronius S., Atticia Att., Egronius Valentinus (the dedicator), his wife Lucre, and his brother Egronius, a soldier of the Legio II Italica, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500142.text_edition: Iulio Aprili beneficiario cohortis I Asturum stipendiorum XXII Cauru Blendonis filia con viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500142.translation: To Iulius Aprilis, beneficiarius of the First Cohort of Astures, with 22 years of service. Cauru, daughter of Blendo, his living wife, made this for him and herself.
EDCS-14500142.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Cauru, daughter of Blendo, for her husband Iulius Aprilis, a beneficiarius of Cohors I Asturum with 22 years of service, and for herself, likely dating to the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500143.text_edition: Caio Iulio Cai filio Velina Basso et Lucio Iulio Cai filio Velina Praesenti speculatoribus Caesaris Augusti Caius Iulius Bassus pater vivus fecit Turpilia Luci filia Iunia Bassi
EDCS-14500143.translation: Gaius Iulius Bassus, father, made this while living for Gaius Iulius Bassus, son of Gaius, of the Velina tribe, and Lucius Iulius Praesens, son of Gaius, of the Velina tribe, both Imperial spies of Caesar Augustus. Turpilia, daughter of Lucius, [wife] of Iunius Bassus.
EDCS-14500143.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Gaius Iulius Bassus for his two sons, Gaius Iulius Bassus and Lucius Iulius Praesens, both 'speculatores Caesaris Augusti', with an additional mention of Turpilia, likely dating to the early Imperial period (1st century AD).

EDCS-14500144.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Adgelei filius Buccio miles cohortis Montanorum primae stipendiorum XL hic situs est a et ciata libertae fecerunt
EDCS-14500144.translation: Tiberius Iulius Buccio, son of Adgeleus, a soldier of the first Cohort of Montani, of 40 years' service, lies here. A. and C. Iata, his freedwomen, made this.
EDCS-14500144.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Iulius Buccio, a soldier, erected by his freedwomen.

EDCS-14500145.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Condolli filius Capatius miles cohortis Montanorum primae stipendiorum XXX hic situs est Tiberius Iulius Crigalo Tiberius Iulius Buccio heredes fecerunt
EDCS-14500145.translation: Tiberius Iulius Capatius, son of Condollus, a soldier of the first Cohort of Montani, of 30 years' service, lies here. Tiberius Iulius Crigalo and Tiberius Iulius Buccio, his heirs, made this.
EDCS-14500145.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Iulius Capatius, a soldier, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-14500146.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Adsedi filius Taulus miles cohortis Montanorum primae stipendiorum XXXVI hic situs est heres fecit
EDCS-14500146.translation: Tiberius Iulius Taulus, son of Adsedus, a soldier of the first Cohort of Montani, of 36 years' service, lies here. His heir made this.
EDCS-14500146.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Iulius Taulus, a soldier, erected by his heir.

EDCS-14500147.text_edition: Lucio Peregrini et Aurelio Insequenti militibus legionis XXII Tertia Castricius et Sossius fecerunt
EDCS-14500147.translation: For Lucius Peregrinus and Aurelius Insequens, soldiers of the XXII Legion, Castricius and Sossius made this.
EDCS-14500147.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription for two soldiers, Lucius Peregrinus and Aurelius Insequens, set up by Castricius and Sossius.

EDCS-14500148.text_edition: Marius Ructicni filius miles cohortis Montanorum primae stipendiorum XXV hic situs est Montanus heres posuit
EDCS-14500148.translation: Marius, son of Ructicnus, a soldier of the first Cohort of Montani, of 25 years' service, lies here. Montanus, his heir, placed this.
EDCS-14500148.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marius, a soldier, erected by his heir Montanus.

EDCS-14500149.text_edition: in canapa sic legionis interfecto a barbaris Marcia Salbia viva fecit coniugi karissimo et sibi et Aurelia Ursa Aelio Leonati coniugi karissimo @(obito) annorum LXX interfecto a barbaris viva fecit et sipi sic
EDCS-14500149.translation: Marcia Salbia, while living, made this for her dearest husband, killed by barbarians in Canapa, in this place of the legion, and for herself. And Aurelia Ursa, while living, made this for her dearest husband Aelius Leonatus, who died at 70 years of age, killed by barbarians, and for herself, in this way.
EDCS-14500149.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two men, a husband of Marcia Salbia and Aelius Leonatus, both killed by barbarians, erected by their wives while still living, also for themselves.

EDCS-14500150.text_edition: Genio Auggustorum nnostrorum Ulpius Moderatus decurio cohortis I Thracum
EDCS-14500150.translation: To the Genius of our Augusti, Ulpius Moderatus, decurion of the first Thracian cohort.
EDCS-14500150.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Genius of the emperors, made by Ulpius Moderatus, a decurion of a Thracian cohort.

EDCS-14500151.text_edition: Sabinia Helenae sic viva fecit sibi et Aellio Tertio beneficiario coniugi optimo et Casto filio nnorum VIIII
EDCS-14500151.translation: Sabinia Helena, while living, made this for herself and for Aelius Tertius, her excellent husband and beneficiarius, and for their son Castus, nine years old.
EDCS-14500151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Tertius, a beneficiarius, and his son Castus, and also for Sabinia Helena, erected by Sabinia Helena herself while still living.

EDCS-14500152.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sestia Redita uxor viva fecit sibi et Tadio Victori veterano legionis II Italicae marito optimo Tadiae Victoris filiae annorum X
EDCS-14500152.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sestia Redita, while living, made this for herself and for Tadius Victor, an excellent husband and veteran of the II Italian Legion, and for their daughter Tadia Victora, ten years old.
EDCS-14500152.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tadius Victor, a veteran, and his daughter Tadia Victora, and also for Sestia Redita, erected by Sestia Redita herself while still living.

EDCS-14500153.text_edition: Sextus Adnamatonis filius et Bonis Anbusuli filius uxori Sextus Antonius Verus evocatus Romae Sporae Sexti filiae heredes vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500153.translation: Sextus, son of Adnamato, and Bonis, son of Anbusulus, while living, made this for his wife, and Sextus Antonius Verus, an evocatus in Rome, for his daughter Spora, daughter of Sextus, their heirs.
EDCS-14500153.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a wife and for Spora, daughter of Sextus, set up by Sextus, Bonis, and Sextus Antonius Verus, who are listed as heirs and dedicated this while still living.

EDCS-14500154.text_edition: Memoriae Valeri Claudi Quinti primo pilo legionis II Italicae duci legionis III Italicae duci et praeposito legionis III Augustae viro innocentissimo Iulius Eutychianus et Iulia Auxanon alumni
EDCS-14500154.translation: To the memory of Valerius Claudius Quintus, chief centurion of the Second Italic Legion, commander of the Third Italic Legion, and prefect of the Third Augustan Legion, a most innocent man. Iulius Eutychianus and Iulia Auxanon, foster-children, [set this up].
EDCS-14500154.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by foster-children to Valerius Claudius Quintus, commemorating his distinguished military career and his virtuous character.

EDCS-14500155.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius Priscianus miles legionis II Italicae piae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Valeriae Surillae coniugi pientissimae
EDCS-14500155.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius Priscianus, soldier of the Second Italic Legion, pious and faithful, made this while living for himself and for Valeria Surilla, his most devoted wife.
EDCS-14500155.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Valerius Priscianus, a soldier, for himself and his beloved wife during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500156.text_edition: Dis Manibus Veponius Avitus vivus fecit sibi et Diacoxiae Meitimae coniugi karissimae et Veponio Quartino militi legionis II Italicae piae fidelis @(obito) nnorum XXXV stipendiorum IIII bello Ducco desiderato Ieilira
EDCS-14500156.translation: To the Divine Manes. Veponius Avitus made this while living for himself and for Diacoxia Meitima, his dearest wife, and for Veponius Quartinus, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion, pious and faithful, of 35 years and 4 years of service, lost in the Ducco War. Ieilira [set this up].
EDCS-14500156.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Veponius Avitus for himself, his wife, and for Veponius Quartinus, a soldier who died in the Ducco War, likely set up by Ieilira.

EDCS-14500157.text_edition: Caius Vettius Quinti filius Pollia eques legionis VIII Augustae nnorum XLIIX stipendiorum XXVIII idem quaestor veteranorum et Quintus Vettius Quinti filius Pollia frater eques legionis VIII Augustae nnorum XL stipendiorum XX hic situs est Marcus Metilius eques legionis VIII Augustae et Publius Arrius heredes Cai Vetti testamento posuerunt
EDCS-14500157.translation: Caius Vettius, son of Quintus, of the Pollia tribe, equestrian of the Eighth Augustan Legion, 48 years old, 28 years of service, also quaestor of the veterans; and Quintus Vettius, son of Quintus, of the Pollia tribe, his brother, equestrian of the Eighth Augustan Legion, 40 years old, 20 years of service, is buried here. Marcus Metilius, equestrian of the Eighth Augustan Legion, and Publius Arrius, heirs of Caius Vettius, placed this according to his will.
EDCS-14500157.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating two brothers, Caius Vettius and Quintus Vettius, both equestrians of the Eighth Augustan Legion, set up by Caius Vettius' heirs according to his will.

EDCS-14500158.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Viatorio IIviro iure dicundo Cl(audii) Vir(uni) --- praefectus cohortis I tribunus legionis V Maced(onicae) --- patri carissimo  Viatoriae Taurinae  et heredes
EDCS-14500158.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Caius Viatorius, duumvir with judicial power, of Claudius Virunus, --- prefect of the First Cohort, tribune of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, --- to a dearest father. Viatoriae Taurina and the heirs [set this up].
EDCS-14500158.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caius Viatorius, a distinguished official and military officer, set up by Viatoriae Taurina and his heirs for a beloved father.

EDCS-14500159.text_edition: Tito Ulpio Insequenti veterano ex beneficiario consularis Titus Silvanius Adiutor heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500159.translation: To Titus Ulpius Insequens, veteran, formerly a beneficiary of the consular. Titus Silvanius Adiutor, his heir, took care to have this made.
EDCS-14500159.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Ulpius Insequens, a veteran, commissioned by his heir, Titus Silvanius Adiutor.

EDCS-14500160.text_edition: nius Proculus |(centurio) legionis II Italicae fratri
EDCS-14500160.translation: ---nius Proculus, centurion of the Second Italic Legion, to his brother.
EDCS-14500160.summary: This is a dedication made by a centurion named Proculus from the Second Italic Legion to his brother, likely a funerary inscription.

EDCS-14500161.text_edition: legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Antoninianae ulia ula
EDCS-14500161.translation: --- of the Second Italic Legion, pious, faithful, Antoninian. Iulia Ula ---.
EDCS-14500161.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the Second Italic Legion, which held the titles 'Pious, Faithful, Antoninian,' and the name Iulia Ula, likely part of a dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-14500163.text_edition: Caio Bottio Mercatori aedilicio Bottia Saxsami sic filia Suaducia uxsor sic et sibi viva fecit et Bottiae Nemetae f(iliae)
EDCS-14500163.translation: To Caius Bottius Mercator, an aedilician. Bottia Saxsami, his daughter, and Suaducia, his wife, made this while living for him and for themselves, and for Bottia Nemeta, their daughter.
EDCS-14500163.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Bottia Saxsami and Suaducia for Caius Bottius Mercator, an aedilician, and also for themselves and their daughter Bottia Nemeta, prepared during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500165.text_edition: Titus Tapponius Titi filius Secundus IIvir iure dicundo vivus fecit sibi et
EDCS-14500165.translation: Titus Tapponius, son of Titus, Secundus, duumvir with judicial power, made this while living for himself and ---.
EDCS-14500165.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Titus Tapponius Secundus, a duumvir, for himself and likely other family members, while he was still alive.

EDCS-14500166.text_edition: Caio Tertinio Statuto aedilicio Catronia Severa marito optimo viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500166.translation: To Gaius Tertinius Statutus, a former aedile, Catronia Severa made this for her excellent husband while alive, and for herself.
EDCS-14500166.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Catronia Severa to her deceased husband, Gaius Tertinius Statutus, a former aedile, and also prepared for herself, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500167.text_edition: Lucius Tuccius Luci filius Pollia Campanus haruspex et Sollonia Publi filia Sabina ob meritum rei publicae Virunensium dederunt
EDCS-14500167.publication: CIL 03, 04868 = ILLPRON 00799 = EQNoricum-V, 00018 = Harusp 00093 = RLK 00045 = AEA 2010, +00014
EDCS-14500167.translation: Lucius Tuccius Campanus, son of Lucius, of the Pollian tribe, an haruspex, and Sollonia Sabina, daughter of Publius, gave this on account of the merit of the state of Virunum.
EDCS-14500167.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Tuccius Campanus and Sollonia Sabina to the public good of the community of Virunum, likely a civic benefaction dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500169.text_edition: Caius Publicius Viruniensium sic libertus Asiaticus fecit sibi et Lupulae coniugi karissimae
EDCS-14500169.translation: Gaius Publicius Asiaticus, freedman of the Virunensians, made this for himself and for his dearest wife Lupula.
EDCS-14500169.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Gaius Publicius Asiaticus, a freedman of the Virunensians, establishing a tomb for himself and his wife Lupula, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500170.text_edition: Caio Acisonio Accepto Copponia Potentina sibi et coniugi et filio annorum VIII
EDCS-14500170.translation: To Gaius Acisonius Acceptus, Copponia Potentina [made this] for herself and her husband and their son, aged 8 years.
EDCS-14500170.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Copponia Potentina for herself, her husband Gaius Acisonius Acceptus, and their 8-year-old son, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500171.text_edition: Adiuto publico servo Paulina coniunx viva fecit
EDCS-14500171.translation: To Adiutus, a public slave, his wife Paulina made this while alive.
EDCS-14500171.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Paulina to her husband, Adiutus, who was a public slave, made during her lifetime, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500172.text_edition: Publio Aelio Candido et Emeritae filiae annorum II Cocceia Modestina et filii fecerunt
EDCS-14500172.translation: To Publius Aelius Candidus and to their daughter Emerita, aged 2 years, Cocceia Modestina and their sons made this.
EDCS-14500172.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Cocceia Modestina and her sons dedicating a monument to Publius Aelius Candidus and their 2-year-old daughter Emerita, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500173.text_edition: Aelio Mattoni Aelia Secundina sibi et coniugi pientissimo viva fecit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14500173.translation: To Aelius Matto, Aelia Secundina made this monument for herself and for her most devoted husband while alive; it shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500173.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aelia Secundina for herself and her devoted husband Aelius Matto, explicitly stating that the monument is not transferable to an heir, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500174.text_edition: Albano Quinti Sabini Verani servo et Silvanae coniugi
EDCS-14500174.translation: To Albanus, slave of Quintus Sabinus Veranus, and to his wife Silvana.
EDCS-14500174.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Albanus, a slave of Quintus Sabinus Veranus, and his wife Silvana, likely made by others and dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500175.text_edition: Lucio Albio Telesphoro marito et Quietae matri et Spectato defuncto annorum XX et Quieto defuncto annorum XVI fratribus karissimis servis Albi Maximi Victorina et Hygia filiae servae Albi Maximi et sibi vivae fecerunt
EDCS-14500175.translation: To Lucius Albius Telesphorus, her husband, and to Quieta, her mother, and to Spectatus, deceased, aged 20, and to Quietus, deceased, aged 16, dearest brothers – slaves of Albius Maximus – Victorina and Hygia, daughters and slaves of Albius Maximus, made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14500175.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Victorina and Hygia, who were slaves and daughters of Albius Maximus, for their deceased husband, mother, and two brothers, all of whom were also slaves of Albius Maximus, and also for themselves while living, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500176.text_edition: Alfiae Publi filiae Sabinae nnorum XVIII Lucius Licinius Luci filius Fabia Syriacus uxori karissimae et Publio Alfio Epitynchano Alfiae Daphnae parentibus piissimis fecit
EDCS-14500176.translation: To Alfia Sabina, daughter of Publius, aged 18 years, Lucius Licinius Syriacus, son of Lucius, of the Fabian tribe, made this for his dearest wife, and for Publius Alfius Epitynchanus and Alfia Daphna, his most devoted parents.
EDCS-14500176.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Licinius Syriacus to his deceased 18-year-old wife Alfia Sabina, and also to his devoted parents, Publius Alfius Epitynchanus and Alfia Daphna, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500178.text_edition: Marcus Annius Rufinus vivus fecit sibi et Ingenuae Titi filiae uxori et Rufinae filiae annorum IV
EDCS-14500178.translation: Marcus Annius Rufinus, while living, made this for himself and for Ingenua, daughter of Titus, his wife, and for Rufina, his daughter, aged 4 years.
EDCS-14500178.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Marcus Annius Rufinus for himself, his wife Ingenua, and his daughter Rufina, likely dating to the early Imperial period given the typical Roman naming conventions.

EDCS-14500179.text_edition: Ariomanus Terti filius et Quarta Masculi filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Vibio filio annorum XXV
EDCS-14500179.translation: Ariomanus, son of Tertius, and Quarta, daughter of Masculus, while living, made this for themselves and for Vibius, their son, aged 25 years.
EDCS-14500179.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tomb built by Ariomanus and Quarta for themselves and their son Vibius, likely dating to the early Imperial period or later based on the use of personal names and filial designations.

EDCS-14500180.text_edition: Quintus Aterius Herma et Ateriae Primigeniae uxori et Ingenuae filiae F F
EDCS-14500180.translation: Quintus Aterius Herma and for Ateria Primigenia, his wife, and for Ingenua, his daughter. They made this.
EDCS-14500180.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a monument made by Quintus Aterius Herma for himself, his wife Ateria Primigenia, and his daughter Ingenua, reflecting common Roman family tomb practices.

EDCS-14500181.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Primula Iulio Caio coniugi karissimo bene merenti viva fecit
EDCS-14500181.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelia Primula, while living, made this for Iulius Caius, her dearest husband, who deserved well.
EDCS-14500181.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Primula to her deceased husband Iulius Caius, indicating a memorial monument erected during her lifetime.

EDCS-14500182.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Sura viva fecit sibi et Aurelio Tricconi coniugi karissimo et Aurelio Urso filio obito nnorum XXXII
EDCS-14500182.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelia Sura, while living, made this for herself and for Aurelius Tricco, her dearest husband, and for Aurelius Ursus, her son, who died aged 32 years.
EDCS-14500182.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a family tomb, set up by Aurelia Sura for herself, her husband Aurelius Tricco, and their deceased son Aurelius Ursus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500183.text_edition: Banona nni Sabini viva fecit sibi et Septumo coniugi
EDCS-14500183.translation: Banona, daughter of Sabinus, while living, made this for herself and for Septumus, her husband.
EDCS-14500183.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Banona, daughter of Sabinus, for herself and her husband Septumus, indicating a pre-mortem dedication.

EDCS-14500184.summary: This appears to be a very brief funerary dedication to Publius Barbius Mogetus, possibly part of a larger monument or a simple marker.

EDCS-14500185.text_edition: Dis Manibus Barbiae Atticae obitae nnorum L Barbia Attia coniux matri karissimae
EDCS-14500185.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Barbia Attica, who died aged 50 years. Barbia Attia, her spouse, made this for her dearest mother.
EDCS-14500185.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Barbia Attia to her deceased mother, Barbia Attica, who died at age 50, indicating a tribute from a child to a parent.

EDCS-14500186.text_edition: Barbiae Publi filiae Verae uxori Publius Titius Publi libertus Adrotus sibi et suis vivus fecit Lucio Titio Publi filio Clementi annorum XII
EDCS-14500186.translation: For Barbia Vera, daughter of Publius, his wife. Publius Titius Adrotus, freedman of Publius, while living, made this for himself and his family, [including] Lucius Titius Clemens, son of Publius, aged 12 years.
EDCS-14500186.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a family tomb built by Publius Titius Adrotus, a freedman, for himself, his wife Barbia Vera, and his son Lucius Titius Clemens, reflecting the social mobility and family structure of freedmen in Roman society.

EDCS-14500187.text_edition: Bassus Congeistli filius vivus fecit sibi et Camuliae Quarti filiae coniugi pientissimae et suis
EDCS-14500187.translation: Bassus, son of Congeistlus, while living, made this for himself and for Camulia, daughter of Quartus, his most devoted wife, and for his family.
EDCS-14500187.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a tomb built by Bassus, son of Congeistlus, for himself, his wife Camulia, and his family, likely dating to a period when individuals prepared their own final resting places.

EDCS-14500188.text_edition: ius Bausonis vivus fecit sibi et Cotoniae niugi optimae et Aino filio annorum II
EDCS-14500188.translation: Ius Bauso made this during his lifetime for himself and for Cotonia, his excellent wife, and for their son Ainus, who lived for two years.
EDCS-14500188.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Ius Bauso for himself, his wife Cotonia, and their young son Ainus, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500189.text_edition: Bauso Cupiti et Rusticae coniugi viva sic fecit sibi et Candidae filiae annorum XXX
EDCS-14500189.translation: Bauso, son of Cupitus, and Rustica, his wife, made this during their lifetime for themselves and for their daughter Candida, who lived for thirty years.
EDCS-14500189.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tomb built by Bauso, son of Cupitus, and his wife Rustica, for themselves and their daughter Candida, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500190.text_edition: Caballo Batavi filio Marcus Ulpius Senecio fratri
EDCS-14500190.translation: Marcus Ulpius Senecio made this for his brother Caballo, son of Batavus.
EDCS-14500190.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcus Ulpius Senecio in memory of his brother Caballo, son of Batavus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500191.text_edition: Lucius Celerius Campester et Celeria Primigenia filio mensium VIII
EDCS-14500191.translation: Lucius Celerius Campester and Celeria Primigenia made this for their son, who lived for eight months.
EDCS-14500191.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating a very young child, likely a gravestone erected by his parents, Lucius Celerius Campester and Celeria Primigenia, probably from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500192.text_edition: Quintus Cervius Faustus et Banona Montani vivi fecerunt sibi et Faustinae filiae nnorum X
EDCS-14500192.translation: Quintus Cervius Faustus and Banona, daughter of Montanus, made this during their lifetime for themselves and for their daughter Faustina, who lived for ten years.
EDCS-14500192.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tomb built by Quintus Cervius Faustus and Banona, daughter of Montanus, for themselves and their daughter Faustina, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500193.text_edition: Camuliano Camuli et Suadrae Senuci heres fecit Facundus libertus
EDCS-14500193.translation: Facundus, a freedman, as heir, made this for Camulianus, son of Camulus, and Suadra, daughter of Senucus.
EDCS-14500193.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by Facundus, a freedman and heir, for Camulianus and Suadra, suggesting a patron-client relationship, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500194.text_edition: Dis Manibus Charitonis Caesaris servi Epagathiani Flavia Faustina uxor et Flavia Nice filia fecerunt
EDCS-14500194.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Faustina, his wife, and Flavia Nice, his daughter, made this for Charito Epagathianus, slave of Caesar.
EDCS-14500194.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes for Charito Epagathianus, an imperial slave, erected by his wife Flavia Faustina and daughter Flavia Nice, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500195.translation: Cinamen, freedman of Primus, made this during his lifetime.
EDCS-14500195.summary: This is an inscription indicating a tomb or monument built by Cinamen, a freedman of Primus, for himself during his lifetime, consistent with Roman funerary practices, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500196.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudiae Firmaes sic Claudia Maximina et Claudia Firmina matri fecerunt
EDCS-14500196.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claudia Maximina and Claudia Firmina made this for their mother, Claudia Firma.
EDCS-14500196.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes of Claudia Firma, erected by her daughters Claudia Maximina and Claudia Firmina, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500197.text_edition: Titus Clufennius Demetrius vivus fecit sibi et Masculiniae Verinae uxori
EDCS-14500197.translation: Titus Clufennius Demetrius made this during his lifetime for himself and for Masculinia Verina, his wife.
EDCS-14500197.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Titus Clufennius Demetrius for himself and his wife Masculinia Verina during their lifetimes, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500198.text_edition: Marco Cocceio Sutuedi filio Quincto et Bacaucuni Togionis filiae vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500198.translation: To Marcus Cocceius Quintus, son of Sutuedus, and to Bacaucunis, daughter of Togio, they made this while living.
EDCS-14500198.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb made by living individuals for themselves and their family members.

EDCS-14500199.text_edition: Marcus Cocceius Verecundus vivus fecit sibi et Flaviae Marciae coniugi pientissimae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14500199.translation: Marcus Cocceius Verecundus made this monument while living for himself and for Flavia Marcia, his most devoted wife. It will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500199.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument by Marcus Cocceius Verecundus for himself and his beloved wife, specifying that it is not intended for inheritance, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500200.text_edition: Coma Lottonis filia Atuiae filiae et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-14500200.translation: Coma, daughter of Lotto, made this while living for her daughter Atuia and for herself.
EDCS-14500200.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tomb made by Coma for herself and her daughter, Atuia, while Coma was still alive.

EDCS-14500201.text_edition: Dis Manibus Conconnus Senogi vivus fecit sibi et Boniatae coniugi karissimae
EDCS-14500201.translation: To the Divine Manes, Conconnus, son of Senogus, made this while living for himself and for Boniata, his dearest wife.
EDCS-14500201.summary: This funerary inscription records Conconnus dedicating a monument to the Divine Manes for himself and his beloved wife Boniata during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500202.text_edition: Dis Manibus Covnerti Ituci AdmaIN Gemellinus sibi faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500202.translation: To the Divine Manes of Covnertus Itucus Admain, Gemellinus took care to have this made for himself.
EDCS-14500202.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Gemellinus arranged for the creation of a monument for Covnertus Itucus Admain, also for himself, under the protection of the Divine Manes.

EDCS-14500203.text_edition: Covnerto Terti filio Secundina Colae filia marito et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-14500203.translation: Secundina, daughter of Cola, made this while living for her husband Covnertus, son of Tertius, and for herself.
EDCS-14500203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a monument made by Secundina for her husband Covnertus and herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-14500204.text_edition: Covnerta Mosgaiti viva fecit sibi et MAT TO SV VON III Massato Babbecio filI(iis#fil(iis#FII
EDCS-14500204.translation: Covnerta, daughter of Mosgaitus, made this while living for herself and for Massato Babbecio, her sons, and [three other unnamed individuals or objects].
EDCS-14500204.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Covnerta for herself and her sons, Massato Babbecio, while she was still alive, with some details about additional recipients or items being unclear due to lacunae.

EDCS-14500206.summary: This very brief inscription is likely a simple name dedication or a fragment of a larger funerary monument, identifying the individual Cupitus Masculus Octavius.

EDCS-14500207.text_edition: Cupitus Atedunae libertus et Litulla Touti filia Genetivo filio nnorum X et sibi vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500207.translation: Cupitus, freedman of Ateduna, and Litulla, daughter of Toutus, made this while living for their son Genetivus, aged 10 years, and for themselves.
EDCS-14500207.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tomb made by Cupitus, a freedman, and Litulla for their son Genetivus, who died at age ten, and for themselves while still living.

EDCS-14500208.text_edition: Cupito Burrani filio et Iuliae Iuili filiae uxsori sic Speratus filius fecit
EDCS-14500208.translation: Speratus, son, made this for Cupitus, son of Burranus, and for Julia, daughter of Iuilus, his wife.
EDCS-14500208.summary: This funerary inscription records Speratus dedicating a monument to his father Cupitus and his mother Julia.

EDCS-14500209.text_edition: Daphino Gai Iuli Vogitouti liberto et Secundae uxori vivae et Asteri et Frontoni vivo filii vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500209.translation: Daphinus, freedman of Gaius Iulius Vogitoutus, and Secunda, his wife, while alive, and Asterius and Fronto, his sons, while alive, made this.
EDCS-14500209.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb made by Daphinus, a freedman, his wife, and their two sons for themselves while still living.

EDCS-14500210.text_edition: Dresiu Luonis filia viva fecit sibi et Tertioni Mannonis filio coniugi et Cerescenti filio
EDCS-14500210.translation: Dresiu, daughter of Luonis, while alive, made this for herself and for Tertio, son of Manno, her husband, and for Cerescens, her son.
EDCS-14500210.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a family, made by Dresiu, daughter of Luonis, for herself, her husband, and their son while she was still alive.

EDCS-14500211.text_edition: Helvisio Saxami filio et Maximae Spirviconis filiae uxori et Restituto filio annorum XVI Tertia et Helvissa filiae fecerunt
EDCS-14500211.translation: To Helvisius, son of Saxamus, and to Maxima, daughter of Spirvico, his wife, and to Restitutus, their son, sixteen years old, Tertia and Helvissa, their daughters, made this.
EDCS-14500211.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Tertia and Helvissa for their parents, Helvisius and Maxima, and their brother, Restitutus, who died at age 16.

EDCS-14500212.text_edition: Non gravis hic texit tumulus te punica virgo Musarum amor et Charitum Erasina voluptas annorum XII
EDCS-14500212.translation: This tomb does not weigh heavily upon you, Punic maiden, Erasina, love of the Muses and delight of the Graces, 12 years old.
EDCS-14500212.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Erasina, describing her as a 'Punic maiden' and praising her connection to the Muses and Graces, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-14500213.text_edition: Felix Cilumbae filius et Titiu Aterae filia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Vercaioni filio
EDCS-14500213.translation: Felix, son of Cilumba, and Titiu, daughter of Atera, his wife, while alive, made this for themselves and for Vercaio, their son.
EDCS-14500213.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Felix and Titiu for themselves and their son Vercaio while they were still living.

EDCS-14500214.text_edition: Flavio Aphobo Flavius Zosimus et Flavia Cupita patrono bene merenti fecerunt et sibi libertis libertabusque hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14500214.translation: Flavius Zosimus and Flavia Cupita made this monument for Flavius Aphobus, their well-deserving patron, and for themselves, their freedmen, and freedwomen. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500214.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Flavius Zosimus and Flavia Cupita to their patron, Flavius Aphobus, also reserving it for themselves and their freedmen and freedwomen, with a clause that it will not pass to an heir, suggesting a mid-1st to 2nd century AD date.

EDCS-14500215.text_edition: Tito Flavio Natali et Domitiae Euctae Caetenia Cleonice amicis optimis fecit
EDCS-14500215.translation: Caetenia Cleonice made this for her dearest friends Titus Flavius Natalis and Domitia Eucta.
EDCS-14500215.summary: This is a memorial inscription dedicated by Caetenia Cleonice to her two best friends, Titus Flavius Natalis and Domitia Eucta.

EDCS-14500216.text_edition: Titus Flavius Priscus et Spuria Flavina vivi fecerunt sibi et Ulpiae Sabinae matri pientissimae
EDCS-14500216.translation: Titus Flavius Priscus and Spuria Flavina, while alive, made this for themselves and for Ulpia Sabina, their most devoted mother.
EDCS-14500216.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb made by Titus Flavius Priscus and Spuria Flavina for themselves and their mother, Ulpia Sabina, while they were still living.

EDCS-14500217.text_edition: Titus Flavius Receptus testamento fieri iussit sibi et Bottiae Bottionis filiae fieri heres fecit
EDCS-14500217.translation: Titus Flavius Receptus ordered this to be made by his will for himself and for Bottia, daughter of Bottio; his heir made it.
EDCS-14500217.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Titus Flavius Receptus, by his will, commissioned a monument for himself and Bottia, daughter of Bottio, which was subsequently executed by his heir.

EDCS-14500218.translation: Publius Fundanius Hospes, freedman of Publius.
EDCS-14500218.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the name and status of Publius Fundanius Hospes, a freedman of a certain Publius.

EDCS-14500219.text_edition: Fuscae Aiacis libertae Restitutus vivus fecit et sibi et Tacito filio nnrum XX et Sextae filiae annorum XXII
EDCS-14500219.translation: Restitutus, during his lifetime, made this for Fusca, freedwoman of Aiax, and for himself, and for his son Tacitus, aged 20 years, and for his daughter Sexta, aged 22 years.
EDCS-14500219.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Restitutus during his lifetime for himself, his children Tacitus and Sexta, and Fusca, the freedwoman of Aiax, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500220.text_edition: Fuscia Citata Exs filia  sibi viva fecit
EDCS-14500220.translation: Fuscia Citata, daughter of Exs, made this for herself during her lifetime.
EDCS-14500220.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Fuscia Citata, daughter of Exs, prepared this monument for herself during her lifetime, typical of Roman funerary practices.

EDCS-14500221.text_edition: Gaia Servanda viva fecit sibi et Valerio Secundino coniugi
EDCS-14500221.translation: Gaia Servanda, during her lifetime, made this for herself and for Valerius Secundinus, her husband.
EDCS-14500221.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Gaia Servanda during her lifetime for herself and her husband Valerius Secundinus, indicating a domestic funerary dedication.

EDCS-14500222.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gambugius, son of Albus, likely a simple epitaph for a deceased individual.

EDCS-14500223.text_edition: Caius Gavillius Peculiaris vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Tertullae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14500223.translation: Gaius Gavillius Peculiaris, during his lifetime, made this monument for himself and for Iulia Tertulla; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500223.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Gaius Gavillius Peculiaris during his lifetime for himself and Iulia Tertulla, with a clause specifying it will not be inherited, common for ensuring familial burial plots.

EDCS-14500225.text_edition: Iucundo Caucavi liberto et Suadruni Vitalis filiae
EDCS-14500225.translation: To Iucundus, freedman of Caucavus, and to Suadruna, daughter of Vitalis.
EDCS-14500225.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iucundus, freedman of Caucavus, and Suadruna, daughter of Vitalis, likely commemorating a freedman and a freeborn woman, possibly a married couple.

EDCS-14500226.text_edition: Caio Iulio Censoni filio et Iuliae Privatae Sabinae libertae filii fecerunt [] MR
EDCS-14500226.translation: For Gaius Iulius Censorinus, the son, and for Iulia Privata, freedwoman of Sabina, the sons made this.
EDCS-14500226.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by sons to their father, Gaius Iulius Censorinus, and Iulia Privata, a freedwoman of Sabina, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-14500227.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Facundus vivus fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-14500227.translation: Tiberius Iulius Facundus, during his lifetime, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-14500227.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Tiberius Iulius Facundus during his lifetime for himself and his family, indicating a pre-arranged burial place for his kin.

EDCS-14500228.text_edition: Lucius Iulius Fambiscianus vivus fecit Iuliae Luci filiae Alpinae Tiberio Iulio Luci filio Passeri Lucio Iulio Luci filio Lucullo
EDCS-14500228.translation: Lucius Iulius Fambiscianus, during his lifetime, made this for Iulia Alpina, daughter of Lucius, Tiberius Iulius Passer, son of Lucius, and Lucius Iulius Lucullus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-14500228.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Lucius Iulius Fambiscianus during his lifetime for Iulia Alpina, Tiberius Iulius Passer, and Lucius Iulius Lucullus, all likely close family members.

EDCS-14500229.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Hermes et Govruna Tertii filia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Adiutori filio et Ingenuae filiae
EDCS-14500229.translation: Tiberius Iulius Hermes and Govruna, daughter of Tertius, his wife, during their lifetimes, made this for themselves and for their son Adiutor and their daughter Ingenua.
EDCS-14500229.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Tiberius Iulius Hermes and his wife Govruna, daughter of Tertius, during their lifetimes for themselves and their children, Adiutor and Ingenua, for a family burial.

EDCS-14500230.text_edition: Tiberio Iulio Blaesati liberto Hospiti et Clodiae Quinti filiae Severae uxori heres vivus fecit
EDCS-14500230.translation: To Tiberius Julius Blaesatius Hospes, freedman, and to Clodia Severa, daughter of Quintus, his wife, the heir made this during his lifetime.
EDCS-14500230.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that an heir, while still alive, dedicated a monument to Tiberius Julius Blaesatius Hospes, a freedman, and his wife, Clodia Severa.

EDCS-14500231.text_edition: Tiberio Iulio Primigenio et suis Cationi coniugi Attiae matri Quartae sorori heres fecit
EDCS-14500231.translation: To Tiberius Julius Primigenius and his own: to Cationa, his spouse; to Attia, his mother; to Quarta, his sister. The heir made this.
EDCS-14500231.summary: This funerary inscription records that an heir dedicated a monument to Tiberius Julius Primigenius and several of his family members: his spouse, mother, and sister.

EDCS-14500232.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tiberio Iulio Senecioni @(obito) et Bell Avetae Tiberius Iulius Asprenas et Tiberius Iulius Iulianus parentibus bene merentibus fecerunt
EDCS-14500232.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Tiberius Julius Senecio, deceased, and to Bell Aveta, Tiberius Julius Asprenas and Tiberius Julius Julianus made this for their well-deserving parents.
EDCS-14500232.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Julius Senecio and Bell Aveta, dedicated by their sons, Tiberius Julius Asprenas and Tiberius Julius Julianus, as a tribute to their well-deserving parents.

EDCS-14500233.text_edition: Titus Iulius Sossius vivus fecit sibi et Severinae uxori pientissimae
EDCS-14500233.translation: Titus Julius Sossius made this during his lifetime for himself and for Severina, his most devoted wife.
EDCS-14500233.summary: This is a funerary inscription that Titus Julius Sossius dedicated for himself and his devoted wife, Severina, while he was still alive.

EDCS-14500234.text_edition: Tiberio Iulio Tiberi liberto Successo et Tertiae coniugi et Invento fratri fideli
EDCS-14500234.translation: To Tiberius Julius Successus, freedman of Tiberius, and to Tertia, his wife, and to Inventus, his loyal brother.
EDCS-14500234.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Tiberius Julius Successus, a freedman, his wife Tertia, and his loyal brother Inventus, likely set up by a family member or the community.

EDCS-14500235.text_edition: Marcus Iulius Tertius et Caius Sempronius Albanus faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-14500235.translation: Marcus Julius Tertius and Gaius Sempronius Albanus took care of having this made.
EDCS-14500235.summary: This inscription records that Marcus Julius Tertius and Gaius Sempronius Albanus were responsible for commissioning the monument, likely a funerary stone given the context of other inscriptions.

EDCS-14500236.text_edition: Iuliae Tiberi filiae Banonae Marcus Ulpius Attio matri optimae
EDCS-14500236.translation: To Julia Banona, daughter of Tiberius. Marcus Ulpius Attio [made this] for his excellent mother.
EDCS-14500236.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Attio to his excellent mother, Julia Banona, daughter of Tiberius.

EDCS-14500237.text_edition: Iusto Ituli Boniata Cintuli marito viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500237.translation: To Iustus, husband of Itula Boniata, daughter of Cintulus. She made this during her lifetime for him and for herself.
EDCS-14500237.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Itula Boniata, daughter of Cintulus, to her husband Iustus, and for herself, while she was still alive.

EDCS-14500238.text_edition: Licinius Tert sibi et Tyronio! Prisco
EDCS-14500238.translation: Licinius Tertius [made this] for himself and for Tyronius Priscus.
EDCS-14500238.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Licinius Tertius for himself and for Tyronius Priscus, likely a relative or close associate.

EDCS-14500239.text_edition: Lucconi Terti filio et Aracuni Attunis filiae eeredes sic fecerunt
EDCS-14500239.translation: To Lucconus, son of Tertius, and to Aracuna, daughter of Attunus. The heirs thus made this.
EDCS-14500239.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucconus, son of Tertius, and Aracuna, daughter of Attunus, which was dedicated by their heirs.

EDCS-14500240.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Masculinius Ianuarius et Caius Sabinius Saturninus faciendum curaverunt et Aureliae Martinae coniugi mihi karissimae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14500240.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Masculinius Ianuarius and Gaius Sabinius Saturninus had this monument made for Aurelia Martina, my dearest wife, and it shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500240.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Gaius Masculinius Ianuarius and Gaius Sabinius Saturninus to Aurelia Martina, specified as the dearest wife of one of the dedicants, with a clause restricting inheritance, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500241.text_edition: Caio Masculinio Olympae et Iuniae coniugi Olympia et Tertulla filiae parentibus optimis fecerunt
EDCS-14500241.translation: To Gaius Masculinius Olympius and Iunia, his wife. Olympia and Tertulla, their daughters, made this for their excellent parents.
EDCS-14500241.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument dedicated by daughters Olympia and Tertulla to their deceased parents, Gaius Masculinius Olympius and Iunia, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500242.translation: To Masculinus, son of Saturninus, aged 16 years.
EDCS-14500242.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription commemorating Masculinus, son of Saturninus, who died at the age of sixteen, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500245.text_edition: Attasaon Maximi servus Tertiae coniugi pientissimae
EDCS-14500245.translation: Attasaon, slave of Maximus, to Tertia, his most devoted wife.
EDCS-14500245.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Attasaon, a slave of Maximus, to his most devoted wife, Tertia, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500246.text_edition: Mestrio Attiano coniugi karissimo et Mestrio Primiano et Mestrio Secundino filiis
EDCS-14500246.translation: To Mestrius Attianus, dearest husband, and to Mestrius Primianus and Mestrius Secundinus, their sons.
EDCS-14500246.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication to Mestrius Attianus, a dearest husband, and his sons, Mestrius Primianus and Mestrius Secundinus, suggesting a family tomb, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500247.text_edition: Montissius Mogianci libertus sibi et Vibiae Bouterionis filiae coniugi et Cituliae filiae nnum XV vivus fecit
EDCS-14500247.translation: Montissius, freedman of Mogiancus, made this while living for himself and for Vibia, daughter of Bouterius, his wife, and for Citulia, his daughter, who lived 15 years.
EDCS-14500247.summary: This inscription records a tomb built by Montissius, a freedman of Mogiancus, for himself and his family—his wife Vibia and his daughter Citulia, who died at age 15—while he was still alive, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500248.text_edition: Nemeto Marcrottae filius et Patiaantae Conbogi filiae sibi et filiae vivus fecit
EDCS-14500248.translation: Nemeto, son of Marcrotta, and Patiaanta, daughter of Conbogus, made this while living for themselves and for their daughter.
EDCS-14500248.summary: This inscription records a monument built by Nemeto, son of Marcrotta, and Patiaanta, daughter of Conbogus, for themselves and their daughter while they were still alive, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500250.text_edition: Cnaeo Octavio Cnaei filio Cantarro Caius Octavius Tattus Lucius Octavius Rufus Octavia Aiu fratris sic et sibi vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500250.translation: To Gnaeus Octavius Cantarrus, son of Gnaeus Octavius. Gaius Octavius Tattus, Lucius Octavius Rufus, and Octavia Aiu, his siblings, made this while living for their brother and for themselves.
EDCS-14500250.summary: This inscription records a monument made by Gaius Octavius Tattus, Lucius Octavius Rufus, and Octavia Aiu for their brother, Gnaeus Octavius Cantarrus, and for themselves while they were still living, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500251.text_edition: Caius Pomponius Felix vivus fecit Belatullae Duni filiae uxori et Pomponio Cai filio Martiali
EDCS-14500251.translation: Gaius Pomponius Felix made this while living for Belatulla, daughter of Dunus, his wife, and for Pomponius Gaius Martialis, his son.
EDCS-14500251.summary: This inscription records a monument built by Gaius Pomponius Felix while he was still alive, for himself, his wife Belatulla, and his son Pomponius Gaius Martialis, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500252.text_edition: Primianus sibi et Valeriae Proculae coniugi karissimae @(obitae) nnorum XXX et Antoniae Ursae coniugibus piis
EDCS-14500252.translation: Primianus [made this] for himself and for Valeria Procula, his dearest wife, who died at 30 years old, and for Antonia Ursa, his devoted wife.
EDCS-14500252.summary: This inscription records a monument set up by Primianus for himself and his two wives, Valeria Procula, who died at age 30, and Antonia Ursa, likely indicating a family tomb dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500253.text_edition: Caius Priscius Cai libertus Surio vivus fecit sibi et Septimae coniugi
EDCS-14500253.translation: Gaius Priscius, freedman of Gaius, made this for himself and his wife Septima, while alive, for Surius.
EDCS-14500253.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Gaius Priscius, a freedman, for himself and his wife Septima, dedicated while they were still living.

EDCS-14500254.text_edition: Tiberio Publicio Atuno et Publiciae Vindae Privatus filius fecit Caio Publicio Privato Aeliae Quintae coniugi LGENIMIL vivus fecit
EDCS-14500254.translation: Privatus, son, made this for Tiberius Publicius Atunus and Publicia Vinda, and for Gaius Publicius Privatus, and his wife Aelia Quinta. LGENIMIL made this while alive.
EDCS-14500254.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Privatus for his parents Tiberius Publicius Atunus and Publicia Vinda, and also for Gaius Publicius Privatus and his wife Aelia Quinta, with an additional mention of LGENIMIL who made it while living.

EDCS-14500255.text_edition: Caius Publicius Optatus vivus fecit sibi et Aiuni
EDCS-14500255.translation: Gaius Publicius Optatus, while alive, made this for himself and Aiunius.
EDCS-14500255.summary: This inscription records a monument made by Gaius Publicius Optatus for himself and Aiunius while he was still alive.

EDCS-14500256.text_edition: Publicius Quartus Publiciae Cupitae coniugi et Maturo filio
EDCS-14500256.translation: Publicius Quartus made this for Publicia Cupita, his wife, and for Maturus, his son.
EDCS-14500256.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument dedicated by Publicius Quartus to his wife Publicia Cupita and his son Maturus.

EDCS-14500257.text_edition: Poblicia Mellita Poblicia Prima Poblicius Quartus de suo fecerunt
EDCS-14500257.translation: Poblicia Mellita, Poblicia Prima, and Poblicius Quartus made this at their own expense.
EDCS-14500257.summary: This inscription records a monument funded by Poblicia Mellita, Poblicia Prima, and Poblicius Quartus.

EDCS-14500258.text_edition: Publilius Sossius et Publilia Silvana vivi fecerunt sibi et Sossiae filiae iuveni nnorum XX
EDCS-14500258.translation: Publilius Sossius and Publilia Silvana, while alive, made this for themselves and for their young daughter Sossia, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-14500258.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Publilius Sossius and Publilia Silvana for themselves, while living, and for their deceased daughter Sossia, who died at age 20.

EDCS-14500259.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Pusinna, a wife, by an individual whose name begins with Tic[---].

EDCS-14500260.text_edition: Quarto et Citatae Privatus filius parentibus fecit et Lupae filiae @(obitae) annorum III
EDCS-14500260.translation: Privatus, son, made this for his parents Quartus and Citata, and for his daughter Lupa, who died at three years old.
EDCS-14500260.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Privatus for his parents, Quartus and Citata, and for his deceased daughter, Lupa, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-14500261.text_edition: Quartus Croutae filius et Crispa Eliomari filia uxor sibi et Restitutae filiae annorum XVIII
EDCS-14500261.translation: Quartus, son of Crouta, and Crispa, daughter of Eliomarus, his wife, made this for themselves and for their daughter Restituta, who lived 18 years.
EDCS-14500261.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Quartus, son of Crouta, and his wife Crispa, daughter of Eliomarus, for themselves and for their deceased daughter Restituta, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-14500262.text_edition: Quarto Iuniani servo Quinta uxor viva fecit
EDCS-14500262.translation: Quinta, his wife, made this while alive for Quartus, a slave of Iunianus.
EDCS-14500262.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Quinta for her husband Quartus, a slave of Iunianus, while she was still living.

EDCS-14500263.text_edition: Quietus Pr Sabinae servus vivus fecit sibi et Crescentinae uxori et Saturnino fratri
EDCS-14500263.translation: Quietus, a slave of Pr. Sabina, made this while living for himself and for Crescentina, his wife, and for Saturninus, his brother.
EDCS-14500263.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Quietus, a slave, making a tomb for himself, his wife, and his brother while still alive, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD based on common Roman funerary practices.

EDCS-14500264.text_edition: Quintiliano et Maguriae et Ianuario et Det Peculiari et Sextillae Crispina domina fecit servis bene merentibus
EDCS-14500264.translation: Crispina, their mistress, made this for Quintilianus and Maguria and Ianuarius and Det Peculiaris and Sextilla, her deserving slaves.
EDCS-14500264.summary: This inscription records a mistress named Crispina dedicating a monument to her five slaves, who she deemed deserving, likely a funerary monument reflecting Roman social hierarchies.

EDCS-14500265.text_edition: Redsato Matugenti filio et Martiali filio Iulia Cai filia Quinctilla uxor et Caius Iulius Bassus filius vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500265.translation: Julia Quinctilla, daughter of Caius, wife, and Caius Julius Bassus, son, made this while living for Redsatus, son of Matugentus, and for Martial, son.
EDCS-14500265.summary: This inscription records a wife and son making a monument while living for Redsatus, son of Matugentus, and for Martial, who is also referred to as a son, suggesting a family burial plot.

EDCS-14500266.text_edition: Restituta Restituti viva fecit sibi et Iulio Finitiano coniugi carissimo
