EDCS-14500266.translation: Restituta, daughter of Restitutus, made this while living for herself and for Julius Finitianus, her dearest husband.
EDCS-14500266.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Restituta, while still alive, dedicates a tomb for herself and her beloved husband, Julius Finitianus.

EDCS-14500267.text_edition: Aelius  Romulus veteranus legionis X geminae piae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et  IV
EDCS-14500267.translation: Aelius Romulus, a veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina Pia Fidelis, made this while living for himself and for IV.
EDCS-14500267.summary: This inscription records a veteran named Aelius Romulus from the Tenth Legion Gemina Pia Fidelis dedicating a monument for himself while still living, with an incomplete reference to another person or detail.

EDCS-14500268.text_edition: Rufiae Firminae Secunda Togionis filia viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500268.translation: Secunda, daughter of Togi, made this while living for Rufia Firmina and for herself.
EDCS-14500268.summary: This inscription records Secunda, daughter of Togi, creating a monument for Rufia Firmina and for herself while still alive, indicating a shared burial place.

EDCS-14500269.text_edition: Avaro Rumonis filius vivus fecit sibi et Tantiae Saxami filiae uxori Bruttae filiae
EDCS-14500269.translation: Avarus, son of Rumo, made this while living for himself and for Tantia, daughter of Saxamus, his wife, and for Brutta, his daughter.
EDCS-14500269.summary: This inscription records Avarus, son of Rumo, establishing a burial monument for himself, his wife Tantia, and his daughter Brutta while he was still alive.

EDCS-14500271.text_edition: Sabinius Peculiaris vivus fecit sibi et Florentinae coniugi et Serotinae filiae nnorum IIII et Vitali SLIMISORI III
EDCS-14500271.translation: Sabinius Peculiaris made this while living for himself and for Florentina, his wife, and for Serotina, his daughter, aged 4, and for Vitalis, SLIMISOR, aged 3.
EDCS-14500271.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Sabinius Peculiaris creating a monument for himself, his wife Florentina, and his young children Serotina and Vitalis, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-14500272.text_edition: Cnaei filius Sabi Cnaei filius Uti  Cnaei filius Rust
EDCS-14500272.translation: Son of Cnaeus, Sabi, son of Cnaeus, Uti, son of Cnaeus, Rust.
EDCS-14500272.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary record listing several individuals identified as sons of Cnaeus, with their individual names Sabi, Uti, and a partially preserved Rust, likely indicating a genealogical or family monument.

EDCS-14500274.text_edition: Samuco Masculi filia viva fecit sibi et Iulio Tertiano marito
EDCS-14500274.translation: Samuco, daughter of Masculus, made this while living for herself and for Julius Tertianus, her husband.
EDCS-14500274.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Samuco, daughter of Masculus, making a tomb for herself and her husband, Julius Tertianus, while still alive.

EDCS-14500275.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sarturonius Securus et Respectia Festa vivi fecerunt sibi et Senicionio Sarturoni et Vindrunae parentibus et Sarturonio Tertiano fratri
EDCS-14500275.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sarturonius Securus and Respectia Festa, in life, made this for themselves and for their parents, Senicionius Sarturonius and Vindruna, and for their brother, Sarturonius Tertianus.
EDCS-14500275.summary: This inscription records a tombstone set up by Sarturonius Securus and Respectia Festa during their lifetime for themselves, their parents, and their brother, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-14500276.text_edition: Dis Manibus Satullus Ursini et Finita Cracci vivi fecerunt sibi et Finitae VI annorum dierum V Musuabrae
EDCS-14500276.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Satullus, son of Ursinus, and Finita Cracci, in life, made this for themselves and for Finita Musuabra, who lived 6 years and 5 days.
EDCS-14500276.summary: This inscription is a tombstone created by Satullus Ursini and Finita Cracci for themselves and for Finita Musuabra, a child who died young, suggesting a family monument.

EDCS-14500278.text_edition: Scipio Sabini Dextri servus vivus fecit sibi et Vicariae coniugi et Exspectato filio annorum VII
EDCS-14500278.translation: Scipio, slave of Sabinus Dexter, in life, made this for himself and for his wife Vicaria and for his son Exspectatus, who was 7 years old.
EDCS-14500278.summary: This inscription records a monument made by Scipio, a slave, for himself, his wife Vicaria, and his son Exspectatus, indicating a family tombstone.

EDCS-14500279.text_edition: Secundinus Finiti vivus fecit sibi et Petroniae Avitae coniugi karissimae
EDCS-14500279.translation: Secundinus, son of Finitus, in life, made this for himself and for his most beloved wife, Petronia Avita.
EDCS-14500279.summary: This inscription is a tombstone set up by Secundinus Finiti during his lifetime for himself and his beloved wife, Petronia Avita.

EDCS-14500280.text_edition: Dis Manibus Secundinius Ursinus vivus fecit sibi et Betautuni coniugi karissimae et Lasscivae nepti @(obitae) anni I mensium V
EDCS-14500280.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Secundinius Ursinus, in life, made this for himself and for his most beloved wife Betautunis and for his granddaughter Lassciva, who died at 1 year and 5 months.
EDCS-14500280.summary: This inscription records a tombstone erected by Secundinius Ursinus during his lifetime for himself, his wife Betautunis, and his young deceased granddaughter Lassciva.

EDCS-14500281.text_edition: Secundus Adiutoris et Melissa Rustici filii fecerunt
EDCS-14500281.translation: Secundus, son of Adiutor, and Melissa, daughter of Rusticus, made this.
EDCS-14500281.summary: This inscription records a monument made by Secundus Adiutoris and Melissa Rustici, likely a tombstone for themselves or another family member.

EDCS-14500282.text_edition: Maxumo et Secundae filiae uxori lius fecit
EDCS-14500282.translation: He made this for Maxumus and for his daughter, his wife Secunda.
EDCS-14500282.summary: This inscription records a monument made by an unmentioned individual for Maxumus and his wife Secunda, who is also identified as his daughter, suggesting a complex family relationship or potential error.

EDCS-14500283.text_edition: Secunda Obiliaes liberta viva fecit sibi et Primo marito optimo
EDCS-14500283.translation: Secunda, freedwoman of Obilia, in life, made this for herself and for her excellent husband, Primus.
EDCS-14500283.summary: This inscription records a tombstone made by Secunda Obiliaes, a freedwoman, during her lifetime for herself and her husband Primus.

EDCS-14500284.text_edition: Caius Sestius Hermes sibi et Tertiae uxori et Niceni filiae et Cornelio Arogo
EDCS-14500284.translation: Gaius Sestius Hermes (made this) for himself and for his wife Tertia and for his daughter Niceni and for Cornelius Arogus.
EDCS-14500284.summary: This inscription records a monument, likely a tombstone, made by Gaius Sestius Hermes for himself, his wife Tertia, his daughter Niceni, and Cornelius Arogus, suggesting a family burial plot.

EDCS-14500285.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sextiliae Sextili vixit annos XXX menses II dies XI Valentinius Ingenuus coniugi pientissimae fecit
EDCS-14500285.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Sextilia Sextili, who lived 30 years, 2 months, 11 days. Valentinius Ingenuus made this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-14500285.summary: This inscription is a tombstone dedicated by Valentinius Ingenuus to his deceased wife, Sextilia Sextili, recording her age at death.

EDCS-14500286.text_edition: Sexstus sic Auli C Severi libertus et Secundae coniugi vivus fecit sibi et Quarto fratri et Crinuoni servo annorum XX
EDCS-14500286.translation: Sextus, freedman of Aulus C. Severus, and his wife Secunda, made this for themselves while living, and for his brother Quartus, and for his slave Crinuo, who was twenty years old.
EDCS-14500286.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Sextus and Secunda, while still alive, for themselves, Sextus's brother Quartus, and their slave Crinuo, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500287.text_edition: Cosutio Silvano et Quartillae coniugi et Mosicaito filio nnorum VIII
EDCS-14500287.translation: To Cosutius Silvanus and his wife Quartilla, and to their son Mosicaitus, eight years old.
EDCS-14500287.summary: This inscription commemorates Cosutius Silvanus, his wife Quartilla, and their son Mosicaitus, likely from a tombstone or funerary dedication.

EDCS-14500288.text_edition: Sisiae matri Tattu filia viva fecit et Germanae sorori
EDCS-14500288.translation: Tattu, daughter, made this for her mother Sisia while living, and for her sister Germana.
EDCS-14500288.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Tattu for her mother Sisia and her sister Germana, presumably erected while Tattu was still alive.

EDCS-14500289.text_edition: Dis Manibus Suadra Severi filia sibi viva fecit et Bassiano coniugi et Quartianus filius karissimus obitus
EDCS-14500289.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Suadra, daughter of Severus, made this for herself while living, and for her husband Bassianus, and her dearest son Quartianus, who has passed away.
EDCS-14500289.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, made by Suadra, daughter of Severus, for herself while alive, her husband Bassianus, and her deceased son Quartianus.

EDCS-14500290.text_edition: Sumario Limmonis filio et Bussullae Atianti filiae uxori et Tertio Sumari filio annorum XXXV
EDCS-14500290.translation: To Sumarius, son of Limmo, and to Bussulla, daughter of Atiantus, his wife, and to Tertius, son of Sumarius, who was thirty-five years old.
EDCS-14500290.summary: This inscription likely commemorates Sumarius, his wife Bussulla, and their son Tertius, who died at age 35, suggesting a funerary context.

EDCS-14500291.text_edition: et Ursae nurae vivae et Tertiniae Tertinae nepti @(obitae) annorum
EDCS-14500291.translation: And to Ursa, their daughter-in-law, who is alive, and to Tertinia, granddaughter of Tertina, who has passed away, [at X] years of age.
EDCS-14500291.summary: This inscription commemorates two women, Ursa (who is alive) and the deceased granddaughter Tertinia, possibly part of a larger family funerary monument.

EDCS-14500292.text_edition: Tertio Caesaris liberto Causia Occi filia uxor viva fecit
EDCS-14500292.translation: Causia, daughter of Occus, wife, made this while living for Tertius, freedman of Caesar.
EDCS-14500292.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Causia, daughter of Occus, for her husband Tertius, who was a freedman of Caesar, erected while she was still alive.

EDCS-14500293.text_edition: Tertius Iuni vivus fecit sibi et Seneciae Covnerti filiae uxori optimae et  Iantullo fratri et Secundae sorori hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14500293.translation: Tertius, son of Iunus, made this for himself while living, and for Senecia, daughter of Covnertus, his excellent wife, and for his brother Iantullus, and his sister Secunda. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500293.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Tertius for himself while living, his wife Senecia, his brother Iantullus, and his sister Secunda, explicitly stating it is not for his heirs, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500295.text_edition: Caius Titi vivus fecit  Men Adiect et Termi
EDCS-14500295.translation: Gaius, son of Titus, made this while living for Men Adiect and Termus.
EDCS-14500295.summary: This inscription records Gaius, son of Titus, making a dedication while alive, possibly a funerary monument or an honorific inscription, for Men Adiect and Termus.

EDCS-14500296.text_edition: Titia Spuri filia Tertia Publius Titius Publi libertus Surus vivi fecerunt sibi et suis Titia Publi liberta Noebia uxsor
EDCS-14500296.translation: Titia Tertia, daughter of Spurius, and Publius Titius Surus, freedman of Publius, made this for themselves and their family while living. Titia Noebia, freedwoman of Publius, wife.
EDCS-14500296.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Titia Tertia and Publius Titius Surus for themselves and their family, including Titia Noebia, while they were still alive, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500297.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerio Secundo et Primulae vivae Secundianus parentibus fecit
EDCS-14500297.translation: To the Divine Manes. Secundianus made this for his parents Valerius Secundus and Primula, while she was still alive.
EDCS-14500297.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Secundianus dedicated a monument to his father and his still-living mother.

EDCS-14500298.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibenius Primitivus et Aiucia Primitivi vivi fecerunt sibi et Peuciniae Quintillae matri
EDCS-14500298.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vibenius Primitivus and Aiucia Primitivi, while alive, made this for themselves and for Peucinia Quintilla, their mother.
EDCS-14500298.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vibenius Primitivus and Aiucia Primitivi to themselves and their mother, Peucinia Quintilla.

EDCS-14500299.text_edition: Vibeniae Ursae obitae annorum XXIII mensium V dierum III Paternus vivus fecit coniugi karissimae
EDCS-14500299.translation: To Vibenia Ursa, who died at 23 years, 5 months, 3 days old. Paternus, while alive, made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-14500299.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Paternus to his beloved wife, Vibenia Ursa, recording her age at death.

EDCS-14500300.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibeni et Surillae Iuniani servis
EDCS-14500300.translation: To the Divine Manes. To the slaves of Vibenius and Surilla Iunianus.
EDCS-14500300.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the slaves belonging to Vibenius and Surilla Iunianus.

EDCS-14500301.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibio Ursi filio @(obito) annorum IIII Vibia havia sic nepoti karissimo fecit
EDCS-14500301.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Vibius, son of Ursus, who died at 4 years old. Vibia Havia, while alive, made this for her dearest grandson.
EDCS-14500301.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vibia Havia to her grandson, Vibius, son of Ursus, who died at the age of four.

EDCS-14500302.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibiae Urbicae @(obitae) Aurelia Tarfna heres viva fecit
EDCS-14500302.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Vibia Urbica, who died. Aurelia Tarfna, her heir, while alive, made this.
EDCS-14500302.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Tarfna, as heir, to the deceased Vibia Urbica.

EDCS-14500303.text_edition: Vitalis vivus fecit sibi et Crescentinae coniugi et parentibus Vireduno Virioni Agrippinae Commae filiae
EDCS-14500303.translation: Vitalis, while alive, made this for himself and for his wife Crescentina and for his parents Viredunus Virio, Agrippina Comma, and for his daughter.
EDCS-14500303.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vitalis to himself, his wife Crescentina, his parents Viredunus Virio and Agrippina Comma, and his daughter.

EDCS-14500304.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Calendinus et Iulia Valentina vivi fecerunt sibi et Ulpiae Calendinae filiae annorum XX
EDCS-14500304.translation: Marcus Ulpius Calendinus and Iulia Valentina, while alive, made this for themselves and for Ulpia Calendina, their daughter, 20 years old.
EDCS-14500304.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Calendinus and Iulia Valentina to themselves and their 20-year-old daughter, Ulpia Calendina.

EDCS-14500305.text_edition: Urbico Buccionis filio et Iuliae Fuscae uxori Iulia Sisia parentibus fecit
EDCS-14500305.translation: To Urbicus, son of Buccio, and to his wife Iulia Fusca. Iulia Sisia made this for her parents.
EDCS-14500305.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Sisia to her parents, Urbicus, son of Buccio, and Iulia Fusca.

EDCS-14500306.text_edition: Perpetuae Securitati Ursulus Iuliani et Covinaerta Covinerti vivi sibi fecerunt
EDCS-14500306.translation: To Perpetua Securitas. Ursulus, son of Iulianus, and Covinaerta, daughter of Covinbertus, while alive, made this for themselves.
EDCS-14500306.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ursulus, son of Iulianus, and Covinaerta, daughter of Covinbertus, to themselves and to Perpetua Securitas.

EDCS-14500308.text_edition: Soliuslo Tourenao patri iea fecit Vibenae matri parentibus pientissumis sic
EDCS-14500308.translation: Soliuslo and Tourenao, children, made this for Vibena, their most devoted mother. Thus.
EDCS-14500308.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Soliuslo and Tourenao dedicating a monument to their mother, Vibena.

EDCS-14500310.text_edition: us  tus   veteranus   Baiae  ae coniugi   Naevi Q VLCIS N V
EDCS-14500310.translation: Vetustus, a veteran, made this for his wife Baiae. Naevius Quintus, of the 'VLCIS' tribe, made this for his wife.
EDCS-14500310.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for a veteran's wife, Baiae, and another dedication by Naevius Quintus for his wife.

EDCS-14500319.text_edition: Ancill3 Lu viva fecit sibi et Ur inconparabili coniugi
EDCS-14500319.translation: Ancilla, alive, made this for herself and for Ur, her incomparable husband.
EDCS-14500319.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Ancilla while still living, for herself and her husband Ur.

EDCS-14500325.text_edition: Lucco Vergillaes et Bacausus Terti filius fecerunt
EDCS-14500325.translation: Lucco, son of Vergilla, and Bacausus, son of Tertius, made this.
EDCS-14500325.summary: This inscription records Lucco, son of Vergilla, and Bacausus, son of Tertius, as the builders or dedicants of a monument.

EDCS-14500326.text_edition: Poio Cribonis filius vivus fieri fecit
EDCS-14500326.translation: Poio, son of Cribo, while alive, had this made.
EDCS-14500326.summary: This inscription records Poio, son of Cribo, having a monument or object made for himself while still living.

EDCS-14500328.translation: Tertius, son of Adsedo, made this.
EDCS-14500328.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating Tertius, son of Adsedo, as the maker or dedicant of a monument or object.

EDCS-14500329.text_edition: Publius Aelius Tertullus vivus fecit sibi et Maximiae Secundae uxori
EDCS-14500329.translation: Publius Aelius Tertullus, while alive, made this for himself and for Maximia Secunda, his wife.
EDCS-14500329.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Publius Aelius Tertullus for himself and his wife, Maximia Secunda, while he was still living.

EDCS-14500330.text_edition: Dis Manibus Commodi fratris et Commodini nepotis Finitus Claudi Prisci fecit
EDCS-14500330.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Finitus, son of Claudius Priscus, made this for Commodus, his brother, and Commodinus, his nephew.
EDCS-14500330.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Finitus, son of Claudius Priscus, in honor of his brother Commodus and nephew Commodinus.

EDCS-14500331.text_edition: Caius Gallius Cai libertus Tertius vivus fecit sibi et Galliae Cai libertae Suadrae uxori
EDCS-14500331.translation: Gaius Gallius Tertius, freedman of Gaius, while alive, made this for himself and for Gallia Suadra, freedwoman of Gaius, his wife.
EDCS-14500331.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Gallius Tertius, a freedman, for himself and his wife, Gallia Suadra, also a freedwoman, while he was still living.

EDCS-14500332.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aper Vibeni vivus fecit FEirminae#Firminae#EIRMINE coniugi karissimae et sibi
EDCS-14500332.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aper, son of Vibeno, while alive, made this for Firmina, his dearest wife, and for himself.
EDCS-14500332.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aper, son of Vibeno, while still alive, for his beloved wife Firmina and for himself.

EDCS-14500333.text_edition: Auno Bellici Iuliani servus Bacauso coniugi fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500333.translation: Aunus, slave of Bellicius Iulianus, made this for his wife Bausaco and for himself.
EDCS-14500333.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a slave for his wife and for himself, likely for a tomb or monument.

EDCS-14500334.text_edition: Cacusius Adnnami sic filius Exapia Croutae filia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Tertio filio
EDCS-14500334.translation: Cacusius, son of Adnnamus, and his wife Exapia, daughter of Crouta, made this while living for themselves and for their son Tertius.
EDCS-14500334.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a family, made by Cacusius and Exapia for themselves and their son while they were still alive, indicating a tomb or memorial.

EDCS-14500335.text_edition: Attalo Donni filio et Aetae libertae coniugi fil=Hii#filii#FHI vivi fecere sic
EDCS-14500335.translation: The children made this while living for Attalus, son of Donnus, and for Aeta, his freedwoman wife.
EDCS-14500335.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by children for their parents, Attalus and his freedwoman wife Aeta, while the children were still alive.

EDCS-14500336.translation: Gracchus, son of Civ[---], to the Iunones.
EDCS-14500336.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddesses Iunones, dedicated by Gracchus.

EDCS-14500337.text_edition: Lucio Dripponio Maximo et Iuniae Cai filiae Bateiae uxori Caio Maximio Cai filio Iuniano decurioni Viruniensium defuncto Romae in legatione nnorum XXX et Dripponiae Maximi filiae Suadrae nnorum XXXVIII
EDCS-14500337.translation: To Lucius Dripponius Maximus, and to Iunia Bateia, daughter of Gaius, his wife; to Gaius Maximius Iunianus, son of Gaius, decurion of the Virunienses, who died in Rome on an embassy at 30 years old; and to Dripponia Suadra, daughter of Maximus, 38 years old.
EDCS-14500337.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a family, honoring Lucius Dripponius Maximus, his wife Iunia Bateia, their son Gaius Maximius Iunianus (a decurion who died on an embassy), and their daughter Dripponia Suadra.

EDCS-14500338.text_edition: Gemellus Quarti filius et Quintilla Quinti filia vivi sibi fecerunt
EDCS-14500338.translation: Gemellus, son of Quartus, and Quintilla, daughter of Quintus, made this while living for themselves.
EDCS-14500338.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gemellus and Quintilla for themselves while still alive, likely for a tomb.

EDCS-14500339.text_edition: Dis Manibus Rufius Mosgaito et Iulia Ingenua vivi faciendum curaverunt sibi et Eburo filio fecerunt @(obito) annorum XXX et Ingenuae coniugi
EDCS-14500339.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Rufius Mosgaito and Iulia Ingenua, while living, arranged for this to be made for themselves and for their son Eburus, who died at 30 years old, and for his wife Ingenua.
EDCS-14500339.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, made by Rufius Mosgaito and Iulia Ingenua for themselves, their deceased son Eburus, and their son's wife Ingenua.

EDCS-14500340.text_edition: Marcus Iuventius Vibenus vivus fecit sibi et Cudsoni Senuci filiae uxori optimae et Pastori filio annorum VII
EDCS-14500340.translation: Marcus Iuventius Vibenus, while living, made this for himself and for his excellent wife Cudsonia, daughter of Senucus, and for his son Pastor, aged 7 years.
EDCS-14500340.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Marcus Iuventius Vibenus for himself, his wife, and his deceased son Pastor, while Vibenus was still alive.

EDCS-14500341.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibena Ursini viva fecit sibi et Urso marito carissimo et Vibeno filio @(obito) annorum XXV
EDCS-14500341.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Vibena Ursini, while living, made this for herself and for her dearest husband Ursus and for her son Vibenus, who died at 25 years old.
EDCS-14500341.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, made by Vibena Ursini for herself, her husband Ursus, and her deceased son Vibenus.

EDCS-14500342.text_edition: Termunibus sic AugC(ustis)#Aug(ustis)#AUC sacrum Quintus Calpurnius Phoebianus conductor ferrariarum Noricarum et Quintus Calpurnius Phoebianus Iunior et Charitonianus filii restituerunt curante Caio Iulio Hermete procuratore
EDCS-14500342.translation: To the Augustan Boundaries, sacred. Quintus Calpurnius Phoebianus, contractor of the Norican iron mines, and Quintus Calpurnius Phoebianus Junior and Charitonianus, his sons, restored this, with Gaius Iulius Hermes, procurator, supervising.
EDCS-14500342.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Augustan Boundaries, restored by Quintus Calpurnius Phoebianus, a mining contractor, and his sons, under the supervision of a procurator.

EDCS-14500343.text_edition: Subitio Suri filius testamento fieri iussit sibi et Vercillae Troucilli filiae uxori Castio filius vivus fecit
EDCS-14500343.translation: Subitius, son of Surus, ordered this to be made by his will for himself and for Vercilla, daughter of Troucillus, his wife. Castius, his son, made it while alive.
EDCS-14500343.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Subitius in his will for himself and his wife Vercilla, and completed by his son Castius while still alive, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500344.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primitiva fecit viva sibi et Augustino coniugi @(obito) annorum LXX
EDCS-14500344.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Primitiva, while alive, made this for herself and for Augustinus, her husband, who died at 70 years of age.
EDCS-14500344.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Primitiva while still alive, for herself and her deceased husband Augustinus, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-14500345.text_edition: Cotulia Masculi et Vibia Vibi viva filia Vibianus fecit
EDCS-14500345.translation: Cotulia, daughter of Masculus, and Vibia, daughter of Vibius, made this while alive. Vibianus, her son, made it.
EDCS-14500345.summary: This funerary monument was made for Cotulia, daughter of Masculus, and Vibia, daughter of Vibius, by her son Vibianus while he was alive.

EDCS-14500346.text_edition: Secundinus Ittes vivus fecit sibi et Vibiae Adiecti et Secundinae filiae annorum XX et Senicioni filio annorum XXX
EDCS-14500346.translation: Secundinus, son of Ittes, made this while alive for himself and for Vibia, daughter of Adiectus, and for Secundina, his daughter, 20 years old, and for Senicio, his son, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500346.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Secundinus Ittes while alive for himself, his wife Vibia, and his deceased children Secundina (20 years old) and Senicio (30 years old).

EDCS-14500347.text_edition: Atero Festi et Cattuni Montani heres fecit
EDCS-14500347.translation: Atero, heir of Festus and Cattunus, son of Montanus, made this.
EDCS-14500347.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Atero, who acted as the heir for both Festus and Cattunus Montanus.

EDCS-14500348.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Super vivus fecit sibi et Vibiae uxori Classici filiae et Ulpio Vibio et Ulpio Supero filiis militibus cohortis IIII praetoriae
EDCS-14500348.translation: Marcus Ulpius Super, while alive, made this for himself and for Vibia, his wife, daughter of Classicus, and for Ulpius Vibius and Ulpius Superus, his sons, soldiers of the Fourth Praetorian Cohort.
EDCS-14500348.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Marcus Ulpius Super during his lifetime for himself, his wife Vibia, and their two sons, Ulpius Vibius and Ulpius Superus, who were both soldiers in the Fourth Praetorian Cohort, suggesting a date in the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500350.text_edition: Aurelia Aureliana viva fecit Aurelio Iantullo fratri carissimo obito annorum XXX
EDCS-14500350.translation: Aurelia Aureliana, while alive, made this for Aurelius Iantullus, her dearest brother, who died at 30 years of age.
EDCS-14500350.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aurelia Aureliana during her lifetime for her beloved brother Aurelius Iantullus, who passed away at the age of 30.

EDCS-14500351.text_edition: Castricius Constitutus vivus fecit sibi et Secundinae Secconis filiae coniugi karissimae
EDCS-14500351.translation: Castricius Constitutus, while alive, made this for himself and for Secundina, daughter of Secco, his dearest wife.
EDCS-14500351.summary: This funerary inscription records Castricius Constitutus commissioning a monument during his lifetime for himself and his beloved wife, Secundina Secco.

EDCS-14500352.text_edition: Dis Manibus Secundina viva fecit sibi et Ingenuo marito et Secundino filio
EDCS-14500352.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Secundina, while alive, made this for herself and for Ingenuus, her husband, and for Secundinus, her son.
EDCS-14500352.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Secundina while she was alive, for herself, her husband Ingenuus, and their son Secundinus, acknowledging her deceased family members.

EDCS-14500353.text_edition: Dis Manibus Surianus servus saltuarius Sabini Maximi et Catulla coniux fecerunt vivi
EDCS-14500353.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Surianus, slave forester of Sabinus Maximus, and Catulla, his wife, made this while alive.
EDCS-14500353.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Surianus, a slave forester belonging to Sabinus Maximus, and his wife Catulla, for themselves while still alive, indicating their status and occupation.

EDCS-14500357.text_edition: Veranus Sammi et Iulia Secundina vivi fecerunt sibi et Secundino filio annorum XX
EDCS-14500357.translation: Veranus, son of Sammus, and Iulia Secundina, made this for themselves while living, and for their son Secundinus, who lived for 20 years.
EDCS-14500357.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Veranus and Iulia Secundina for themselves and their deceased son Secundinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500358.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Rapido Titiae Titi filiae Paulae uxori Titio Praesenti filio Caius Titius Censor heres utriusque filius fecit
EDCS-14500358.translation: Gaius Titius Censor, heir of both, his son, made this for Tiberius Claudius Rapidus, for Titia Paula, daughter of Titus, his wife, and for Titius Praesens, his son.
EDCS-14500358.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Titius Censor for his parents, Tiberius Claudius Rapidus and Titia Paula, and his brother Titius Praesens, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500359.text_edition: Tittionius Maturus vivus fecit sibi et Citatiae Citatae coniugi karissimae obitae annorum L
EDCS-14500359.translation: Tittionius Maturus made this for himself while living, and for Citata Citatae, his dearest wife, who died at the age of 50.
EDCS-14500359.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tittionius Maturus for himself and his beloved deceased wife Citata Citatae, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500360.text_edition: Accepto Secci filio qui SVIN et Bateli Deusonis filiae uxori filii fecerunt
EDCS-14500360.translation: They made this for Acceptus, son of Seccus, who [---], and for Batelis, daughter of Deuson, wife of their son.
EDCS-14500360.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by unknown individuals for Acceptus, son of Seccus, and for Batelis, wife of their son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500362.text_edition: Victoriae Priminiae carissimae dulcissimae coniugi Pacatianus p f q cum qua vixit annos piam memoriam fecserit sic et fili- carissim- obit- annorum XV
EDCS-14500362.translation: Pacatianus [made this] for Victoria Priminia, his dearest, sweetest wife, with whom he lived for [many] years, and he made a pious memorial; likewise for his dearest son, who died at the age of 15.
EDCS-14500362.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Pacatianus for his beloved deceased wife Victoria Priminia and his deceased son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500363.text_edition: Publius Veturius Clarus vivus fecit sibi et Publio Veturio Amphioravo IIIIII patrono et Veturiae Publi filiae Tertullae uxori
EDCS-14500363.translation: Publius Veturius Clarus, while living, made this for himself and for Publius Veturius Amphioravus, his patron, and for Veturia Tertulla, daughter of Publius, his wife.
EDCS-14500363.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Publius Veturius Clarus for himself, his patron Publius Veturius Amphioravus, and his wife Veturia Tertulla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500364.text_edition: Calupa Sabinaes sic serva viva fecit sibi et Upponi coniugi karissimo annorum XXV et Albanae Sabinaes sic ancillae vivae
EDCS-14500364.translation: Calupa, servant of Sabinaes, made this while living for herself and for Uppon, her dearest husband, who lived for 25 years, and for Albana, servant of Sabinaes, who also lived.
EDCS-14500364.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Calupa, a female slave, for herself, her deceased husband Uppon, and another living female slave Albana, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500365.text_edition: Caio Acilio Cai filio Aciliano fratres fecerunt
EDCS-14500365.translation: The brothers made this for Gaius Acilius Acilianus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-14500365.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by unnamed brothers for Gaius Acilius Acilianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500366.text_edition: Sacretius Speratus et Primitiva coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Adiutorinae nepti bene merenti @(obitae) annorum XX
EDCS-14500366.translation: Sacretius Speratus and his wife Primitiva, while living, made this for themselves and for their well-deserving granddaughter Adiutorina, who died at the age of 20.
EDCS-14500366.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Sacretius Speratus and Primitiva for themselves and their deceased granddaughter Adiutorina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500367.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Suro veterano Publius Aelius Gaius patri optimo vivus fecit
EDCS-14500367.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Gaius, while living, made this for his excellent father Publius Aelius Surus, a veteran.
EDCS-14500367.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Aelius Gaius to the spirits of the dead for his father, Publius Aelius Surus, a veteran, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500368.text_edition: Caius Annius Lucius vivus fecit sibi et Anniae Sedatinae uxorii sic
EDCS-14500368.translation: Gaius Annius Lucius made this in his lifetime for himself and for Annia Sedatina, his wife.
EDCS-14500368.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by Gaius Annius Lucius for himself and his wife, Annia Sedatina, while they were alive, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500369.text_edition: Caio Cominio Lucano et Attiae Litugenae uxori heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500369.translation: For Gaius Cominius Lucanus and Attia Litugena, his wife, the heir took care to have this made.
EDCS-14500369.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by an heir for Gaius Cominius Lucanus and his wife, Attia Litugena, suggesting it was erected after their deaths, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500370.text_edition: Cottaiio sic Anni Senecae servus vivus fecit sibi et Iustae coniugi optimae
EDCS-14500370.translation: Cottaius, slave of Annius Seneca, made this in his lifetime for himself and Iusta, his excellent wife.
EDCS-14500370.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by Cottaius, a slave, for himself and his wife, Iusta, during his lifetime, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500371.text_edition: Titus Sammius Passer vivus fecit sibi et Eppaeae Lucriae coniugi
EDCS-14500371.translation: Titus Sammius Passer made this in his lifetime for himself and Eppaea Lucria, his wife.
EDCS-14500371.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by Titus Sammius Passer for himself and his wife, Eppaea Lucria, during his lifetime, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500372.text_edition: Cattia Cai filia Serena sibi et Marco Iunio Censorino filio veterano et Iuniae Boudae filiae et Samudae filiae?
EDCS-14500372.translation: Cattia, daughter of Gaius, Serena, for herself and Marcus Iunius Censorinus, her son, a veteran, and Iunia Bouda, her daughter, and Samuda, her daughter.
EDCS-14500372.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by Cattia Serena for herself and her children, Marcus Iunius Censorinus (a veteran), Iunia Bouda, and Samuda, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500373.text_edition: Dis deabusque omnibus Claudius Valerius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500373.translation: To all the gods and goddesses, Claudius Valerius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500373.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Claudius Valerius fulfilled a vow to all the gods and goddesses, indicating a religious dedication, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500374.text_edition: Dis Manibus Acceptus Acceptiani vivus fecit sibi et Acceptiano et Adnamato filiis et Iliae Successi filiae coniugi karissimae obitae annorum XXX
EDCS-14500374.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Acceptus, son of Acceptianus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Acceptianus and Adnamatus, his sons, and for Ilia Successa, his dearest wife, who died at 30 years old.
EDCS-14500374.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by Acceptus, son of Acceptianus, for himself, his sons, and his beloved wife Ilia Successa, who died at age 30, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500375.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Volcacius Martialis beneficiarius consularis Valeria Sulpiciana
EDCS-14500375.publication: CIL 03, 05072 (p 1825) = ILLPRON 00095 = Legio-II-Ital-Nk, 00016 = Leber 00237a = CBI 00247 = AEA 2006, +00012
EDCS-14500375.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Volcacius Martialis, a beneficiarius consularis, and Valeria Sulpiciana (made this).
EDCS-14500375.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Volcacius Martialis, a beneficiarius consularis, and Valeria Sulpiciana, indicating a religious offering, likely in the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500376.text_edition: Lucio Barbio Vercaio aedilicio et Barbiae Suadullae uxori vivae et Lucio Barbio Verano militi cohortis I praetoriae filii filiaeque fecerunt
EDCS-14500376.translation: For Lucius Barbius Vercaius, an aedilician, and Barbia Suadulla, his wife, while alive, and Lucius Barbius Veranus, a soldier of the First Praetorian Cohort, their sons and daughters made this.
EDCS-14500376.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by the children for their parents, Lucius Barbius Vercaius (an aedilician) and Barbia Suadulla, and their brother, Lucius Barbius Veranus (a Praetorian soldier), likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500377.text_edition: Caio Crispinio Rufo aedilicio et Baebiae Boniae uxori filii et filiae vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500377.translation: For Gaius Crispinus Rufus, an aedilician, and Baebia Bonia, his wife, their sons and daughters made this while alive.
EDCS-14500377.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by the sons and daughters for their parents, Gaius Crispinus Rufus (an aedilician) and Baebia Bonia, while the children were still alive, likely in the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500379.text_edition: Disocno Dulsonis filio et Boniatae Vindonis filiae coniugi et Quinto Strittonis filio et Bateiae Disocni filiae coniugi Banio Verecundo bene merentibus
EDCS-14500379.translation: To Disocnus, son of Dulso, and to Boniata, daughter of Vindo, his wife, and to Quintus, son of Stritto, and to Bateia, daughter of Disocnus, his wife, Banio Verecundus [made this] for the well-deserving.
EDCS-14500379.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Banio Verecundus for Disocnus, Boniata, Quintus, and Bateia, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500380.text_edition: Attico Rimi filio fecit Caius Iulius Maxsumus sic hic situs est annorum XX
EDCS-14500380.translation: Gaius Iulius Maxsumus made this for Atticus, son of Rimus. Thus he is buried here, aged 20.
EDCS-14500380.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atticus, son of Rimus, erected by Gaius Iulius Maxsumus, commemorating his death at 20 years old.

EDCS-14500381.text_edition: Dis Manibus et perpetut(ae) Securitati Romanius Iuvenis et ceoniunx Aquilina fecerunt fileae! Iunillae annorum XVII Secundinius avi Iunillae Iucundianus et Mira
EDCS-14500381.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to everlasting Security. Romanius Iuvenis and his wife Aquilina made this for their daughter Iunilla, aged 17. Secundinius, Iunilla's grandfather, Iucundianus, and Mira [are also mentioned].
EDCS-14500381.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Romanius Iuvenis and Aquilina to their daughter Iunilla, who died at 17, with mention of her grandfather Secundinius, Iucundianus, and Mira, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500382.text_edition: C Secundinius Secundus aedilis Celeiae sibi et Maternae Vibeni filiae coniugi annorum
EDCS-14500382.translation: Gaius Secundinius Secundus, aedile of Celeia, [made this] for himself and for Materna, daughter of Vibeno, his wife, aged [---].
EDCS-14500382.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, set up by Gaius Secundinius Secundus, an aedile of Celeia, for himself and his wife Materna.

EDCS-14500383.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tamacus Titucaes sic et Secundina coniunx et Primianus filius et Finita coniunx vivi fecerunt sibi et Secundo filio annorum X et Secundinus filius Ursula coniunx
EDCS-14500383.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tamacus Titucaes [made this], and Secundina his wife, and Primianus his son, and Finita his wife, while living, made this for themselves and for their son Secundus, aged 10, and [for] Secundinus his son [and] Ursula his wife.
EDCS-14500383.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Tamacus Titucaes, his wife Secundina, his son Primianus, and Primianus's wife Finita while they were still alive, for themselves and for Secundus, who died at 10, and for Secundinus and his wife Ursula.

EDCS-14500384.text_edition: Lucius Turpilius Luci libertus Secundus Peccia Luci filia Ingenua Lucio Turpilio Luci filio Probo Turpiliae Luci filiae Urbanae et sibi vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500384.translation: Lucius Turpilius Secundus, freedman of Lucius, Peccia Ingenua, daughter of Lucius, while living, made this for Lucius Turpilius Probus, son of Lucius, and for Turpilia Urbana, daughter of Lucius, and for themselves.
EDCS-14500384.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucius Turpilius Secundus and Peccia Ingenua, while alive, for themselves and for Lucius Turpilius Probus and Turpilia Urbana, all part of the Turpilius family, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500385.translation: To Geminus, son of Vicarius, [son] of Marcellus.
EDCS-14500385.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Geminus, identifying him by his father Vicarius and his grandfather Marcellus.

EDCS-14500386.text_edition: a uxor pientissima  XXXI et Ingenuinae
EDCS-14500386.translation: His most devoted wife [---], aged 31, and to Ingenuina [---].
EDCS-14500386.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely commemorating a deceased individual and mentioning his devoted wife, aged 31, and Ingenuina.

EDCS-14500387.text_edition: Vercaius Ucconis filius vivus fecit sibi et Diacuni Suri filiae coniugi et Fuscae filiae annorum XVIII
EDCS-14500387.translation: Vercaius, son of Uccon, while living, made this for himself and for Diacunis, daughter of Surus, his wife, and for Fusca, his daughter, aged 18.
EDCS-14500387.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Vercaius, son of Uccon, while still alive, for himself, his wife Diacunis, and their daughter Fusca, who died at 18 years old, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500388.text_edition: Albino Donconis filio et Comae Rustici filiae coniugi fili- fec-
EDCS-14500388.translation: To Albinus, son of Donco, and to Coma, daughter of Rusticus, his wife, his children made this.
EDCS-14500388.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Albinus, son of Donco, and his wife Coma, daughter of Rusticus, erected by their children.

EDCS-14500389.text_edition: Surus Bricionis vivus fecit sibi et Tertiae D filiae uxori et Quarto filio militi cohortis V Breucorum
EDCS-14500389.translation: Surus, son of Bricio, made this in his lifetime for himself, and for Tertia D, his daughter-wife, and for Quartus, his son, a soldier of the Fifth Cohort of Breucians.
EDCS-14500389.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Surus for himself, his wife (who was also his daughter), and his son, a soldier, during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14500390.text_edition: Reburrus I et Asseda  et Gracco atobitis  Goutae  et Atticin
EDCS-14500390.translation: Reburrus I and Asseda and Gracco, parents, for Gouta and Atticin.
EDCS-14500390.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Reburrus, Asseda, and Gracco (likely a family group) to Gouta and Atticin, probably their children.

EDCS-14500393.text_edition: Iulius Annamus vivus sibi fecit et Iuliae Secundae uxori et Iuliae Ianuariae filiae
EDCS-14500393.translation: Iulius Annamus made this in his lifetime for himself, and for Iulia Secunda, his wife, and for Iulia Ianuaria, his daughter.
EDCS-14500393.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Iulius Annamus for himself, his wife, and his daughter during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500394.text_edition: Lollio Secundo Fabia et Attiae Secundinae uxori filius fecit et Attio Accepto militi alae Commagenorum
EDCS-14500394.translation: Fabia and Attia Secundina, his wife, made this for Lollio Secundus, their son, and for Attius Acceptus, a soldier of the Ala Commagenorum.
EDCS-14500394.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Fabia and Attia Secundina to their son Lollio Secundus, and to Attius Acceptus, a soldier.

EDCS-14500395.text_edition: Marco Longinio Vero quaestorio et Porciae Cai filiae Ritumarae coniugi filiae fecerunt
EDCS-14500395.translation: For Marcus Longinius Verus, a quaestor, and for Porcia, daughter of Gaius, Ritumara, his wife, their daughters made this.
EDCS-14500395.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the daughters to their father, Marcus Longinius Verus, a quaestor, and their mother, Porcia Ritumara.

EDCS-14500396.text_edition: Iano Gemino Consutius Ianuarius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500396.translation: To Ianus Geminus, Consutius Ianuarius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500396.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Consutius Ianuarius fulfilling a vow to the god Ianus Geminus.

EDCS-14500397.text_edition: Tert Ateva  Sabin et Publio Aelio T II Mi fratri
EDCS-14500397.translation: Tertia Ateva Sabina and Publius Aelius T. II M., her brother.
EDCS-14500397.summary: This inscription likely commemorates Tertia Ateva Sabina and her brother Publius Aelius.

EDCS-14500398.text_edition: Silvano Saxano Augusto sacrum Adiutor et Secundinus
EDCS-14500398.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Saxanus Augustus, Adiutor and Secundinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-14500398.summary: This is a religious inscription dedicated by Adiutor and Secundinus to the deity Silvanus Saxanus Augustus.

EDCS-14500399.text_edition: Dis Manibus Superis et Severi filiorum annorum VIIII et VI Vetucius Tutor pater
EDCS-14500399.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, and to the Superior Spirits, and to Severus, [one] of the sons, aged 9 and 6 years, Vetucius Tutor, the father [dedicated this].
EDCS-14500399.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vetucius Tutor, the father, to his deceased sons, one named Severus, who died at ages 9 and 6 (implying two sons, one of whom was Severus).

EDCS-14500400.text_edition: Primus Suronis et Tertiae coniugi vivus fecit sibi
EDCS-14500400.translation: Primus, son of Suro, and Tertia, his wife, made this in his lifetime for himself.
EDCS-14500400.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Primus, son of Suro, for himself and his wife Tertia during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500401.text_edition: Sirus Tricci filius et Primae coniugi
EDCS-14500401.translation: Sirus, son of Tricco, and for his wife Prima.
EDCS-14500401.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Sirus, son of Tricco, and his wife Prima, likely made by Sirus for himself and his wife.

EDCS-14500402.text_edition: Latobio Augusto sacrum Lucius Caesernius Avitus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500402.translation: Sacred to Latobius Augustus. Lucius Caesernius Avitus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500402.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Latobius Augustus, made by Lucius Caesernius Avitus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14500403.text_edition: Latobio Augusto sacrum pro salute Nammoniae Sabinae et Iuliae Bassillae Vindonia Vera mater votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500403.translation: Sacred to Latobius Augustus. For the well-being of Nammonia Sabina and Iulia Bassilla, Vindonia Vera, their mother, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-14500403.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Latobius Augustus, made by Vindonia Vera for the health of Nammonia Sabina and Iulia Bassilla.

EDCS-14500404.text_edition: Caio Priminio Tertio et Litugenae sibi
EDCS-14500404.translation: To Gaius Priminius Tertius and to Litugena, for himself/herself.
EDCS-14500404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Priminius Tertius and Litugena, likely set up by one of them for themselves and the other.

EDCS-14500406.text_edition: Caio Iulio Accisi filio Cisiaco et Devognatae Ationis filiae uxsori sic Florus et Valerius filii et Brogimara filia vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500406.translation: To Gaius Julius Cisiacus, son of Accisus, and to Devognata, daughter of Atio, his wife; Florus and Valerius, their sons, and Brogimara, their daughter, made this while living.
EDCS-14500406.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Florus, Valerius, and Brogimara while still alive for their parents, Gaius Julius Cisiacus and Devognata.

EDCS-14500407.text_edition: Gannico Manni filio filii et filiae vivi fecere
EDCS-14500407.translation: To Gannicus, son of Mannus; his sons and daughters made this while living.
EDCS-14500407.summary: This is a funerary monument made by the children of Gannicus, son of Mannus, for their father, while they themselves were still alive.

EDCS-14500408.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli
EDCS-14500408.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the well-being of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius.
EDCS-14500408.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Dolichenus, offered for the health of Emperor Marcus Aurelius (likely 161-180 AD).

EDCS-14500409.text_edition: Vibenus Cousonis filius vivus fecit sibi et Secundae Catulli coniugi annorum LI et Successo filio annorum XX
EDCS-14500409.translation: Vibenus, son of Couso, made this while living for himself and for Secunda, wife of Catullus, aged 51, and for Successus, his son, aged 20.
EDCS-14500409.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Vibenus, son of Couso, for himself and for his wife Secunda and his son Successus.

EDCS-14500410.text_edition: Vibenus Vindonis filius vivus fecit sibi et Secconiae Vetullae coniugi pientissimae annorum  et Secconio Vibio filio militi cohortis II praetoriae annorum X et Secconio equis  Urso  Victori nnum  et Seneciano Severo? --- militi? --- Vibeni filio annorum  ATVRM ONI
EDCS-14500410.translation: Vibenus, son of Vindo, made this while living for himself and for Secconia Vetulla, his most devoted wife, aged [---] years, and for Secconius Vibius, his son, a soldier of the Second Praetorian Cohort, aged 10 years, and for Secconius, [---] horseman, Urso, Victor, [---] year, and for Senecianus Severus?, [---] soldier?, [---] son of Vibenus, aged [---] years. ATURM ONI
EDCS-14500410.summary: This is a complex funerary monument made by Vibenus, son of Vindo, for himself and multiple family members including his devoted wife, a son who was a praetorian soldier, and other children.

EDCS-14500411.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulia Calandina viva fecit sibi et Sacronio Verino coniugi karissimo veterano legionis II Italicae custos armorum obitus annorum L
EDCS-14500411.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Calandina, while living, made this for herself and for Sacronius Verinus, her dearest husband, a veteran of the Second Italian Legion, custos armorum, who died aged 50.
EDCS-14500411.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Iulia Calandina for herself and for her deceased husband, Sacronius Verinus, a veteran of Legio II Italica who served as a custos armorum.

EDCS-14500412.text_edition: Cotulia Rusci filius vivus fecit sibi et Asediae Maximi filiae coniugi et Restutae filiae annorum XX et Boniae Successi filiae
EDCS-14500412.translation: Cotulia, son of Ruscus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Asedia, daughter of Maximus, his wife, and for Restuta, his daughter, 20 years old, and for Bonia, daughter of Successus.
EDCS-14500412.summary: This is a funerary inscription made in advance by Cotulia for himself, his wife, and his daughters, suggesting a family burial plot.

EDCS-14500414.text_edition: Secundinus Pallonis filius et Cupita Nemetonis filia et Secundina filia annorum XXV et Nertomariae Avitae nepti annorum XXX
EDCS-14500414.translation: Secundinus, son of Pallo, and Cupita, daughter of Nemeto, and Secundina, their daughter, 25 years old, and for Nertomaria Avita, their granddaughter, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500414.summary: This inscription records a family monument set up by Secundinus and Cupita for themselves, their daughter, and their granddaughter, likely indicating a familial burial.

EDCS-14500415.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Sextius Masculinus votum solvit
EDCS-14500415.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Sextius Masculinus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500415.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Sextius Masculinus to the god Mithras, indicating an act of religious devotion.

EDCS-14500416.text_edition: Metilius Maximinus vir egregius omnibus muneribus functus et Aurelia Vera coniux Metilii Aelianus Dumviranus Lucc3 Simplicianus Metilianus Victorianus Severa filii ex suis posuerunt curante Aristonico fratre
EDCS-14500416.translation: Metilius Maximinus, an exceptional man who performed all duties, and Aurelia Vera, his wife; Metilius Aelianus Dumviranus, Luccius Simplicianus, Metilianus Victorianus, Severa, their children, placed this from their own resources, with their brother Aristonius supervising.
EDCS-14500416.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by the children of Metilius Maximinus and Aurelia Vera, funded by the family, and overseen by a brother, likely for the parents and possibly themselves.

EDCS-14500417.text_edition: Albanus Hespani servus vivus fecit sibi et Surae uxori annorum XXXV
EDCS-14500417.translation: Albanus, slave of Hispanus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Sura, his wife, 35 years old.
EDCS-14500417.summary: This is a funerary inscription made in advance by Albanus, a slave, for himself and his wife, indicating a burial monument for the couple.

EDCS-14500418.text_edition: Maximus Antoniorum libertus et Vercilla vivi fecerunt sibi et Nundinae filiae annorum V et Urso filio annorum XXX
EDCS-14500418.translation: Maximus, freedman of the Antonii, and Vercilla, made this in their lifetimes for themselves and for Nundina, their daughter, 5 years old, and for Ursus, their son, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500418.summary: This is a funerary inscription made in advance by Maximus and Vercilla for themselves and their children, suggesting a family tomb.

EDCS-14500419.text_edition: Ulpia Firmina annorum triginta Marcus Ulpius Firminus et Connonia Statuta vivi fecerunt sibi et Ulpio Primiano filio annorum XIII
EDCS-14500419.translation: Ulpia Firmina, 30 years old; Marcus Ulpius Firminus and Connonia Statuta made this in their lifetimes for themselves and for Ulpius Primianus, their son, 13 years old.
EDCS-14500419.summary: This is a funerary inscription made in advance by Marcus Ulpius Firminus and Connonia Statuta for themselves, their deceased daughter Ulpia Firmina, and their son Ulpius Primianus.

EDCS-14500420.text_edition: Carvoniae Augustae sacrum pro salute Cnaei Atili Iuliani
EDCS-14500420.translation: Sacred to Carvonia Augusta, for the health of Gnaeus Atilius Julianus.
EDCS-14500420.summary: This is a religious dedication to Carvonia Augusta made on behalf of Gnaeus Atilius Julianus, likely a prayer for his well-being.

EDCS-14500421.text_edition: fili- Claudia Celeia --- o IIvir- iure dicundo   Marci filio Materno ---  Trosi- Paul--- et ---  Claud-ae Eunae? ---
EDCS-14500421.translation: To the son of Claudia Celeia [---] a duovir with judicial power [---] to Marcus, son of Maternus [---] Trosius Paulus [---] and [---] Claudia Euna [---].
EDCS-14500421.summary: This fragmented inscription likely commemorates a man who served as a duovir (a local magistrate), possibly a son of Claudia Celeia and a Marcus, suggesting a public or funerary monument from a Roman provincial town.

EDCS-14500422.text_edition: Atranti Augustae sacrum Fortunatus Cai Antoni Rufi procuratoris Augusti servus vilicus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500422.translation: Sacred to Atrantus Augusta, Fortunatus, slave and overseer of Gaius Antonius Rufus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500422.summary: This is a religious dedication to Atrantus Augusta made by Fortunatus, a slave and overseer of an imperial procurator, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14500423.text_edition: vantit Regius Regalis Iulia Aquilina ex voto
EDCS-14500423.translation: Vantit Regius Regalis and Iulia Aquilina, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-14500423.summary: This inscription records a dedication made in fulfillment of a vow by Vantit Regius Regalis and Iulia Aquilina.

EDCS-14500424.text_edition: Hecate Augustae Aurelii Asclepiodotus et Lucius pro salute sua et suorum votum solverunt libentes maximo
EDCS-14500424.translation: To Hecate Augusta, Aurelius Asclepiodotus and Lucius gladly and most willingly fulfilled their vow for their own well-being and that of their kin.
EDCS-14500424.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Hecate Augusta by Aurelius Asclepiodotus and Lucius for the health and well-being of themselves and their family.

EDCS-14500425.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Antonius Iulianus procurator portorii publici X votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500425.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Antonius Iulianus, procurator of the public portorium, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the tenth time.
EDCS-14500425.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Antonius Iulianus, a procurator of public customs, marking the tenth fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14500426.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Eutyches Iuliorum conductorum portorii publici servus |contrascriptor stationis Boiodurensis ex vikario! Benigni vilici stationis Atrantinae aram cum signo Lunae ex voto posuit prosedente Tito Claudio Senillo
EDCS-14500426.publication: CIL 03, 05121 (p 2198) = RINMS 00105 = ILLPRON 01928 = D 01857 = CIMRM-02, 01484 = ROPR p 91 = Kritzinger-01, 00012 = RICeleia 00430
EDCS-14500426.translation: To the Invincible God Mithras, Eutyches, a slave of the Iulii, contractors of the public portorium, a clerk of the Boiodurensis station, formerly an assistant to Benignus, the manager of the Atrantinae station, placed an altar with a statue of Luna in fulfillment of a vow, with Titus Claudius Senillus presiding.
EDCS-14500426.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Mithras by Eutyches, a slave and customs clerk, who set up an altar with a statue of Luna, with Titus Claudius Senillus overseeing the act.

EDCS-14500427.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Abascantus Antoni Rufi servus scrutator votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500427.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater, Abascantus, slave and inspector of Antonius Rufus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500427.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Liber Pater by Abascantus, a slave and inspector, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-14500428.text_edition: Noreiae Augustae et Honori stationis Atrantinae Bellicus et Eutyches |contrascriptores stationis eiusdem ex voto
EDCS-14500428.publication: CIL 03, 05123 = ILLPRON 01934 = D 01858 = IGLFriuli 00035 = Kritzinger-01, 00013 = RICeleia 00431
EDCS-14500428.translation: To Noreia Augusta and the Honor of the Atrantinae station, Bellicus and Eutyches, clerks of the same station, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-14500428.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Noreia Augusta and the 'Honor' of the Atrantinae customs station by two clerks, Bellicus and Eutyches.

EDCS-14500429.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hilari fili qui annos vixit III Secundianus contrascriptor et Rufilla parentes filio faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-14500429.publication: CIL 03, 05124 = ILLPRON 01935 = ILJug-01, 00381 = Kritzinger-01, 00014 = AE 2003, 01300 = RICeleia 00435
EDCS-14500429.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for their son Hilarus, who lived for three years, Secundianus, a clerk, and Rufilla, his parents, had this made for their son.
EDCS-14500429.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by parents, Secundianus and Rufilla, for their son Hilarus, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-14500432.text_edition: Maronius Marcellinus decurio Celeiae vivus fecit sibi et filio Maronio Martino @(obito) annorum XXX et Maronio Marcelliano  et Maronio Marcellino nepoti @(obito) annorum XXXIII
EDCS-14500432.publication: CIL 03, 05127 = ILLPRON 01969 = AIJ 00031 = RIS 00347 = EQNoricum-C, 00004 = RICeleia 00449
EDCS-14500432.translation: Maronius Marcellinus, decurion of Celeia, while living, made this for himself and for his son Maronius Martinus, who died at thirty years of age, and for Maronius Marcellianus, and for his grandson Maronius Marcellinus, who died at thirty-three years of age.
EDCS-14500432.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Maronius Marcellinus, a decurion, for himself and several deceased family members, including a son and a grandson, during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500433.text_edition: Viatorinus annorum L Dincen annorum LXX Avitianus Aviti vivus fecit sibi et suis coniugi Viatorinae et Marcius Secundinus et Maximae uxori annorum XXX Maximianus Maximi annorum X
EDCS-14500433.translation: Viatorinus, aged 50, Dincen, aged 70, Avitianus, son of Avitus, while living, made this for himself and his kin: for his wife Viatorina, and for Marcius Secundinus, and for his wife Maxima, aged 30, and for Maximianus, son of Maximus, aged 10.
EDCS-14500433.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Avitianus, son of Avitus, for himself and several family members including his wife Viatorina, Marcius Secundinus, his wife Maxima, and Maximianus, son of Maximus, during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500434.summary: This inscription is a very brief record of two individuals named Viator Adiec and Adium, likely a funerary or dedicatory context given its brevity and format.

EDCS-14500435.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Vibius Sabinus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae annorum LXV P Paulinus vivus
EDCS-14500435.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Vibius Sabinus, a veteran of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, aged 65. P. Paulinus made this while living.
EDCS-14500435.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Vibius Sabinus, a veteran of Legio V Macedonica, set up by P. Paulinus during his lifetime, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500436.text_edition: Tutor Ioiti et Piaii Coudom=NIari#Coudomari#COUDONIA Acceptus Pi Vibius Nertomari LX Tertius Vibi filius annorum XXV Exortus Secundinus annorum V Antia Suri annorum XXXV
EDCS-14500436.translation: Tutor of Ioitus and Piaius Coudomarius. Acceptus Pi Vibius, son of Nertomarus, aged 60. Tertius, son of Vibius, aged 25. Exortus Secundinus, aged 5. Antia Suri, aged 35.
EDCS-14500436.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of multiple individuals, possibly a family group or household, detailing their names, relationships, and ages, likely from a tombstone or a family monument.

EDCS-14500437.text_edition: Seccio Maximi filius annorum IX Anecila OAMLIEA
EDCS-14500437.translation: Seccio, son of Maximus, aged 9. Anecila OAMLIEA.
EDCS-14500437.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Seccio, son of Maximus, who died at the age of nine, and also mentions Anecila, likely a relative.

EDCS-14500438.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Sundinus intona vivi fecerunt VDP filio annorum XX
EDCS-14500438.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sundinus Intona made this while living for his son, aged 20.
EDCS-14500438.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Sundinus Intona during his lifetime for his son who died at age 20, dedicated to the Divine Manes.

EDCS-14500439.text_edition: Savo et Adsallutae votum solvit libens merito Secundio
EDCS-14500439.translation: To Savus and Adsalluta, Secundio willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500439.summary: This is a votive inscription where Secundio dedicates an offering to the deities Savus and Adsalluta to fulfill a vow, likely in gratitude.

EDCS-14500440.text_edition: Adsallutae Augustae sacrum Gaius Caecina Faustinus
EDCS-14500440.translation: Sacred to Adsalluta Augusta. Gaius Caecina Faustinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-14500440.summary: This is a sacred dedication to the goddess Adsalluta Augusta, made by Gaius Caecina Faustinus.

EDCS-14500441.text_edition: Adsallutae Augustae sacrum Ocellio Castrici Marcelli servus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500441.translation: Sacred to Adsalluta Augusta. Ocellio, slave of Castricius Marcellus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500441.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Adsalluta Augusta, made by Ocellio, a slave of Castricius Marcellus, to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-14500442.text_edition: Neptuno Augusto sacrum Caius Castricius Optatus solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500442.translation: Sacred to Neptune Augustus. Gaius Castricius Optatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500442.summary: This is a votive inscription to Neptune Augustus, where Gaius Castricius Optatus fulfilled a vow, likely in gratitude for safe passage or prosperity.

EDCS-14500443.text_edition: Savo et Adsallutae sacrum Publius Antonius Secundus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500443.translation: Sacred to Savus and Adsalluta. Publius Antonius Secundus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500443.summary: This is a votive inscription where Publius Antonius Secundus dedicated an offering to the deities Savus and Adsalluta to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-14500444.text_edition: Bassus Quincti libertus vivus fecit sibi et Catae uxori annorum LXV
EDCS-14500444.translation: Bassus, freedman of Quinctus, made this for himself and for his wife Cata, aged 65, while living.
EDCS-14500444.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Bassus, a freedman of Quinctus, for himself and his wife Cata, who was 65 years old, made during his lifetime, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500447.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Vibius Secundus veteranus fecit vivus sibi et suis  annorum C  Cerula Tutoris annorum LXXXX Vibia Avita annorum XXII Valeria Marcellina obita annorum XXII
EDCS-14500447.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vibius Secundus, a veteran, made this while alive for himself and his family. He lived 100 years. Cerula, daughter of Tutor, lived 90 years. Vibia Avita lived 22 years. Valeria Marcellina died at 22 years of age.
EDCS-14500447.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Vibius Secundus for himself and his family, commemorating their ages at death.

EDCS-14500448.text_edition: Publius Albinius Antonius aedilis Claudiae Celeiae et Terentia Finita vivi fecerunt sibi et Publio Albinio Finito filio quaestori Claudiae Celeiae annorum XXXX et Publio Albinio Marcellino filio annorum L et Publio Albinio Marciano nepoti quaestori annorum XXX et Iantullae nurui annorum XX
EDCS-14500448.translation: Publius Albinius Antonius, aedile of Claudia Celeia, and Terentia Finita, while alive, made this for themselves and for Publius Albinius Finitus, their son, quaestor of Claudia Celeia, who lived 40 years, and for Publius Albinius Marcellinus, their son, who lived 50 years, and for Publius Albinius Marcianus, their grandson, quaestor, who lived 30 years, and for Iantulla, their daughter-in-law, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-14500448.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius Albinius Antonius and Terentia Finita for themselves and various deceased family members, noting their civic offices and ages.

EDCS-14500449.text_edition: Barbius Cupitus annorum LX Latina Saturnini annorum LV Marina Barbi annorum XXXX
EDCS-14500449.translation: Barbius Cupitus, 60 years old. Latina, daughter of Saturninus, 55 years old. Marina, daughter of Barbius, 40 years old.
EDCS-14500449.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names and ages of three individuals: Barbius Cupitus, Latina, and Marina.

EDCS-14500450.text_edition: Diastumarus Ibliendi filius annorum C Coma Chilonis filia annorum LXXV Viator filius annorum L Crispus filius annorum XXXXV Soa filia annorum
EDCS-14500450.translation: Diastumarus, son of Ibliendus, 100 years old. Coma, daughter of Chilonis, 75 years old. Viator, son, 50 years old. Crispus, son, 45 years old. Soa, daughter, [age missing].
EDCS-14500450.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing a family and their ages, likely a father, mother, and their children.

EDCS-14500451.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Uxellimo Serandius Verinus decurio Celeiae poni iussit Pompeia Ursula eius cum Urso filio votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500451.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Uxellimus. Serandius Verinus, decurion of Celeia, ordered this to be placed. Pompeia Ursula, his wife, with their son Ursus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14500451.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Uxellimus, placed by Serandius Verinus, a decurion of Celeia, and his family, to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-14500452.text_edition: Nymphis Augustis Fructus Quinti Sabini Verani conductoris publici portorii servus vilicus posuit
EDCS-14500452.translation: To the Augustan Nymphs. Fructus, a slave and estate manager of Quintus Sabinus Veranus, a public tax collector for the customs duty, set this up.
EDCS-14500452.summary: This is a votive offering dedicated to the Augustan Nymphs by Fructus, a slave and bailiff, on behalf of his master, a public customs collector.

EDCS-14500453.text_edition: Nymphis Augustis Matius Finitus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500453.translation: To the Augustan Nymphs. Matius Finitus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500453.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Augustan Nymphs by Matius Finitus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14500454.text_edition: Nymphis Augustis sacrum Caius Veponius Phoebus et Felix eius votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14500454.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Nymphs. Gaius Veponius Phoebus and Felix, his wife, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14500454.summary: This is a sacred votive dedication to the Augustan Nymphs made by Gaius Veponius Phoebus and his wife Felix, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14500456.text_edition: Caius Baebius Acceptus annorum LXX Quarta Damionis filia nnorum L heres fecit et Caio Baebio Vitellio filio
EDCS-14500456.translation: Gaius Baebius Acceptus, 70 years old. Quarta, daughter of Damio, 50 years old. The heir made this for him and for Gaius Baebius Vitellius, his son.
EDCS-14500456.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Gaius Baebius Acceptus and Quarta, and also Gaius Baebius Vitellius, set up by an heir.

EDCS-14500457.text_edition: Donata liberta Titi Postumi viva fecit sibi et
EDCS-14500457.translation: Donata, freedwoman of Titus Postumus, made this while alive for herself and...
EDCS-14500457.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Donata, a freedwoman of Titus Postumus, for herself and likely other family members, while she was still alive.

EDCS-14500458.text_edition: Cupitus Aviti filius vivus fecit sibi et Boniatae Secundi filiae annorum LXV hic sita est Antonio filio annorum XX Castus Cupiti annorum LXXV Iustus Cupiti annorum LXXV
EDCS-14500458.translation: Cupitus, son of Avitus, made this while alive for himself and for Boniata, daughter of Secundus, 65 years old, who lies here. Also for his son Antonius, 20 years old. Castus, son of Cupitus, 75 years old. Iustus, son of Cupitus, 75 years old.
EDCS-14500458.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Cupitus for himself and several family members, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500459.text_edition: Saturio Decimi Castrici Sabini servus vivus fecit sibi et Septumae sic coniugi nnorum LX
EDCS-14500459.translation: Saturio, slave of Decimus Castricius Sabinus, made this while alive for himself and for Septuma, his wife, 60 years old.
EDCS-14500459.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Saturio, a slave, for himself and his wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500460.text_edition: Seubrinubi Proba viva fecit et Surato filio annorum XXI LL II
EDCS-14500460.translation: Seubrinubi Proba, while alive, made this for herself and for her son Suratus, 21 years old.
EDCS-14500460.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Seubrinubi Proba for herself and her son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500461.text_edition: Imperatore Antonino IIII  et Balbino consulibus Celeiae Augustae Publius Aelius Verinus beneficiarius consularis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500461.publication: CIL 03, 05154 (p 1830) = CIL 10, *00357d = ILLPRON 01614 = Legio-II-Ital-Nsl, 00010 = CBI 00218 = RICeleia 00002
EDCS-14500461.translation: When Antoninus for the fourth time and Balbinus were consuls, at Celeia Augusta, Publius Aelius Verinus, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-14500461.summary: This is a votive inscription to Celeia Augusta, dedicated by Publius Aelius Verinus, a consular beneficiarius, dated to 238 AD.

EDCS-14500462.text_edition: Dis deabusque omnibus Bellia Sorana votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500462.translation: Bellia Sorana willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to all gods and goddesses.
EDCS-14500462.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Bellia Sorana to all gods and goddesses, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500463.text_edition: Fortunae curatio oppidana de sua pecunia faciundum sic curavit Caius Attius [Caius SI&
EDCS-14500463.translation: Caius Attius, the urban curator, took care to have this made for Fortuna with his own money.
EDCS-14500463.summary: This is a public inscription recording the construction of a monument for Fortuna, funded and overseen by Caius Attius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500464.text_edition: Fortunae Stabili pro salute dominorum nnostrorum Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severii sic Pertinacis et Marci Aureli Antonini Auggustorum [et Publi Septimi Getae] Ulpius Valerianus |(centurio) legionis X geminae
EDCS-14500464.translation: To Fortuna Stabilis, for the health of our lords the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti, and Publius Septimius Geta, Ulpius Valerianus, a centurion of the Tenth Legion Gemina, [dedicates this].
EDCS-14500464.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Ulpius Valerianus, a centurion, to Fortuna Stabilis for the health of the Imperial family, specifically dated to the reign of Septimius Severus and Caracalla (before Geta's damnatio memoriae, thus around 209-211 AD).

EDCS-14500466.text_edition: Genio Augusti et Laribus Publius Ursinius Maturus et Cassia Censorina
EDCS-14500466.translation: To the Genius of the Augustus and to the Lares, Publius Ursinius Maturus and Cassia Censorina [dedicate this].
EDCS-14500466.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Publius Ursinius Maturus and Cassia Censorina to the Genius of the Emperor and the Lares, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500469.text_edition: Iovi Depulsori sacrum Aurelius Paterclus beneficiarius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500469.publication: CIL 03, 05160 (p 1830) = ILLPRON 01621 = Legio-II-Ital-Nsl, 00009 = CBI 00219 = RICeleia 00041 = AEA 2016/17, +00076
EDCS-14500469.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Depulsor, Aurelius Paterclus, a beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-14500469.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Aurelius Paterclus, a beneficiarius, to Jupiter Depulsor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500470.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Adnamius Flavinus beneficiarius Ulpi Victoris procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500470.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Adnamius Flavinus, a beneficiarius of Ulpius Victor, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-14500470.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Adnamius Flavinus, a beneficiarius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500471.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Adnamius Flavinus beneficiarius Useni Secundi procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500471.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Adnamius Flavinus, a beneficiarius of Usenus Secundus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500471.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Adnamius Flavinus, a beneficiarius, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-14500472.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Antonius Maximus beneficiarius Quinti Caecili Redditi procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500472.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Antonius Maximus, a beneficiarius of Quintus Caecilius Redditus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500472.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Antonius Maximus, a beneficiarius.

EDCS-14500473.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Anonius Valens beneficiarius Flavi Titiani procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500473.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Antonius Valens, a beneficiarius of Flavius Titianus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500473.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Antonius Valens, who served as a beneficiarius.

EDCS-14500474.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Augustanus beneficiarius Gai Rasini Silonis procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500474.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Augustanus, a beneficiarius of Gaius Rasinius Silo, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500474.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Augustanus, a beneficiarius.

EDCS-14500475.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Kaninius sic Lucanus beneficiarius Usieni Secundi procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito Tertullo et Sacerdote consulibus
