EDCS-14500475.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Quintus Caninius Lucanus, a beneficiarius of Usenus Secundus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Tertullus and Sacerdos.
EDCS-14500475.summary: This inscription, dated to the consulship of Tertullus and Sacerdos (200 AD), records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Caninius Lucanus, a beneficiarius.

EDCS-14500476.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Quintus Crescentius Marcellus beneficiarius Quinti Lisini Sabini procuratoris Augusti solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500476.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Quintus Crescentius Marcellus, a beneficiarius of Quintus Lisinius Sabinus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500476.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Crescentius Marcellus, a beneficiarius.

EDCS-14500477.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Dubitatus beneficiarius Lisini Sabini procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500477.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Flavius Dubitatus, a beneficiarius of Lisinius Sabinus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500477.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Flavius Dubitatus, a beneficiarius.

EDCS-14500478.text_edition: Caius Rufius Apolaustus et Iulia Successa vivi sibi fecerunt Iulia Firmina annorum XX hic sita est
EDCS-14500478.translation: Gaius Rufius Apolaustus and Iulia Successa made this for themselves while living. Iulia Firmina, twenty years old, is buried here.
EDCS-14500478.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Gaius Rufius Apolaustus and Iulia Successa for themselves, also commemorating the burial of Iulia Firmina, who died at age twenty.

EDCS-14500479.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Fuscinius Catullus beneficiarius Ulpi Victoris procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500479.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Fuscinius Catullus, a beneficiarius of Ulpius Victor, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500479.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Fuscinius Catullus, a beneficiarius.

EDCS-14500480.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gemellius Adiutor beneficiarius Drusi Proculi procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500480.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gemellius Adiutor, a beneficiarius of Drusus Proculus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500480.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gemellius Adiutor, a beneficiarius.

EDCS-14500482.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucilius Finitus beneficiarius Flavi Titiani procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500482.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucilius Finitus, a beneficiarius of Flavius Titianus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500482.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Lucilius Finitus, a beneficiarius serving under the imperial procurator Flavius Titianus.

EDCS-14500483.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Masculinius Successus beneficiarius Cai Antisti Auspicis procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500483.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Masculinius Successus, a beneficiarius of Gaius Antistius Auspex, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500483.summary: This inscription commemorates a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Masculinius Successus, a beneficiarius under the imperial procurator Gaius Antistius Auspex.

EDCS-14500484.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Messius Frontinus beneficiarius Cai Censori Nigri procuratoris Augusti
EDCS-14500484.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Messius Frontinus, a beneficiarius of Gaius Censorinus Niger, procurator Augusti.
EDCS-14500484.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Lucius Messius Frontinus, a beneficiarius serving the imperial procurator Gaius Censorinus Niger.

EDCS-14500485.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Mustius Tettianus beneficiarius Lisini Sabini procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500485.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Mustius Tettianus, a beneficiarius of Lisinius Sabinus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500485.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Gaius Mustius Tettianus, a beneficiarius under the imperial procurator Lisinius Sabinus.

EDCS-14500486.text_edition: Eponae Augustae sacrum Caius Mustius Tettianus beneficiarius Lisini Sabini procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500486.translation: Sacred to Epona Augusta, Gaius Mustius Tettianus, a beneficiarius of Lisinius Sabinus, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500486.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to the goddess Epona Augusta by Gaius Mustius Tettianus, a beneficiarius serving the imperial procurator Lisinius Sabinus.

EDCS-14500487.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo onius Primus beneficiarius Plauti Caesiani procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500487.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Onius Primus, a beneficiarius of Plautius Caesanius, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500487.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Onius Primus, a beneficiarius serving under the imperial procurator Plautius Caesanius.

EDCS-14500488.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Sextius Pullaenius beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae votum solvit libens merito [Imperatore Commodo VII] et Pertinace consulibus
EDCS-14500488.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Quintus Sextius Pullaenius, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Imperator Commodus for the seventh time and Pertinax were consuls.
EDCS-14500488.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Quintus Sextius Pullaenius, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Italica, dated to the consulship of Commodus (VII) and Pertinax in 190 CE.

EDCS-14500489.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Surus beneficiarius Memmi Apollinaris procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500489.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Surus, a beneficiarius of Memmius Apollinaris, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500489.summary: This inscription commemorates a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Surus, a beneficiarius under the imperial procurator Memmius Apollinaris.

EDCS-14500490.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Acilianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500490.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Acilianus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500490.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Marcus Ulpius Acilianus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Italica.

EDCS-14500491.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Crescens beneficiarius Cai Censori Nigri procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500491.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Crescens, a beneficiarius of Gaius Censorinus Niger, procurator Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500491.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Marcus Ulpius Crescens, a beneficiarius serving under the imperial procurator Gaius Censorinus Niger.

EDCS-14500492.translation: Of Caecilius Iuventianus, procurator of the emperor.
EDCS-14500492.summary: This inscription is a dedication to or records the name and title of Caecilius Iuventianus, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-14500493.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pos(uerunt) Caius Bellicius Ingenuus IIvir Claudiae Celeiae et Aurelia Aurelia coniunx pro salute sua suorumque omnium
EDCS-14500493.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Caius Bellicius Ingenuus, duumvir of Claudia Celeia, and Aurelia Aurelia, his wife, placed this for their own well-being and that of all their kin.
EDCS-14500493.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, set up by Caius Bellicius Ingenuus, a duumvir of Claudia Celeia, and his wife Aurelia Aurelia, for the health and safety of themselves and their family.

EDCS-14500494.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Calcinius Tertianus conductor publici portorii tribunus militum legionis XX Valeriae Victricis cum Petronia Tertia uxore pro se et filiis votum solvit
EDCS-14500494.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Caius Calcinius Tertianus, contractor of the public customs and military tribune of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix, with Petronia Tertia, his wife, fulfilled their vow for himself and his sons.
EDCS-14500494.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Caius Calcinius Tertianus, a public tax collector and military tribune, along with his wife Petronia Tertia, for himself and their sons.

EDCS-14500495.text_edition: Pro salute domini nostri  Imperatoris Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti  Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Arubiano et Celeiae sanctae Vibius Cassius Victorinus beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito  Laeto II et Ceriale consulibus
EDCS-14500495.translation: For the well-being of our lord Emperor Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator Arubianus and to holy Celeia, Vibius Cassius Victorinus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italic Legion, pious, faithful, Antoninian, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Laetus II and Cerialis.
EDCS-14500495.summary: This votive inscription, dated to 215 AD, was dedicated by Vibius Cassius Victorinus, a beneficiarius consularis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator Arubianus and holy Celeia, for the health of Emperor Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus (Caracalla).

EDCS-14500496.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Culminali et dis deabusque omnibus Titus Mattius Hecato pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500496.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminalis and all gods and goddesses, Titus Mattius Hecato willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being and that of his kin.
EDCS-14500496.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminalis and all gods and goddesses, dedicated by Titus Mattius Hecato for the health and safety of himself and his family.

EDCS-14500497.text_edition: [Pro salute Auggustorum] nnostrorum Iovi Optimo Maximo et Celeiae sanctae Caius Licinius Bellicianus beneficiarius legionis II Italicae piae fidelis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito Gentiano et Basso consulibus Idibus Decembribus
EDCS-14500497.translation: For the well-being of our emperors, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to holy Celeia, Caius Licinius Bellicianus, a beneficiarius of the Second Italic Legion, pious, faithful, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his kin on the Ides of December, in the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus.
EDCS-14500497.summary: This votive inscription, dated to December 13, 211 AD, was dedicated by Caius Licinius Bellicianus, a beneficiarius of the Second Italic Legion, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and holy Celeia, for the health of the emperors and his family.

EDCS-14500498.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Celeiae et Noreiae Sanctae Rufius Senilis beneficiarius consularis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500498.publication: CIL 03, 05188 (p 1830) = ILLPRON 01649 = Legio-II-Ital-Nsl, 00001 = D 04860 = CBI 00229 = RICeleia 00043
EDCS-14500498.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to holy Celeia and Noreia, Rufius Senilis, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his kin.
EDCS-14500498.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, holy Celeia, and Noreia, dedicated by Rufius Senilis, a beneficiarius consularis, for the health and safety of himself and his family.

EDCS-14500499.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et dis deabusque omnibus Marcus Aurelius Iustus beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito Praesente et Extricato consulibus
EDCS-14500499.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses, Marcus Aurelius Iustus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italic Legion, pious, faithful, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his kin, in the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus.
EDCS-14500499.summary: This votive inscription, dated to 217 AD, was dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Iustus, a beneficiarius consularis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses, for the health and safety of himself and his family.

EDCS-14500501.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et cultoribusltoribus Caius Rufius aram cum? scr(ipturam) donum dedit  Sentonius Spectatus Petronius Marcus Mamilius Respectinus Antonius Saturninus Helvius Iustus Turravius Castor Auro Iantulli Boto Noni Verus Saturnini Sentonius Albucius Pompeius Surus Publicius Callistus Mamilius Crescens Petronius Celer Rufius Herma Claudius Maximus Restutus Saturnini Iantullus Orgetei Maximianus Severi
EDCS-14500501.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and his worshippers, Caius Rufius gave an altar with an inscription as a gift. Sentonius Spectatus, Petronius Marcus, Mamilius Respectinus, Antonius Saturninus, Helvius Iustus, Turravius Castor, Auro Iantulli, Boto Noni, Verus Saturnini, Sentonius Albucius, Pompeius Surus, Publicius Callistus, Mamilius Crescens, Petronius Celer, Rufius Herma, Claudius Maximus, Restutus Saturnini, Iantullus Orgetei, Maximianus Severi.
EDCS-14500501.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and his worshippers by Caius Rufius, followed by a list of individuals, likely members of the cult or community.

EDCS-14500503.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Eponae et Celeiae sanctae Marcus S I
EDCS-14500503.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Epona, and holy Celeia, Marcus S. I.
EDCS-14500503.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Epona, and holy Celeia, initiated by Marcus S. I.

EDCS-14500505.text_edition: Matri Deum Magnae Blaundiae Cnaeus Pompeius Iustinus decurio et IIvir Claudiae Celeiae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500505.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods of Blaundus, Gnaeus Pompeius Iustinus, decurion and duovir of Claudia Celeia, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500505.summary: This inscription records a vow made to the Great Mother of the Gods by a local magistrate, Gnaeus Pompeius Iustinus, in Celeia.

EDCS-14500507.text_edition: Mercurio Augusto sacrum et cultoribus eius  Iulius Lucifer titulum cum scriptura testamento dedit  Viator Gaetuli Avitus Magni Spectatus Sexti Secundinus Secundi Finitus Magni Crescens Nertomari Maximus Quinti Calvenius Martialis Aemilius Spectatus Iulius Maximianus Calvenius Avitus Avitus Secundi  Maximus Viatoris Maximus Terti Iulius Secundi Atilius Firmus Cassius Senilis Secundinus Maximi Saturninus Viatoris Praesens Respecti
EDCS-14500507.publication: CIL 03, 05196a = ILLPRON 01657 = ILLPRON 01658 = EQNoricum-C, 00012 = AE 2009, 00981 = RICeleia 00051
EDCS-14500507.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus and his worshippers. Iulius Lucifer dedicated this stele with its inscription by testament. Viator Gaetuli, Avitus Magni, Spectatus Sexti, Secundinus Secundi, Finitus Magni, Crescens Nertomari, Maximus Quinti, Calvenius Martialis, Aemilius Spectatus, Iulius Maximianus, Calvenius Avitus, Avitus Secundi, Maximus Viatoris, Maximus Terti, Iulius Secundi, Atilius Firmus, Cassius Senilis, Secundinus Maximi, Saturninus Viatoris, Praesens Respecti.
EDCS-14500507.summary: This inscription is a testamentary dedication to Mercury Augustus and his worshippers by Iulius Lucifer, followed by a list of individuals, likely members of the cult.

EDCS-14500511.text_edition: ProPro Quinti Rufi Atticiani Quintus Rufius Hiero votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500511.translation: For Quintus Rufius Atticianus, Quintus Rufius Hiero willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500511.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Quintus Rufius Hiero, made on behalf of Quintus Rufius Atticianus.

EDCS-14500512.text_edition: posuit Kalendis Augustis Flacco et Gallo consulibus
EDCS-14500512.translation: He set it up on the Kalends of August, in the consulship of Flaccus and Gallus.
EDCS-14500512.summary: This inscription records the precise date of an unknown monument's erection, specified by consular dating to August 1st of 162 AD.

EDCS-14500513.text_edition: Imperatori Vespasiano Caesari Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X imperatori XX consuli VIIII patri patriae Caius Domitius Florus testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-14500513.translation: To Imperator Vespasian Caesar Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the tenth time, acclaimed Imperator for the twentieth time, Consul for the ninth time, Father of the Fatherland, Gaius Domitius Florus ordered this to be made by testament.
EDCS-14500513.summary: This inscription is a posthumous dedication to the emperor Vespasian, commissioned by Gaius Domitius Florus through his will, likely dating to 79 AD based on Vespasian's titles.

EDCS-14500514.text_edition: Imperatori divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano Caesari Augusto Germanico Dacico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli V Caius Rufius Moderatus Iunianus Iuncinus praefectus cohortis VI Raetorum tribunus militum legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis codicillo fieri iussit
EDCS-14500514.translation: To Imperator Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Consul for the fifth time, Gaius Rufius Moderatus Iunianus Iuncinus, prefect of the Sixth Cohort of Raetians, military tribune of the Seventh Claudian Legion, loyal and faithful, ordered this to be made by codicil.
EDCS-14500514.summary: This inscription records a testamentary dedication to Emperor Trajan by Gaius Rufius Moderatus Iunianus Iuncinus, a military officer, dating to 103-111 AD based on Trajan's titles.

EDCS-14500517.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Caro Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae proconsuli ordo Celeiensium
EDCS-14500517.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Carus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, the order of the Celeians.
EDCS-14500517.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Carus by the municipal council (ordo) of Celeia, likely dating to 282-283 AD.

EDCS-14500519.text_edition: [Domino nostro Flavio Claudio Constantino] [nobilis]simo [Caesari] filio domini nostri Constantini Maximi victoriosissimi semper AugC(usti)#Aug(usti)#AUC nepoti Marci Aureli Maximiani et Flavi Constanti divorum et divi Claudi abnepoti Norici mediterranei devoti numini maiestatique eorum
EDCS-14500519.translation: To our Lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus, noblest Caesar, son of our Lord Constantine Maximus, most victorious and ever Augustus, grandson of the deified Marcus Aurelius Maximianus and Flavius Constantius, and great-great-grandson of the deified Claudius, the devoted inhabitants of Noricum Mediterraneum [dedicate this] to their divinity and majesty.
EDCS-14500519.summary: This inscription is a public dedication from Noricum Mediterraneum to the young Flavius Claudius Constantinus as Caesar, acknowledging his imperial lineage, likely dating to the early 4th century.

EDCS-14500520.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Iulio  Constantio nobilissimo Caesari  filio domini nostri Constantini maximi victoriosissimi semper Augusti nepoti Marci Aureli Valeri Maximiani et Flavi Constanti divorum Norici mediterranei numini maiestatique eorum
EDCS-14500520.translation: To our Lord Flavius Iulius Constantius, noblest Caesar, son of our Lord Constantine Maximus, most victorious and ever Augustus, grandson of the deified Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus and Flavius Constantius, the inhabitants of Noricum Mediterraneum [dedicate this] to their divinity and majesty.
EDCS-14500520.summary: This inscription from Noricum Mediterraneum honors Flavius Iulius Constantius as nobilissimus Caesar, emphasizing his imperial descent from Constantine the Great and earlier deified emperors, likely from the early 4th century.

EDCS-14500521.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Constantio clementissimo adque victori Augusto Martinianus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Norici mediterranei devotus numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-14500521.translation: To our Lord Flavius Constantius, most clement and victorious Augustus, Martinianus, a man of perfect rank, governor of the province of Noricum Mediterraneum, [dedicates this], devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-14500521.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Flavius Constantius by Martinianus, the governor of Noricum Mediterraneum, expressing loyalty and devotion, likely from the mid-4th century.

EDCS-14500522.summary: This is a short, possibly dedicatory or acclamatory inscription, expressing triumph.

EDCS-14500523.text_edition: Tito Vario Titi filio Clementi Claudia Celeia procuratori Augusti provinciarum Raetiae Mauretaniae Caesariensis Lusitaniae Ciliciae praefecto alae Britannicae miliariae praefecto auxiliariorum tempore expeditionis in Tingitaniam missorum praefecto equitum alae II Pannoniorum tribuno legionis XXX Ulpiae praefecto cohortis II Gallorum Macedonicae Valerius Urbanus Licinius Secundinus decuriones alares provinciae Mauretaniae Caesariensis
EDCS-14500523.translation: To Titus Varius Clemens, son of Titus, of the tribe Claudia, from Celeia, procurator of Augustus in the provinces of Raetia, Mauretania Caesariensis, Lusitania, Cilicia; prefect of the ala Britannica milliaria; prefect of auxiliaries sent during the expedition into Mauretania Tingitana; prefect of the cavalry of the Ala II Pannoniorum; tribune of Legion XXX Ulpia; prefect of Cohort II Gallorum Macedonica. Valerius Urbanus and Licinius Secundinus, decurions of alae from the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, [set this up].
EDCS-14500523.summary: This inscription is a dedication by two cavalry decurions from Mauretania Caesariensis to Titus Varius Clemens, detailing his extensive military and administrative career as an imperial procurator and officer.

EDCS-14500524.text_edition: Tito Vario Clementi procuratori provinciarum Belgicae Germaniae superioris Germaniae inferioris Raetiae Mauretaniae Caesariensis Lusitaniae Ciliciae praefecto equitum alae nnae miliariae praefecto auxiliorum in Mauretaniam Tingitanam ex Hispania missorum praefecto equitum alae II nnum tribuno militum legionis XXX Ulpiae victricis praefecto cohortis II Gallorum Macedonicae cives Romani ex Italia et aliis provinciis in Raetia consistentes
EDCS-14500524.translation: To Titus Varius Clemens, procurator of the provinces of Belgica, Germania Superior, Germania Inferior, Raetia, Mauretania Caesariensis, Lusitania, Cilicia; prefect of the cavalry of a milliaria ala; prefect of auxiliaries sent from Hispania into Mauretania Tingitana; prefect of the cavalry of the Ala II Pannoniorum; military tribune of Legion XXX Ulpia Victrix; prefect of Cohort II Gallorum Macedonica. The Roman citizens residing in Raetia, from Italy and other provinces, [set this up].
EDCS-14500524.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Roman citizens residing in Raetia to Titus Varius Clemens, commemorating his distinguished career as an imperial procurator and military officer.

EDCS-14500527.text_edition: Tito Vario Clementi ab epistulis Augustorum procuratori provinciarum Belgicae et utriusque Germaniae Raetiae Mauretaniae Caesariensis Lusitaniae Ciliciae praefecto equitum alae Britannicae miliariae praefecto auxiliorum in Mauretaniam Tingitanam ex Hispania missorum praefecto equitum alae II Pannoniorum tribuno legionis XXX Ulpiae victricis praefecto praef(ecto) cohortis II Gallorum Macedonicae civitas Treverorum praesidi optimo
EDCS-14500527.translation: To Titus Varius Clemens, secretary for letters to the emperors, procurator of the provinces of Belgica and both Germaniae, Raetia, Mauretania Caesariensis, Lusitania, Cilicia; prefect of the cavalry of the Ala Britannica milliaria; prefect of auxiliaries sent from Hispania into Mauretania Tingitana; prefect of the cavalry of the Ala II Pannoniorum; tribune of Legion XXX Ulpia Victrix; prefect of Cohort II Gallorum Macedonica. The civitas Treverorum [set this up] to their excellent governor.
EDCS-14500527.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the civitas Treverorum to their governor, Titus Varius Clemens, detailing his impressive career as an imperial secretary, procurator, and military officer.

EDCS-14500528.text_edition: nus  Cato us Cornelius Saturninus S patroni Causarum provinciae Mauretaniae Caesariensis
EDCS-14500528.translation: …nus Cato, …us Cornelius Saturninus S…, patrons of cases in the province of Mauretania Caesariensis.
EDCS-14500528.summary: This fragment of an inscription records the names of individuals, including Cato and Cornelius Saturninus, who served as legal patrons in the province of Mauretania Caesariensis.

EDCS-14500529.text_edition: Autonius RSC Velina Aquileia et  Antonius Respectus  amico optimo
EDCS-14500529.translation: Autonius, of the Velina tribe, from Aquileia, and Antonius Respectus, to their best friend.
EDCS-14500529.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Autonius and Antonius Respectus to their dear friend, possibly a funerary monument or a token of esteem.

EDCS-14500530.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Iustino militi legionis II Italicae @(obito) in expeditione Daccisca sic annorum XXIII Aurelius Verinus veteranus et Messia Quartina parentes fecerunt
EDCS-14500530.publication: CIL 03, 05218 = CIL 03, 11691 = ILLPRON 01679 = Legio-II-Ital-Nsl, 00007 = AIJ 00045 = D 02309 = Schober 00199 = AEA 2005, +00068 = IDRE-02, 00256 = RICeleia 00077 = Sasel-Kos-2016, p 216
EDCS-14500530.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Iustinus, a soldier of Legion II Italica, who died in the Dacian expedition, aged 23 years. Aurelius Verinus, a veteran, and Messia Quartina, his parents, made this.
EDCS-14500530.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Verinus and Messia Quartina for their son, Aurelius Iustinus, a soldier of Legion II Italica who died at 23 during the Dacian expedition.

EDCS-14500531.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Saturnino veterano @(obito) annorum XLV et Aureliae Secundinae coniugi @(obitae) annorum XXXV et Aurelio Secundino fratri @(obito) annorum VII Decimia Quaeta sic avia Aurelius Crescentinus parentibus carissimis
EDCS-14500531.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Saturninus, a veteran, who died aged 45; and Aurelia Secundina, his wife, who died aged 35; and Aurelius Secundinus, his brother, who died aged 7. Decimia Quaeta, his grandmother, and Aurelius Crescentinus [made this] for their dearest parents.
EDCS-14500531.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Aurelius Saturninus, his wife Aurelia Secundina, and his brother Aurelius Secundinus, set up by their grandmother Decimia Quaeta and Aurelius Crescentinus.

EDCS-14500532.text_edition: Lucius Braetius Luci filius Publilia Verona veteranus legionis VIII Augustae nnorum LXX hic situs est f f Magiona Verulli filia annorum LXXV hic sita est f f
EDCS-14500532.translation: Lucius Braetius, son of Lucius, of the Publilia tribe, from Verona, a veteran of Legion VIII Augusta, aged 70, lies here. Magiona, daughter of Verullus, aged 75, lies here.
EDCS-14500532.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Braetius, a veteran of Legion VIII Augusta, and Magiona, both of advanced age.

EDCS-14500533.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cupitius Cupitianus |(obitus) annorum LX et Baebia Maximina coniux |(obita) annorum L Flavius Decoratianus beneficiarius et Cupitia Iuliana coniux vivi parentibus carissimis faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-14500533.publication: CIL 03, 05221 (p 2285) = ILLPRON 01583 = Legio-II-Ital-Nsl, 00008 = AIJ 00047 = Schober 00184 = CBI 00245 = RICeleia 00099
EDCS-14500533.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cupitius Cupitianus, who died aged 60, and Baebia Maximina, his wife, who died aged 50. Flavius Decoratianus, a beneficiarius, and Cupitia Iuliana, his wife, while living, took care to have this made for their dearest parents.
EDCS-14500533.summary: This is a funerary monument for Cupitius Cupitianus and his wife Baebia Maximina, commissioned by their son Flavius Decoratianus, a beneficiarius, and his wife Cupitia Iuliana during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500534.text_edition: Ennio Secundiano equiti cohortis VII praetoriae annorum XXV et Quinto Ennio Apollonio annorum LX et Valeriae Maximillae annorum L
EDCS-14500534.translation: To Ennius Secundianus, knight of the Seventh Praetorian Cohort, 25 years old, and to Quintus Ennius Apollonius, 60 years old, and to Valeria Maximilla, 50 years old.
EDCS-14500534.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Ennius Secundianus, a praetorian knight, along with Quintus Ennius Apollonius and Valeria Maximilla, likely family members.

EDCS-14500535.text_edition: Gaviae Publi filiae Virunae Lucius Dindius Respectus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae Publius Dindius Speratus |(centurio) speculatorum Augusti filii matri indulgentissimae et sibi fecerunt
EDCS-14500535.translation: To Gavia Viruna, daughter of Publius. Lucius Dindius Respectus, centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and Publius Dindius Speratus, centurion of the Augustan Speculators, sons, made this for their most indulgent mother and for themselves.
EDCS-14500535.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by two centurion sons, Lucius Dindius Respectus and Publius Dindius Speratus, for their mother Gavia Viruna and also for themselves, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-14500536.text_edition: et Caio Iunio Nicandro filio nnorum XXXVIII Mattiae Publi filiae Verinae nnorum XXIIII et Caio Iunio Isaeo veterano ex decurione alae I Commagenorum annorum LX et Ulpiae Afrodisiae annorum XXV mensis I
EDCS-14500536.translation: And to Gaius Iunius Nicander, son, 38 years old, to Mattia Verina, daughter of Publius, 24 years old, and to Gaius Iunius Isaeus, veteran, formerly a decurion of the First Cohort of Commagenians, 60 years old, and to Ulpia Afrodisia, 25 years and 1 month old.
EDCS-14500536.summary: This appears to be a multi-person funerary inscription, likely commemorating family members including a veteran decurion and younger individuals, potentially from a family tomb.

EDCS-14500537.text_edition: Caio Atilio Secundiano aedili Claudiae Celeiae annorum LVIIII et Veponiae Bellicinae eius Atilius Secundus parentibus et Calvisiae Tutorinae coniugi pudicissimae annorum XXXII  et Atiliae Secundinae filiae stolatae annorum
EDCS-14500537.translation: To Gaius Atilius Secundianus, aedile of Claudia Celeia, 59 years old, and to Veponia Bellicina, his (wife). Atilius Secundus (made this) for his parents, and for Calvisia Tutorina, his most chaste wife, 32 years old, and for Atilia Secundina, his daughter, wearing the stola, (age missing).
EDCS-14500537.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Atilius Secundus to his parents, Gaius Atilius Secundianus (an aedile) and Veponia Bellicina, as well as to his own wife Calvisia Tutorina and his daughter Atilia Secundina.

EDCS-14500538.text_edition: Dis Manibus Decimo Castricio Vero Antonio Avito decurioni Claudiae Celeiae annorum XXVI Iulia Quinti filia Vera mater viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500538.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Decimus Castricius Verus Antonius Avitus, decurion of Claudia Celeia, 26 years old. Julia Vera, daughter of Quintus, his mother, made this while living, and for herself.
EDCS-14500538.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Julia Vera to her son, Decimus Castricius Verus Antonius Avitus, a decurion of Claudia Celeia, and also for herself while still alive, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-14500539.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius municipii Celeiae libertus Favor vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Pusillae coniugi suae et suis
EDCS-14500539.publication: CIL 03, 05227 (p 1830) = ILLPRON 01593 = AGN-2, 00298 = AIJ 00048 = ILJug-01, 00401 = Schober 00324 = EQNoricum-C, 00017 = RICeleia 00097
EDCS-14500539.translation: Tiberius Claudius Favor, freedman of the municipality of Celeia, made this while living, for himself and for Julia Pusilla, his wife, and for his household.
EDCS-14500539.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Tiberius Claudius Favor, a freedman, for himself, his wife Julia Pusilla, and his family, indicating a private tomb.

EDCS-14500540.text_edition: Claudiae Secundae nnum XXXII contubernali hic sitae et Caio Cornelio Felici et vivis et sibi Cupitus Celeianorum f(aciendum) curavit
EDCS-14500540.translation: To Claudia Secunda, 32 years old, his companion, buried here, and to Gaius Cornelius Felix, both for the living and for himself. Cupitus, of the Celeians, had it made.
EDCS-14500540.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Claudia Secunda and Gaius Cornelius Felix, possibly by Cupitus, suggesting a personal or family monument.

EDCS-14500541.text_edition: Enniae Marci filiae Verae decreto decurionum publice ob singularem eius pudicitiam Masculinius Surus
EDCS-14500541.translation: To Ennia Vera, daughter of Marcus, by decree of the decurions, publicly, because of her singular modesty. Masculinius Surus (made this).
EDCS-14500541.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Ennia Vera by Masculinius Surus, publicly decreed by the local decurions to commend her exceptional modesty.

EDCS-14500542.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavia Valeria Aurelio Adiutori civi Afro negotianti obito annorum XXXV coniugi karissimo titulum posuit
EDCS-14500542.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Valeria placed this monument for Aurelius Adiutor, an African citizen and merchant, 35 years old, her dearest husband, who passed away.
EDCS-14500542.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Flavia Valeria for her deceased husband, Aurelius Adiutor, an African merchant.

EDCS-14500543.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Euhodiae Fructi uxori annorum XL domo Epheso Insequens servus fecit
EDCS-14500543.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Euhodia, wife of Fructus, 40 years old, from Ephesus. Insequens, a slave, made this.
EDCS-14500543.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Euhodia, originally from Ephesus, erected by Insequens, who is identified as a slave, suggesting a memorial from a fellow slave or a former dependent.

EDCS-14500544.text_edition: Caius Iulius Vepo donatus civitate Romana viritim et inmunitate ab divo Augusto vivos sic fecit sibi et Boniatae Antoni filiae coniugi et suis
EDCS-14500544.translation: Gaius Iulius Vepo, individually granted Roman citizenship and immunity by the deified Augustus, while alive, thus made this for himself and for Boniata, daughter of Antonius, his wife, and for his dependents.
EDCS-14500544.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Gaius Iulius Vepo's grant of Roman citizenship and immunity by Augustus, and his dedication of a tomb for himself, his wife Boniata, and his family.

EDCS-14500545.text_edition: Maximo IIviro iure dicundo Iulia Tiberi filia Maximina marito
EDCS-14500545.translation: To Maximus, duumvir with judicial power, Iulia Maximina, daughter of Tiberius, made this for her husband.
EDCS-14500545.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Iulia Maximina to her husband Maximus, who held the public office of duumvir with judicial authority.

EDCS-14500546.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Mattio Adiecto interfectus a Mattzaris annorum XL Antonia Quincta coniugi viva fecit
EDCS-14500546.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Mattius Adiectus, killed by the Mattzari, aged 40. Antonia Quincta, while alive, made this for her husband.
EDCS-14500546.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Antonia Quincta for her husband, Mattius Adiectus, who was killed by the Mattzari at the age of 40.

EDCS-14500547.text_edition: Sextus Publicius Fronto nnrum LX Batro Celeianorum nnrum LXX Servatus Celeianorum nnrum XXXV Insequens Celeianorum nnrum LXX hic siti
EDCS-14500547.translation: Sextus Publicius Fronto, aged 60; Batro of the Celeiani, aged 70; Servatus of the Celeiani, aged 35; Insequens of the Celeiani, aged 70; are buried here.
EDCS-14500547.summary: This is a collective funerary inscription for four individuals, including Sextus Publicius Fronto and three members of the Celeiani, indicating their ages and place of burial.

EDCS-14500548.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records a dedication or monument by a decurion named Terentius to Claudia Celeia.

EDCS-14500549.text_edition: Terenti Proculi LILS optimi IIviri Claudiae Celeiae
EDCS-14500549.translation: Of Terentius Proculus, L.I.L.S., excellent duumvir, to Claudia Celeia.
EDCS-14500549.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication for Terentius Proculus, described as an excellent duumvir, and also references Claudia Celeia, possibly as a co-dedicant or recipient.

EDCS-14500550.text_edition: Marcus Vibius Dioscorus Iulia Concordia annorum LXXX et Tae
EDCS-14500550.translation: Marcus Vibius Dioscorus, Iulia Concordia, aged 80, and Tae[...]
EDCS-14500550.summary: This is a fragmented inscription likely marking a tomb for Marcus Vibius Dioscorus, Iulia Concordia (aged 80), and at least one other individual.

EDCS-14500551.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription by Secundinus to Antonius Torquatus, likely for a monument or offering.

EDCS-14500552.text_edition: Antoniae Marullae annorum III Antonius Felix et Speratia Iuliana parentes filiae fecerunt
EDCS-14500552.translation: To Antonia Marulla, aged 3. Antonius Felix and Speratia Iuliana, her parents, made this for their daughter.
EDCS-14500552.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antonius Felix and Speratia Iuliana to their young daughter, Antonia Marulla, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-14500553.text_edition: Caio Atilio Secundino vixit annos LXII obito annorum VII filio pientissimo libens merito vivus curavit
EDCS-14500553.translation: To Gaius Atilius Secundinus, he lived 62 years. [His father] willingly and deservedly took care of this while alive, for his most devoted son, who died at the age of 7.
EDCS-14500553.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by a father, while still alive, for his most devoted son, Gaius Atilius Secundinus, who died at the age of 7, though the inscription also mentions a lifespan of 62 years which likely refers to the father.

EDCS-14500554.text_edition: Aouius Ittonis libertus annorum C et Boniata coniux annorum LX Saturninus filius Ittonis libertus Genialis Ittonis libertus annorum L Finita Ittonis liberta annorum XL Saturnina Saturnini filia  annorum
EDCS-14500554.translation: Aovius, freedman of Itto, 100 years old, and Boniata, his wife, 60 years old. Saturninus, freedman of Itto. Genialis, freedman of Itto, 50 years old. Finita, freedwoman of Itto, 40 years old. Saturnina, daughter of Saturninus, [---] years old.
EDCS-14500554.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording several individuals, likely family members or members of a household, with some identified as freedpersons of Itto, indicating a late Republican or Imperial date.

EDCS-14500555.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Attio Ursulo annorum LXX et Ursino filio annorum XXIII Tertia coniux viva fecit
EDCS-14500555.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Marcus Attius Ursulus, 70 years old, and for Ursinus, his son, 23 years old. Tertia, his wife, made this while living.
EDCS-14500555.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Tertia during her lifetime for her husband, Marcus Attius Ursulus, and their son, Ursinus, suggesting an Imperial date.

EDCS-14500556.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Gaudentius filius Aeli Graeci matri vivae et Aurelio Sereniano fratri obito annorum XVI fecit
EDCS-14500556.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Gaudentius, son of Aelius Graecus, made this for his mother, while she was alive, and for Aurelius Serenianus, his deceased brother, who was 16 years old.
EDCS-14500556.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Gaudentius to his living mother and his deceased brother, Aurelius Serenianus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500557.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Iustinus obitus annorum LX Iustus filius et Respecta uxor vivi posuerunt
EDCS-14500557.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Iustinus, deceased, 60 years old. Iustus, his son, and Respecta, his wife, placed this while living.
EDCS-14500557.summary: This is a funerary monument placed by Iustus, the son, and Respecta, the wife, for the deceased Aurelius Iustinus, indicating a typical Imperial dedication.

EDCS-14500558.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Secundinus et Aurelia Valentina coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Aurelius Secundianus filius obitus annorum VII crudeles parentes facere curaverunt
EDCS-14500558.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Secundinus and Aurelia Valentina, husband and wife, made this for themselves while living, and arranged for it to be made for Aurelius Secundianus, their deceased son, 7 years old – cruel parents.
EDCS-14500558.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription for a young son, Aurelius Secundianus, erected by his parents, Aurelius Secundinus and Aurelia Valentina, who also prepared their own burial place, likely dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-14500559.text_edition: Campanus Atebodui filius vivus fecit sibi et Quartae coniugi et suis
EDCS-14500559.translation: Campanus, son of Ateboduus, made this for himself while living, and for Quarta, his wife, and his family.
EDCS-14500559.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Campanus for himself, his wife Quarta, and his family during his lifetime, consistent with Roman funerary practices of the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500560.text_edition: Titi Carmaei Finiti annorum LXXX Sorniae Secundae annorum Tito Carmaeo Avito annorum XX Sornia Maxima parentibus faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500560.translation: For Titus Carmaeus Finitus, 80 years old. For Sornia Secunda, [---] years old. For Titus Carmaeus Avitus, 20 years old. Sornia Maxima arranged for this to be made for her parents.
EDCS-14500560.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Sornia Maxima to her parents, Titus Carmaeus Finitus and Sornia Secunda, and possibly another relative, Titus Carmaeus Avitus, suggesting a family tomb from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500561.text_edition: Lucius Decius Luci filius Ianuarius nnorum L hic situs est Cassia Viatoris filia annorum LX
EDCS-14500561.translation: Lucius Decius, son of Lucius, Ianuarius, 50 years old, lies here. Cassia, daughter of Viator, 60 years old.
EDCS-14500561.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Lucius Decius Ianuarius and Cassia, daughter of Viator, implying a shared burial plot from the Imperial era.

EDCS-14500562.text_edition: annorum LV hic situs est  vivus fecit O Diastulli N annorum LX nta filiae annorum XXV
EDCS-14500562.translation: [---] 55 years old, lies here. [---] made this while living. O Diastullus N. [---] 60 years old. [---] for his daughter, 25 years old.
EDCS-14500562.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records several individuals and ages, with one person making a dedication for a daughter and another making a monument for himself while living, indicative of Imperial burial practices.

EDCS-14500564.text_edition: Tiberius Exsoratuys sic et Ianuariae Ianuari vixit annos XXX et Atilio Victori annorum XXV
EDCS-14500564.translation: Tiberius Exsoratuys thus [lived] and for Ianuaria Ianuari, he lived 30 years, and for Atilius Victor, 25 years.
EDCS-14500564.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary record for Tiberius Exsoratuys, noting the lifespans of Ianuaria Ianuari and Atilius Victor, likely indicating their relationship or shared memorial, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500565.text_edition: Finitus Maximi filius vivus fecit sibi et
EDCS-14500565.translation: Finitus, son of Maximus, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-14500565.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Finitus, son of Maximus, prepared his own tomb during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500566.text_edition: Liberalis et Curena Quinti Marii Iuliani heredes vivi fecerunt sibi et Crescentino filio annorum XI et Iulianae nepoti annorum XV et Salutari filio annorum
EDCS-14500566.translation: Liberalis and Curena, heirs of Quintus Marius Iulianus, made this while alive for themselves and for their son Crescentinus, aged 11 years, and for their granddaughter Iuliana, aged 15 years, and for their son Salutaris, aged [---] years.
EDCS-14500566.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument prepared by Liberalis and Curena, heirs of Quintus Marius Iulianus, for themselves and several deceased family members, including their son Crescentinus, their granddaughter Iuliana, and their son Salutaris.

EDCS-14500567.text_edition: Magena Magemari viva fecit sibi et Primulae Primi filiae annorum V
EDCS-14500567.translation: Magena, daughter of Magemarus, made this for herself while alive, and for Primula, daughter of Primus, aged 5 years.
EDCS-14500567.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Magena, daughter of Magemarus, for herself and for Primula, daughter of Primus, aged five, indicating a shared or family tomb.

EDCS-14500568.text_edition: Marcellinus Aviti vivus fecit sibi et suis Aureliae Bassinae coniugi karissimae! obitae annorum LXXX Aureliae Pothentinae filiae karissimae! obitae annorum XXX
EDCS-14500568.translation: Marcellinus, son of Avitus, made this for himself and his family while alive: for Aurelia Bassina, his dearest wife, deceased at 80 years; for Aurelia Pothentina, his dearest daughter, deceased at 30 years.
EDCS-14500568.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Marcellinus, son of Avitus, for himself and for his deceased wife, Aurelia Bassina, and his deceased daughter, Aurelia Pothentina, during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500569.text_edition: Mariccae Atemeri filiae nnorum LXXX Caius Cassius Civis heres fecit
EDCS-14500569.translation: For Maricca, daughter of Atemarus, aged 80 years. Gaius Cassius Civis, heir, made this.
EDCS-14500569.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Maricca, daughter of Atemarus, aged 80, erected by her heir, Gaius Cassius Civis.

EDCS-14500570.text_edition: Publius Petronius Publi filius Velina Priscus fecit sibi et Atiliae Nigellae coniugi Publio Petronio Severo filio annorum X Petroniae Priscae filiae annorum
EDCS-14500570.translation: Publius Petronius Priscus, son of Publius, of the Velina tribe, made this for himself and for Atilia Nigella, his wife, for Publius Petronius Severus, his son, aged 10 years, and for Petronia Prisca, his daughter, aged [---] years.
EDCS-14500570.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Publius Petronius Priscus for himself, his wife Atilia Nigella, and his deceased children, Publius Petronius Severus and Petronia Prisca.

EDCS-14500571.text_edition: Petronio Publi filio Accepto etmae Almiae  Publi filio Ved annorum LXVI sibi IB Marco Petronio II Censorino fratri rarissimo
EDCS-14500571.translation: To Petronius Acceptus, son of Publius, and Almia, son of Publius, aged 66 years, for himself. [Also] to Marcus Petronius Censorinus, his dearest brother.
EDCS-14500571.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Petronius Acceptus and Almia, both sons of Publius, aged 66, and also commemorating Marcus Petronius Censorinus as a dear brother, likely set up by one of the Petronii mentioned.

EDCS-14500572.text_edition: Petronia Publi filia Maximilla hic quiescit Lucius Petronius Luci libertus RSA  Petronius   Petronius Luci libertus  annorum XXII
EDCS-14500572.translation: Petronia Maximilla, daughter of Publius, rests here. Lucius Petronius, freedman of Lucius [---]. Petronius [---]. Petronius, freedman of Lucius, aged 22 years.
EDCS-14500572.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Petronia Maximilla, daughter of Publius, and also mentions Lucius Petronius, a freedman of Lucius, who may be interred nearby or associated with the monument.

EDCS-14500573.text_edition: Pompeius Agilis vivus fecit sibi et Pompeiae Primigeniae coniugi nnorum XXXV et Pompeiae Spectatae filiae nnorum XVII et Couriae filiae nnorum XVI et Sextiae Suputae annorum XXX et Vitali filio annorum XX
EDCS-14500573.publication: CIL 03, 05262 = ILLPRON 01715 = AIJ 00056 = ILJug-01, 00403 = RIOeN 00003 = RICeleia 00113
EDCS-14500573.translation: Pompeius Agilis made this while alive for himself and for Pompeia Primigenia, his wife, aged 35 years, and for Pompeia Spectata, his daughter, aged 17 years, and for Couria, his daughter, aged 16 years, and for Sextia Suputa, aged 30 years, and for Vitalis, his son, aged 20 years.
EDCS-14500573.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Pompeius Agilis during his lifetime for himself and for several deceased family members, including his wife Pompeia Primigenia, his daughters Pompeia Spectata and Couria, Sextia Suputa, and his son Vitalis.

EDCS-14500574.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pomponio Fortunato nnorum XXX EP CNA EN annorum XL Aelia Candida fecit sibi et coniugi pientissimo
EDCS-14500574.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Pomponius Fortunatus, aged 30 years. Aelia Candida made this for herself and for her most devoted husband, aged 40 years.
EDCS-14500574.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Pomponius Fortunatus by his wife, Aelia Candida, who also prepared the monument for herself and her most devoted husband.

EDCS-14500575.text_edition: Quintus Publicius Lucifer testamento fieri iussit Cominiae Maximinae uxori optimae annorum XXX et sibi et suis Cominio Maximo filio annorum XX
EDCS-14500575.translation: Quintus Publicius Lucifer ordered this to be made by his will for Cominia Maximina, his most excellent wife, aged 30, and for himself and his family, and for Cominius Maximus, his son, aged 20.
EDCS-14500575.summary: This inscription records a tomb or monument commissioned by Quintus Publicius Lucifer through his will for his wife, son, and himself, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500576.text_edition: Quartus Sirae vivus fecit sibi et Licoviae Ingenuae uxori annorum L et Leucimarae filiae annorum X Finito filio annorum VI Licovio Sexto annorum XX Ausco Muscionis filia annorum XXX Dubnae filiae annorum XXXX Marcus Licovius Barus annorum Marcus Licovius Boniatus nnorum L Vetulla Bucia Urbani filia annorum L Marcus Licovius Ursus annorum XX
EDCS-14500576.publication: CIL 03, 05265 (p 1830, 2285) = ILLPRON 01597 = AIJ 00057 = Schober 00223 = Mander 00493 = RICeleia 00143
EDCS-14500576.translation: Quartus, son of Sira, while living, made this for himself and for Licovia Ingenua, his wife, aged 50, and for Leucimara, his daughter, aged 10, for Finitus, his son, aged 6, for Licovius Sextus, aged 20, for Ausco, daughter of Muscio, aged 30, for Dubna, his daughter, aged 40, Marcus Licovius Barus, aged [blank], Marcus Licovius Boniatus, aged 50, Vetulla Bucia, daughter of Urbanus, aged 50, Marcus Licovius Ursus, aged 20.
EDCS-14500576.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Quartus, son of Sira, while still living, for himself and a large number of family members including his wife, children, and other relatives, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500577.text_edition: Respectus Titi Postumi Severi et Titus Postumius Titi libertus Provincialis vivi fecerunt sibi et Domestico et Ionicae parentibus
EDCS-14500577.translation: Respectus, slave of Titus Postumius Severus, and Titus Postumius Provincialis, freedman of Titus, while living, made this for themselves and for their parents, Domesticus and Ionica.
EDCS-14500577.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Respectus, a slave, and Titus Postumius Provincialis, a freedman, for themselves and their parents, Domesticus and Ionica, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500578.text_edition: Dis Manibus Respectus Vari Luciferi vivus fecit sibi et Fortunatae uxori carissimae annorum XXX et Respectae filiae annorum VI et Ursinae filiae annorum XXII
EDCS-14500578.translation: To the Divine Manes. Respectus, son of Varius Lucifer, while living, made this for himself and for Fortunata, his dearest wife, aged 30, and for Respecta, his daughter, aged 6, and for Ursina, his daughter, aged 22.
EDCS-14500578.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Respectus, son of Varius Lucifer, while still alive, for himself, his wife, and two daughters, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500579.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sabina Quarti viva fecit sibi et Sabino filio pientissimo annorum XXX et posuit Litugenae filiae annorum XL
EDCS-14500579.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sabina, daughter of Quartus, while living, made this for herself and for Sabinus, her most devoted son, aged 30, and placed it for Litugena, her daughter, aged 40.
EDCS-14500579.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Sabina, daughter of Quartus, while still alive, for herself, her devoted son, and her daughter, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500580.text_edition: arsi Tiberi filio nnorum LII Secundus Marcelli filius vivus fecit
EDCS-14500580.translation: [made this for ---] Arsus, son of Tiberius, aged 52. Secundus, son of Marcellus, while living, made this.
EDCS-14500580.summary: This fragmented inscription records a funerary monument made by Secundus, son of Marcellus, while living, likely for Arsus, son of Tiberius, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500581.text_edition: Dis Manibus Secundinus Secundi et Attia Ursula vivi fecerunt sibi et Secundo filio annorum VII
EDCS-14500581.translation: To the Divine Manes. Secundinus, son of Secundus, and Attia Ursula, while living, made this for themselves and for Secundus, their son, aged 7.
EDCS-14500581.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Secundinus and Attia Ursula, while alive, for themselves and their young son Secundus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500582.text_edition: Secundus Magimari filius vivus fecit sibi et suis et Magimaro Auctomari et Cupitae Atevorti filiae parentibus et Exorato nepoti annorum II
EDCS-14500582.translation: Secundus, son of Magimarus, while living, made this for himself and his family, and for Magimarus Auctomarus and Cupita, daughter of Atevortus, his parents, and for Exoratus, his grandson, aged 2.
EDCS-14500582.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Secundus, son of Magimarus, while living, for himself, his family, his parents, and his young grandson, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500584.text_edition: Belliciani vivus fecit sibi et Statiliae Cervae coniugi optimae obitae annorum XL Tertullinae filiae annorum XXX
EDCS-14500584.translation: Bellicianus, while living, made this for himself and for Statilia Cerva, his most excellent wife, deceased at the age of 40, and for Tertullina, his daughter, aged 30.
EDCS-14500584.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Bellicianus, while alive, for himself and his deceased wife and daughter, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500585.text_edition: Lucius Trosius Propincus et Iantulla coniunx vivi fecerunt sibi et Iuveni filio annorum VIII
EDCS-14500585.translation: Lucius Trosius Propincus and his wife Iantulla, while living, made this for themselves and for Iuvenis, their son, aged 8.
EDCS-14500585.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Lucius Trosius Propincus and his wife Iantulla, while living, for themselves and their young son Iuvenis, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500586.text_edition: Titus Vedius Atressus vivus fecit sibi et Corneliae Publi libertae Valentinae coniugi annorum XXX et Secundinae filiae nnorum XXX
EDCS-14500586.translation: Titus Vedius Atressus made this in his lifetime for himself and for Cornelia Valentina, freedwoman of Publius, his wife, who lived for 30 years, and for Secundina, his daughter, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-14500586.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Titus Vedius Atressus in his lifetime for himself, his wife Cornelia Valentina, and his daughter Secundina, dating to the Roman Imperial period based on typical onomastics and formulation.

EDCS-14500587.translation: Gaius Vedius Valen[tinus?], son of Publius.
EDCS-14500587.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Gaius Vedius, son of Publius, potentially part of a funerary monument or dedication, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500592.text_edition: Caius Rufius Cai libertus Symphonus et Rufia Primula et
EDCS-14500592.translation: Gaius Rufius Symphonus, freedman of Gaius, and Rufia Primula, and [...].
EDCS-14500592.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely part of a funerary monument, set up by or for Gaius Rufius Symphonus, a freedman, and Rufia Primula, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500593.text_edition: Celeianorum liberto  annorum LX et llae uxori parentibus et lo filio annorum XXV
EDCS-14500593.translation: [To the] freedman of the Celeiani, 60 years old, and to [---]lla his wife, parents, and to [---]lo his son, 25 years old.
EDCS-14500593.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely erected by a family in honor of a freedman of the Celeiani and his wife, as well as their son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500594.text_edition: Veriniae Aureliae coniugi carissimae stolatae feminae natione Dardana quae vixit annis XXXII Aurelius Valentinus vir egregius maritus vivus posuit
EDCS-14500594.translation: To Verinia Aurelia, most beloved wife, a 'stolatus' woman of Dardanian origin, who lived for 32 years. Aurelius Valentinus, an excellent man, her husband, placed this in his lifetime.
EDCS-14500594.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelius Valentinus for his most beloved wife, Verinia Aurelia, a woman of Dardanian origin, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500596.text_edition: Severa Apronis filia viva fecit sibi et Boniatae filiae annorum XX Surio Genialis filius annorum L et Briga Aviti filia annorum XL et Ianuarius Genialis et Saturia coniux Severus Surio annorum LX
EDCS-14500596.translation: Severa, daughter of Apro, made this in her lifetime for herself and for Boniata, her daughter, 20 years old. Surius, son of Genialis, 50 years old, and Briga, daughter of Avitus, 40 years old, and Ianuarius Genialis, and Saturia his wife. Severus, [son of] Surius, 60 years old.
EDCS-14500596.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Severa for herself and her daughter, with additional individuals mentioned, likely indicating a family burial plot, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500597.text_edition: us Atto filio annorum XX et Nonnae Tatuci filiae uxori annorum C Successus filius annorum L
EDCS-14500597.translation: [To] us Atto, son, 20 years old, and to Nonna, daughter of Tatucus, wife, 100 years old. Successus, son, 50 years old.
EDCS-14500597.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, possibly for a family including Atto, Nonna, and Successus, indicating multiple generations, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500598.text_edition: Trogimarus Maeni filius annorum LX Quadratus Trogimari filius annorum LXX et Secundinus annorum XX Sabianus Trogimara Cai Severa Quarti  Firmus
EDCS-14500598.translation: Trogimarus, son of Maenius, 60 years old. Quadratus, son of Trogimarus, 70 years old, and Secundinus, 20 years old. Sabianus Trogimara Cai Severa Quarti Firmus.
EDCS-14500598.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription commemorating Trogimarus and his family, including Quadratus and Secundinus, with additional names that suggest a larger family group, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500600.text_edition: Messor Caturei annorum L et Dumba Silvani filia annorum XXXV heres faciendum curavit Loucita Messori filia annorum XXX Restumarus filius annorum XIV Armianus
EDCS-14500600.translation: Messor, [son] of Catureius, 50 years old, and Dumba, daughter of Silvanus, 35 years old. The heir, Loucita, daughter of Messor, 30 years old, ensured this was made. Restumarus, son, 14 years old. Armianus.
EDCS-14500600.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Messor and Dumba, with their heir Loucita taking care of its creation, and other family members mentioned, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500601.text_edition: Silvanus Iantumari filius et Successa coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et suis Iunia filia annorum X
EDCS-14500601.translation: Silvanus, son of Iantumara, and Successa his wife, made this in their lifetime for themselves and their family. Iunia, their daughter, 10 years old.
EDCS-14500601.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Silvanus and his wife Successa in their lifetime for themselves and their family, including their daughter Iunia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500602.text_edition: Ingenuus Assedomari f(ilius) annorum LII Quinta Camuinui annorum
EDCS-14500602.translation: Ingenuus, son of Assedomarus, 52 years old. Quinta Camuinus, [---] years old.
EDCS-14500602.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names and ages of Ingenuus and Quinta Camuinus.

EDCS-14500608.text_edition: Iustio Iusto Tutina anus obitus annorum LX IFR annorum LX Iustius Tutorinus annorum XL
EDCS-14500608.translation: Iustio Iustus, Tutina the old woman, died at 60 years old. IFR, 60 years old. Iustius Tutorinus, 40 years old.
EDCS-14500608.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Iustio Iustus, Tutina, IFR, and Iustius Tutorinus, noting their ages at death.

EDCS-14500609.text_edition: AI Masculus AI Avita C PIA
EDCS-14500609.translation: AI Masculus, AI Avita, C. PIA.
EDCS-14500609.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely mentioning multiple individuals, possibly family members or colleagues.

EDCS-14500612.text_edition: Marti Augusto pro salute Quinti Mai Ce
EDCS-14500612.translation: To Mars Augustus, for the well-being of Quintus Maius Ce[---].
EDCS-14500612.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars Augustus for the health and well-being of Quintus Maius.

EDCS-14500613.text_edition: o Augusto  Marcus Messius  IIvir iure dicundo pro salute Spectatae  eorum  et Sequ
EDCS-14500613.translation: To Augustus. Marcus Messius, duumvir with judicial power, for the well-being of Spectata, their [---], and Sequ[---].
EDCS-14500613.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Augustus by Marcus Messius, a duumvir, for the well-being of Spectata and other named individuals.

EDCS-14500614.text_edition: ia VercET Deusa liberta arcam I fecit patronae suae
EDCS-14500614.translation: Vercet Deusa, a freedwoman, made this ark for her patroness.
EDCS-14500614.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that the freedwoman Vercet Deusa made an arca (a type of funerary container or chest) for her patroness.

EDCS-14500615.text_edition: Honoratiano XVIII et Honoratae nepti II O Honoratiano
EDCS-14500615.translation: To Honoratianus, 18 years old, and Honorata, his niece, 2 years old. Oh Honoratianus!
EDCS-14500615.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Honoratianus and his niece Honorata, noting their young ages at death.

EDCS-14500616.text_edition: IL Honorat-- annorum  coniux viv-- fec--- sibi et Conus filius
EDCS-14500616.translation: IL Honorat[ianus], [---] years old. His living wife made [this] for herself and their son Conus.
EDCS-14500616.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a wife to her husband Honoratianus and their son Conus, also for herself.

EDCS-14500618.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Marti Augusto et ceteris dis deabusque omnibus inmortalibus Ulpius Martinus pro se suisque liberis ex voto posuit votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500618.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mars Augustus and all other immortal gods and goddesses, Ulpius Martinus, for himself and his children, placed this vow and willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500618.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Mars Augustus, and all other immortal gods and goddesses by Ulpius Martinus, fulfilling a vow for himself and his children.

EDCS-14500619.text_edition: Cai Iuli Attiani negotiatoris veteranorum  R
EDCS-14500619.translation: Of Gaius Iulius Attianus, merchant of veterans, R[---].
EDCS-14500619.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Gaius Iulius Attianus as a merchant of veterans.

EDCS-14500620.text_edition: Caius Iustius Proculus aedilis Fl(aviae) Solvae Orionia Sexti filia Maxima
EDCS-14500620.translation: Gaius Iustius Proculus, aedile of Flavia Solva. Orionia Maxima, daughter of Sextus.
EDCS-14500620.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or record, possibly of a public servant and a woman, suggesting a municipal context in Flavia Solva.

EDCS-14500621.text_edition: Mercurio sacrum Primigenius votum solvit libens merito Titi Atti libertus
EDCS-14500621.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Primigenius, freedman of Titus Attius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500621.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mercury by Primigenius, a freedman of Titus Attius, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500622.text_edition: Sennus Sacri filius hic situs est et Cona Modesti filia coniugi filii et filiae vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500622.translation: Sennus, son of Sacer, is buried here, and Cona, daughter of Modestus. His wife, children, and daughters made this during their lifetime.
EDCS-14500622.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Sennus and Cona, erected by his wife and children while still alive, typical of Roman tomb monuments.

EDCS-14500623.text_edition: Eponae Augustae sacrum Volusius Equester votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500623.translation: Sacred to Epona Augusta. Volusius Equester willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500623.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Epona Augusta by Volusius Equester, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500624.text_edition: Firmus Sabini filius vivus fecit sibi et
EDCS-14500624.translation: Firmus, son of Sabinus, made this for himself during his lifetime and...
EDCS-14500624.summary: This is a funerary inscription, made by Firmus for himself during his lifetime, anticipating his death and potentially that of others.

EDCS-14500625.text_edition: Nutricibus Augustae sacrum pro salute  Valeriae Marcellae pater votum solvit
EDCS-14500625.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Nurses, for the health of Valeria Marcella. Her father fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500625.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Augustan Nurses by a father for the health of his daughter, Valeria Marcella, indicating a prayer for healing or well-being.

EDCS-14500626.text_edition: Secundinus qui Spittuli et Aurelia Gemella vivi fecerunt sibi et Respecto filio annorum XVI
EDCS-14500626.translation: Secundinus, who is [also known as] Spittulus, and Aurelia Gemella, made this during their lifetimes for themselves and for their son Respectus, 16 years old.
EDCS-14500626.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Secundinus and Aurelia Gemella for themselves and their deceased son Respectus, who was 16 years old at the time of his death.

EDCS-14500627.text_edition: Finitus Titionis filius vivus fecit sibi et
EDCS-14500627.translation: Finitus, son of Titio, made this for himself during his lifetime and...
EDCS-14500627.summary: This is a funerary inscription, made by Finitus for himself while still alive, anticipating his future burial and possibly that of others.

EDCS-14500628.text_edition: Marcus Porcius Verus procurator Augusti  me posuit
EDCS-14500628.translation: Marcus Porcius Verus, procurator of Augustus, placed me.
EDCS-14500628.summary: This is an inscription stating that Marcus Porcius Verus, an Augustan procurator, erected the monument on which the text is found, likely a boundary stone, statue base, or public dedication.

EDCS-14500629.text_edition: B Me vivus fecit sibi et  filio annorum X Valeria  Restituta
EDCS-14500629.translation: B. He made me during his lifetime for himself and for his son, 10 years old. Valeria Restituta.
EDCS-14500629.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a man and his 10-year-old son, made by the father during his lifetime, with Valeria Restituta possibly being another family member or dedicant.

EDCS-14500630.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Attius Vitalis et Senius Iuvenis curam agentes civitasque   Iulius Cogitatus et Cunaito Titiani inius Ursinus et Polius Ianuarius estus et Surillio Appiani tinus et Priscianus Salvi  Vibianus et Ursinus Vitalis  Aquilinus et Caesernius Iulianus i Sammio et Hermadio ii Valentinus Inv et Iulius Primitivus Iulii Ursinus Secundi et Mogio Verani  Uranius Domesticus et Aurelius Martius I Appuleius Ursus et Aurelius Secundinus II Malius Decianus et
EDCS-14500630.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Attius Vitalis and Senius Iuvenis, acting as supervisors, and the community. Iulius Cogitatus and Cunaito Titiani, Iunius Ursinus and Polius Ianuarius, [---]estus and Surillio Appiani, [---]tinus and Priscianus Salvi, Vibianus and Ursinus Vitalis, Aquilinus and Caesernius Iulianus, I Sammio and Hermadio, II Valentinus Inv[---] and Iulius Primitivus, Iulius Ursinus Secundus and Mogio Verani, Uranius Domesticus and Aurelius Martius, I Appuleius Ursus and Aurelius Secundinus, II Malius Decianus and [---]
EDCS-14500630.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a long list of individuals and the community, likely recording an act of devotion or public work overseen by Attius Vitalis and Senius Iuvenis, possibly dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500631.text_edition: Marti Latobio Marmogio Toutati Sinati Mogetio Caius Valerius Valerinus ex voto
EDCS-14500631.translation: To Mars Latobius Marmogius Toutates Sinates Mogetius, Gaius Valerius Valerinus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-14500631.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Gaius Valerius Valerinus to a group of Celtic deities associated with Mars, likely a local cult in the Alpine region.

EDCS-14500632.text_edition: Marti Latobio Quintus Morsius Quinti filius Titianus votum solvit
EDCS-14500632.translation: To Mars Latobius, Quintus Morsius Titianus, son of Quintus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500632.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Quintus Morsius Titianus to the Celtic god Mars Latobius, typical of religious dedications in the Roman provinces.

EDCS-14500633.translation: Aurelius Diso, heir, placed this vow.
EDCS-14500633.summary: This is a votive inscription made by Aurelius Diso, presumably on behalf of a deceased person or as an heir fulfilling an obligation, likely a simple religious dedication.

EDCS-14500634.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo proconsuli patri patriae tribunicia potestate Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filio Marcus Tullius Maximus ob honorem decurionatus
EDCS-14500634.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, proconsul, father of his country, with tribunician power, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Marcus Tullius Maximus [erected this] for the honor of the decurionatus.
EDCS-14500634.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius) by Marcus Tullius Maximus to celebrate his induction into the decurion status, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500635.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo proconsuli patri patriae tribunicia potestate Parthico Britannico maximo Gaius Castius Avitus decurio et IIvir Solvae ob honorem decurionatus ita ut promisit plene obtulit
EDCS-14500635.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, proconsul, father of his country, with tribunician power, Parthicus Britannicus Maximus, Gaius Castius Avitus, decurion and duovir of Solva, fully offered this as he promised, for the honor of the decurionatus.
EDCS-14500635.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Emperor Caracalla by Gaius Castius Avitus, a decurion and duovir of the city of Solva, in fulfillment of a promise made upon receiving the honor of decurion, dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500636.translation: To the deified Jovius Maximianus, the order of the Solvensians.
EDCS-14500636.summary: This is a posthumous dedication by the city council (ordo) of Solva to the deified Emperor Maximian, likely after his death and deification, probably in the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-14500637.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino maximo beatissimo ac supra omnes retro principes piissimo et victoriosissimo semper Augusto bono rei publicae nato Fabius Claudius vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Norici mediterranei devotus numini maiestatique eius semper   TOIS
EDCS-14500637.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, the greatest, most blessed, and above all previous emperors, most pious and victorious, always Augustus, born for the good of the Republic, Fabius Claudius, a most perfect man, president of the province of Noricum Mediterraneum, ever devoted to his divine power and majesty [---]
EDCS-14500637.summary: This inscription is a laudatory dedication to Emperor Constantine the Great, praising his virtues and titles, erected by Fabius Claudius, the governor of Noricum Mediterraneum, and dates to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-14500638.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari [Marco Aur][elio Antonino] Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo patri patriae tribunicia potestate consuli proconsuli ordo Solvensium
EDCS-14500638.translation: To the Imperator Caesar [Marcus Aur]elius Antoninus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, father of his country, with tribunician power, consul, proconsul, the order of the Solvensians.
EDCS-14500638.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the city council (ordo) of Solva to Emperor Caracalla, highlighting his numerous titles and virtues, and dates to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500639.text_edition: Marco Gavio Maximo praefecto praetorio Lucius Cammius Secundinus primus pilus praefectus legionis X geminae procurator Augusti amico
EDCS-14500639.translation: To Marcus Gavius Maximus, praetorian prefect, Lucius Cammius Secundinus, primus pilus, prefect of the Tenth Legion Gemina, procurator of Augustus, to his friend.
EDCS-14500639.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Lucius Cammius Secundinus, a high-ranking military officer and imperial procurator, to Marcus Gavius Maximus, a praetorian prefect, acknowledging their friendship, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500640.text_edition: Aelio Leucamulo veterano annorum LXX et Iuliae Iustinae filiae annorum XXVII  Caius Kanulanius Eumitus vivus fecit sibi et Nansiniae Verecundae coniugi et Caio Kanulanio Nepoti filio militi alae III Thracum annorum XXV stipendiorum VI loco et impensa Anni Festi
EDCS-14500640.translation: To Aelius Leucamulus, veteran, aged 70, and to Julia Justina, daughter, aged 27. Gaius Kanulanius Eumitus, while living, made this for himself and for Nansinia Verecunda, his wife, and for Gaius Kanulanius Nepos, his son, a soldier of the 3rd Thracian cavalry unit, aged 25, with 6 years of service, at the expense and in the place provided by Annius Festus.
EDCS-14500640.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a burial plot prepared by Gaius Kanulanius Eumitus for himself, his wife, his veteran son, and his daughter and son-in-law, with the plot arranged by Annius Festus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500641.text_edition: Marco Annio annorum LX Annia Mar coniugi  et Marco Annio Marcello filio annorum XXV et Marco Annio Marcelliano |(centurioni) cohortis I Asturum annorum XXX
EDCS-14500641.translation: To Marcus Annius, aged 60. Annia Mar, to her husband, and to Marcus Annius Marcellus, son, aged 25, and to Marcus Annius Marcellianus, centurion of the 1st cohort of Astures, aged 30.
EDCS-14500641.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Annia Mar to her husband Marcus Annius and their two sons, Marcus Annius Marcellus and Marcus Annius Marcellianus, a centurion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500642.text_edition: annorum XXV et Marcellino filio annorum XVI
EDCS-14500642.translation: aged 25, and to Marcellinus, son, aged 16.
EDCS-14500642.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning two individuals, one aged 25 and Marcellinus, a son, aged 16, lacking the dedicant or primary recipient, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500643.text_edition: Tito Attio Cai filio Tutori praefecto alae I Batavorum miliariae praefecto alae I Tungrorum Frontonianae tribuno militum legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis praefecto cohortis I Baetasiorum civium Romanorum decurioni Flavia Solva Sedatius Quietus
EDCS-14500643.translation: To Titus Attius, son of Gaius, Tutor, prefect of the 1st Batavian cavalry unit, one thousand strong, prefect of the 1st Tungrian cavalry unit Frontoniana, military tribune of the Legion II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, prefect of the 1st cohort of Baetasii, Roman citizens, decurion of Flavia Solva. Sedatius Quietus [made this].
EDCS-14500643.summary: This is a career inscription (cursus honorum) for Titus Attius Tutor, listing his extensive military and municipal appointments, dedicated by Sedatius Quietus, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500645.text_edition: Quinto Carminio Latino et Carminio Cupito optioni legionis I Minerviae Catulla avunculo et fratri faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500645.translation: To Quintus Carminius Latinus and to Carminius Cupitus, optiō of the Legion I Minervia. Catulla arranged for this to be made for her uncle and brother.
EDCS-14500645.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Catulla for her uncle, Quintus Carminius Latinus, and her brother, Carminius Cupitus, an optiō of Legion I Minervia, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500646.text_edition: Titus Cassius Secundus |(centurio) legionis XV Apollinaris donis donatus corona murali phaleris torquibus armillis testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Marci Saxi Primi heres faciendum curavit locus sepulturae datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-14500646.translation: Titus Cassius Secundus, centurion of the Legion XV Apollinaris, honored with gifts: a mural crown, phalerae, torques, armlets, ordered this to be made by his will, under the judgment of Marcus Saxius Primus.
