His heir arranged for it to be made. The burial place was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-14500646.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Cassius Secundus, a decorated centurion, detailing that his tomb was built posthumously according to his will and at the direction of his heir, on land granted by the decurions, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500647.text_edition: Caio Ciamillio Prisco veterano annorum LXXX et Vindillae Crielonis filiae coniugi annorum LX
EDCS-14500647.translation: To Gaius Ciamillius Priscus, veteran, aged 80, and to Vindilla, daughter of Crielon, his wife, aged 60.
EDCS-14500647.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Ciamillius Priscus, a veteran, and his wife Vindilla, dedicated by an unnamed party, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500648.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Tiberi filio Secundino IIviro iure dicundo Artoriae Cai filiae Florae Marco Claudio Tiberi filio Lucullo |(centurioni) legionis XIIII |(centurioni) legionis XIII Tiberio Claudio Tiberi filio Secundo Valeria Crispa fecit
EDCS-14500648.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Secundinus, son of Tiberius, duumvir with judicial power; to Artoria Flora, daughter of Gaius; to Marcus Claudius Lucullus, son of Tiberius, centurion of Legion XIV, centurion of Legion XIII; to Tiberius Claudius Secundus, son of Tiberius. Valeria Crispa made this.
EDCS-14500648.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription by Valeria Crispa, commemorating several members of the Claudian family, including a duumvir and two centurions, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500649.text_edition: Caio Donnio Attico veterano cohortis V praetoriae @(obito) annorum LXI Cerviae Ingenuae coniugi vivae fili fecerunt
EDCS-14500649.translation: To Gaius Donnius Atticus, veteran of the 5th praetorian cohort, who died at the age of 61. His sons made this for Cervia Ingenua, his living wife.
EDCS-14500649.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by the sons of Gaius Donnius Atticus, a veteran, for their deceased father and their living mother, Cervia Ingenua, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500650.text_edition: Caius Iunius Provincialis vivus fecit sibi et Crispinae Finiti filiae uxori optimae et Quintinio Catullo filio optimo militi cohortis X urbanae p r annorum  et Quintiniae Catullae annorum?   VEN
EDCS-14500650.translation: Gaius Iunius Provincialis, while living, made this for himself and for Crispina, daughter of Finitus, his excellent wife, and for Quintinius Catullus, his excellent son, a soldier of the 10th urban cohort (p r), aged [age missing], and for Quintinia Catulla, aged [age missing]. VEN [unclear].
EDCS-14500650.summary: This is a funerary inscription prepared by Gaius Iunius Provincialis during his lifetime for himself, his wife, Crispina, and his two children, Quintinius Catullus, a soldier, and Quintinia Catulla, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-14500651.text_edition: Macerio  evocato legionis et Maceriae  et Macerio O
EDCS-14500651.translation: To Macerius, re-enlisted veteran of the legion, and to Maceria, and to Macerius the younger.
EDCS-14500651.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Macerius, a re-enlisted legionary veteran, and two family members, Maceria and Macerius the younger.

EDCS-14500652.text_edition: e coniugi Memmio o filio alae I Augustae Thracum annorum XXVI
EDCS-14500652.translation: [To his/her] spouse Memmius, and to their son, of the First Augustan Thracian Wing, 26 years old.
EDCS-14500652.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Memmius, a spouse, and their son, a cavalryman of the First Augustan Thracian Wing who died at age 26.

EDCS-14500655.text_edition: Claudius Crispinus et Censorina Cetti filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Acceptiano annorum XVIII et Crispino Crispini filio aedili Flavia Solva annorum XXV
EDCS-14500655.translation: Claudius Crispinus and Censorina, daughter of Cettus, made this while living for themselves and for Acceptianus, 18 years old, and for Crispinus, son of Crispinus, aedile of Flavia Solva, 25 years old.
EDCS-14500655.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Claudius Crispinus and Censorina for themselves and their two sons, Acceptianus and Crispinus, the latter an aedile of Flavia Solva, likely during the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500656.text_edition: Lucius Gautius Finitus aedilis Solvae vivus fecit sibi et Firmidiae Mussae uxori optimae annorum L et Finitae filiae annorum V
EDCS-14500656.translation: Lucius Gautius Finitus, aedile of Solva, made this while living for himself and for his excellent wife Firmidia Mussa, 50 years old, and for his daughter Finita, 5 years old.
EDCS-14500656.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Lucius Gautius Finitus, an aedile of Solva, for himself, his wife Firmidia Mussa, and his daughter Finita, probably from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500657.text_edition: Iulius Sabinus aedilis Solvae Iuliae Secundinae uxori optimae et sibi et Iulio Sabiniano filio ---? an(norum) XXX
EDCS-14500657.translation: Iulius Sabinus, aedile of Solva, [made this] for his excellent wife Iulia Secundina, and for himself, and for his son Iulius Sabinianus, [who died at] 30 years old.
EDCS-14500657.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Iulius Sabinus, an aedile of Solva, to his wife Iulia Secundina, himself, and their son Iulius Sabinianus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500659.text_edition: Pollybio sic Solvensium et Velleciae matri
EDCS-14500659.translation: To Pollybius, thus of Solva, and to Vellecia, mother.
EDCS-14500659.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Pollybius, likely a citizen of Solva, and his mother Vellecia.

EDCS-14500662.text_edition: Adiatullus Vepotali filius Tatucae Vervici filiae uxori et Rumae filiae annorum XXV et filiis sibi et suis fecit
EDCS-14500662.translation: Adiatullus, son of Vepotalus, made this for Tatuca, daughter of Vervicus, his wife, and for his daughter Ruma, 25 years old, and for his sons, for himself and his family.
EDCS-14500662.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Adiatullus, son of Vepotalus, for his wife Tatuca, daughter Ruma, his sons, and for himself and his family.

EDCS-14500663.text_edition: Marco Annio Marci liberto Decumo annorum LXX Sacra Sauronis filia coniugi et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-14500663.translation: Sacra, daughter of Sauron, made this while living for her husband Marcus Annius Decumus, freedman of Marcus, 70 years old, and for herself.
EDCS-14500663.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Sacra, daughter of Sauron, while still alive, for her husband Marcus Annius Decumus, a freedman, and for herself, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500665.text_edition: Sextus Baebius Pudens vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Festae coniugi nnorum XXV et Iuliae Verecundae matri
EDCS-14500665.translation: Sextus Baebius Pudens made this while living for himself and for Iulia Festa, his wife, 25 years old, and for Iulia Verecunda, his mother.
EDCS-14500665.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Sextus Baebius Pudens for himself, his wife Iulia Festa, and his mother Iulia Verecunda.

EDCS-14500666.text_edition: Bonioni Atti filio annorum LXXV et Baebiae Suadullae
EDCS-14500666.translation: To Bonionius, son of Attus, 75 years old, and to Baebia Suadulla.
EDCS-14500666.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Bonionius, son of Attus, who died at age 75, and to Baebia Suadulla.

EDCS-14500667.text_edition: Lucius Cassius Togio vivus fecit sibi et Bisugnatae defunctae nnorum LXV
EDCS-14500667.translation: Lucius Cassius Togio, alive, made this for himself and for Bisugnata, deceased, aged 65 years.
EDCS-14500667.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Cassius Togio for himself and his deceased wife Bisugnata, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500668.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cassius Profuturus vivus fecit sibi et Iucundae coniugi @(obitae) annorum XV
EDCS-14500668.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cassius Profuturus, alive, made this for himself and for Iucunda, his wife, who died at 15 years of age.
EDCS-14500668.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, made by Cassius Profuturus for himself and his deceased wife Iucunda, common in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500669.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Claudius Restutus et Cassia Quarta coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Cassius! Ingenuus sic et Cassius! Restutianus sic filiis
EDCS-14500669.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudius Restutus and his wife Cassia Quarta, alive, made this for themselves and for their sons, Cassius Ingenuus and Cassius Restutianus.
EDCS-14500669.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Claudius Restutus and Cassia Quarta for themselves and their two sons, Cassius Ingenuus and Cassius Restutianus.

EDCS-14500670.text_edition: Claudiae Tiberi filiae Iucundae annorum XXIII
EDCS-14500670.translation: To Claudia Iucunda, daughter of Tiberius, aged 23 years.
EDCS-14500670.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Iucunda, daughter of Tiberius, who died at 23 years old, indicating a common Roman tombstone dedication.

EDCS-14500672.text_edition: EN CN II F Dubnae coniugi optimae annorum XL Candius frater  fecit
EDCS-14500672.translation: Candius, brother, made this for his excellent wife Dubna, aged 40 years.
EDCS-14500672.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Candius for his wife Dubna, likely on a tombstone, and includes an unclear initial dedication.

EDCS-14500673.text_edition: Finito Lappi annorum LX et Salviae Iantumari filiae coniugi optimae filii fecerunt
EDCS-14500673.translation: The sons made this for Finitus Lappi, aged 60 years, and for Salvia Iantumari, his excellent wife.
EDCS-14500673.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by children for their parents, Finitus Lappi and Salvia Iantumari, common for family tombs.

EDCS-14500674.text_edition: Dis Manibus  securitati perpetuae Iulius Secundinus vivus fecit sibi et Prisciae Albinae coniugi et Romaniae Catullinae coniugi annorum XXV et Iuliae Secundinae sorori et Auraelio Secundino filio
EDCS-14500674.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for eternal security. Iulius Secundinus, alive, made this for himself and for Priscia Albina, his wife, and for Romania Catullina, his wife, aged 25 years, and for Iulia Secundina, his sister, and for Aurelius Secundinus, his son.
EDCS-14500674.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Iulius Secundinus for himself and multiple family members, including two wives, a sister, and a son, dedicated to the Manes for eternal security.

EDCS-14500675.text_edition: Marcus Iunius Marci libertus Cerialis et Iunia Aphrodisia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Iuniae Ingenuae filiae annorum III et M(arco) Iunio S
EDCS-14500675.translation: Marcus Iunius Cerialis, freedman of Marcus, and Iunia Aphrodisia, his wife, alive, made this for themselves and for Iunia Ingenua, their daughter, aged 3 years, and for Marcus Iunius S[---].
EDCS-14500675.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Marcus Iunius Cerialis and Iunia Aphrodisia for themselves and their young daughter, Iunia Ingenua, and another family member, Marcus Iunius S[---].

EDCS-14500677.text_edition: Publius Laelius Publi libertus Heracla vivus fecit sibi et Samudae Musonis filiae coniugi pientissimae annorum LV et Laeliae Publi filiae Secundinae annorum XXX et Laeliae Bononiae nepti annorum
EDCS-14500677.translation: Publius Laelius Heracla, freedman of Publius, alive, made this for himself and for Samuda, daughter of Muso, his most devoted wife, aged 55 years, and for Laelia Secundina, daughter of Publius, aged 30 years, and for Laelia Bononia, his granddaughter, aged [---] years.
EDCS-14500677.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Publius Laelius Heracla for himself and several female family members, including his wife, daughter, and granddaughter, common in Roman family burials.

EDCS-14500679.text_edition: Marcus Laetilius Laetiliae libertus Paccius vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Successae coniugi optimae et Scipioni filio annorum VI et Iustae filiae annorum VI et Iustinae filiae annorum VI et Laetilio Iuveni filio annorum XXX
EDCS-14500679.translation: Marcus Laetilius Paccius, freedman of Laetilia, alive, made this for himself and for Iulia Successa, his excellent wife, and for Scipio, his son, aged 6 years, and for Iusta, his daughter, aged 6 years, and for Iustina, his daughter, aged 6 years, and for Laetilius Iuvenis, his son, aged 30 years.
EDCS-14500679.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Marcus Laetilius Paccius for himself and multiple family members, including his wife and several children, indicating a family tomb dedication.

EDCS-14500680.text_edition: Lucconi Suri filio annorum LXX et Dubnae Lucconis filio annorum XL etani filiae coniugi
EDCS-14500680.translation: To Luccon, son of Surus, aged 70, and to Dubna, daughter of Luccon, aged 40, and to [---]ani, daughter, wife.
EDCS-14500680.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deceased Luccon and Dubna, likely made by a surviving family member, potentially from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500681.text_edition: filio annorum XX et Mascillioni filio annorum XVAN et Coviomaro
EDCS-14500681.translation: To [---] son, aged 20, and to Mascillio, son, aged 15, and to Coviomarus.
EDCS-14500681.summary: This fragment of a funerary inscription records the deceased, including two sons, and was likely part of a larger family monument, probably from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500682.text_edition: Masculus Deusi filius vivus fecit sibi et Pettuni Secundi filiae coniugi annorum LX
EDCS-14500682.translation: Masculus, son of Deusis, while alive, made this for himself and for Pettuni, daughter of Secundus, his wife, aged 60.
EDCS-14500682.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Masculus for himself and his wife Pettuni during his lifetime, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500684.text_edition: Lucio Memmio Luci liberto Liberali et Sammae coniugi patronus fecit
EDCS-14500684.translation: To Lucius Memmius Liberalis, freedman of Lucius, and to Samma, his wife, his patron made this.
EDCS-14500684.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a patron for his freedman, Lucius Memmius Liberalis, and the freedman's wife Samma, likely from the 1st century BC to 1st century AD.

EDCS-14500685.text_edition: Caio Mimisio Cai liberto Ianuario Caio Mimisio Draconiae annorum X Mosicu Adsedili fecit
EDCS-14500685.translation: Mosicus Adsedilis made this for Gaius Mimisius Ianuarius, freedman of Gaius, and for Gaius Mimisius Draconia, aged 10.
EDCS-14500685.summary: This is a funerary inscription created by Mosicus Adsedilis for Gaius Mimisius Ianuarius, a freedman, and for Gaius Mimisius Draconia, a child, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500686.text_edition: Marci filius Fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Turboniae Nigrinae uxori et Finitae filiae defunctae annis III o Rufi Valenti ann-  mensibus II diebus IIio Rufi filio  annis VII mensibus III diebus XVI
EDCS-14500686.translation: Fidelis, son of Marcus, while alive, made this for himself and for Turbonia Nigrina, his wife, and for Finita, his deceased daughter, aged 3 years, 2 months, 2 days; and for Rufius Valens, his son, aged 7 years, 3 months, 16 days.
EDCS-14500686.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Fidelis for himself and his wife Turbonia, and commemorating his two deceased children, Finita and Rufius, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500688.text_edition: Quintus Pompeius Eugamus et Pompeia Venusta vivi fecerunt sibi et Pompeiae Ingenuae filiae defunctae annorum XX
EDCS-14500688.translation: Quintus Pompeius Eugamus and Pompeia Venusta, while alive, made this for themselves and for Pompeia Ingenua, their deceased daughter, aged 20.
EDCS-14500688.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quintus Pompeius Eugamus and Pompeia Venusta for themselves and their deceased daughter, Pompeia Ingenua, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500689.text_edition: Quinto Pompeio Quinti liberto Eutycho annorum LX et filiis Annia Quinta uxsor sic viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500689.translation: Annia Quinta, his wife, while alive, made this for Quintus Pompeius Eutychus, freedman of Quintus, aged 60, and for their sons, and for herself.
EDCS-14500689.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Annia Quinta for her husband Quintus Pompeius Eutychus, their sons, and for herself, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500690.text_edition: Quinto Pompeio Quinti filio Iuliano annorum LV et
EDCS-14500690.translation: To Quintus Pompeius Julianus, son of Quintus, aged 55, and [---].
EDCS-14500690.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription commemorating Quintus Pompeius Julianus, likely part of a larger family monument, probably from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500692.text_edition: Tito Raecio Narcisso annorum L Claudia Cara viva fecit sibi et coniugi optimo
EDCS-14500692.translation: Claudia Cara, while alive, made this for Titus Raecius Narcissus, aged 50, and for herself, to her excellent husband.
EDCS-14500692.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Claudia Cara for her husband, Titus Raecius Narcissus, and for herself during her lifetime, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500693.text_edition: ae Rufi filiae Albae Caius Memmius Cavarinus matri
EDCS-14500693.translation: To Alba, daughter of Rufa. Gaius Memmius Cavarinus [made this] for his mother.
EDCS-14500693.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Memmius Cavarinus to his mother, Alba, daughter of Rufa.

EDCS-14500694.text_edition: Lucio Secundinio Suriano annorum LI Socer annorum XXX
EDCS-14500694.translation: To Lucius Secundinius Surianus, 51 years old. Socer, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500694.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Secundinius Surianus, aged 51, and another individual named Socer, aged 30, likely from the same family or monument.

EDCS-14500695.text_edition: Secundino Suri filio annorum LX et Tottiae filiae coniugi annorum XXX f e s
EDCS-14500695.translation: To Secundinus, son of Surus, 60 years old, and to Tottia, his daughter and wife, 30 years old. He made this for them (f.e.s. - *fecit ei sibi* or *faciendum curavit*).
EDCS-14500695.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secundinus, son of Surus, aged 60, and his daughter and wife, Tottia, aged 30, presumably erected by a family member.

EDCS-14500697.translation: To Spectatus, his son, 25 years old.
EDCS-14500697.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Spectatus, aged 25, presumably erected by his father.

EDCS-14500698.text_edition: Tertius vivus fecit sibi et Ateboduae Quarti filiae coniugi nnorum XXXV et Secundo filio annorum V
EDCS-14500698.translation: Tertius, while living, made this for himself and for Atebodua, daughter of Quartus, his wife, 35 years old, and for Secundus, his son, 5 years old.
EDCS-14500698.summary: This is a funerary inscription prepared by Tertius during his lifetime for himself, his wife Atebodua (daughter of Quartus, 35 years old), and his son Secundus (5 years old).

EDCS-14500699.text_edition: Tertio Quarti filio annorum LV et Avitae Secundini filiae coniugi Tertinus filius faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500699.translation: To Tertius, son of Quartus, 55 years old, and to Avita, daughter of Secundinus, his wife. Tertinus, his son, took care of having this made.
EDCS-14500699.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tertius, son of Quartus, aged 55, and his wife Avita, daughter of Secundinus, commissioned by their son Tertinus.

EDCS-14500701.text_edition: Titiae Tiberi Cotuconi annorum CXX et Iuliae Luci filiae Myrinae annorum XXX
EDCS-14500701.translation: To Titia, wife of Tiberius Cotuco, 120 years old, and to Julia Myrina, daughter of Lucius, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500701.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titia, wife of Tiberius Cotuco, aged 120, and Julia Myrina, daughter of Lucius, aged 30, possibly commemorating two family members.

EDCS-14500702.text_edition: Caius Trebonius M vivus fecit Quartae Uperaci filiae et Treboniae Vfiliae annorum XXXX et Treboni
EDCS-14500702.translation: Gaius Trebonius, son of Marcus, while living, made this for Quarta, daughter of Uperax, and for Trebonia, his daughter, 40 years old, and for Trebonius [---].
EDCS-14500702.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Gaius Trebonius during his lifetime for himself, Quarta (daughter of Uperax), his daughter Trebonia (40 years old), and another Trebonius.

EDCS-14500703.text_edition: Marcus Turbonius et Turbonia Suadra vivi fecerunt sibi et Turbonio Successo filio annorum V et Sexto genero annorum XXX et Secundinae filiae annorum XX
EDCS-14500703.translation: Marcus Turbonius and Turbonia Suadra, while living, made this for themselves and for Turbonius Successus, their son, 5 years old, and for Sextus, their son-in-law, 30 years old, and for Secundina, their daughter, 20 years old.
EDCS-14500703.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Marcus Turbonius and Turbonia Suadra during their lifetime for themselves and for their son Turbonius Successus (5 years old), their son-in-law Sextus (30 years old), and their daughter Secundina (20 years old).

EDCS-14500704.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibius Catussa vivus fecit sibi et Marcellinae uxori annorum XXXII et Vibiae Finitae matri carissimae
EDCS-14500704.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vibius Catussa, while living, made this for himself and for Marcellina, his wife, 32 years old, and for Vibii Finita, his dearest mother.
EDCS-14500704.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, prepared by Vibius Catussa during his lifetime for himself, his wife Marcellina (32 years old), and his beloved mother, Vibia Finita.

EDCS-14500705.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names of Gaius Volusius Vibius and Quartina, likely a couple or relatives, who commissioned or are commemorated on a monument.

EDCS-14500707.summary: This fragment likely records a freedman and another individual, possibly for a funerary or dedicatory purpose, but the full context is lost.

EDCS-14500708.summary: This is a very brief fragment, possibly part of a dedicatory or funerary inscription, naming an individual by their abbreviated praenomen, nomen, and cognomen.

EDCS-14500709.text_edition: Occia Iuli filius Saitae libertus et Ingenuae Malei filiae coniugi annorum XXXX Occianus parentibus et Emeritae sorori pientissimae et Ingenuinae sorori
EDCS-14500709.translation: Occia, daughter of Iulus, freedwoman of Saitas, and to Ingenua, daughter of Maleus, his wife of 40 years. Occianus [made this] for his most devoted parents and for his sister Emerita and his sister Ingenuina.
EDCS-14500709.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Occianus for his parents, Occia (a freedwoman) and Ingenua, who was his wife, and also for his two sisters, Emerita and Ingenuina.

EDCS-14500710.summary: This inscription records Divico dedicating or setting up a monument for Marcus Claudius, possibly a funerary or honorary dedication.

EDCS-14500711.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, identifying Verina as the freedwoman of Iulus.

EDCS-14500715.text_edition: Publius Albius Calandinus vivus fecit sibi et Silviae Ursulae coniugi annorum XL
EDCS-14500715.translation: Publius Albius Calandinus made this alive for himself and for Silvia Ursulae, his wife of 40 years.
EDCS-14500715.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Albius Calandinus while still living, for himself and his wife, Silvia Ursulae, who was 40 years old.

EDCS-14500716.text_edition: Atilius Autoscutti filius Brigia Brigi filia Latinae filiae annorum XX
EDCS-14500716.translation: Atilius, son of Autoscutti, and Brigia, daughter of Brigus, [made this] for Latina, their daughter, of 20 years.
EDCS-14500716.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Atilius and Brigia for their 20-year-old daughter, Latina.

EDCS-14500717.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Secundinus veteranus legionis X geminae piae fidelis Antoninianae vivus fecit sibi et Aureliae Sabinae coniugi annorum et Aurelio filio annorum XX
EDCS-14500717.translation: Marcus Aurelius Secundinus, a veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina Pia Fidelis Antoniniana, made this while alive for himself, and for Aurelia Sabina, his wife, and for Aurelius, his son, of 20 years.
EDCS-14500717.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Marcus Aurelius Secundinus, a veteran, for himself, his wife Aurelia Sabina, and his 20-year-old son Aurelius.

EDCS-14500718.text_edition: Saturninius Verinus et Aurelia Secundina vivi fecerunt sibi et Aurelio filio
EDCS-14500718.translation: Saturninius Verinus and Aurelia Secundina, while alive, made this for themselves and for their son Aurelius.
EDCS-14500718.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Saturninius Verinus and Aurelia Secundina while they were still living, for themselves and their son Aurelius.

EDCS-14500719.text_edition: Senaca sic Palumbi et Materna coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Palumbo patri annorum LX
EDCS-14500719.translation: Senaca, son of Palumbus, and Materna his wife, made this in their lifetime for themselves and for Palumbus, his father, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-14500719.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Senaca and Materna for themselves and for Senaca's father, Palumbus.

EDCS-14500720.text_edition: Caio Atucio Albano veterano ex praetorio et Aeliae Sabinae uxori optimae et Atuciae Aventinae filiae annorum XXXIII Exupereus milis sic legionis secundae Italicae obitus XXVIII
EDCS-14500720.translation: To Gaius Atucius Albanus, veteran from the Praetorian Guard, and to Aelia Sabina, his excellent wife, and to Atucia Aventina, their daughter, who lived 33 years. Exupereus, soldier of the Second Italic Legion, died on the 28th.
EDCS-14500720.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Gaius Atucius Albanus, a veteran, and his family, including his wife Aelia Sabina and daughter Atucia Aventina, also noting the death of Exupereus, a soldier.

EDCS-14500721.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, mentioning the name Atilia.

EDCS-14500722.text_edition: Vibius Vindi filius vivus fecit sibi et Dubitatae coniugi
EDCS-14500722.translation: Vibius, son of Vindus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Dubitata, his wife.
EDCS-14500722.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Vibius for himself and his wife, Dubitata, during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500725.text_edition: Boius Boniati et Maximae Dievionis filiae coniugi optimae annorum LX et Comato Boi filio annorum XL et Rustiae Tertullae coniugi et Kalendinus Celati filius miles legionis II adiutricis annorum XVI
EDCS-14500725.translation: To Boius, son of Boniatis, and to Maxima, daughter of Dievio, his excellent wife, who lived 60 years, and to Comatus, son of Boius, who lived 40 years, and to Rustia Tertulla, his wife. And Kalendinus, son of Celatus, soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion, who lived 16 years.
EDCS-14500725.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Boius and his family, including his wife Maxima, son Comatus, and Kalendinus, a soldier of the Second Adiutrix Legion.

EDCS-14500726.text_edition: Burrus Suri filius et Suaducia Boniati filia coniux
EDCS-14500726.translation: Burrus, son of Surus, and Suaducia, daughter of Boniatis, his wife.
EDCS-14500726.summary: This inscription, likely funerary, records the names of Burrus and his wife Suaducia.

EDCS-14500727.text_edition: Lucius Domitius Secundinus et Derva Malai filia coniux sibi et Iuniano filio annorum XXV
EDCS-14500727.translation: Lucius Domitius Secundinus and Derva, daughter of Malus, his wife, [made this] for themselves and for Iunianus, their son, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-14500727.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Lucius Domitius Secundinus and his wife Derva for themselves and their son Iunianus.

EDCS-14500728.text_edition: Gemellus Marconis filius et Adnama coniux et Marcellinus filius et Vitellia coniux optima vivi fecerunt sibi Ursacinae
EDCS-14500728.translation: Gemellus, son of Marconus, and Adnama, his wife, and Marcellinus, their son, and Vitellia, his excellent wife, made this in their lifetime for themselves and for Ursacina.
EDCS-14500728.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Gemellus and his wife Adnama, along with their son Marcellinus and his wife Vitellia, for themselves and for Ursacina, during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500729.text_edition: Saturninus Aubnissi filius vivus fecit sibi et Suaduciae Vanni filiae coniugi et  fili- annorum XXII
EDCS-14500729.translation: Saturninus, son of Aubnissus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Suaducia, daughter of Vannus, his wife, and for their son/daughter, who lived 22 years.
EDCS-14500729.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Saturninus for himself and his wife Suaducia, and their child, during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500730.text_edition: Vercaius Saitulli filius vivus fecit sibi et Caixuni Quarti filiae annorum
EDCS-14500730.translation: Vercaius, son of Saitullus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Caixuni, daughter of Quartus, who lived [---] years.
EDCS-14500730.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument made by Vercaius for himself and for Caixuni, during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500731.text_edition: Avitus Optati filius defunctus annorum LV et Covrunae Ianuari filiae coniugi optimae et Marco Ulpio IAII et DiCmaEEsae annorum XI et Hostiliia VENEINA coniux II et Marco Ulpio Acilio filio annorum XXII
EDCS-14500731.translation: Avitus, son of Optatus, deceased, 55 years old, and to Covruna, daughter of Ianuarius, his excellent wife, and to Marcus Ulpius IAII, and to DiCmaEEsa, 11 years old, and to Hostilia VENEINA, his second wife, and to Marcus Ulpius Acilius, his son, 22 years old.
EDCS-14500731.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deceased Avitus, his wives, and children, likely commissioned by surviving family members or Avitus himself while alive for his entire household.

EDCS-14500732.text_edition: Comatus Atianti filius fecit sibi et Suntrae Darseti filiae coniugi et Adnamato filio annorum LX et Suadullae Sammi filiae Cenicelloni filio annorum XX
EDCS-14500732.translation: Comatus, son of Atians, made this for himself and for Suntra, daughter of Darsetus, his wife, and for Adnamatus, his son, 60 years old, and for Suadulla, daughter of Sammus, and for Cenicello, his son, 20 years old.
EDCS-14500732.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Comatus for himself, his wife, and their two sons, one of whom was 60 years old and the other 20.

EDCS-14500733.text_edition: Caius Sempronius Summinus vivus fecit sibi et Musae uxori et Primo filio annorum VIIII
EDCS-14500733.translation: Gaius Sempronius Summinus, while living, made this for himself and for Musa, his wife, and for Primus, his son, 9 years old.
EDCS-14500733.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Sempronius Summinus during his lifetime for himself, his wife, and their young son.

EDCS-14500734.text_edition: Velleco Cacurdae filius et Deusa Ituli filia coniux et Angulato filio annorum X
EDCS-14500734.translation: Velleco, son of Cacurda, and Deusa, daughter of Itulus, his wife, and Angulatus, their son, 10 years old.
EDCS-14500734.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Velleco, his wife Deusa, and their 10-year-old son Angulatus, likely erected by surviving family or by the parents while alive for their son.

EDCS-14500735.text_edition: Cirpo Seni filius et Pameta Deusonis filia coniux filiis filiabusque fecerunt et Autoscuttae nnorum XX et Prisco filio annorum LX
EDCS-14500735.translation: Cirpo, son of Senus, and Pameta, daughter of Deusos, his wife, made this for their sons and daughters, and for Autoscutta, 20 years old, and for Priscus, their son, 60 years old.
EDCS-14500735.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Cirpo and his wife Pameta for their children, including a 20-year-old Autoscutta and a 60-year-old son Priscus.

EDCS-14500736.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Saturnus domu Ulpia Poetovione vivus fecit sibi et Pomponiae Secundinae coniugi et Insequenti filio annorum XV
EDCS-14500736.translation: Marcus Ulpius Saturnus, from the Ulpian gens, from Poetovio, while living, made this for himself and for Pomponia Secundina, his wife, and for Insequens, his son, 15 years old.
EDCS-14500736.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Marcus Ulpius Saturnus during his lifetime for himself, his wife, and their 15-year-old son.

EDCS-14500738.text_edition: Nammonius Mussa et Kalandina coniux vivi et Saturninus Saturionis vivus fecerunt
EDCS-14500738.translation: Nammonius Mussa and Kalandina, his wife, while living, and Saturninus, son of Saturio, while living, made this.
EDCS-14500738.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Nammonius Mussa and his wife Kalandina, along with Saturninus, son of Saturio, all while still alive, for their eventual burial place.

EDCS-14500739.text_edition: Caius Firminius Primus veteranus legionis II adiutricis et Covnerta Adabi filia coniux optima Caius Firminius Castricius aedilis Solvae vivus fecit sibi et Seiae Urbici filiae Litugenae uxori optimae annorum XX
EDCS-14500739.translation: Gaius Firminius Primus, veteran of the Second Adjutrix Legion, and Covnerta, daughter of Adabus, his excellent wife. Gaius Firminius Castricius, aedile of Solva, while living, made this for himself and for Seia Litugena, daughter of Urbicus, his excellent wife, 20 years old.
EDCS-14500739.summary: This inscription seems to combine two separate funerary commemorations: one for Gaius Firminius Primus, a veteran and his wife, and another by Gaius Firminius Castricius, an aedile of Solva, for himself and his 20-year-old excellent wife Seia Litugena, made during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500741.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Mansuetus ni[---
EDCS-14500741.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication to the Manes (Spirits of the Departed) of a person named Mansuetus, indicating a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-14500742.text_edition: Pompeiae Theodorae annorum XXX parentes fecerunt
EDCS-14500742.translation: The parents made this for Pompeia Theodora, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500742.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by parents for their daughter, Pompeia Theodora, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-14500744.text_edition: Nammonia Materiu viva fecit sibi et Gaio Sempronio Secundino marito decurioni Solvae et Gaio Sempronio Secundino filio librario consularis annorum XVIIII
EDCS-14500744.translation: Nammonia Materia, while alive, made this for herself and for Gaius Sempronius Secundinus, her husband, a decurion of Solva, and for Gaius Sempronius Secundinus, her son, a consular's librarian, 19 years old.
EDCS-14500744.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Nammonia Materia for herself, her husband, a decurion of Solva, and her son, a consular's librarian, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500745.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cai Licini Trionis et Sabiniae Severinae coniugis Licini
EDCS-14500745.translation: To the Divine Manes of Gaius Licinius Trio and Sabinia Severina, his wife, [this monument of] Licinius.
EDCS-14500745.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the spirits of the dead, dedicated to Gaius Licinius Trio and his wife Sabinia Severina, probably erected by Licinius himself or a relative.

EDCS-14500746.text_edition: Lucius Cantius Secundus vivus fecit sibi et Cantiae Iuni filiae Boniae uxori et Cantiae Luci filiae Boniatae
EDCS-14500746.translation: Lucius Cantius Secundus, while alive, made this for himself and for Cantia Bonia, daughter of Iunius, his wife, and for Cantia Boniata, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-14500746.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Cantius Secundus for himself, his wife Cantia Bonia, and Cantia Boniata, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500747.text_edition: Insequens Senilis et Censoria Tervina vivi fecerunt sibi et Ingenuae Terti filiae Insequentis matri et Maximiano Maximi filio vitrico
EDCS-14500747.translation: Insequens Senilis and Censoria Tervina, while alive, made this for themselves and for Ingenua, daughter of Tertius, mother of Insequens, and for Maximianus, son of Maximus, stepfather.
EDCS-14500747.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Insequens Senilis and Censoria Tervina for themselves and their relatives, Ingenua and Maximianus, likely in the Imperial era.

EDCS-14500748.text_edition: Iunius Vercai filius vivus fecit sibi et Bugiae Secundi filiae coniugi annorum XXX
EDCS-14500748.translation: Iunius, son of Vercaius, while alive, made this for himself and for Bugia, daughter of Secundus, his wife, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500748.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Iunius, son of Vercaius, for himself and his wife, Bugia, who died at age 30, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500749.text_edition: Speratus Sironis et Sporilla Commodi liberta vivi fecerunt sibi
EDCS-14500749.translation: Speratus, son of Siro, and Sporilla, freedwoman of Commodus, while alive, made this for themselves.
EDCS-14500749.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Speratus, son of Siro, and Sporilla, a freedwoman of Commodus, for their own future burials, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500750.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae Hostilia Cai filia Crispa
EDCS-14500750.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, Minerva. Hostilia Crispa, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-14500750.summary: This is a religious dedication to the Capitoline Triad (Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva) made by Hostilia Crispa, daughter of Gaius, indicating a public or private votive offering.

EDCS-14500751.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Arubiano Lucius Campanius Celer sacerdos urbis Romae aeternae et Iulia Honorata coniux pro se et suis votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14500751.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Arubianus, Lucius Campanius Celer, priest of the eternal city of Rome, and Iulia Honorata, his wife, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow for themselves and their family.
EDCS-14500751.summary: This is a votive dedication made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Arubianus by Lucius Campanius Celer, a priest of Rome, and his wife Iulia Honorata, fulfilling a vow on behalf of their family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500752.text_edition: Iuniano Iuni filio annorum XXXV Saxia Marci filia Amm coniugi et sibi
EDCS-14500752.translation: To Iunianus, son of Iunius, 35 years old. Saxia Amm, daughter of Marcus, to her husband and herself.
EDCS-14500752.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Saxia Amm, daughter of Marcus, to her husband Iunianus, who died at 35, and also for herself, from the Imperial era.

EDCS-14500753.text_edition: Vercaius Titiae vivus fecit sibi et Boniae coniugi annorum L et Secundino Adiutoris filio annorum
EDCS-14500753.translation: Vercaius, son of Titia, while alive, made this for himself and for Bonia, his wife, 50 years old, and for Secundinus, son of Adiutor.
EDCS-14500753.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Vercaius, son of Titia, for himself, his wife Bonia who died at age 50, and their son Secundinus, son of Adiutor, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500754.text_edition: Accepto Atti Tutoris annorum XC et Mogiae Accepti filiae annorum L Fabro filius parentibus fecit
EDCS-14500754.translation: Fabro, the son, made this for his parents Acceptus Attius Tutor, 90 years old, and Mogia, daughter of Acceptus, 50 years old.
EDCS-14500754.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a son, Fabro, to his parents, Acceptus Attius Tutor and Mogia.

EDCS-14500755.text_edition: Saturnus annorum LI et Vibia coniux et Elvima filia annorum XXX et Surio vivus filius Ianuarius annorum L
EDCS-14500755.translation: Saturnus, 51 years old, and his wife Vibia, and their daughter Elvima, 30 years old, and their son Surio, alive, and Ianuarius, 50 years old.
EDCS-14500755.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names and ages of Saturnus, Vibia, Elvima, Surio, and Ianuarius, likely for a family tomb where some were still living at the time of its creation.

EDCS-14500756.text_edition: Caius Attius Propinqui libertus Senno annorum LX et Elvia Oclatius et Finita coniux vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500756.translation: Caius Attius Senno, freedman of Propinquus, 60 years old, and Elvia Oclatius, and his wife Finita, made this while living.
EDCS-14500756.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Caius Attius Senno, Elvia Oclatius, and Finita for themselves while they were still alive.

EDCS-14500757.text_edition: Sabinus Masculi et Candida Potentis vivi fecerunt sibi et Nigellioni filio militi legionis II Italicae annorum XXX
EDCS-14500757.translation: Sabinus, son of Masculus, and Candida, daughter of Potens, made this while living for themselves and for their son Nigellio, a soldier of the Second Italian Legion, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500757.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Sabinus and Candida for themselves and their deceased son, Nigellio, a soldier of Legio II Italica.

EDCS-14500758.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Salvianus veteranus legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Severianae ex stratore consularis et Aurelia Martia coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Marco Aurelio Ursici=Gno#Ursicino#URSIGNO filio militi praetoriano cohortis IIII praetoriae stipendiorum III @(obito) annorum XX
EDCS-14500758.translation: Marcus Aurelius Salvianus, a veteran of the Second Italian Legion, loyal and faithful to the Severan dynasty, formerly a consular groom, and his wife Aurelia Martia, made this while living for themselves and for their son Marcus Aurelius Ursicinus, a Praetorian soldier of the Fourth Praetorian Cohort, with three years of service, who died at 20 years old.
EDCS-14500758.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Marcus Aurelius Salvianus, a veteran, and his wife Aurelia Martia, for themselves and their son, Marcus Aurelius Ursicinus, a Praetorian soldier who died young.

EDCS-14500759.text_edition: Hostilius Tunger et Ingenua Vicari filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Tacito filio militi cohortis VII praetoriae annorum XXX
EDCS-14500759.translation: Hostilius Tunger and Ingenua, daughter of Vicarius, made this while living for themselves and for their son Tacitus, a soldier of the Seventh Praetorian Cohort, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500759.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Hostilius Tunger and Ingenua for themselves and their deceased son, Tacitus, a Praetorian soldier.

EDCS-14500760.text_edition: Candido Cassi filio annorum L et Candidae Ucci filiae coniugi filius fecit et Candidiano filio annorum XXI
EDCS-14500760.translation: The son made this for Candidus, son of Cassius, 50 years old, and for Candida, daughter of Uccus, his wife, and for their son Candidianus, 21 years old.
EDCS-14500760.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a son for his parents, Candidus and Candida, and his brother Candidianus.

EDCS-14500762.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae Dii libertae Quintae Iulius Dii libertus Amianthus et Iulia Dii liberta Amanda parentes vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500762.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Amianthus, freedman of Dius, and Iulia Amanda, freedwoman of Dius, parents, made this while living for Iulia Quinta, freedwoman of Dius.
EDCS-14500762.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the deceased Iulia Quinta by her parents, Iulius Amianthus and Iulia Amanda, all of whom were freed by a man named Dius.

EDCS-14500764.text_edition: Marcus Mogius Valentinus Vibi filius et Mogia Iustina coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Mogio Urso filio militi cohortis I Brittonum annorum XXX
EDCS-14500764.translation: Marcus Mogius Valentinus, son of Vibius, and his wife Mogia Iustina, made this while living for themselves and for their son Mogius Ursus, a soldier of the First Cohort of Britons, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500764.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Marcus Mogius Valentinus and Mogia Iustina for themselves and their deceased son, Mogius Ursus, a soldier of Cohors I Brittonum.

EDCS-14500765.text_edition: Caius Vitalius Vitulus Tertiniae Sabinae coniugi annorum XXXII
EDCS-14500765.translation: Caius Vitalius Vitulus [made this] for Tertinia Sabina, his wife, 32 years old.
EDCS-14500765.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Caius Vitalius Vitulus to his deceased wife, Tertinia Sabina.

EDCS-14500766.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco T filio Iucundo IIviro iure dicundo Flaviae Solvae et Atiliaeae parentibus I
EDCS-14500766.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus, son of T., the fortunate duumvir with judicial power. To Flavia Solva and Atiliaea, parents. I.
EDCS-14500766.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus, a son who served as a duumvir, and to his parents, Flavia Solva and Atiliaea.

EDCS-14500767.translation: Passerinus made this alive for himself and...
EDCS-14500767.summary: This is a funerary inscription, made by Passerinus during his lifetime for himself and presumably other family members, though the text is incomplete.

EDCS-14500768.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Menelao  Lucia  fil annorum?
EDCS-14500768.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Menelaus. Lucia, daughter, of years...
EDCS-14500768.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Menelaus by Lucia, his daughter, with the age of Menelaus missing.

EDCS-14500769.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Debulsori sic Caius Iulius Probus miles legionis X geminae Severianae votum solvit libens laetus merito Maximo II et Agricola consulibus
EDCS-14500769.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Expeller, Gaius Julius Probus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina Severiana, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In the consulship of Maximus II and Agricola.
EDCS-14500769.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Debulsor by Gaius Julius Probus, a soldier, made in the year 234 AD during the consulship of Maximus II and Agricola.

EDCS-14500771.text_edition: Caius Atilius po annorum LXX et Melissaconiux Caius Atilius Emeritus  parentibus fecit sibi et Atiliae ae uxori et ae filiae annorum III Emeritus supra scriptus decurio municipii Claudi Teurniae
EDCS-14500771.translation: Gaius Atilius, priest, of 70 years, and Melissa, his wife. Gaius Atilius Emeritus made this for his parents, and for himself and Atilia, his wife, and for his daughter of 3 years. The above-written Emeritus, decurion of the municipium Claudi Teurniae.
EDCS-14500771.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Atilius Emeritus, a decurion, to his parents, Gaius Atilius and Melissa, and also for himself, his wife Atilia, and their three-year-old daughter.

EDCS-14500772.text_edition: Iuniano Burrani filio annorum L Sucela Ucconis filia marito et Terentino et Iulianae Tertini filiae nepti
EDCS-14500772.translation: To Junianus, son of Burranus, of 50 years. Sucela, daughter of Uccon, made this for her husband and for Terentinus and Juliana, daughter of Tertinus, her granddaughter.
EDCS-14500772.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sucela to her husband Junianus, and also for Terentinus and her granddaughter Juliana.

EDCS-14500773.text_edition: Caius Sabinius Primigenius et Sabinia Magiona Cai Sabini filia Veci Cinti filio nnorum XXII et sibi et Sucellae nepti annorum XII
EDCS-14500773.translation: Gaius Sabinius Primigenius and Sabinia Magiona, daughter of Gaius Sabinius, made this for Vecus Cintus, son of Cintus, of 22 years, and for themselves and for Sucella, their granddaughter, of 12 years.
EDCS-14500773.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Sabinius Primigenius and Sabinia Magiona to Vecus Cintus, their son, and also for themselves and their granddaughter Sucella.

EDCS-14500774.text_edition: Sato Togionis filio et Bussullae Saggonis filiae coniugi heres fecit
EDCS-14500774.translation: To Sato, son of Togio, and to Bussulla, daughter of Saggo, his wife. The heir made this.
EDCS-14500774.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by an heir to Sato and his wife Bussulla.

EDCS-14500776.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vicario Suri liberto et Profuturae coniugi Septumi sic et Secundinae libertae Viatorina @(obita) annorum XXXXV et Libertio vivus fecit sibi et coniugi filiis
EDCS-14500776.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Vicarius, freedman of Surus, and to Profutura, his wife. Also to Septumus and Secundina, freedwoman Viatorina, deceased at 45 years. And to Libertius, he made this alive for himself and his wife and children.
EDCS-14500776.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription dedicated to Vicarius and his wife Profutura, to Septumus and Secundina, and by Libertius for himself, his wife, and children, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-14500777.text_edition: Vibius Helvisiani et Attia Brigantia coniux annorum XXXX et Boniata coniux et Vibiano filio annorum XX
EDCS-14500777.translation: Vibius, son of Helvisianus, and Attia Brigantia, his wife, of 40 years. And Boniata, his wife, and Vibianus, his son, of 20 years.
EDCS-14500777.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely for Vibius, son of Helvisianus, and his two wives, Attia Brigantia and Boniata, as well as his son Vibianus.

EDCS-14500778.text_edition: Buccioni Ressimari filio et Taparuni Senici filiae coniugi et Vindoni filio annorum XXXX
EDCS-14500778.translation: To Buccionius, son of Ressimarus, and to Taparunia, daughter of Senicus, his wife, and to Vindo, their son, aged 40 years.
EDCS-14500778.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Buccionius, his wife Taparunia, and their son Vindo.

EDCS-14500779.text_edition: Propeoni Ottonis filio et Togiae Cassi filiae coniugi et Aelio Parato
EDCS-14500779.translation: To Propeo, son of Otto, and to Togia, daughter of Cassius, his wife, and to Aelius Paratus.
EDCS-14500779.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Propeo, his wife Togia, and Aelius Paratus.

EDCS-14500781.text_edition: Dis Manibus perpetuae securitati Aurelius Saturio veteranus cohortis V Breucorum vivus fecit sibi et Claudiae Faustinae coniugi @(obitae) annorum LX
EDCS-14500781.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for perpetual security. Aurelius Saturio, a veteran of the Fifth Cohort of Breuci, made this while living for himself and for Claudia Faustina, his departed wife, aged 60 years.
EDCS-14500781.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Aurelius Saturio, a veteran, for himself and his deceased wife Claudia Faustina.

EDCS-14500782.text_edition: Saturio Talionis vivus fecit sibi et Banonae Bardi annorum LX
EDCS-14500782.translation: Saturio, son of Talio, made this while living for himself and for Banona, daughter of Bardus, aged 60 years.
EDCS-14500782.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Saturio, son of Talio, for himself and for Banona, daughter of Bardus.

EDCS-14500783.text_edition: Pluncus Victoris et Caletiu Adnamati vivi fecerunt sibi et Banonae filiae annorum XVIII
EDCS-14500783.translation: Pluncus, son of Victor, and Caletia, daughter of Adnamatus, made this while living for themselves and for Banona, their daughter, aged 18 years.
EDCS-14500783.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Pluncus and Caletia for themselves and their daughter Banona.

EDCS-14500785.text_edition: Aurelio Candido militi legionis II Augustae annorum XX
EDCS-14500785.translation: To Aurelius Candidus, a soldier of the Second Augustan Legion, aged 20 years.
EDCS-14500785.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Candidus, a soldier of the Legio II Augusta.

EDCS-14500786.text_edition: Quintianus Cittonis filius et Nigella liberta Boudes vivi fecerunt sibi et Adnamato filio annorum VI
EDCS-14500786.translation: Quintianus, son of Citto, and Nigella, freedwoman of Bouda, made this while living for themselves and for Adnamatus, their son, aged 6 years.
EDCS-14500786.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Quintianus and Nigella for themselves and their young son Adnamatus.

EDCS-14500787.text_edition: Muntissius Privati annorum XXXX et Quinta Aspronis viva fecit sibi et marito
EDCS-14500787.translation: To Muntissius, son of Privatus, aged 40 years. And Quinta, daughter of Aspron, made this while living for herself and her husband.
EDCS-14500787.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Muntissius, and made by Quinta for herself and her husband Muntissius.

EDCS-14500788.text_edition: Quarta Camulae liberta fecit sibi et Totto filio annorum XX
EDCS-14500788.translation: Quarta, freedwoman of Camula, made this for herself and for Totto, her son, aged 20 years.
EDCS-14500788.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Quarta, a freedwoman, for herself and her son Totto.

EDCS-14500789.text_edition: Quintus Septueius Adiectus sibi et Samicantuni Goutonis filiae uxori vivus fecit
EDCS-14500789.translation: Quintus Septueius Adiectus made this while living for himself and for Samicantunia, daughter of Gouto, his wife.
EDCS-14500789.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Quintus Septueius Adiectus for himself and his wife Samicantunia.

EDCS-14500790.text_edition: Serotinus et Verecunda Cai Anni Rufini liberta vivi fecerunt sibi et Quintae filiae annorum XIII
EDCS-14500790.translation: Serotinus and Verecunda, freedwoman of Gaius Annius Rufinus, made this while living for themselves and for their daughter Quinta, aged 13 years.
EDCS-14500790.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb made by Serotinus and Verecunda, a freedwoman, for themselves and their young daughter Quinta, indicating a private family monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500791.text_edition: Vibia Terti filia Gracilio Secundino marito et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-14500791.translation: Vibia, daughter of Tertius, made this while living for her husband Gracilius Secundinus and for herself.
EDCS-14500791.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a tomb prepared by Vibia, daughter of Tertius, during her lifetime for herself and her husband Gracilius Secundinus, typical of Roman self-dedication practices.

EDCS-14500792.text_edition: Cribuionis filius annorum LX hic situs est et Blendo coniux annorum LXXX
EDCS-14500792.translation: The son of Cribuo, aged 60 years, lies here, and his wife Blendo, aged 80 years.
EDCS-14500792.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking a burial site for a man, the son of Cribuo, and his wife Blendo, indicating a shared tomb and providing their ages at death.

EDCS-14500794.text_edition: Totulo Mufonis filius vivus fecit sibi et Giamillae Necorni filiae coniugi annorum LX
EDCS-14500794.translation: Totulus, son of Mufo, made this while living for himself and for Giamilla, daughter of Necorno, his wife, aged 60 years.
EDCS-14500794.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the creation of a monument by Totulus, son of Mufo, during his lifetime for himself and his wife Giamilla, daughter of Necorno, who was 60 years old at the time of her death or the inscription's making.

EDCS-14500795.text_edition: C Tertullinus et D Tertulla coniux et Tertino filio et Kalandino filio militi legionis X et Crispa et Digna
EDCS-14500795.translation: Gaius Tertullinus and Decima Tertulla his wife, and for their son Tertinus, and for their son Kalandinus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion, and Crispa and Digna.
EDCS-14500795.summary: This appears to be a funerary or commemorative inscription dedicated by Gaius Tertullinus and his wife Decima Tertulla to several family members, including a soldier in the Tenth Legion, reflecting a family's record of their dead.

EDCS-14500796.text_edition: Nammo Cabalionis filio annorum LX et Soliae Loturi filiae annorum XXXX
EDCS-14500796.translation: To Nammo, son of Cabalio, aged 60 years, and to Solia, daughter of Loturus, aged 40 years.
EDCS-14500796.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Nammo, son of Cabalio, and Solia, daughter of Loturus, providing their ages at death and likely marking their shared or individual tombs.

EDCS-14500797.text_edition: Marcus Annius Romanius Successa Atuali filia coniux Annio Saturnino filio
EDCS-14500797.translation: Marcus Annius Romanius, Successa, daughter of Atualus, his wife, for their son Annius Saturninus.
EDCS-14500797.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Marcus Annius Romanius and his wife Successa, daughter of Atualus, for their son Annius Saturninus, recording a family's remembrance of their deceased child.

EDCS-14500798.text_edition: Masculus Ituli filius sibi et Sabinae Quinti filiae coniugi testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-14500798.translation: Masculus, son of Itulus, ordered this to be made by will for himself and for Sabina, daughter of Quintus, his wife.
EDCS-14500798.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Masculus, son of Itulus, arranged for a monument for himself and his wife Sabina, daughter of Quintus, to be made posthumously through his will.

EDCS-14500799.text_edition: Cucio Romulo Quarto KA F I
EDCS-14500799.translation: To Cucius Romulus Quartus. K.A.F.I. (meaning unknown, possibly Kalendis Augustis Fieri Iussit - 'Ordered to be made on the Kalends of August' or similar).
EDCS-14500799.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cucius Romulus Quartus, with an unclear abbreviation that likely pertains to the monument's execution or timing.

EDCS-14500800.summary: This is an incomplete inscription, likely a funerary dedication, for Quintus Annius Terentius, with the rest of the text missing.

EDCS-14500801.text_edition: Adiutor Romani filius vivus fecit sibi et Saturninae Massae filiae coniugi annorum L et Attico filio et Luciae Veturiae Pontentinae coniugi pientissimae annorum XL
EDCS-14500801.translation: Adiutor, son of Romanus, made this in his lifetime for himself, and for Saturnina Massa, daughter of Massa, his wife, 50 years old, and for his son Atticus, and for Lucia Veturia Pontentina, his most devoted wife, 40 years old.
EDCS-14500801.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Adiutor for himself and his two wives and a son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500802.text_edition: Quintus Capitonius Potens vivus fecit sibi et Belliciae Spectatae coniugi optimae annorum XXXVII et Capitoniae Veneriae coniugi
EDCS-14500802.translation: Quintus Capitonius Potens made this in his lifetime for himself and for Bellicia Spectata, his most excellent wife, 37 years old, and for Capitonia Veneria, his wife.
EDCS-14500802.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a monument prepared by Quintus Capitonius Potens for himself and his two wives, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500803.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Lucius Arruntius Marcellinus pro salute sua et Baebiae Proculeiae coniugi votum solvit
EDCS-14500803.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Depulsor, Lucius Arruntius Marcellinus fulfilled his vow for his own health and for the health of Baebia Proculeia, his wife.
EDCS-14500803.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Depulsor, dedicated by Lucius Arruntius Marcellinus for the well-being of himself and his wife, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500804.text_edition: Calventius Masculi filius vivus fecit sibi et Avetae Adiutoris filiae coniugi annorum L
EDCS-14500804.translation: Calventius, son of Masculus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Aveta, daughter of Adiutor, his wife, 50 years old.
EDCS-14500804.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a monument made by Calventius for himself and his wife, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500805.text_edition: Urbano Ressimari filio annorum LXX et Resillae Adnamati filiae coniugi annorum LX et Iantumaro filio annorum XXX et iae filiae annorum XVIII heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500805.translation: For Urbanus, son of Ressimarus, 70 years old, and for Resilla, daughter of Adnamatus, his wife, 60 years old, and for Iantumaro, their son, 30 years old, and for [---]ia, their daughter, 18 years old, the heir took care to have this made.
EDCS-14500805.summary: This is a funerary inscription for several family members, commissioned by an heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500806.text_edition: Titus Flavius Maximianus vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Respectae coniugi vivae et Tito Flavio Maximo militi legionis XIIII geminae filio annorum XXVII
EDCS-14500806.translation: Titus Flavius Maximianus made this in his lifetime for himself and for Julia Respecta, his living wife, and for Titus Flavius Maximus, soldier of the Legio XIIII Gemina, his son, 27 years old.
EDCS-14500806.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Titus Flavius Maximianus for himself, his living wife, and his son, a soldier, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500807.text_edition: Maleius Atressi filius annorum LXXX Blassia Quinti filia Brutta uxor annorum filii faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-14500807.translation: For Maleius, son of Atressus, 80 years old, Blassia, daughter of Quintus, Brutta, his wife, and his son took care to have this made.
EDCS-14500807.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Maleius by his wife and son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500808.text_edition: Giamillius Senilis vivus fecit sibi et B Valentinae et Giamillio Priscino filio et Avitae Iustini et Giamillio Valentino filio et Giamilliae Valentinae parentes fecerunt
EDCS-14500808.translation: Giamillius Senilis made this in his lifetime for himself and for B. Valentina and for Giamillius Priscinus, his son, and for Avita, daughter of Iustinus, and for Giamillius Valentinus, his son, and for Giamillia Valentina; the parents made this.
EDCS-14500808.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Giamillius Senilis and his wife, Avita, for themselves and their children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500810.text_edition: Secundinus C annorum LX et Iulia Litugena et Secundinae filiae annorum
EDCS-14500810.translation: For Secundinus C. 60 years old, and Julia Litugena, and Secundina, his daughter, [---] years old.
EDCS-14500810.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Secundinus, Julia Litugena, and their daughter Secundina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500811.text_edition: S SERQVIA coniux Iuniae et Iuliae filiae filiis nepotibus
EDCS-14500811.translation: S. SERQVIA, wife of Iunius, and for Iulia, his daughter, for her sons, for her grandsons.
EDCS-14500811.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating a woman named Serqvia, possibly as part of a family monument for her husband, daughter, sons, and grandsons, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500812.text_edition: Caio Vindio Crescenti et Claudiae Crispinae et Vindio Verino filio militi cohortis  urbanae annorum XXIII
EDCS-14500812.translation: To Gaius Vindius Crescens and Claudia Crispina and their son Vindius Verinus, a soldier of the urban cohort, 23 years old.
EDCS-14500812.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Gaius Vindius Crescens, Claudia Crispina, and their son Vindius Verinus, a soldier of the urban cohort who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-14500814.text_edition: Ittu Peculiaris annorum LXXX et Successae coniugi annorum LXXX Vindio Ittunis filius vivus fecit sibi et Emeritae coniugi et Dubitato filio an(ni) I
EDCS-14500814.translation: Ittu, son of Peculiaris, 80 years old, and Successa, his wife, 80 years old. Vindius, son of Ittu, made this while living for himself and Emerita, his wife, and Dubitatus, his son, 1 year old.
EDCS-14500814.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Vindius, son of Ittu, for himself, his wife Emerita, and their infant son Dubitatus, also commemorating his parents Ittu Peculiaris and Successa, both deceased at age 80.

EDCS-14500815.text_edition: Valerius Togionis filius Vibennae coniugi et Valerianus filius annorum XVI
EDCS-14500815.translation: Valerius, son of Togonius, to Vibenna, his wife, and their son Valerianus, 16 years old.
EDCS-14500815.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Valerius, son of Togonius, to his wife Vibenna and their son Valerianus, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-14500817.text_edition: mocca Adiutoris filius et Tacita Proi annorum LX coniux Arsto filius et Aelia Iuliana coniux Aelia Iulia filia et Aelius Iulianus et Claudia Iustina coniux Ursulus Marcelli annorum XXX
EDCS-14500817.translation: Mocca, son of Adiutor; and Tacita, wife of Proius, 60 years old; Arsto, son; and Aelia Iuliana, wife; Aelia Iulia, daughter; and Aelius Iulianus; and Claudia Iustina, wife of Ursulus Marcelli, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500817.summary: This inscription appears to be a complex familial funerary monument, possibly dedicated to several individuals including Mocca, Tacita, Arsto, Aelia Iuliana, Aelia Iulia, Aelius Iulianus, and Claudia Iustina, with ages specified for some.

EDCS-14500818.text_edition: Lucius Laetilius Castus vivus fecit sibi et Claudiae Primigeniae coniugi annorum XXXX et Anniae Valentinae
EDCS-14500818.translation: Lucius Laetilius Castus made this while living for himself and Claudia Primigenia, his wife, 40 years old, and Annia Valentina.
EDCS-14500818.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument created by Lucius Laetilius Castus for himself, his wife Claudia Primigenia, who was 40 years old, and Annia Valentina, while he was still alive.

EDCS-14500819.text_edition: militi legionis X? g(eminae)? p(iae)? f(idelis) et Tiberio Claudio Blando filio annorum XXX et Tiberio Claudio Tertio militi legionis X geminae piae fidelis beneficario conssulari annorum XXX
EDCS-14500819.translation: To a soldier of the Legio X Gemina Pia Fidelis; and to Tiberius Claudius Blandus, son, 30 years old; and to Tiberius Claudius Tertius, soldier of the Legio X Gemina Pia Fidelis, beneficiarius consularis, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500819.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to an unnamed soldier of the Legio X Gemina Pia Fidelis, and specifically to Tiberius Claudius Blandus and Tiberius Claudius Tertius, both 30-year-old soldiers of the same legion, the latter a beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-14500821.text_edition: Finitus Accepti annorum L Cirpia Elvima coniux viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500821.translation: Finitus, son of Acceptus, 50 years old. Cirpia Elvima, his wife, made this while living, also for herself.
EDCS-14500821.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Cirpia Elvima for herself and her husband Finitus, son of Acceptus, who died at age 50.

EDCS-14500822.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Avitus et Caixu Rantilli filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Tiberio Iulio Adnnamo annorum IX
EDCS-14500822.translation: Tiberius Iulius Avitus and Caixu, daughter of Rantillus, made this while living for themselves and Tiberius Iulius Adnnamus, 9 years old.
EDCS-14500822.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument created by Tiberius Iulius Avitus and Caixu, daughter of Rantillus, for themselves and for Tiberius Iulius Adnnamus, who died at the age of 9, while the dedicants were still alive.

EDCS-14500824.text_edition: Caio Sacretio Spectatino IIviro iure dicundo Flaviae Solvae annorum L et Secundiniae Severinae coniugi filii faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-14500824.translation: To Gaius Sacretius Spectatinus, duumvir with judicial power, to Flavia Solva, 50 years old, and to Secundinia Severina, his wife; his sons took care of the making of this.
EDCS-14500824.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by the sons to Gaius Sacretius Spectatinus, a duumvir iure dicundo, and to Flavia Solva, aged 50, and to their mother Secundinia Severina.

EDCS-14500826.text_edition: Calventius Iutumari filius obitus annorum LV Iantumara coniux eius Severinus filius eius Ursa filia
EDCS-14500826.translation: Calventius, son of Iutumarus, deceased at 55 years old; Iantumara, his wife; Severinus, his son; Ursa, his daughter.
EDCS-14500826.summary: This inscription is a funerary commemoration for Calventius, son of Iutumarus, who died at 55, placed by his wife Iantumara and his children Severinus and Ursa.

EDCS-14500827.text_edition: Atitto Atevali filius nnorum LXXV hic situs est Uttu Helvissonis filia uxor Mommus Atittonis filius Conginna Quordaionis filia filius et nurus parentibus fecerunt
EDCS-14500827.translation: Atittus, son of Atevalus, aged 75, is buried here. Uttu, daughter of Helvisson, his wife; Mommus, son of Atittus; Conginna, daughter of Quordaio, his son and daughter-in-law, made this for their parents.
EDCS-14500827.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Atittus and Uttu, erected by their son Mommus and daughter-in-law Conginna, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14500828.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Viis Semitibusque pro salute sua Quintus Sabinius Asclepiades votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500828.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the Roads and Paths, Quintus Sabinius Asclepiades willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being.
EDCS-14500828.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the deities of roads, made by Quintus Sabinius Asclepiades for his health, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500829.text_edition: Caius Attius Iustus vivus fecit sibi et Tocatiae Cai filiae Romanae coniugi optimae obitae annorum L filii faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-14500829.translation: Gaius Attius Iustus made this for himself while living, and for Tocatia Romana, daughter of Gaius, his excellent wife, who died at the age of 50; their sons saw to its erection.
EDCS-14500829.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Gaius Attius Iustus for himself and his deceased wife, Tocatia Romana, with their sons overseeing the final erection, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500830.text_edition: Herculi Augusto Lucius Petilius Alianus ex voto
EDCS-14500830.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Lucius Petilius Alianus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-14500830.summary: This is a votive dedication to Hercules Augustus made by Lucius Petilius Alianus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500831.text_edition: Dis Manibus nius Victor aedilicius civitatis Iuvavensium  Q Dignilla eiius sic uriae Marcianae filiae piissimae @(obitae) nnum XXX et suis vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500831.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [---]nius Victor, aedile of the community of Iuvavum, and Q. Dignilla, for their most dutiful daughter, Marcia, who died at the age of 30, and for themselves, made this while living.
EDCS-14500831.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by [---]nius Victor, an aedile of Iuvavum, and Q. Dignilla, for their deceased daughter Marcia and for themselves while still living, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500832.text_edition: Alpinus Silvani filius obitus nnorum VIII
EDCS-14500832.translation: Alpinus, son of Silvanus, died at the age of 8 years.
EDCS-14500832.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Alpinus, son of Silvanus, at a young age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500833.translation: To [An]tonius Gemellus, aged [---] years, and Occo.
EDCS-14500833.summary: This is likely a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Antonius Gemellus and Occo, with their ages partially preserved, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500834.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Albius Florus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500834.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Albius Florus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500834.summary: This is a votive dedication to Hercules Augustus made by Albius Florus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500835.text_edition: Herculi Augusto nostro Pudentius Maximinus veteranus ex decurione alae Tampianae votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-14500835.translation: To our Hercules Augustus, Pudentius Maximinus, a veteran who was formerly a decurion of the Ala Tampiana, willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500835.summary: This is a votive dedication to Hercules Augustus made by Pudentius Maximinus, a veteran and former decurion, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500836.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Venustinus summarum signum Iovis Arubiani cultoribus cum base donum dedit
EDCS-14500836.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Venustinus, a financial officer (or treasurer) of the signum Iovis Arubiani, gave this gift with its base to the worshipers.
EDCS-14500836.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, specifically the 'signum Iovis Arubiani,' made by Venustinus, likely a religious or financial official, for its worshipers, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500837.text_edition: Mercurio aedem fecit et signum posuit Caius Togionius Cupitus Caius Togionius Summus filius refecit
EDCS-14500837.translation: Caius Togionius Cupitus made a temple and placed a statue for Mercury. Caius Togionius Summus, his son, restored it.
EDCS-14500837.summary: This inscription records the original construction of a temple and statue for Mercury by Caius Togionius Cupitus, and its subsequent restoration by his son, Caius Togionius Summus.

EDCS-14500839.text_edition: pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi et Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti [et Getae Caesaris?] Iuvavenses donum dederunt
EDCS-14500839.translation: For the health of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus [and Geta Caesar?], the Iuvavenses gave this gift.
EDCS-14500839.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the citizens of Iuvavum (the Iuvavenses) to the health of the emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla (and possibly Geta), likely a public monument or offering from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500841.text_edition: Quintus Munatius Quinti filius Ouffentina sic Lupus Saena miles cohortis VIIII praetoriae |(centuriae) Ligusti vixit nnos XXX militavit nnos XI heres faciendum curavit Aulus Terentius Priscus
EDCS-14500841.translation: Quintus Munatius Lupus Saena, son of Quintus, of the Oufentina tribe, a soldier of the 9th Praetorian cohort, in the century of Ligustus, lived 30 years and served 11 years. Aulus Terentius Priscus, his heir, had this made.
EDCS-14500841.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Munatius Lupus Saena, a Praetorian Guard soldier who died at age 30, erected by his heir, Aulus Terentius Priscus.

EDCS-14500842.text_edition: Lucius Naevius Luci filius Proculus |(centurio) cohortis Asturum nnorum XXV stipendiorum V Pacciae Cai libertae Placidae et Pacciae Cai libertae Ansirae et Naeviae Luci libertae Clementi vivus fecit
EDCS-14500842.translation: Lucius Naevius Proculus, son of Lucius, a centurion of the Asturian cohort, 25 years old, with 5 years of service, made this for himself while living, and for Paccia Placida, freedwoman of Caius, Paccia Ansira, freedwoman of Caius, and Naevia Clementia, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-14500842.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made during his lifetime by Lucius Naevius Proculus, a centurion, for himself and several freedwomen, possibly family members or associates.

EDCS-14500843.text_edition: Honoratius vilicus et Avetonia Veneria coniuges vivi sibi fecerunt
EDCS-14500843.translation: Honoratius the steward and Avetonia Veneria, his wife, made this for themselves while living.
EDCS-14500843.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Honoratius, a steward, and his wife Avetonia Veneria, for themselves during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500844.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Hermes vivus fecit sibi et Felici filio annorum VI
EDCS-14500844.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Flavius Hermes made this for himself while living, and for his son Felix, aged 6.
EDCS-14500844.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, prepared by Titus Flavius Hermes during his lifetime for himself and his deceased son, Felix.

EDCS-14500845.text_edition: Fronto Cossonis filius annorum LXXX Banona Agisi filia obita annorum LXXXV
EDCS-14500845.translation: Fronto, son of Cosso, 80 years old. Banona, daughter of Agis, deceased, 85 years old.
EDCS-14500845.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument recording the deaths of Fronto, aged 80, and Banona, aged 85, likely a couple or family members.

EDCS-14500846.text_edition: sibi et Iuliae Emeritae coniugi pientissimae annorum LXXXX vivis fecit
EDCS-14500846.translation: He made this for himself and for Julia Emerita, his most devoted wife, 90 years old, while they were living.
EDCS-14500846.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument prepared during his lifetime by a man for himself and his exceptionally devoted wife, Julia Emerita, who lived to be 90 years old.

EDCS-14500847.text_edition: Iuliae Hilarae coniugi @(obitae) annorum XL Titus Cornelius Marcellinus et Paternia Florina coniux vivi sibi fecerunt
EDCS-14500847.translation: To Julia Hilara, his deceased wife, 40 years old. Titus Cornelius Marcellinus and Paternia Florina, his wife, made this for themselves while living.
EDCS-14500847.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Hilara, set up by her husband Titus Cornelius Marcellinus and his wife Paternia Florina (presumably a later wife or perhaps a mistake in the text), who made it for themselves during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14500848.text_edition: Iuliae Procillae @(obitae) annorum XLVIII Lucius Cocceius Cupitus maritus et Iulia Iuniana et Coccei Proculus Florentina Cupitus filii fecerunt
EDCS-14500848.translation: To Julia Procilla, deceased, 48 years old. Lucius Cocceius Cupitus, her husband, and Julia Juniana and Coccei Proculus Florentina Cupitus, her children, made this.
EDCS-14500848.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Procilla, who died at 48, erected by her husband Lucius Cocceius Cupitus and their children, Julia Juniana and Coccei Proculus Florentina Cupitus.

EDCS-14500849.text_edition: Iustiniae Aprae coniugi pientissimae @(obitae) nnorum XXXVII Marcus Lollius Victor
EDCS-14500849.translation: To Iustinia Apra, his most devoted wife, who died at 37 years of age. Marcus Lollius Victor [made this].
EDCS-14500849.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a husband's dedication to his deceased wife, who died at the age of 37, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500850.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Lollio Achillae @(obito) annorum LXV et Agilliae Gallae @(obitae) nnorum LXX Albia Melitine merentibus fecit
EDCS-14500850.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Albia Melitine made this for Lucius Lollius Achilla, who died at 65 years of age, and Agillia Galla, who died at 70 years of age, for their merits.
EDCS-14500850.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Albia Melitine for the deceased Lucius Lollius Achilla and Agillia Galla, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500851.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lollius Noricus et Iulia Ingenua uxor et Iuliae Secundae sorori et Aquilino fratri sibi et suis vivi fecerunt
