EDCS-14500851.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lollius Noricus and his wife Iulia Ingenua, and for Iulia Secunda, his sister, and for Aquilinus, his brother, made this for themselves and their family while living.
EDCS-14500851.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, set up by Lollius Noricus and Iulia Ingenua while still alive, for themselves and their immediate family members, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500853.text_edition: Rennius Firmus Samianta Viveni Urso filio obito annorum XXX vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500853.translation: Rennius Firmus Samianta Viveni made this for their son Urso, who died at 30 years of age, while they were living.
EDCS-14500853.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Rennius Firmus Samianta Viveni to their deceased son Urso, who died at 30, and was set up while the dedicants were still alive, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500855.text_edition: Saxsioni sic Volovici liberto Aulia Venusta viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500855.translation: Aulia Venusta made this while living for Saxsio Volovici, her freedman, and for herself.
EDCS-14500855.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aulia Venusta during her lifetime for her freedman Saxsio Volovici and for herself, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500856.text_edition: Sextus Titianus vivus fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-14500856.translation: Sextus Titianus made this while living for himself and his family.
EDCS-14500856.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Sextus Titianus while he was still alive, for himself and his family, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500857.text_edition: Statuto filio dulcissimo obito annorum XXXI Albius Leonas et Statuta coniuges carissimi et sibi posterisque suis fecerunt
EDCS-14500857.translation: Albius Leonas and Statuta, his dearest wife, made this for their sweetest son Statutus, who died at 31 years of age, and for themselves and their descendants.
EDCS-14500857.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Albius Leonas and his wife Statuta to their deceased son Statutus, who died at 31, and for their own future use and that of their descendants, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500866.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcello Prima Nivi viva fecit sibi et Mattio Seccio hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14500866.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Prima Nivi made this monument while living for herself and for Mattius Seccius; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500866.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Prima Nivi while alive for herself and Mattius Seccius, with a clause specifying it is not for an heir, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500867.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae sacrum pro salutem sic ddominorum nnostrorum Maximini et Constantini et Licini semper Auggustorum Aurelius Senecio vir perfectissimus dux templum numini eius ex voto a novo fieri iussit per instantiam Valeri Sambarrae praepositi eqquitibus Dalmatis Aquesianis comitatensibus libens laetus merito ob victoriam factam IV Kalendas Iulias Andronico et Probo consulibus
EDCS-14500867.translation: Sacred to Victoria Augusta, for the salvation of our lords Maximinius, Constantinus, and Licinius, always Augusti. Aurelius Senecio, a most perfect man, a dux, ordered this temple to her divinity to be made anew from a vow, through the diligence of Valerius Sambarra, commander of the Dalmatian Aquensian comitatensian cavalry, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly, on account of the victory achieved on the 4th Kalends of July, during the consulship of Andronicus and Probus.
EDCS-14500867.summary: This inscription records the renovation or construction of a temple to Victoria Augusta, vowed and completed by Aurelius Senecio, a dux, for the well-being of the Augusti Maximinius, Constantinus, and Licinius, on June 28th, 310 AD, when Andronicus and Probus were consuls.

EDCS-14500868.text_edition: rio  Iuvavo et Veriae Verae Metilia Marciana marito et filiae et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-14500868.translation: Metilia Marciana made this while living for ---rius Iuvavus, her husband, and for Veria Vera, her daughter, and for herself.
EDCS-14500868.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Metilia Marciana during her lifetime for her husband (name partially lost), her daughter Veria Vera, and herself, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500869.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulius Victorinus Martialis filius obitus annorum LV Bessa Iuvenis filia uxor @(obita) annorum XLV Novella Essibni filia obita annorum XVIII Victorinus parentibus et coniugi et Victorinae filiae fecit qui per luem vita functi sunt Mamertino et Rufo consulibus et Aurelio Iustino fratri militi legionis II Italicae stipendiorum X @(obito) annorum XXX
EDCS-14500869.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Victorinus made this for his parents and his wife and his daughter Victorina, who died from the plague: Martialis, his son, who died at 55 years; Bessa Iuvenis, his daughter; Novella Essibni, his daughter, who died at 18 years; and his wife, who died at 45 years. This was made during the consulship of Mamertinus and Rufus. And for Aurelius Iustinus, his brother, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion, who served for 10 years and died at 30 years.
EDCS-14500869.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the family members of Iulius Victorinus, who erected the monument for his plague-victims parents, wife, and daughters, along with his soldier brother, and can be dated to 284 CE based on the consulship.

EDCS-14500870.text_edition: Lucio Terentio Vero IIviro Teurniae praefecto iure dicundo obito annorum  et Seppiae Cai filiae Praesentinae Lucius Terentius Verinus et  Terentius Praesentinus parentibus optumis sic fecerunt
EDCS-14500870.translation: To Lucius Terentius Verus, duumvir of Teurnia, prefect with judicial power, who died in his [---] year, and to Seppia, daughter of Caius, Praesentina, Lucius Terentius Verinus and Terentius Praesentinus thus made this for their most excellent parents.
EDCS-14500870.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Terentius Verinus and Terentius Praesentinus to their deceased parents, Lucius Terentius Verus, a prominent magistrate of Teurnia, and Seppia Praesentina.

EDCS-14500871.text_edition: Lucio Attonio Adnamato aedilicio Teurniae et Secundae Vogitouti filiae uxori
EDCS-14500871.translation: To Lucius Attonius Adnamatus, former aedile of Teurnia, and to Secunda, daughter of Vogitoutus, his wife.
EDCS-14500871.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Attonius Adnamatus, a former aedile of Teurnia, and his wife, Secunda Vogitouti.

EDCS-14500872.text_edition: Bellicus Secundi filius vivus fecit sibi et Paulinae coniugi obitae annorum L et Secundino filio
EDCS-14500872.translation: Bellicus, son of Secundus, made this while living for himself and for his wife Paulina, who died at 50 years, and for his son Secundinus.
EDCS-14500872.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Bellicus, still living, for himself, his deceased wife Paulina, and his son Secundinus.

EDCS-14500873.text_edition: Cupito Secundi obito nnorum LXXXI et Avetontiae Romanae matri Secundus filius fecit
EDCS-14500873.translation: To Cupitus, son of Secundus, who died at 81 years, and to Avetontia Romana, his mother, Secundus, his son, made this.
EDCS-14500873.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Secundus for his deceased father Cupitus and his mother Avetontia Romana.

EDCS-14500874.text_edition: Bedaio Augusto sacrum Alounarum sic Setonius Maximianus et Firminius Firminianus IIviri  Perpetuo et Corneliano consulibus
EDCS-14500874.publication: CIL 03, 05572 = ILLPRON 01503 = D 04857 = IBR 00012 = NIBayern 00044 = EQNoricum-I, 00006
EDCS-14500874.translation: Sacred to Bedaius Augustus of the Alounae. Thus Setonius Maximianus and Firminius Firminianus, duumvirs. During the consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelianus.
EDCS-14500874.summary: This is a religious dedication to the deity Bedaius Augustus of the Alounae, made by the duumvirs Setonius Maximianus and Firminius Firminianus, datable to 237 CE based on the consulship.

EDCS-14500875.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino maximo semper Augusto bono rei publicae natus sic milia passuum XX
EDCS-14500875.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, greatest ever Augustus, born for the good of the Republic. Thus, 20 miles.
EDCS-14500875.summary: This is a milestone or public inscription honoring Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantinus (Constantine the Great), marking a distance of 20 miles.

EDCS-14500877.text_edition: In honorem domus divinae Iovi Optimo Maximo Arubiano et sancto Bedaio Vindius Verus beneficarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Severianae ex voto posuit Idibus Mais Imperatore domino nostro Severo Alexandro Augusto II et Marcello II consulibus
EDCS-14500877.publication: CIL 03, 05575 = ILLPRON 01550 = Legio-II-Ital-Nb, 00005 = IBR 00020 = NIBayern 00074 = CBI 00214
EDCS-14500877.translation: In honor of the divine house, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Arubianus and to holy Bedaius, Vindius Verus, a consular beneficiary of the Second Italic Legion, pious, faithful, Severian, placed this according to his vow on the Ides of May, during the second consulship of our lord Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus and the second consulship of Marcellus.
EDCS-14500877.summary: This is a religious dedication made by Vindius Verus, a military officer, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Arubianus and Bedaius, fulfilling a vow, and can be dated to May 15, 227 CE, based on the consulships.

EDCS-14500878.text_edition: Vivi fecerunt Acutus Luci Mari Nobilis libertus et Amanda Pametae libertae
EDCS-14500878.translation: Acutus, freedman of Lucius Marius Nobilis, and Amanda, freedwoman of Pameta, made this while living.
EDCS-14500878.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by two freedpersons, Acutus and Amanda, for themselves during their lifetime.

EDCS-14500879.text_edition: Dis Manibus et perpetuae securitati Cornelio Ca |(centurioni) legionis XX militi in praetorio annos  |(centurioni) annos IIII vixit annos  fecerunt Cornelii Niceforus et Euprepes liberti eius patrono bene merenti memoriam posuerunt opto terra sit levis tibi
EDCS-14500879.translation: To the Divine Manes and to the perpetual security of Cornelius, centurion of the Twentieth Legion, who served in the praetorium for [---] years, [and was] centurion for 4 years, and lived for [---] years. His freedmen, Cornelius Nicephorus and Euprepes, made this and placed this memorial for their well-deserving patron. May the earth be light to you.
EDCS-14500879.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Cornelius Nicephorus and Euprepes, freedmen of Cornelius, for their patron, a centurion of the Twentieth Legion, expressing a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-14500880.text_edition: et Lucius Cassius Potentinus IIviri Cilone II et Libone consulibus XV Kalendas Novembres
EDCS-14500880.translation: and Lucius Cassius Potentinus, duumvir, on the 15th Kalends of November (October 18) when Cilo II and Libo were consuls.
EDCS-14500880.summary: This inscription records the names and titles of a duumvir and the consular dating of an event, likely an official act or dedication.

EDCS-14500881.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Clodio Mariano frumentario legionis VII geminae Lucanius Laurentius faceundum sic curavit
EDCS-14500881.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucanius Laurentius ensured this was made for Clodius Marianus, a grain distributor of the Seventh Gemina Legion.
EDCS-14500881.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Clodius Marianus, a soldier of the Legio VII Gemina, set up by Lucanius Laurentius.

EDCS-14500882.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Arubiano et Bedaio sancto Tullius Iuvenis beneficarius consularis legionis II Italicae Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito Idibus Maiis domino [nostro] [Imperatore Antonino]  II et Sacerdote consulibus
EDCS-14500882.publication: CIL 03, 05580 = ILLPRON 01536 = Legio-II-Ital-Nb, 00004 = D 04853 = IBR 00025 = NIBayern 00065 = CBI 00257
EDCS-14500882.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Arubianus and holy Bedaius, Tullius Iuvenis, a beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italic Antoninian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow on the Ides of May (May 15th) when our Lord Emperor Antoninus II and Sacerdos were consuls.
EDCS-14500882.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Arubianus and Bedaius by Tullius Iuvenis, a soldier of the Legio II Italica Antoniniana, dating to the consulship of Antoninus II and Sacerdos (217 AD).

EDCS-14500883.text_edition: Bedaio Augusto et Alounis sic sacrum Caius Catius Secundianus IIvir Imperatore Antonino II et Sacerdote consulibus
EDCS-14500883.translation: To Bedaius Augustus and Alounis, this sacred offering from Gaius Catius Secundianus, duumvir, when Emperor Antoninus II and Sacerdos were consuls.
EDCS-14500883.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication by Gaius Catius Secundianus, a duumvir, to the deities Bedaius Augustus and Alounis, dated to the consulship of Antoninus II and Sacerdos (217 AD).

EDCS-14500884.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque Caius Restitutius Restitutianus cG(enturio)#c(enturio)#G lIegionis#legionis#IEG etI#et#EI Iulia Honorata coniux eius votum solverunt laeti libentes merito
EDCS-14500884.publication: CIL 03, 05582 (p 1839, 2328,201) = ILLPRON 01544 = Legio-II-Ital-Nb, 00002 = CCID 00289 = IBR 00027 = NIBayern 00071
EDCS-14500884.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to all gods and goddesses, Gaius Restitutius Restitutianus, centurion of the legion, and Iulia Honorata, his wife, happily and willingly fulfilled their vow deservedly.
EDCS-14500884.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Gaius Restitutius Restitutianus, a centurion, and his wife Iulia Honorata, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all gods and goddesses.

EDCS-14500885.text_edition: Cnaeo Trebonio Firmo et Valeriae Iantumarae Caius Trebonius Faustus IIvirum et praefectus iure dicundo civitatis Aguontensium sic parentibus optimis et sibi et Treboniae Maximi filiae Exoratae coniugi fecit
EDCS-14500885.translation: For Gnaeus Trebonius Firmus and Valeria Iantumara, Gaius Trebonius Faustus, duumvir and prefect with judicial power of the civitas Aguontensium, made this for his excellent parents, and for himself, and for Trebonia Exorata, daughter of Maximus, his wife.
EDCS-14500885.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Trebonius Faustus, a local magistrate, for his parents, himself, and his wife, Trebonia Exorata.

EDCS-14500886.text_edition: Fuscia Proculi filia Secunda obita nnorum XXX hic sita est heredes pudenti matri  fecerunt
EDCS-14500886.translation: Fuscia Secunda, daughter of Proculus, who died at the age of 30, is buried here. Her heirs made this for their modest mother.
EDCS-14500886.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fuscia Secunda, who died at age 30, erected by her heirs for their mother.

EDCS-14500888.text_edition: Maximiae Publi filiae Maximae obitae annorum XXXV Iulia Avita mater faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500888.translation: For Maxima, daughter of Publius Maxima, who died at the age of 35, Iulia Avita, her mother, ensured this was made.
EDCS-14500888.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maxima, who died at age 35, set up by her mother, Iulia Avita.

EDCS-14500889.text_edition: Imperatore domino nostro Severo Alexandro Augusto III et Cassio Dione iterum consulibus Lucius Pomponius Constans et Marcus Ursinius Verus IIviri dicaverunt
EDCS-14500889.publication: CIL 03, 05587 = CIL 03, 11773 = ILLPRON 01538 = IBR 00032 = NIBayern 00066 = EQNoricum-I, 00010
EDCS-14500889.translation: When our Lord Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus III and Cassius Dio II were consuls, Lucius Pomponius Constans and Marcus Ursinius Verus, duumvirs, dedicated this.
EDCS-14500889.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Pomponius Constans and Marcus Ursinius Verus, duumvirs, dating to the consulship of Severus Alexander III and Cassius Dio II (229 AD).

EDCS-14500891.text_edition: Lucio Bellicio Luci filio Quartioni decurioni Iuvavensium IIviro iuris dicundi vixit anos LVIII Sapliae Belatumarae coniugi annorum LXII Bellicius Seccio et Bellicius Achilles cum coniugibus ex testamento faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-14500891.translation: For Lucius Bellicius Quartio, son of Lucius, decurion of the Iuvavensians, duumvir with judicial power, who lived 58 years, and for Saplia Belatumara, his wife, aged 62 years, Bellicius Seccio and Bellicius Achilles, with their wives, arranged for this to be made according to their will.
EDCS-14500891.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Bellicius Quartio, a decurion and duumvir, and his wife Saplia Belatumara, erected by Bellicius Seccio and Bellicius Achilles and their wives, as specified in a will.

EDCS-14500892.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Placidus Tinci Loli Honorati servus vivus fecit sibi et Firmae coniugi et Placidiano filio et suisque omnibus
EDCS-14500892.translation: To the Divine Manes. Placidus, slave of Tincus Lolius Honoratus, made this while living for himself and his wife Firma, and for his son Placidianus, and for all his dependents.
EDCS-14500892.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Placidus, a slave, for himself, his wife, son, and household, indicating it was created during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14500893.text_edition: Lucius Virius  @(obit-) et Lucius Virius Maximianus decurio et IIvir Claudi Iuvavi et Viria Moderata et sibi vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500893.translation: Lucius Virius, deceased, and Lucius Virius Maximianus, decurion and duumvir of Claudia Iuvavum, and Viria Moderata, made this for themselves while living.
EDCS-14500893.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument built by Lucius Virius Maximianus, a local magistrate, and Viria Moderata, for themselves and the deceased Lucius Virius, while they were still alive.

EDCS-14500894.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae pro salute Marci Lolli Prisci patroni bene merentis Ianuar(ius) Lupercus liberti
EDCS-14500894.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, for the well-being of Marcus Lollius Priscus, their well-deserving patron, his freedmen Januarius and Lupercus [dedicated this].
EDCS-14500894.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by the freedmen Januarius and Lupercus to the god Mithras, on behalf of their patron, Marcus Lollius Priscus.

EDCS-14500895.text_edition: Quintus Septueius Faustus et Attica Placidi filia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Quinto Septueio Marino filio annorum XIII obito
EDCS-14500895.translation: Quintus Septueius Faustus and Attica, daughter of Placidus, his wife, made this while living for themselves and for Quintus Septueius Marinus, their son, who died at 13 years old.
EDCS-14500895.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Quintus Septueius Faustus and his wife Attica for themselves while living, and for their deceased son Quintus Septueius Marinus, who died at the age of 13.

EDCS-14500896.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Quietus vivus fecit sibi et Veratiae Serotinae coniugi et Veratio Primioni filio obito nnorum XXX posterisque suis hoc monumentum heredem non sequitur
EDCS-14500896.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julius Quietus made this while living for himself and his wife Veratia Serotina, and for his deceased son Veratius Primio, who died at 30 years old, and for his descendants. This monument does not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14500896.summary: This is a funerary monument constructed by Julius Quietus during his lifetime for himself, his wife, his deceased son Veratius Primio, and his future descendants, explicitly stating it is not transferable to an heir.

EDCS-14500897.text_edition: Sextus Iulius Aptus veteranus ex praetorio obitus annorum L Maximilla marito optimo et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-14500897.translation: Sextus Julius Aptus, a veteran from the Praetorian Guard, deceased at 50 years old. Maximilla made this for her excellent husband and for herself while living.
EDCS-14500897.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Maximilla for her deceased husband, Sextus Julius Aptus, a Praetorian veteran, and for herself, during her lifetime.

EDCS-14500898.text_edition: Marcius Attius Marcellinus veteranus cohortis II praetoriae obitus annorum XXXV Marcius Marcus fratri ex testamento faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500898.translation: Marcius Attius Marcellinus, a veteran of the Second Praetorian Cohort, deceased at 35 years old. Marcius Marcus, his brother, had this made according to his will.
EDCS-14500898.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcius Attius Marcellinus, a Praetorian veteran, which his brother Marcius Marcus arranged to be built in accordance with the deceased's will.

EDCS-14500899.text_edition: In memoriam Marci Proculei Iustini Marcus Proculeius Martialis decurio et IIvir et Gavia Iustina parentes
EDCS-14500899.publication: CIL 03, 05597 = ILLPRON 01532 = IBR 00042 (p V) = NIBayern 00087 = EQNoricum-I, 00013
EDCS-14500899.translation: In memory of Marcus Proculeius Justinus. Marcus Proculeius Martialis, decurion and duumvir, and Gavia Justina, his parents [made this].
EDCS-14500899.summary: This inscription commemorates Marcus Proculeius Justinus, erected by his parents, Marcus Proculeius Martialis, a local magistrate, and Gavia Justina.

EDCS-14500900.text_edition: Claudius Seneca obitus annorum LXV Senecio Terentiae Honoratae servus matri et patri et sibi et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-14500900.translation: Claudius Seneca, deceased at 65 years old. Senecio, slave of Terentia Honorata, made this while living for his mother and father, and for himself and his dependents.
EDCS-14500900.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Senecio, a slave, during his lifetime for his deceased father Claudius Seneca, his mother, himself, and his household.

EDCS-14500903.text_edition: Quinto Sabinio Constituto @(obito) nnorum LXXX Constanti filio @(obito) nnorum XXX et Servandae filiae @(obitae) annorum XXV et Sabinia Optuma liberta et heres viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500903.translation: To Quintus Sabinius Constitutus, deceased at 80 years old, to Constantinus, his deceased son, at 30 years old, and to Servanda, his deceased daughter, at 25 years old. Sabinia Optuma, his freedwoman and heir, made this while living, also for herself.
EDCS-14500903.summary: This is a funerary monument built by Sabinia Optuma, a freedwoman and heir, during her lifetime, for herself and for the deceased Quintus Sabinius Constitutus and his two deceased children.

EDCS-14500904.text_edition: Nymphis Caius Lollius Honoratus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500904.translation: To the Nymphs, Gaius Lollius Honoratus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500904.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Gaius Lollius Honoratus, likely for a granted wish.

EDCS-14500906.summary: This is a brief votive inscription dedicated to a local deity, Sumeliedilis, by Cobru Aur.

EDCS-14500907.text_edition: Lucio Terentio Restituto  et Terentio Quieto Erbonia Optata coniugi piissimo et filio faciundum! cur(avit)
EDCS-14500907.translation: To Lucius Terentius Restitutus and Terentius Quietius. Erbonia Optata had this made for her most devoted husband and son.
EDCS-14500907.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Erbonia Optata for her deceased husband, Lucius Terentius Restitutus, and son, Terentius Quietius.

EDCS-14500908.text_edition: Dis  Manibus  Lucius Saplius Agrippa decurio et II vir coloniae Ovilavensium sibi et Primiae Honoratae coniugi et Lucio Saplio Optato decurioni coloniae supra scriptae filio et Lucio Saplio Agrippae filio vivis fecit et Lucio Saplio Optato aedili coloniae supra scriptae fratri @(obito) annorum L et Lucio Saplio Honorato militi praetoriano filio @(obito) annorum XXI dierum Antiochia Syria et Sapliae Optatae nepti
EDCS-14500908.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Saplius Agrippa, decurion and duumvir of the colony of Ovilava, made this for himself and his wife Primia Honorata, and for his living son Lucius Saplius Optatus, decurion of the above-written colony, and for his living son Lucius Saplius Agrippa, and for his brother Lucius Saplius Optatus, aedile of the above-written colony, who died at 50 years of age, and for his son Lucius Saplius Honoratus, a praetorian soldier, who died at 21 years of age in Antioch, Syria, and for his granddaughter Saplia Optata.
EDCS-14500908.summary: This is a large family funerary monument, erected by Lucius Saplius Agrippa for himself and multiple deceased and living family members, highlighting their civic roles and a son's military service and death abroad.

EDCS-14500910.text_edition: na obita nnorum XVIIII bene merenti ser Florentin B Sura mater fil  et sibi vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500910.translation: She who died at 19 years of age, well-deserving. Her mother Florentina B. Sura and her son made this while living for her and for themselves.
EDCS-14500910.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 19-year-old female, set up by her mother, Florentina B. Sura, and her son for their collective memorial while still alive.

EDCS-14500911.text_edition: Lollia Pocca viva fecit sibi et Viatori coniugi obito annorum L et Annoni filio obito annorum XXX posterisque suis
EDCS-14500911.translation: Lollia Pocca, while living, made this for herself and for her husband Viator, who died at 50 years of age, and for her son Annon, who died at 30 years of age, and for their descendants.
EDCS-14500911.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Lollia Pocca for herself and her deceased husband and son, as well as their future descendants.

EDCS-14500912.text_edition: nus sibi et Optatae coniugi benignae Genetivo filio vivo et Augustiano filio  vilicus  vilica
EDCS-14500912.translation: [Someone] made this for himself and for his kind wife Optata, for his living son Genetivus, and for his son Augustianus. The farm manager and the farm manager's wife.
EDCS-14500912.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription, likely for a family of estate managers (vilicus and vilica), created by an unnamed individual for himself and his wife and two sons.

EDCS-14500915.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae Lucius Restitutius Perpetuus et Attiania Matrona pro se et Restitutiis Restituto et Florino filiis votum solverunt laeti libentes meritae
EDCS-14500915.translation: To Victory Augusta, Lucius Restitutius Perpetuus and Attiania Matrona, for themselves and for their sons Restitutius Restitutus and Florinus, happily, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14500915.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Victoria Augusta by Lucius Restitutius Perpetuus and Attiania Matrona, on behalf of themselves and their two sons.

EDCS-14500917.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Secundinus Augusti nostri vilicus stationis Escensis votum rettulit libens laetus merito
EDCS-14500917.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Secundinus, our emperor's farm manager of the Escensian station, willingly, happily, and deservedly rendered his vow.
EDCS-14500917.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mithras, dedicated by Secundinus, an imperial slave or freedman serving as a farm manager.

EDCS-14500918.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Romanus Materni filius vivus sibi et Romanae Argentoniae coniugi @(obitae) annorum LXXX bene merenti
EDCS-14500918.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Romanus, son of Maternus, made this while living for himself and for his well-deserving wife Romana Argentonia, who died at 80 years of age.
EDCS-14500918.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Romanus for himself and his deceased wife, Romana Argentonia, who lived to an old age.

EDCS-14500920.text_edition: Masso Praetoriani et Valentina Flori vivi sibi fecerunt et Optato filio @(obito) annorum II
EDCS-14500920.translation: Masso, Praetorian, and Valentina Flori, in their lifetime, made this for themselves and for their deceased son Optatus, who lived for two years.
EDCS-14500920.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Masso, a Praetorian, and Valentina Flori for themselves and their deceased two-year-old son Optatus.

EDCS-14500921.text_edition: Messorius Matuco veteranus legionis II Italica obitus nnorum LXX Mottu coniux viva
EDCS-14500921.translation: Messorius Matuco, a veteran of the Second Italic Legion, deceased at seventy years of age. Mottu, his wife, while alive.
EDCS-14500921.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Messorius Matuco, a veteran of the Second Italic Legion, erected by his wife Mottu during her lifetime.

EDCS-14500922.text_edition: Lucius Cotinius Luci filius Martialis decurio Iuvavensium IIvir iure dicundo sibi et Pecciae Latinae uxori vivus
EDCS-14500922.translation: Lucius Cotinius, son of Lucius, Martialis, decurion of the Iuvavenses, duumvir with judicial power, in his lifetime [made this] for himself and for Peccia Latina, his wife.
EDCS-14500922.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius Cotinius Martialis, a local official, for himself and his wife Peccia Latina during his lifetime.

EDCS-14500923.text_edition: Novelli Optati patris @(obiti) annorum L- et Iuliae Secundinae matri @(obitae) annorum XC et Secundino Secundi avunculo @(obito) annorum X et Secundo
EDCS-14500923.translation: To Novellius Optatus, deceased father, aged 50; and to Iulia Secundina, deceased mother, aged 90; and to Secundinius Secundus, deceased uncle, aged 10; and to Secundus...
EDCS-14500923.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Novellius Optatus (father), Iulia Secundina (mother), Secundinius Secundus (uncle), and Secundus, likely erected by a family member.

EDCS-14500924.text_edition: Serenus Claudi Arionis filius Praesentinae coniugi optimae obitae annorum LIII et patri obito annorum CII et sibi vivus fecit
EDCS-14500924.translation: Serenus, son of Claudius Arion, in his lifetime, made this for Praesentina, his excellent deceased wife, who lived for 53 years, and for his deceased father, who lived for 102 years, and for himself.
EDCS-14500924.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Serenus for his deceased wife Praesentina, his deceased father, and for himself while still living.

EDCS-14500926.text_edition: Apollini Augusto in memoriam Marci Modesti Marci fili Repentini Marcus Modestius Repentinus pater veteranus ex decurio alae I Aravacorum
EDCS-14500926.translation: To Apollo Augustus, in memory of Marcus Modestus Repentinus, son of Marcus. Marcus Modestius Repentinus, his father, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the First Ala of the Aravaci.
EDCS-14500926.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Apollo Augustus, set up by Marcus Modestius Repentinus, a veteran and former decurion, in memory of his son, Marcus Modestus Repentinus.

EDCS-14500927.text_edition: Publi Aeli Flavi decurionis et IIviri et flaminis Aelium Cetiensium item decurionis et IIviri et pontificis colonia Aurelia Antoniniana Ovilavensium tribuni legionis III Augustae et Aeliae Publi filiae Flavinae filiae eiusdem et Aeli Mansueti patris eius et Orgetiae Sisiae matris ex praecepto eius Orgetia Ursa propinqua inpendio heredis fieri instituit
EDCS-14500927.translation: Orgetia Ursa, a kinswoman, by his instruction, arranged for this to be made at the expense of his heir for Publius Aelius Flavus, decurion, duumvir, and flamen of Aelium Cetiensium, also decurion, duumvir, and pontifex of the colonia Aurelia Antoniniana Ovilavensium, tribune of the Third Augustan Legion; and for Aelia Flavina, daughter of Publius, his daughter; and for Aelius Mansuetus, his father; and Orgetia Sisia, his mother.
EDCS-14500927.summary: This is a complex funerary monument commissioned by Orgetia Ursa, a relative, at the heir's expense, commemorating Publius Aelius Flavus, a distinguished Roman official and soldier, along with his daughter Aelia Flavina, and his parents Aelius Mansuetus and Orgetia Sisia.

EDCS-14500928.text_edition: Titus Flavius Campestrinus veteranus et Iulia Exorata Iulio Exorato librario consulari filio obito annorum XX et Secundinio Candidiano beneficiario consulari genero vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500928.translation: Titus Flavius Campestrinus, a veteran, and Iulia Exorata, in their lifetime, made this for Iulius Exoratus, their deceased son, a consular clerk, who lived for 20 years, and for Secundinius Candidianus, their son-in-law, a consular beneficiarius.
EDCS-14500928.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titus Flavius Campestrinus and Iulia Exorata for their deceased son Iulius Exoratus, a consular clerk, and their son-in-law Secundinius Candidianus, a consular beneficiarius.

EDCS-14500929.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Victorinus veteranus ex decurione alae Tampianae vivus fecit sibi et Flaviae Victorinae filiae @(obitae) annorum XXIII et Cosutiae Verae coniugi @(obitae) annorum L et Cosutio Firmo fE(ilio#f(ilio#E dulcissimo @(obito) annorum XV
EDCS-14500929.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Flavius Victorinus, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the Ala Tampiana, in his lifetime, made this for himself and for Flavia Victorina, his deceased daughter, aged 23, and for Cosutia Vera, his deceased wife, aged 50, and for Cosutius Firmus, his sweetest deceased son, aged 15.
EDCS-14500929.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Manes by Titus Flavius Victorinus, a veteran and former decurion, for himself, his deceased daughter Flavia Victorina, his deceased wife Cosutia Vera, and his deceased son Cosutius Firmus.

EDCS-14500930.summary: This is an incomplete inscription likely related to Marcus Ulpius Romulus, possibly a consul, indicating a public or official monument.

EDCS-14500931.text_edition: Titus Flavius Ingenuus CS  CA VCTI LII et Ingenuino nnorum  meritae  Supero L  vivus
EDCS-14500931.translation: Titus Flavius Ingenuus, citizen, for Vctilius and Ingenuinus, who were deserving, and for Superus, while alive.
EDCS-14500931.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Titus Flavius Ingenuus, a Roman citizen, dedicating a monument while still alive for Vctilius, Ingenuinus, and Superus.

EDCS-14500932.text_edition: Mogetius Martialis vivus fecit sibi et Melissae coniugi innocentissimae annorum XL filiae Capitonis
EDCS-14500932.translation: Mogetius Martialis, while alive, made this for himself and for Melissa, his most innocent wife, 40 years old, daughter of Capito.
EDCS-14500932.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Mogetius Martialis for himself and his deceased wife, Melissa, daughter of Capito.

EDCS-14500933.text_edition: Masculus Iantumari et Iulia Anna vivi fecerunt sibi et Constituto filio A
EDCS-14500933.translation: Masculus, son of Iantumari, and Iulia Anna, while alive, made this for themselves and for their son Constitutus.
EDCS-14500933.summary: This is a funerary inscription created by Masculus, son of Iantumari, and Iulia Anna, while still living, for themselves and their son Constitutus.

EDCS-14500934.text_edition: Dis Manibus Racconi Lucani obito annorum LX et Sumeloni Secundini Successianus filius fecit parentibus
EDCS-14500934.translation: To the Divine Manes. Successianus, son of Secundinus, made this for his parents, Racconus Lucanus, who died at 60 years old, and Sumelo.
EDCS-14500934.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, made by Successianus for his deceased parents, Racconus Lucanus and Sumelo Secundini.

EDCS-14500935.text_edition: Dis Manibus Senecio Lucio Monia Secundina marito optimo viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500935.translation: To the Divine Manes. Monia Secundina, while alive, made this for her excellent husband Senecio Lucius, and for herself.
EDCS-14500935.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, set up by Monia Secundina while living, for her husband Senecio Lucius and herself.

EDCS-14500936.text_edition: Ittu Ripani vivus fecit sibi et Viatorinae coniugi @(obitae) annorum LX et Hilarae Ittunis filiae @(obitae) annorum XXX
EDCS-14500936.translation: Ittus Ripani, while alive, made this for himself and for his wife Viatorina, who died at 60 years old, and for his daughter Hilara, daughter of Ittus, who died at 30 years old.
EDCS-14500936.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Ittus Ripani while living, for himself and his deceased wife Viatorina, and his deceased daughter Hilara.

EDCS-14500937.text_edition: Secundus Vercai filius et Secunda Calventi filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Firmo filio @(obito) annorum XX
EDCS-14500937.translation: Secundus, son of Vercai, and Secunda, daughter of Calvens, while alive, made this for themselves and for their son Firmus, who died at 20 years old.
EDCS-14500937.summary: This is a funerary inscription created by Secundus and Secunda while living, for themselves and their deceased son Firmus.

EDCS-14500938.text_edition: Lucius? Senecius Valens vivus fecit sibi et Brogiae coniugi @(obitae)? annorum XXXVII et Lucio Senecio Valentino filio equiti cohortis annorum XXVII stipendiorum VIIII
EDCS-14500938.translation: Lucius Senecius Valens, while alive, made this for himself and for his wife Brogia, who died at 37 years old, and for his son Lucius Senecius Valentinus, a cavalryman of the cohort, 27 years old, with 9 years of service.
EDCS-14500938.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius Senecius Valens while living, for himself, his deceased wife Brogia, and his deceased son Lucius Senecius Valentinus, a cavalryman.

EDCS-14500939.text_edition: Sul Ressatus et Atigenta coniux vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500939.translation: Sul Ressatus and his wife Atigenta, while alive, made this.
EDCS-14500939.summary: This is a funerary inscription created by Sul Ressatus and his wife Atigenta while they were still living, likely for themselves.

EDCS-14500940.text_edition: Carmo Adnami libertus sibi et Iuliae Titi libertae uxsori sic vivus fecit
EDCS-14500940.translation: Carmo, freedman of Adnamus, while alive, made this for himself and for Iulia, freedwoman of Titus, his wife.
EDCS-14500940.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Carmo, a freedman, while still alive, for himself and his wife Iulia, also a freedwoman.

EDCS-14500941.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Verus pro salute Comaciae et Commagenorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500941.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Verus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the well-being of Comacia and the Commageni.
EDCS-14500941.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Verus on behalf of Comacia and the Commageni, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14500942.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudiae Tiberi filiae Aquinae annorum XXXV Rufinius Rufus uxsori sic pientissimae faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500942.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Rufinius Rufus had this made for his most devoted wife, Claudia Aquina, daughter of Tiberius, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-14500942.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Rufinius Rufus for his deceased wife, Claudia Aquina, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-14500943.text_edition: Publio Aelio Germano veterano ex decurione et Cassiae Valentinae eius et Publiis Aeliis Sabiniano et Germano deccG(urionibus)#decc(urionibus)#DECG II(viris) iure dicundo coloniae Aureliae OQvilei=AVensium#Ovileiensium#QVILAVENSI a militiis et Ael=P(iae)#Ael(iae)#P Valentinae sorori egQ(regiae)#eg(regiae)#EQ memoriae puellae Publius Aeli=Tus#Aelius#AETUS Rufinius decurio et IIvir <iure=L>#<iure=L>#IL dicundo Ael Cetiensium perfici curavit
EDCS-14500943.translation: Publius Aelius Rufinius, decurion and duumvir with judicial power for Ael(ia) Cetiensium, had this completed for Publius Aelius Germanus, veteran and former decurion, and Cassia Valentina, his wife, and for Publius Aelius Sabinianus and Publius Aelius Germanus, decurions and duumvirs with judicial power of the colony Aurelia Ovileiensium, discharged from military service, and for Aelia Valentina, his excellent sister of memory, a young girl.
EDCS-14500943.summary: This inscription records the completion of a monument by Publius Aelius Rufinius for Publius Aelius Germanus, his wife Cassia Valentina, his sons Publius Aelius Sabinianus and Publius Aelius Germanus, and his sister Aelia Valentina, commemorating their roles as veterans, decurions, and duumvirs in the colonies of Aurelia Ovileiensium and Aelia Cetiensium.

EDCS-14500944.text_edition: Obiledo Quinti vivus fecit sibi et Speratiae Maximae vivae Speratius Lupulus annorum LXX
EDCS-14500944.translation: Obiledo, son of Quintus, made this for himself while living, and for Speratia Maxima, while living. Speratius Lupulus, 70 years old.
EDCS-14500944.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Obiledo, son of Quintus, for himself and Speratia Maxima during their lifetimes, also mentioning Speratius Lupulus who was 70 years old.

EDCS-14500945.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis consuli III patri patriae ala I Augusta Thracum optimo principi
EDCS-14500945.translation: To the Imperator Caesar, son of the divine Hadrian, grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the divine Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the --th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, the First Augustan Thracian Cavalry Wing (dedicates this) to the best princeps.
EDCS-14500945.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated by the First Augustan Thracian Cavalry Wing to Emperor Antoninus Pius, detailing his imperial titles and lineage, likely dating to the early-mid 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500946.text_edition: Caio Iulio Agricolae veterano exs sic armorum custodi alae I Augustae annorum XXXXV Titus Aelius Quartio veteranus alae eiusdem heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500946.translation: Titus Aelius Quartio, veteran of the same cavalry wing, as heir, had this made for Gaius Julius Agricola, veteran, custodian of arms for the First Augustan Cavalry Wing, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-14500946.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Titus Aelius Quartio for his fellow veteran and heir, Gaius Julius Agricola, who served as a custodian of arms in the First Augustan Cavalry Wing and died at age 45.

EDCS-14500947.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Iusto  anno--- LXX ---? f(aciendum?) c(uravit?) ---
EDCS-14500947.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aelius Iustus, in his --th year. [---] had this made [---].
EDCS-14500947.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Aelius Iustus, with the name of the dedicator and his exact age mostly lost.

EDCS-14500948.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aracintho Petroni Prisci tribuni laticlavii servo collegia Herculis et Dianae fecerunt
EDCS-14500948.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. The guilds of Hercules and Diana made this for Aracinthus, slave of Petronius Priscus, laticlavian tribune.
EDCS-14500948.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the collegia (guilds) of Hercules and Diana to Aracinthus, a slave of Petronius Priscus, a laticlavian tribune, indicating a social and religious affiliation.

EDCS-14500949.text_edition: August- sacrum in memoriam Marci Ulpi Sperati patris optimi Marcus Ulpius Spectatus IIvir iure dicundo augur municipii Aeli Cetiensium
EDCS-14500949.translation: Sacred to Augustus. In memory of Marcus Ulpius Speratus, an excellent father. Marcus Ulpius Spectatus, duumvir with judicial power, augur of the municipium Aeli Cetiensium.
EDCS-14500949.summary: This is a commemorative inscription dedicated to Augustus and erected by Marcus Ulpius Spectatus, a duumvir and augur of the municipium Aeli Cetiensium, in memory of his father, Marcus Ulpius Speratus.

EDCS-14500951.text_edition: obito expeditione Parthica annorum V et Tito Aelio et Aeliae Ursae  et ? Aelio Verino vivo  ex beneficiario procuratoris Augusti filius vivus fecit ni Aquilini filiae uxori optimae
EDCS-14500951.translation: having died in the Parthian expedition at the age of 5 years, and for Titus Aelius and Aelia Ursa, and for Aelius Verinus, still living, son of a beneficiarius of the imperial procurator, made this for himself while living, for his excellent wife, daughter of Aquilinus.
EDCS-14500951.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription, likely erected by Aelius Verinus, a living son of an imperial procurator's beneficiarius, for his deceased child who died during a Parthian expedition, and also for himself, Titus Aelius, Aelia Ursa, and his wife, daughter of Aquilinus.

EDCS-14500952.text_edition: Marco Nammio Marci liberto Surioni nnorum L Ulpius Speratus amico fecit
EDCS-14500952.translation: To Marcus Nammius Surio, freedman of Marcus, 50 years old. Ulpius Speratus made this for his friend.
EDCS-14500952.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a freedman named Marcus Nammius Surio, erected by his friend Ulpius Speratus.

EDCS-14500953.text_edition: Marco Sextio Vettoniano aedili municipii Aelii Cetiensium annorum LXX et Vindae Terti filiae coniugi annorum L Publio Aelio Marciano filio militi cohortis IIII praetoriae annorum XX stipendiorum III et Avvae Maxsimi sic filiae coniugi annorum LXXX Marco Sextio Sextiano quaestorio municipii Aelii Cetiensium annorum L filio Potta Croti filia et Marco Sextio Sammo  annorum XLVII filio suo et ae annorum et Sextio Sammo militi legionis II Severianae annorum XXV
EDCS-14500953.translation: To Marcus Sextius Vettonianus, aedile of the municipality of Aelium Cetiensium, 70 years old, and to Vinda, daughter of Tertius, his wife, 50 years old. To Publius Aelius Marcianus, their son, a soldier of the Fourth Praetorian Cohort, 20 years old, with 3 years of service. And to Avva, daughter of Maximus, his wife, 80 years old. To Marcus Sextius Sextianus, quaestor of the municipality of Aelium Cetiensium, 50 years old, his son by Potta, daughter of Crotus. And to Marcus Sextius Sammo, 47 years old, his son, and to his wife, and to Sextius Sammo, soldier of the Second Severian Legion, 25 years old.
EDCS-14500953.summary: This extensive funerary inscription records multiple generations of a prominent family from the municipality of Aelium Cetiensium, including officials and soldiers.

EDCS-14500954.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tutuia Crispae liberta viva fecit sibi et Obilo Iusti liberto marito annorum LX
EDCS-14500954.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Tutuia, freedwoman of Crispus, made this alive for herself and for Obilus, freedman of Justus, her husband, 60 years old.
EDCS-14500954.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Tutuia, a freedwoman, for herself and her husband Obilus, also a freedman, while she was still alive.

EDCS-14500955.text_edition: Dis Manibus Conalius Domiti filius vivus fecit sibi et Ioventinae Curionis filiae
EDCS-14500955.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Conalius, son of Domitius, made this alive for himself and for Ioventina, daughter of Curio.
EDCS-14500955.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Conalius for himself and Ioventina, likely his wife, while he was still alive.

EDCS-14500956.text_edition: V E Ingenuae Primigeni filiae matri
EDCS-14500956.translation: V. E. To Ingenua, daughter of Primigenius, mother.
EDCS-14500956.summary: This brief inscription is a dedication to Ingenua, daughter of Primigenius, identifying her as a mother, possibly a funerary or commemorative text.

EDCS-14500957.text_edition: Successus et Ursina coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Successiano filio obito Romae annorum XX ex Bon Belnaes servo
EDCS-14500957.translation: Successus and Ursina, his wife, made this alive for themselves and for their son Successianus, who died in Rome at 20 years old, formerly a slave of Bon Belna.
EDCS-14500957.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Successus and his wife Ursina for themselves and their son Successianus, who died in Rome and was formerly a slave.

EDCS-14500958.text_edition: Tertius Sennonis miles cohortis I Flaviae Brittonum stipendiorum VI annorum XXI Lucius Baebius Buttus heres et parentes vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14500958.translation: Tertius, son of Senno, soldier of the First Flavian Cohort of Britons, 6 years of service, 21 years old. Lucius Baebius Buttus, his heir, and his parents made this while alive.
EDCS-14500958.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Tertius, a soldier of a British cohort, erected by his heir Lucius Baebius Buttus and his parents while they were still alive.

EDCS-14500959.text_edition: Aiuccioni Nigrini filio annorum XVI et Secundinae filiae annorum VI Cupita mater viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14500959.translation: To Aiuccio, son of Nigrinus, 16 years old, and to Secundina, his daughter, 6 years old. Cupita, their mother, made this while alive, also for herself.
EDCS-14500959.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Cupita for herself and her two children, Aiuccio and Secundina, while she was still alive.

EDCS-14500960.text_edition: Marco Ulpio Melei filio Longino veterano annorum LX Firmus libertus faciendum curavit
EDCS-14500960.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Longinus, son of Meleus, a veteran, 60 years old. Firmus, his freedman, took care of having this made.
EDCS-14500960.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Longinus, a veteran, commissioned by his freedman Firmus.

EDCS-14500962.text_edition: Dis Manibus Seccius Secundinus veteranus legionis II Italicae piae fidelis et Iulia Severio coniux eius sibi et Secciae Secundinae filiae et Mariis Maximo et Secundo nepotibus suis vivi fecerunt et Iulio Apricio filio militi legionis suprascriptae beneficiario praefecti stipendiorum VI @(obito) annorum XXV
EDCS-14500962.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Seccius Secundinus, a veteran of the Second Italic Legion, 'Pia Fidelis', and Iulia Severia, his wife, made this while alive for themselves and for Seccia Secundina, their daughter, and for their grandsons Marius Maximus and Marius Secundus. And for Iulius Apricius, their son, a soldier of the aforesaid legion, a beneficiarius of the prefect, who died after 6 years of service at 25 years old.
EDCS-14500962.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Seccius Secundinus, a veteran, and his wife Iulia Severia for themselves, their daughter, their grandsons, and their son Iulius Apricius, a soldier who died in service.

EDCS-14500963.text_edition: Marmogio sacrum Marcus V Rutilius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14500963.translation: Sacred to Marmogius. Marcus V. Rutilius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14500963.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deity Marmogius, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Marcus V. Rutilius, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14500964.text_edition: Aelio Valenti veterano legionis II Italicae obito annorum LX et Annamatiae Verae coniugi eiius sic et Aelio Eutichiano sic liberto eiius sic Aurelius Ursinianus immunis legionis supra scriptae primus heres et Vera coniux Valentis fiendum sic curaaverunt et Luus libertus eiius sic
EDCS-14500964.translation: To Aelius Valens, veteran of the Second Italic Legion, who died at 60 years of age, and to Annamatia Vera, his wife, and also to Aelius Eutychianus, his freedman. Aurelius Ursinianus, immune from the aforementioned legion, first heir, and Vera, wife of Valens, ensured this was made, as did Lucius, his freedman.
EDCS-14500964.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran of the Legio II Italica, Aelius Valens, his wife, and his freedman, commissioned by his heir, his wife, and another freedman, likely from the late 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500965.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument or dedication, recording the name Vibius, son of Togio.

EDCS-14500966.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Publicio Ingenuo nnorum VIIII Caius Publicius Synecdemus nnorum L testamento f(ieri)? i(ussit)
EDCS-14500966.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Publicius Ingenuus, nine years old. Gaius Publicius Synecdemus, 50 years old, ordered this to be made by will.
EDCS-14500966.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Publicius Ingenuus and Gaius Publicius Synecdemus, commissioned by the latter through his will, indicating a family or patronage relationship.

EDCS-14500967.text_edition: Secundinus vi filius annorum LX Secundina ri filia I C R filius
EDCS-14500967.translation: Secundinus, son, 60 years old. Secundina, daughter, I.C.R. son.
EDCS-14500967.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription listing Secundinus and Secundina, indicating familial relationships with ages and partial names.

EDCS-14500968.summary: This is a fragmentary dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely made by someone named Claudius or a member of the gens Claudia.

EDCS-14500969.text_edition: Nymphis Augustis sacrum Mallius Vicarius et Valerius Crispinus aediles collegii iuvenum votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14500969.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Nymphs. Mallius Vicarius and Valerius Crispinus, aediles of the college of youths, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14500969.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Nymphs, made by two aediles from a college of youths, fulfilling a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14500971.text_edition: Aulo Barbio Auli filio Grato Velina annorum LXXV et Cominiae Titi filiae Pupae nnorum LXX Tito Barbio Auli filio Quinto militi legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXV hic siti sunt Aulus Barbius Adiutor Lucius Barbius Iustus Caius Barbius Aptus Barbia Tertia Barbia Hospita Barbia Satulla Barbia Lucia filii et filiae pro pietate
EDCS-14500971.translation: To Aulus Barbius Gratus, son of Aulus, of the Velina tribe, 75 years old, and to Cominia Pupa, daughter of Titus, 70 years old. To Titus Barbius Quintus, son of Aulus, soldier of the Fifteenth Apollinaris Legion, 25 years old. They are buried here. Aulus Barbius Adiutor, Lucius Barbius Iustus, Gaius Barbius Aptus, Barbia Tertia, Barbia Hospita, Barbia Satulla, Barbia Lucia, sons and daughters, for their piety.
EDCS-14500971.summary: This is a large family funerary inscription commemorating Aulus Barbius Gratus, Cominia Pupa, and Titus Barbius Quintus, a soldier, erected by their many children as a testament to their devotion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14500972.text_edition: Aurelius I Celeia signifer legionis II Italicae Annamato militi  @(obito) annorum  stipendiorum XIIX et Aano  @(obito) annorum fecit et sibi et  Epictesi coniugi et Aureliis Priscin et Florent
EDCS-14500972.translation: Aurelius Iucundus of Celeia, standard-bearer of the Second Italic Legion, made this for Annamatus, a soldier who died at [---] years old, with 18 years of service, and for Annus, who died at [---] years old, and for himself, and for Epictesis, his wife, and for Aurelius Priscinus and Aurelius Florentinus.
EDCS-14500972.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Aurelius Iucundus, a standard-bearer of Legio II Italica, for two fallen soldiers, Annamatus and Annus, as well as for himself, his wife, and two others named Aurelius Priscinus and Florentinus, likely from the late 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14500973.text_edition: Iulio Ptano optioni legionis II Italicae @(obito) annorum XL stipendiorum XXmarito et Rufinio Sta
EDCS-14500973.translation: To Julius Ptanus, opto of the Second Italic Legion, who died at 40 years old, with 20 years of service, my husband, and to Rufinius Sta[---].
EDCS-14500973.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Julius Ptanus, an opto of Legio II Italica, noting his age and years of service, and also mentioning Rufinius, likely erected by his wife.

EDCS-14500975.text_edition: Aelio Gannaloni @(obito) annorum LXX et Aeliae Secundae coniugi @(obitae) annorum XL Aelius Candidus
EDCS-14500975.translation: To Aelius Gannalo, deceased at 70 years of age, and to Aelia Secunda, his wife, deceased at 40 years of age. Aelius Candidus [made this].
EDCS-14500975.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Candidus to his deceased parents, Aelius Gannalo and Aelia Secunda, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-14500976.text_edition: Privatius Silvester vivus fecit sibi et Privatiae Silvinae filiae obitae annorum XII
EDCS-14500976.translation: Privatius Silvester made this in his lifetime for himself and for Privatia Silvina, his daughter, who died at the age of 12.
EDCS-14500976.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Privatius Silvester for himself and his deceased daughter, Privatia Silvina, who died at the age of twelve.

EDCS-14500977.text_edition: Publi filio Fusco militi legionis II Italicae @(obito) annorum XLV Ulpia Aventina heres coniugi karissimo et sibi et Ulpio Mariniano filio vivi fecerunt FElorius#Florius#ELORIUS Bellicianus
EDCS-14500977.translation: To Publius Fuscus, son of Publius, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion, deceased at 45 years of age. Ulpia Aventina, his heir, and his beloved wife, along with Ulpius Marinianus, their son, made this for themselves while living. F(l)orius Bellicianus.
EDCS-14500977.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Ulpia Aventina, the wife and heir, and their son Ulpius Marinianus, to Publius Fuscus, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion, and also for themselves while still living.

EDCS-14500978.text_edition: lio nitoni tonis filio ex beneficario procuratoris
EDCS-14500978.translation: To Lio Nitonius, son of Nitonius, formerly a beneficiarius of the procurator.
EDCS-14500978.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication to Lio Nitonius, indicating his name, filiation, and former military or administrative role as a beneficiarius of a procurator.

EDCS-14500979.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Rustius Iunianus beneficarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis Severianae  pro salute sua suorumque votum solvit libens merito Agricola et Clementino consulibus  pridie Idus Maias
EDCS-14500979.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Rustius Iunianus, a consular beneficiarius of the Second Italic Legion, Pious and Faithful, Severian, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of his family, in the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus, the day before the Ides of May.
EDCS-14500979.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Rustius Iunianus, a soldier, fulfilling a vow for the safety of himself and his family, dated to May 14, 230 AD during the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus.

EDCS-14500980.text_edition: Dis Manibus Faustiniano vectigalis Illyrici vilico Ingenuus filius et Felix contrascriptor ex vikario sic eius bene merenti pro pietate
EDCS-14500980.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Faustinianus, a bailiff of the Illyrian tax collection, Ingenuus his son, and Felix, a scribe from his deputy, dedicated this out of piety to him, who deserved well.
EDCS-14500980.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Ingenuus and Felix to Faustinianus, a bailiff of the Illyrian tax collection, acknowledging his good service and their piety.

EDCS-14500983.text_edition: Caius Duronius Martialis vivus fecit sibi et Proculeiae Proclae coniugi annorum XXX et Caio Duronio Ursino filio annorum XXX
EDCS-14500983.translation: Gaius Duronius Martialis made this in his lifetime for himself and for Proculeia Procla, his wife, 30 years old, and for Gaius Duronius Ursinus, his son, 30 years old.
EDCS-14500983.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Gaius Duronius Martialis for himself, his wife Proculeia Procla, and his son Gaius Duronius Ursinus, all while still living, with the ages of the wife and son recorded.

EDCS-14500988.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II pater patriae consul pro consule et Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus nobilissimus Caesar princeps iuventutis providentissimi Auggusti fecerunt ab Agunto milia passuum LVI
EDCS-14500988.translation: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, father of his country, consul, proconsul, and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, the most provident Augusti, made [this road or milestone] 56 miles from Agunto.
EDCS-14500988.summary: This is a milestone or road dedication recording the construction or repair of a road 56 miles from Agunto by the Emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, dating to their reign (217-218 AD).

EDCS-14500989.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus    I
EDCS-14500989.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, first [time].
EDCS-14500989.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely from a milestone or public work, attributing it to Emperor Claudius, possibly indicating a specific iteration of his reign or office.

EDCS-14500993.summary: This is a brief, honorific inscription dedicated to an unnamed Emperor, referring to him as the 'eternal Augustus,' likely a public dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-14500994.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex sic tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XII consul II pater patriae pro consule et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus tribunicia potestate IIII pro consule a Teurnia milia passuum IIXXX
EDCS-14500994.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia and Adiabene, greatest conqueror of Parthia, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, acclaimed imperator for the 12th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country, proconsul; and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, holding tribunician power for the 4th time, proconsul. From Teurnia, 28 miles.
EDCS-14500994.summary: This inscription records the distance of 28 miles from Teurnia, dedicated to Emperor Septimius Severus and his son Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), dating to 201 CE.

EDCS-14500997.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate consul a Teurnia milia passuum XLI
EDCS-14500997.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul. From Teurnia, 41 miles.
EDCS-14500997.summary: This inscription is a mileage marker indicating 41 miles from Teurnia, dedicated to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely dating to 244-247 CE.

EDCS-14500998.text_edition: Domino nostro Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari Pio Felici Invicto Augusto
EDCS-14500998.translation: To our Lord Marcus Iulius Philippus, the most noble Caesar, Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-14500998.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Iulius Philippus, who is likely Philip the Arab, possibly dating to 244-249 CE.

EDCS-14500999.text_edition: Inperator sic Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex sic tribunicia potestate VIIII inperator sic XII consul II proconsul et Inperator sic Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus tribunicia potestate IX pater patriae proconsul XLII
EDCS-14500999.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia and Adiabene, greatest conqueror of Parthia, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, acclaimed imperator for the 12th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul; and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, father of the country, proconsul. 42 miles.
EDCS-14500999.summary: This inscription is a mileage marker dedicated to Emperor Septimius Severus and his son Caracalla, dating to 206 CE and marking 42 miles from an unspecified location.

EDCS-14501001.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus tribunicia potestate IX imperator XII consul II proconsul et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus tribunicia potestate IIII proconsul a Teurnia milia passuum LIIII
EDCS-14501001.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia and Adiabene, greatest conqueror of Parthia, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, acclaimed imperator for the 12th time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul; and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, holding tribunician power for the 4th time, proconsul. From Teurnia, 54 miles.
EDCS-14501001.summary: This inscription records the distance of 54 miles from Teurnia, dedicated to Emperor Septimius Severus and his son Caracalla, dating to 201 CE.

EDCS-14501004.text_edition: DDominis nnostris Flavio Constantino Pio Felici Maximo victori semper  Augusto bono generis humani nato Crispo et Constantino beatissimis Caesaribus a Iuvavo milia passuum XIII
EDCS-14501004.translation: To our Lords Flavius Constantinus Pius Felix Maximus, always victorious Augustus, born for the good of humankind; Crispus and Constantinus, the most blessed Caesars. From Iuvavum, 13 miles.
EDCS-14501004.summary: This inscription is a mileage marker indicating 13 miles from Iuvavum, dedicated to Emperor Constantine the Great and his sons Crispus and Constatine II, dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-14501007.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestati sic II pater patriae consul proconsul et Marcus Opellius Diadumenianus nobilissimus Caesar princeps iuventutis Auggusti fecerunt a Viruni sic milia passuum XV
EDCS-14501007.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, father of the country, consul, proconsul; and Marcus Opellius Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth. They made this. From Virunum, 15 miles.
EDCS-14501007.summary: This inscription is a mileage marker indicating 15 miles from Virunum, dedicated by Emperor Macrinus and his son Diadumenianus, dating to 217 CE.

EDCS-14501009.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae proconsul a Viruno milia passuum XV
EDCS-14501009.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, father of the country, proconsul. From Virunum, 15 miles.
EDCS-14501009.summary: This inscription is a mileage marker indicating 15 miles from Virunum, dedicated to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely dating to 247 CE.

EDCS-14501010.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto V milia passuum XXXII
EDCS-14501010.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus. V miles, 32 miles.
EDCS-14501010.summary: This inscription is a mileage marker dedicated to Emperor Constantine the Great, likely dating to the early 4th century CE and indicating two different distances of 5 and 32 miles, likely from a shared but unspecified origin point.

EDCS-14501011.text_edition: Imperator Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul IIII VI
EDCS-14501011.translation: The Emperor Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul for the 4th time. 6 miles.
EDCS-14501011.summary: This inscription is a mileage marker dedicated to Emperor Trajan, dating to 101 CE and indicating a distance of 6 miles from an unspecified location.

EDCS-14501012.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVI consul III pater patriae proconsul VI
EDCS-14501012.translation: The Emperor Caesar Traianus Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Traianus Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixteenth time, consul for the third time, father of the country, proconsul for the sixth time.
EDCS-14501012.summary: This inscription records the full imperial titulature of Emperor Hadrian, likely dating to 132 AD based on his sixteenth tribunician power.

EDCS-14501013.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pater patriae pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis imperator II consul III VI
EDCS-14501013.translation: The Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, imperator for the second time, consul for the third time, six.
EDCS-14501013.summary: This inscription lists the imperial titles of Antoninus Pius, likely from a milestone or public monument, providing his official name and key offices.

EDCS-14501014.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIII imperator XII pater patriae consul proconsul et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus Germanicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVII imperator III consul IIII pater patriae proconsul miliaria vetustate conlabsa sic restitui iusserunt VI
EDCS-14501014.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the eighth time, imperator for the twelfth time, father of the country, consul, proconsul, and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus Germanicus maximus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventeenth time, imperator for the third time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, proconsul, ordered milestones, collapsed from old age, to be thus restored, six.
EDCS-14501014.summary: This inscription commemorates the restoration of old milestones by Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, providing their extensive imperial titles and dating the restoration to around 214 AD.

EDCS-14501015.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II pater patriae consul proconsul et Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus nobilissimus Caesar princeps iuventutis providentissimi Augusti fecerunt VI  S C LX IIII S
EDCS-14501015.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, father of the country, consul, proconsul, and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, provident Augusti, made this, six, sixty-four sesterces.
EDCS-14501015.summary: This inscription records a dedication or construction project by Emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, specifying their titles and a monetary amount, likely dating to 218 AD.

EDCS-14501017.text_edition: Imp (erator) Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate pater patriae consul IIII VIII
EDCS-14501017.translation: The Emperor Nerva Traianus Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul for the fourth time, eight.
EDCS-14501017.summary: This inscription presents the imperial titulature of Emperor Trajan, likely dating to his early reign around 101 AD and possibly from a milestone.

EDCS-14501018.text_edition: Domino nostro Cornelio Val(eriano) nobilissimo Caesari   Domino nostro Flavio Claudio Constantino Pio Felici Augusto bono rei publicae nato
EDCS-14501018.translation: To our lord Cornelius Valerianus, most noble Caesar. To our lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus Pius Felix Augustus, born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-14501018.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-emperors Valerian and Constantine I, likely a milestone or honorific inscription from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-14501021.text_edition: Imp (erator) Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus pater patriae tribunicia potestate consul pro consule imperator bis et Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus
EDCS-14501021.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, father of the country, holding tribunician power, consul, proconsul, imperator twice, and Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus.
EDCS-14501021.summary: This inscription lists the imperial titles of Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, likely dating to their joint reign between 236-238 AD.

EDCS-14501022.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pater patriae pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate imperator II consul III XII
EDCS-14501022.translation: The Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, imperator for the second time, consul for the third time, twelve.
EDCS-14501022.summary: This inscription details the official titles of Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely from a public work or milestone, dating to 140 AD based on the consular count.

EDCS-14501024.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo Britannico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III imperatori VII consuli II patri patriae proconsuli et Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Invicto Augusto Parthico maximo Britannico maximo Germanico maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVI imperatori III consuli IIII patri patriae proconsuli fortissimo ac felicissimo principi domino indulgentissimo milia passuum XI
EDCS-14501024.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the third time, imperator for the seventh time, consul for the second time, father of the country, proconsul; and to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Invictus Augustus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus Germanicus maximus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the sixteenth time, imperator for the third time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, proconsul, the bravest and most fortunate prince, most indulgent lord, eleven miles.
EDCS-14501024.summary: This inscription is a milestone dedicated to Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, highlighting their numerous military victories and imperial titles, likely dating to 213 AD.

EDCS-14501025.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Parthicus maximus pontifE(ex)#pontif(ex)#PONTIE maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XII consul II proconsul et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurellius sic Antoninus Pius Augustus tribunicia potestate IIII proconsul et pius Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar miliaria vetustate conlapsa restituerunt curante Marco Iuventio Suro Proculo legato pro praetore ab Iuvavo milia passuum XXXI
EDCS-14501025.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Parthicus maximus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, imperator for the twelfth time, consul for the second time, proconsul; and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, proconsul; and the pious Lucius Septimius Geta Caesar, restored milestones collapsed from old age, with Marcus Iuventius Surus Proculus, legate pro praetore, in charge, thirty-one miles from Iuvavum.
EDCS-14501025.summary: This inscription records the restoration of milestones by Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, under the supervision of a legate, dating to 201 AD and indicating the distance from Iuvavum (Salzburg).

EDCS-14501030.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus tribunicia potestate XV imperator III consul designatus IIII viam iuxta amnem Danuvium fieri iussit a Boiioduru sic in SALOATO milia passuum XV
EDCS-14501030.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest Parthicus, greatest Britannicus, holding tribunician power for the 15th time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul designate for the 4th time, ordered a road to be built next to the Danube River from Boiodurum thus in Saloato, for 15 miles.
EDCS-14501030.summary: This inscription records the construction of a road along the Danube River ordered by Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus) in 212 CE.

EDCS-14501035.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Masunno Antonia Nicae sic marito optimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-14501035.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Antonia Nicae took care to have this made for her excellent husband, Iulius Masunno.
EDCS-14501035.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antonia Nicae to her husband, Iulius Masunno.

EDCS-14501036.text_edition: Lotucus Cirponis filius vivus fecit sibi et Medsiae coniugi et Spectato Cuc fE(ilio)#f(ilio)#E et Surae coniugi eius vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14501036.translation: Lotucus, son of Cirpo, while living, made this for himself and for Medsia, his wife, and for Spectatus, his son, and they, while living, made it for Sura, his wife.
EDCS-14501036.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Lotucus, son of Cirpo, for himself, his wife Medsia, his son Spectatus, and Spectatus' wife Sura, all while they were alive.

EDCS-14501041.text_edition: Dis Manibus Octaviae Successi nnorum XXV Ingenuus fecit
EDCS-14501041.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Ingenuus made this for Octavia Successa, 25 years old.
EDCS-14501041.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ingenuus to Octavia Successa, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-14501044.text_edition: S pientissim EC E  annorum XXVI PVELAEN nus
EDCS-14501044.translation: S. The most devoted Puelaennus, 26 years old.
EDCS-14501044.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a funerary inscription, likely for a person named Puelaennus, who lived to be 26 years old.

EDCS-14501045.text_edition: OLN SAPV ON ANFIC ANINI IVP NEPC
EDCS-14501045.translation: OLN SAPV ON ANFIC ANINI IVP NEPC (untranslatable)
EDCS-14501045.summary: This inscription consists of highly fragmented and unintelligible text, making its purpose or content impossible to determine.

EDCS-14501073.text_edition: iusserunt patELV non fore illi nunc matris pietaten sic habet per dominica rura et licuit matri corpus sepelire dolendum quae nunc habet titulum non terrae inimicae iacentem set sic potius donis sollemnibus esse colendum Surilla ego som sic quae peremor sic filia Chloes
EDCS-14501073.translation: They ordered that her mother's devotion should not be for him now, thus she has it through the lord's lands, and it was permitted for the mother to bury the sorrowful body, which now has a title not lying on hostile earth, but rather thus to be honored with solemn gifts. I, Surilla, thus, who thus perish, daughter of Chloe.
