EDCS-14501073.summary: This is a poetic funerary inscription where Surilla, daughter of Chloe, speaks of her death and burial, emphasizing the enduring respect and solemn gifts given to her memory.

EDCS-14501075.text_edition: Bruto Gintussae filio annorum LXXXXV fecit sibi et Meleiae Bricosticis filiae uxori et Ressico filio et Arioni filio annorum XXX et Vetuo filio et Vaenoni filiae annorum XXX et Annico Ve filio annorum XXV
EDCS-14501075.translation: Brutus, son of Gintussa, 95 years old, made this for himself and for Meleia, daughter of Bricostica, his wife, and for Ressicus, his son, and for Arion, his son, 30 years old, and for Vetuo, his son, and for Vaeno, his daughter, 30 years old, and for Annicus, his son, 25 years old.
EDCS-14501075.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Brutus, son of Gintussa, aged 95, for himself and several members of his family, including his wife and children.

EDCS-14501076.text_edition: Celatus Tertulli annorum C vibus sic fecit sibi et suis Successa Successi annorum CI--
EDCS-14501076.translation: Celatus, son of Tertullus, 100 years old, while living, made this for himself and his family. Successa, daughter of Successus, 101 years old.
EDCS-14501076.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Celatus, son of Tertullus, for himself and his family while he was alive, also mentioning Successa, daughter of Successus.

EDCS-14501077.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Tapurio Vestali et Mercusenae coniugi eius Lucius Tapurius filius X legionis tribunus parentibus pientissimis
EDCS-14501077.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Tapurius, tribune of the X Legion, made this for his most devoted parents, Lucius Tapurius Vestalis and his wife Mercusena.
EDCS-14501077.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Tapurius, a tribune of the X Legion, to his parents, Lucius Tapurius Vestalis and Mercusena.

EDCS-14600001.text_edition: Iunius Sabini et Ursina Motti Sabinus Iuni filius fecit
EDCS-14600001.translation: Junius, son of Sabinus, and Ursina, son of Mottus, Sabinus, son of Junius, made this.
EDCS-14600001.summary: This inscription records the creation of a monument by Junius, Ursina, and Sabinus, likely a family undertaking.

EDCS-14600003.text_edition: Dis Manibus Secunda Missici viva fecit Lottio Camulio filio defuncto annorum XXIII
EDCS-14600003.translation: To the Divine Manes. Secunda Missici, while alive, made this for her deceased son, Lottius Camulus, who lived for 23 years.
EDCS-14600003.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Secunda Missici, while still living, dedicated a tomb to her son Lottius Camulus who died at age 23.

EDCS-14600004.text_edition: Dis Manibus Acceptiano Accepti filio ob voluntatem testamenti Secundus AVcceIpti#Accepti#VCCIPTI fratri piissimo
EDCS-14600004.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Acceptianus, son of Acceptus, according to the terms of his will, Secundus Acceptus made this for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-14600004.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Acceptianus by his brother Secundus Acceptus, fulfilling the will of the deceased.

EDCS-14600006.text_edition: Botucae Magiovindi filiae mater viva fecit
EDCS-14600006.translation: To Botuca, daughter of Magiovindus. Her mother, while alive, made this.
EDCS-14600006.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a mother for her daughter, Botuca, during the mother's lifetime.

EDCS-14600007.text_edition: Dis Manibus Capitonio Atticio Titia Atimeria fratri karissimo
EDCS-14600007.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Capitonius Atticus, Titia Atimeria made this for her dearest brother.
EDCS-14600007.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titia Atimeria to her beloved brother, Capitonius Atticus.

EDCS-14600008.text_edition: co Corbi filio et unae Galli filiae uxori vivae filii vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14600008.translation: To [---] Corbus, son of Corbus, and to Una, daughter of Gallus, his wife, while alive, their sons, while alive, made this.
EDCS-14600008.summary: This is a funerary inscription where living sons dedicated a monument to their parents, Corbus and Una, while the parents were still alive, indicating a pre-prepared family tomb.

EDCS-14600009.text_edition: Cupitus Quinti filius patri fecit et Maturo avunculo
EDCS-14600009.translation: Cupitus, son of Quintus, made this for his father and for Maturus, his maternal uncle.
EDCS-14600009.summary: This inscription records Cupitus dedicating a monument to his father and his maternal uncle Maturus.

EDCS-14600010.text_edition: Domesticus et Crescentina servi Cassio patrono
EDCS-14600010.translation: Domesticus and Crescentina, slaves, [made this] for their patron Cassius.
EDCS-14600010.summary: This inscription records Domesticus and Crescentina, who were slaves, dedicating a monument to their patron Cassius, likely after his death.

EDCS-14600011.text_edition: Caius Fabius  Advena Fabia Cai liberta Buxa vivi fecerunt sibi et suis
EDCS-14600011.translation: Gaius Fabius Advena and Fabia Buxa, freedwoman of Gaius, made this for themselves and their family while alive.
EDCS-14600011.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Gaius Fabius Advena and Fabia Buxa, a freedwoman, prepared a tomb for themselves and their family during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14600012.text_edition: Ollodevu sic Successius Florianus et Summianius Maximus
EDCS-14600012.translation: Ollodevus, thus Successius Florianus and Summianius Maximus.
EDCS-14600012.summary: This inscription, somewhat unusual in its phrasing, appears to acknowledge Ollodevus and possibly links him with Successius Florianus and Summianius Maximus, though the exact context (e.g., dedication, witness, ownership) is unclear.

EDCS-14600014.text_edition: Iunio Primi filio et Obiliae Attonis filiae coniugi
EDCS-14600014.translation: To Iunius, son of Primus, and to Obilia, daughter of Atto, his wife.
EDCS-14600014.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iunius, son of Primus, and his wife Obilia, daughter of Atto, likely by themselves or their family.

EDCS-14600015.translation: To Latinus, son of Attunis, his sister Iusta (set this up).
EDCS-14600015.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Latinus, son of Attunis, dedicated by his sister Iusta.

EDCS-14600016.text_edition: Mivilae Adiatulli Secundus Arronis filius uxori vivus fecit
EDCS-14600016.translation: Secundus, son of Arro, made this for Mivila, daughter of Adiatullus, his wife, while alive.
EDCS-14600016.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Secundus, son of Arro, for his wife Mivila, daughter of Adiatullus, during his lifetime.

EDCS-14600017.text_edition: Sextus Secundini et Iunia Firmi vivi fecerunt sibi et Mogetio filio annorum IX et Sextiano filio annorum VII
EDCS-14600017.translation: Sextus, son of Secundinus, and Iunia, daughter of Firmus, made this for themselves while alive, and for their son Mogetius, aged nine years, and their son Sextianus, aged seven years.
EDCS-14600017.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Sextus, son of Secundinus, and Iunia, daughter of Firmus, for themselves during their lifetime and for their two young sons, Mogetius and Sextianus.

EDCS-14600018.text_edition: Publius Veturius Publi libertus Amphio sibei sic et sueis sic fieri iussit arbitratu Publi Veturi Publi liberti Amphioravi fratris
EDCS-14600018.translation: Publius Veturius Amphio, freedman of Publius, ordered this to be made for himself and for his household, by the judgment of Publius Veturius Amphius, freedman of Publius, his brother.
EDCS-14600018.summary: This inscription records the command of Publius Veturius Amphio, a freedman, for a monument to be built for himself and his family, executed under the direction of his brother, also named Publius Veturius Amphio.

EDCS-14600020.translation: [Possibly: Sacrum] to Deivora, daughter of Cionus, while living.
EDCS-14600020.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly a dedication to a woman named Deivora, daughter of Cionus, set up during her lifetime.

EDCS-14600022.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, dedicated to a woman whose name ends in '-neisu' and whose father's name is lost.

EDCS-14600023.text_edition: Surus Aetae filiae et Quartae Bovieri filiae uxsori sic et sibi et Secundo filio annorum XX
EDCS-14600023.translation: To Surus, daughter of Aeta, and to Quarta, daughter of Bovierus, his wife; also for himself and for his son Secundus, aged twenty years.
EDCS-14600023.summary: This is a funerary monument for Surus, daughter of Aeta, and for Quarta, daughter of Bovierus and his wife, as well as for the dedicator himself and his twenty-year-old son Secundus.

EDCS-14600024.text_edition: Adiutorino Adiutoris et Prigmeniae Barbiani filiae
EDCS-14600024.translation: To Adiutorinus, son of Adiutor, and to Prigmenia, daughter of Barbianus.
EDCS-14600024.summary: This inscription is a dedication, likely funerary, to Adiutorinus, son of Adiutor, and Prigmenia, daughter of Barbianus.

EDCS-14600026.text_edition: Adnamus Terti filius et Devignata Tituconis filia Adnamatus Adnami et Siora Adnami filia
EDCS-14600026.translation: Adnamus, son of Tertius, and Devignata, daughter of Tituco, Adnamatus, son of Adnamus, and Siora, daughter of Adnamus.
EDCS-14600026.summary: This inscription lists multiple individuals, likely family members, including Adnamus, son of Tertius, Devignata, daughter of Tituco, and their children Adnamatus and Siora, likely for a shared funerary monument.

EDCS-14600027.text_edition: vivus fecit Avareae uxori eius pr T un
EDCS-14600027.translation: While alive, he made this for his wife, Avarea, and for himself.
EDCS-14600027.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a husband creating a tomb or monument for his wife, Avarea, and for himself, indicating it was prepared during his lifetime.

EDCS-14600028.text_edition: Quartinus Quarti et Messia Crescentis et Saxxuni et Secundino filiis
EDCS-14600028.translation: Quartinus, son of Quartus, and Messia, daughter of Crescens, [made this] for their sons Saxxuni and Secundino.
EDCS-14600028.summary: This funerary inscription records Quartinus and Messia dedicating a monument to their sons, Saxxuni and Secundino.

EDCS-14600032.text_edition: Flori Cai fili annorum XL Ianuari o optimo fecit et sibi
EDCS-14600032.translation: For Florus, son of Gaius, an excellent man, aged 40 years, and for himself, Ianuarius made this.
EDCS-14600032.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, made by Ianuarius for Florus, son of Gaius, who died at age 40, and for himself.

EDCS-14600036.text_edition: Perpetuis Imperatoribus nostris Diocletiano et Maxsimiano sic et Constantio et Maxsimiano sic nobilissimis Caesaribus milia passuum VIII
EDCS-14600036.translation: For our eternal Emperors Diocletian and Maximian, and likewise for the most noble Caesars Constantius and Maximianus, eight miles.
EDCS-14600036.summary: This is a milestone inscription, dating to the Tetrarchy (293-305 AD), dedicating a marker at the eight-mile point to the ruling emperors and caesars.

EDCS-14600038.translation: Ulpius, son of Saturninus, while alive, [made this] for himself.
EDCS-14600038.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Ulpius, son of Saturninus, prepared his own tomb or monument during his lifetime.

EDCS-14600043.text_edition: Vitalis Hilari filius vivus fecit sibi et Surae Lupponis filiae coniugi
EDCS-14600043.translation: Vitalis, son of Hilarus, while alive, made this for himself and for his wife Sura, daughter of Luppo.
EDCS-14600043.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Vitalis, son of Hilarus, creating a monument during his lifetime for himself and his wife, Sura, daughter of Luppo.

EDCS-14600045.text_edition: Vitali Festi filio coniugi et Atecto filio annorum XXX Aiiu Macci filia et Priscus Vitalis filius vivi fecerunt et sibi et Hel(viae) Bouniae et Helviae Litunae
EDCS-14600045.translation: Aia, daughter of Maccius, and Priscus, son of Vitalis, while alive, made this for Vitalis, son of Festus, her husband, and for their son Atectus, aged 30 years, and for themselves, and for Helvia Bounia, and for Helvia Lituna.
EDCS-14600045.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Aia, daughter of Maccius, and Priscus, son of Vitalis, making a monument during their lifetime for Aia's husband Vitalis, their son Atectus (who died at 30), themselves, and two other women, Helvia Bounia and Helvia Lituna.

EDCS-14600046.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio domini nostri Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Restitutius Tutor beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito  Laeto II et Ceriaile sic consulibus
EDCS-14600046.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the Genius of our lord Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Restitutius Tutor, a beneficiary of consular rank in the Second Italian Legion Antoniniana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. In the consulship of Laetus (II) and Cerialis.
EDCS-14600046.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Emperor Antoninus Pius (Caracalla), made by a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Italica Antoniniana, Restitutius Tutor, dating to 215 AD.

EDCS-14600047.text_edition: Aesonius Crescens votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600047.translation: Aesonius Crescens willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600047.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Aesonius Crescens fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity, as indicated by the standard formula 'votum solvit libens merito'.

EDCS-14600048.text_edition: Locus sepulturae cultorum Genii municipii Aguntensium Secundus Antoni Pudentis titulum sumptu suo memoriae causa donum dedit  IVC
EDCS-14600048.translation: This is the burial place of the worshippers of the Genius of the municipality of Aguntum. Secundus, son of Antonius Pudens, gave this title as a gift at his own expense for the sake of memory. IVC.
EDCS-14600048.summary: This inscription marks a collective burial plot for members of a collegium dedicated to the Genius of the municipality of Aguntum, with Secundus, son of Antonius Pudens, donating the plot or monument for remembrance.

EDCS-14600049.text_edition: Titus Pollius Severus  Titus Pollius Severus
EDCS-14600049.translation: Titus Pollius Severus, Titus Pollius Severus.
EDCS-14600049.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple record of two individuals named Titus Pollius Severus, possibly indicating a father and son or two co-dedicants on a shared monument.

EDCS-14600051.text_edition: Lucius Herennius Luci libertus Oclatius sibi et Veneriae uxori optimae vivus fecit
EDCS-14600051.translation: Lucius Herennius Oclatius, freedman of Lucius, made this in his lifetime for himself and for his excellent wife Veneria.
EDCS-14600051.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Lucius Herennius Oclatius, a freedman, prepared a tomb in his lifetime for himself and his wife Veneria.

EDCS-14600052.translation: Saturnina, daughter of Secundus, to her husband.
EDCS-14600052.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Saturnina, daughter of Secundus, dedicated to her husband.

EDCS-14600053.text_edition: Lucio Seccio Summo |(obito) et Secciae Cupitae coniugi eius parentibus optimis Lucius Seccius Summus filius vivus fecit et sibi et Iuniae Fuscae coniugi optimae hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-14600053.translation: Lucius Seccius Summus, son, made this in his lifetime for Lucius Seccius Summus, deceased, and Seccia Cupita, his wife, excellent parents, and for himself and Iunia Fusca, his excellent wife. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-14600053.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Lucius Seccius Summus the son, during his lifetime, created a monument for his deceased parents and also for himself and his wife, with a clause preventing it from being inherited by others.

EDCS-14600054.text_edition: Antoniae Iuli Prisci ancillae nnorum XII Cloutius pater vivus fecit
EDCS-14600054.translation: Cloutius, her father, made this in his lifetime for Antonia, enslaved woman of Iulius Priscus, aged 12 years.
EDCS-14600054.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cloutius, the father, to his enslaved daughter Antonia, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-14600059.text_edition: Bacacu Atuni filia viva fecit sibi et Arimano filio Arioni filio et Cotuni Messici filio coniugi carissimo vivo
EDCS-14600059.translation: Bacacu, daughter of Atunus, made this in her lifetime for herself and for her son Arimanus, for her son Arion, and for Cotuni, son of Messicus, her dearest husband, while he was alive.
EDCS-14600059.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Bacacu, daughter of Atunus, during her lifetime, prepared a tomb for herself, her two sons, and her husband Cotuni.

EDCS-14600060.text_edition: Asclepio Augusto sacrum Volusius Ursus votum solvit merito
EDCS-14600060.translation: To Asclepius Augustus, sacred. Volusius Ursus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600060.summary: This is a votive inscription where Volusius Ursus dedicated an altar or offering to the god Asclepius Augustus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14600061.text_edition: Cautopati Gavillius Respectus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600061.translation: To Cautopates. Gavillius Respectus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600061.summary: This is a votive inscription where Gavillius Respectus dedicated an altar or offering to the deity Cautopates in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14600062.text_edition: Genio pro salute Successini et Proximinae eius Primitivus libertus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600062.translation: To the Genius, for the well-being of Successinus and his wife Proximina. Primitivus, a freedman, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600062.summary: This is a votive inscription where Primitivus, a freedman, dedicated an offering to the Genius for the health and well-being of Successinus and Proximina.

EDCS-14600063.text_edition: Genio domestico Augusto sacrum Tertinius Firminus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600063.translation: To the Domestic Genius Augustus, sacred. Tertinius Firminus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600063.summary: This is a votive inscription where Tertinius Firminus dedicated an altar or offering to the Domestic Genius Augustus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-14600065.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Quartus Burri filius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600065.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Quartus, son of Burrus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600065.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a sacred monument to Hercules Augustus by Quartus, son of Burrus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14600072.translation: Petronius Urbicus, procurator of the emperor.
EDCS-14600072.summary: This inscription simply identifies Petronius Urbicus as an imperial procurator, likely part of a larger dedication or monument.

EDCS-14600073.text_edition: Caius Cassius Maximus veteranus cohortis VI praetoriae sibi et Messiae Belatussae uxori testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-14600073.translation: Gaius Cassius Maximus, veteran of the Sixth Praetorian Cohort, ordered this to be made by his will for himself and for Messia Belatussa, his wife.
EDCS-14600073.summary: This funerary inscription records that Gaius Cassius Maximus, a veteran of the Praetorian Guard, commissioned a tomb for himself and his wife, Messia Belatussa, through his will.

EDCS-14600074.text_edition: Firmino militi legionis II Italicae piae fidelis nnorum XXV mater et soror pientissimo
EDCS-14600074.translation: To Firminus, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion, 'Pia Fidelis', 25 years old. His mother and sister dedicated this to their most devoted son/brother.
EDCS-14600074.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the mother and sister of Firminus, a 25-year-old soldier of Legio II Italica, indicating a monument erected for him after his death.

EDCS-14600075.text_edition: Tiberius Iulius Giamilli filius Sextus miles cohortis Montanorum stipendiorum XL hic situs est et Ingenuae filiae vixit Iulia Sexti liberta A
EDCS-14600075.translation: Tiberius Julius, son of Giamillus, Sextus, a soldier of the Cohort of Montani, of 40 years of service, is buried here, and for Ingenua, his daughter, who lived. Julia, freedwoman of Sextus, (made this).
EDCS-14600075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Julius Sextus, a veteran of the Cohort of Montani, and his daughter Ingenua, likely commissioned by Julia, his freedwoman.

EDCS-14600078.translation: Albinus, son of Secundus, 10 years old.
EDCS-14600078.summary: This inscription records the name and age of a child, Albinus, son of Secundus, likely from a funerary monument for a young boy who died at age ten.

EDCS-14600079.text_edition: Atara Noeibionis filius Bulluni uxori et sibi et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-14600079.translation: Atara, son of Noeibio, made this while living for Bulluna, his wife, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-14600079.summary: This inscription records a tomb or monument prepared by Atara, son of Noeibio, during his lifetime for his wife Bulluna, himself, and his family.

EDCS-14600080.text_edition: Atuco Mattonis filio Ateia liberta viva fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-14600080.translation: Ateia, freedwoman, made this while living for Atucus, son of Matto, and for herself and her family.
EDCS-14600080.summary: This inscription records a monument prepared by Ateia, a freedwoman, during her lifetime for Atucus, son of Matto, herself, and her family.

EDCS-14600081.text_edition: Caius Baebius Adiectus vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Melissae coniugi carissimae et pientissimae
EDCS-14600081.translation: Gaius Baebius Adiectus made this while living for himself and for Julia Melissa, his dearest and most devoted wife.
EDCS-14600081.summary: This inscription records a tomb or monument prepared by Gaius Baebius Adiectus during his lifetime for himself and his beloved wife, Julia Melissa.

EDCS-14600082.text_edition: Publius Barbius Publi filius Niger et mater sua
EDCS-14600082.translation: Publius Barbius Niger, son of Publius, and his mother.
EDCS-14600082.summary: This inscription identifies Publius Barbius Niger, son of Publius, and his mother, likely forming part of a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-14600083.text_edition: Publius Barbius Spuri filius Primus Barbia Publi filia Rustica Barbia Publi filia Casta Quintus Barbius Publi filius Mercator uxor et filii sibi et suis vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14600083.translation: Publius Barbius Primus, son of Spurius, Barbia Rustica, daughter of Publius, Barbia Casta, daughter of Publius, Quintus Barbius Mercator, son of Publius, his wife and children, made this for themselves and their own, while living.
EDCS-14600083.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a family tomb built by Publius Barbius Primus and his family for themselves and their descendants during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14600084.text_edition: Publius Barbius Spuri filius Cirro Barbia Publi liberta Amma mater viva Publius Barbius Publi libertus Amphio Publius Barbius Spuri filius Boio Publius Barbius Spuri filius Rusticus fratres de suo fecerunt vivi
EDCS-14600084.translation: Publius Barbius Cirro, son of Spurius, Barbia Amma, freedwoman of Publius, his living mother, Publius Barbius Amphio, freedman of Publius, Publius Barbius Boio, son of Spurius, Publius Barbius Rusticus, son of Spurius, brothers, made this from their own funds, while living.
EDCS-14600084.summary: This inscription records a tomb built by several members of the Barbius family, including freedmen and brothers, for themselves from their own resources while they were still alive.

EDCS-14600085.text_edition: Publio Barbio Spuri filio Rustico Publius Barbius Publi filius Proculus vivus fecit
EDCS-14600085.translation: To Publius Barbius Rusticus, son of Spurius, Publius Barbius Proculus, son of Publius, made this while living.
EDCS-14600085.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Barbius Proculus to Publius Barbius Rusticus, indicating a living person creating a memorial for another, likely a family member.

EDCS-14600086.text_edition: Barbiae Malusiae Publius Barbius Publi filius Firmus matri et sibi et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-14600086.translation: To Barbia Malusia, Publius Barbius Firmus, son of Publius, made this for his mother and for himself and his own, while living.
EDCS-14600086.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Barbia Malusia, erected by her son Publius Barbius Firmus, who also reserved space for himself and his family in the tomb while still alive.

EDCS-14600087.text_edition: vivus fecit sibi et Caio Bottio Mercatori et Caio Bottio Adiutori annorum XXIIII et Bottio annorum VII Bottio Paulino
EDCS-14600087.translation: While living, he made this for himself and for Gaius Bottius Mercator and for Gaius Bottius Adiutor, 24 years old, and for Bottius, 7 years old, Bottius Paulinus.
EDCS-14600087.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tomb built by an unnamed individual, while living, for himself and several other individuals named Bottius, including two young children.

EDCS-14600088.text_edition: Catroni Allonis filio et Singoriae Busturi filiae filii fecerunt
EDCS-14600088.translation: To Catro, son of Allo, and to Singoria, daughter of Busturus, their sons made this.
EDCS-14600088.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by sons for their parents, Catro and Singoria.

EDCS-14600089.text_edition: Ceudo Ariomani filius Caesiae sorori et Suavi nepoti de suo vivus fecit
EDCS-14600089.translation: Ceudo, son of Ariomanus, made this from his own funds, while living, for his sister Caesia and his grandson Suavus.
EDCS-14600089.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesia and Suavus, erected by Ceudo, who was her brother and his grandfather, using his own money while still alive.

EDCS-14600090.text_edition: Chiloni Magio parens sibi et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-14600090.translation: To Chilo Magius, a parent made this for himself and his own, while living.
EDCS-14600090.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a tomb made by an unnamed parent for Chilo Magius, themselves, and their family, during the parent's lifetime.

EDCS-14600091.text_edition: Consagioni Saturionis filio et Uppiae Adiatulli filiae filii vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14600091.translation: To Consagio, son of Saturio, and to Uppia, daughter of Adiatullus, their living sons made this.
EDCS-14600091.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Consagio and Uppia, erected by their sons while the sons were still alive.

EDCS-14600092.text_edition: Dumiae Ateponis filiae et Adnamae Anectionis filiae coniugi et sibi vivus fecit
EDCS-14600092.translation: To Dumia, daughter of Atepo, and to Adnama, daughter of Anectio, his wife, he made this for himself while living.
EDCS-14600092.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dumia and Adnama, erected by an unnamed husband, who also included himself in the monument while he was still alive.

EDCS-14600093.text_edition: Quinto Fabio Cai filio Tertio Caius Fabius Cai libertus Zeno parens sibi et sueis sic et nepotibus vivus fecit
EDCS-14600093.translation: Gaius Fabius Zeno, freedman of Gaius, made this in his lifetime for Quintus Fabius Tertius, son of Gaius, and for himself and his kin, and likewise for his grandchildren.
EDCS-14600093.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a freedman for his son, himself, his family, and his grandchildren, during his lifetime, likely in the Augustan or Julio-Claudian period.

EDCS-14600094.text_edition: Caio Fabricio Cai liberto Primo Pomponia Luci filia Matta uxor viva fecit et filii filiae parenti Caio Fabricio Covincio
EDCS-14600094.translation: Pomponia Matta, daughter of Lucius, wife, made this in her lifetime for Gaius Fabricius Primus, freedman of Gaius, and the children, sons and daughters, made this for their father Gaius Fabricius Covincius.
EDCS-14600094.summary: This funerary inscription was made by a wife for her freedman husband during her lifetime, and by their children for their father, probably in the 1st century AD.

EDCS-14600096.text_edition: Itria Achillis filia Tertio Viredonis filio coniugi suo viva fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-14600096.translation: Itria, daughter of Achilles, made this in her lifetime for Tertius, son of Viredonis, her husband, for herself and her kin.
EDCS-14600096.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by a wife to her husband, herself, and her family during her lifetime, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14600097.text_edition: Tito Kanio Titi liberto Philocrati Banona coniunx de suo fecit
EDCS-14600097.translation: Banona, his wife, made this out of her own funds for Titus Canius Philocrates, freedman of Titus.
EDCS-14600097.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a wife from her own resources to her freedman husband, probably in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14600098.text_edition: Loco Congoneti et Maesiae Parti filiae coniugi suae vivus fecit
EDCS-14600098.translation: He made this in his lifetime for the place of Congonetius and Maesia Partus, his wife.
EDCS-14600098.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a man in his lifetime for his wife and for the burial place of Congonetius, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14600099.text_edition: Magiomarus Dononis filius vivus fecit sibi et Buciae Sacronis filiae uxori vivae et Secundo filio
EDCS-14600099.translation: Magiomarus, son of Dono, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Bucia, daughter of Sacro, his wife, who is alive, and for his son Secundus.
EDCS-14600099.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a man during his lifetime for himself, his living wife, and his son, probably in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14600100.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marius Euhodius et Iulia Vegeta Iuliae Euhodiae filiae @(obitae) annorum XVI et Iulio Venantio filio @(obito) annorum X parentes filiis karissimis fecerunt
EDCS-14600100.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Marius Euhodius and Julia Vegeta, parents, made this for their dearest children, Julia Euhodia, their daughter, who died at 16 years of age, and Julius Venantius, their son, who died at 10 years of age.
EDCS-14600100.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by parents to their two deceased children, a daughter who died at 16 and a son who died at 10, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14600101.text_edition: Matu Csoni Patronio Urbici filio F vivus fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-14600101.translation: Matu Csoni made this in his lifetime for Patronius, son of Urbicus, for himself and his kin.
EDCS-14600101.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Matu Csoni during his lifetime for Patronius, himself, and his family, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14600102.text_edition: Nigro Marini servo Sura viva fecit sibi et marito optimo
EDCS-14600102.translation: Sura, while alive, made this for herself and for Niger, a slave of Marinus, an excellent husband.
EDCS-14600102.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Sura, during her lifetime, for herself and her 'excellent husband', Niger, who was a slave, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14600103.text_edition: Cnaeo Octavio Cnaei liberto Teucro Vetiliai Luci filiae Senecae filii vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14600103.translation: The living children of Seneca, sons of Vetilia, daughter of Lucius, made this for Gnaeus Octavius Teucer, freedman of Gnaeus.
EDCS-14600103.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by the living children of Seneca to Gnaeus Octavius Teucer, a freedman, probably in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14600104.text_edition: Optatus Nammoni Moderati vivus fecit sibi et Avetae uxori et Maiusae filiae annorum XX
EDCS-14600104.translation: Optatus, son of Nammo Moderatus, made this while living for himself and for Aveta, his wife, and for Maiusa, his daughter, aged 20 years.
EDCS-14600104.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Optatus for himself, his wife Aveta, and his 20-year-old daughter Maiusa.

EDCS-14600105.text_edition: Publio Postumio Publi liberto Bromio Postumia Publi liberta Battu patrono et sibi viva fecit et sueis sic
EDCS-14600105.translation: Postumia Battu, freedwoman of Publius, made this while living for Publius Postumius Bromius, freedman of Publius, her patron, and for herself and her own people, thus.
EDCS-14600105.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Postumia Battu, a freedwoman, for her patron Publius Postumius Bromius, another freedman, and for herself and her family.

EDCS-14600106.text_edition: Postumia Publi liberta Adginna Publius Postumius Calonis libertus Donatus sibi et suis vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14600106.translation: Postumia Adginna, freedwoman of Publius, and Publius Postumius Donatus, freedman of Calo, made this while living for themselves and their own.
EDCS-14600106.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a monument made by Postumia Adginna and Publius Postumius Donatus, both freedpersons, for themselves and their families.

EDCS-14600107.text_edition: Postumiae Publi libertae Bouni Caius Sempronius Cai libertus Receptus sibi et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-14600107.translation: Gaius Sempronius Receptus, freedman of Gaius, made this while living for Postumia Bouni, freedwoman of Publius, and for himself and his own.
EDCS-14600107.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Gaius Sempronius Receptus, a freedman, for Postumia Bouni, a freedwoman, and for himself and his family.

EDCS-14600108.text_edition: Tertius Primigeni et Banona Secundi vivi sibi fecerunt et Primigenio filio karissimo annorum XXV
EDCS-14600108.translation: Tertius, son of Primigenius, and Banona, daughter of Secundus, made this while living for themselves and for Primigenius, their dearest son, aged 25 years.
EDCS-14600108.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a monument made by Tertius and Banona for themselves and for their 25-year-old son, Primigenius.

EDCS-14600109.text_edition: Primigeniae Longinus et Lutetua filiae et Longinae filiae
EDCS-14600109.translation: To Primigenia, Longinus and Lutetua, her daughters, and to Longina, her daughter.
EDCS-14600109.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Primigenia, with specific mention of her daughters Longinus, Lutetua, and Longina.

EDCS-14600110.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription commemorating an individual named Privatia Vafo Quinta.

EDCS-14600111.text_edition: Quarto Lutnaes liberto et Sextae coniugi fili- fec---
EDCS-14600111.translation: To Quartus, freedman of Lutnaes, and to Sexta, his wife, the children made...
EDCS-14600111.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Quartus, a freedman, and his wife Sexta, presumably made by their children.

EDCS-14600112.text_edition: Quincto Caionis filio Grata uxor Marcio filius Tertius filius Catta filia de suo fecere
EDCS-14600112.translation: Grata, his wife, Marcius, his son, Tertius, his son, and Catta, his daughter, made this from their own resources for Quinctus, son of Caio.
EDCS-14600112.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Grata, her sons Marcius and Tertius, and her daughter Catta, from their own resources, for Quinctus, son of Caio.

EDCS-14600113.text_edition: Dis Manibus Respectianus Respecti Quarto Quartici vivus fecit et sibi
EDCS-14600113.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Respectianus, son of Respectus, made this while living for Quartus, son of Quarticus, and for himself.
EDCS-14600113.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording a monument made by Respectianus for Quartus and for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-14600115.text_edition: Satucioni Montaionis filio et Boniae Privati filiae coniugi
EDCS-14600115.translation: To Satucio, son of Montaio, and to Bonia, daughter of Privatus, his wife.
EDCS-14600115.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Satucio and his wife Bonia, likely erected by their family or descendants.

EDCS-14600116.text_edition: Secundo Cirronis filio filii vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14600116.translation: To Secundus, son of Cirro; his living sons made this.
EDCS-14600116.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by the living sons of Secundus, son of Cirro, presumably for their father.

EDCS-14600117.text_edition: Somarioni Lottonis filio Castilla Sennonis filia et Corro Somarionis filius vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14600117.translation: To Somario, son of Lotto; Castilla, daughter of Senno, and Corro, son of Somario, made this while living.
EDCS-14600117.summary: This is a funerary monument that Somario's wife Castilla and his son Corro had built for him while they were still alive.

EDCS-14600118.text_edition: Statutae iuveni defunctae annorum XVIII Statutus frater fecit
EDCS-14600118.translation: To Statuta, a young woman who died at 18 years of age; her brother Statutus made this.
EDCS-14600118.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by a brother, Statutus, for his deceased sister, Statuta, who died at the age of eighteen.

EDCS-14600119.translation: Quintus Stlaccius Bassus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-14600119.summary: This inscription simply records the name of Quintus Stlaccius Bassus, son of Lucius, likely from a funerary or dedicatory context.

EDCS-14600120.translation: To Tattuno; she made this while living.
EDCS-14600120.summary: This is a funerary monument that Tattuno made for herself while she was still alive.

EDCS-14600121.text_edition: Publius Titius Publi libertus Iuvenes sic Publius Postumius Publi libertus Cardacanus Vettidia Marci liberta Flora vivi patrono parenti matri Publius Titius Publi libertus Communis vivos sic fecit de suo
EDCS-14600121.translation: Publius Titius Iuvenes, freedman of Publius; likewise Publius Postumius Cardacanus, freedman of Publius; Vettidia Flora, freedwoman of Marcus, while living, for their patron, parent, and mother. Publius Titius Communis, freedman of Publius, made this while living, from his own expense.
EDCS-14600121.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription, likely a collective tomb, where several freedpersons (Publius Titius Iuvenes, Publius Postumius Cardacanus, Vettidia Flora, and Publius Titius Communis) dedicate a monument to their patrons/parents, with Communis contributing from his own funds.

EDCS-14600122.text_edition: Titius Spuri filius Ingenuos hic situs
EDCS-14600122.translation: Titius Ingenuos, son of Spurius, is buried here.
EDCS-14600122.summary: This inscription is a funerary marker indicating the burial place of Titius Ingenuos, son of Spurius.

EDCS-14600123.text_edition: Titia Publi liberta Entia Publius Titius Publi libertus Temaio vivi fecerunt sibi et suis
EDCS-14600123.translation: Titia Entia, freedwoman of Publius, and Publius Titius Temaio, freedman of Publius, made this for themselves and their family while living.
EDCS-14600123.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument that Titia Entia and Publius Titius Temaio, both freedpersons, built for themselves and their family during their lifetimes.

EDCS-14600124.summary: This inscription simply records the name of Vetilia Philumina, freedwoman of Lucius, likely from a funerary context.

EDCS-14600125.translation: Verus, from the third cohort, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-14600125.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Verus, likely a soldier, made by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-14600129.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely specifies dimensions or a property boundary for someone named O[---].

EDCS-14600130.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, likely part of a personal dedication or record, mentioning Publius.

EDCS-14600132.translation: AMOMM and his parents to Postumius.
EDCS-14600132.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by AMOMM and his parents to Postumius.

EDCS-14600134.translation: Of Sabinus Secundus, son of PA[---].
EDCS-14600134.summary: This fragmentary inscription indicates a funerary monument belonging to Sabinus Secundus, specifying his filiation.

EDCS-14600135.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly part of a dedication or epitaph involving someone named Blastio.

EDCS-14600136.text_edition: Sacro Cotunis et Quarta Quarti vivi fecerunt sibi et Sacretiae filiae
EDCS-14600136.translation: Sacer Cotunis and Quarta, daughter of Quartus, while alive, made this for themselves and for their daughter Sacretia.
EDCS-14600136.summary: This inscription records the construction of a funerary monument by Sacer Cotunis and Quarta for themselves and their daughter Sacretia while they were still alive.

EDCS-14600137.text_edition: FDEEFETIE decurio Iae Titio vivus fecit
EDCS-14600137.translation: FDEEFETIE, a decurion, made this for Iae Titius while alive.
EDCS-14600137.summary: This inscription records that FDEEFETIE, a decurion, built a monument for Iae Titius during his lifetime.

EDCS-14600139.text_edition: Dis Manibus Saturionis Fortionis vivus fecit sibi et Vibenae coniugi et Fortioni
EDCS-14600139.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Fortio, son of Saturio, made this while alive for himself, and for his wife Vibena, and for Fortio.
EDCS-14600139.summary: This funerary inscription records that Fortio, son of Saturio, built a tomb during his lifetime for himself, his wife Vibena, and another Fortio, under the protection of the Dis Manibus.

EDCS-14600140.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valentinas sic annorum XXXV Marcellinus fecit compari dulcissimae
EDCS-14600140.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Valentina, thus 35 years old. Marcellinus made this for his sweetest companion.
EDCS-14600140.summary: This funerary inscription records that Marcellinus dedicated a monument to his beloved companion Valentina, who died at the age of 35, under the protection of the Dis Manibus.

EDCS-14600141.text_edition: Victorinus Vebeni vivus fecit sibi et Secundinae coniugi karissimae
EDCS-14600141.translation: Victorinus, son of Vebenus, made this for himself and for his dearest wife Secundina while alive.
EDCS-14600141.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Victorinus for himself and his wife Secundina during their lifetime.

EDCS-14600142.text_edition: Dis Inferis Manibus Vibenus Quarti et Quintilla Quintiani vivi fecerunt sibi
EDCS-14600142.translation: To the Gods of the Underworld, the Manes. Vibenus, son of Quartus, and Quintilla, daughter of Quintianus, made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14600142.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording a tomb built by Vibenus and Quintilla for themselves during their lifetime.

EDCS-14600144.text_edition: Publius Aelius CE V Receptusonis filius
EDCS-14600144.translation: Publius Aelius C. E. V., son of Receptusonis.
EDCS-14600144.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, identifying Publius Aelius as the son of Receptuso.

EDCS-14600146.text_edition: Dis Manibus Saturninae Manneani Optatae veti viva fecit sibi et Optato filio militi leIgionis#legionis#LIG II Italicae annorum XXV
EDCS-14600146.translation: To the Manes. Optata, daughter of Saturnina, a veteran, made this while alive for herself and for her son Optatus, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion, aged 25.
EDCS-14600146.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording a tomb built by Optata for herself and her son Optatus, a soldier who died at age 25.

EDCS-14600147.text_edition: Covnertus sic Cupiti libertus et Venima coniux vivi fecerunt sibi
EDCS-14600147.translation: Covnertus, a freedman of Cupitus, and his wife Venima, made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-14600147.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Covnertus, a freedman, and his wife Venima for themselves during their lifetime.

EDCS-14600148.translation: Catta willingly fulfilled her vow to Juno Augusta.
EDCS-14600148.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording Catta fulfilling a vow to the goddess Juno.

EDCS-14600149.text_edition: Capito Sirae filius vivus fecit sibi et Mariccae Blendonis filiae uxori et Atedunae filiae
EDCS-14600149.translation: Capito, son of Sira, made this for himself, for his wife Maricca, daughter of Blendo, and for his daughter Ateduna, while alive.
EDCS-14600149.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Capito for himself, his wife Maricca, and his daughter Ateduna during his lifetime.

EDCS-14600150.text_edition: ronis filius et Ingenua ontis filia sibi vivi ffecerunt o filio nnorum XXV
EDCS-14600150.translation: The son of ---ronis and Ingenua, daughter of ---ontis, made this for themselves while alive, and for their son, aged 25.
EDCS-14600150.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription recording a tomb built by a couple for themselves and their 25-year-old son during their lifetime.

EDCS-14600151.text_edition: Cai- L T et I Ateris  co
EDCS-14600151.translation: Caius L. T. and I. Ateris...
EDCS-14600151.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, likely part of a larger dedication or funerary monument, mentioning Caius L. T. and I. Ateris.

EDCS-14600152.text_edition: Quincto Rudsci filio et Atecinae Sinomari filiae coniugi parentes et filii filiaeque et Viriae Taeti filiae
EDCS-14600152.translation: To Quintus, son of Rudscus, and to Atecina, daughter of Sinomarus, his wife, their parents and sons and daughters, and Viria, daughter of Taetus, (made this).
EDCS-14600152.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus and his wife Atecina, erected by their parents, children, and Viria.

EDCS-14600153.text_edition: Romani servus Sextilius vivus fecit sibi et Secundinae coniugi karissimae annorum XXXV
EDCS-14600153.translation: Sextilius, a slave of Romanus, made this while living, for himself and for Secundina, his dearest wife, 35 years old.
EDCS-14600153.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Sextilius, a slave, establishing a tomb for himself and his beloved wife Secundina during their lifetime.

EDCS-14600154.text_edition: Cotuni Mocconis filiae Bardo mater et Adiutori fratri et sibi et Banonae obitae annorum II
EDCS-14600154.translation: To Cotuni, daughter of Mocconis, Bardo, her mother, [made this] and to Adiutor, her brother, and for herself, and to Banona, who died at 2 years of age.
EDCS-14600154.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Bardo, a mother, for her children Cotuni, Adiutor, and Banona, as well as for herself.

EDCS-14600155.text_edition: ILV Vindillae cundi filiae uxori tasi
EDCS-14600155.translation: ILV Vindillae, daughter of Cundus, wife of Tasus.
EDCS-14600155.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vindilla, daughter of Cundus and wife of Tasus, with an unclear initial abbreviation.

EDCS-14600156.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Instituta Sperata, who died at the age of 12, likely erected by a relative or guardian.

EDCS-14600157.text_edition: Primo Herenni Veri servo Hilaricae uxor
EDCS-14600157.translation: To Primus, slave of Herennius Verus, his wife Hilarica [made this].
EDCS-14600157.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Hilarica to her husband Primus, who was a slave of Herennius Verus.

EDCS-14600158.text_edition: Cotulo Patali filius et Vindae Arimani filiae uxori vivus fecit sibi et Iantumaro filio annorum XX
EDCS-14600158.translation: Cotulo, son of Patalus, made this while living for himself and for Vinda, daughter of Arimanus, his wife, and for Iantumarus, his son, 20 years old.
EDCS-14600158.summary: This is a funerary inscription established during his lifetime by Cotulo, son of Patalus, for himself, his wife Vinda, and his son Iantumarus.

EDCS-14600159.text_edition: Sextilius Sexti filius vivus fecit sibi et Terentiae coniugi
EDCS-14600159.translation: Sextilius, son of Sextus, made this while living for himself and for Terentia, his wife.
EDCS-14600159.summary: This is a funerary inscription created during his lifetime by Sextilius, son of Sextus, for himself and his wife Terentia.

EDCS-14600162.text_edition: Veponiae eriae Atto M fecit et sibi et Quartillae sorori et Tertino et Apollinari nepotibus et Optatae
EDCS-14600162.translation: To Veponia Eria, Atto M. made this, and for himself, and for Quartilla, his sister, and for Tertinus and Apollinaris, his grandsons, and for Optata.
EDCS-14600162.summary: This is a funerary monument established by Atto M. for Veponia Eria, himself, his sister Quartilla, his grandsons Tertinus and Apollinaris, and Optata, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-14600165.text_edition: Quinto Aurelio Primo et Auruminio Iuliae uxori heres fecit Iulia F F nnrum
EDCS-14600165.translation: To Quintus Aurelius Primus and to Auruminia Iulia, his wife, his heir Iulia F.F. made this.
EDCS-14600165.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia F.F., the heir, to Quintus Aurelius Primus and his wife Auruminia Iulia.

EDCS-14600166.translation: Primus, son of Celsus, made this for himself.
EDCS-14600166.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Primus, son of Celsus, for his own tomb.

EDCS-14600168.text_edition: Dianae Augustae pro salute imperatorum Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis et
EDCS-14600168.translation: To Diana Augusta, for the health of the emperors Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and [---].
EDCS-14600168.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta for the well-being of Emperor Septimius Severus and likely other imperial family members, dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14600169.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Culminali Chresimus Auggustorum
EDCS-14600169.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminalis. Chresimus, of the Augusti, [---].
EDCS-14600169.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminalis by Chresimus, likely an imperial freedman or slave, probably dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14600170.text_edition: Marti Augusto pro salute Primigeni Auggustorum nnostrorum vilici stationis Atrantinae
EDCS-14600170.translation: To Mars Augustus, for the health of Primigenius, our Augusti's vilicus of the station at Atrantum.
EDCS-14600170.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mars Augustus for the health of Primigenius, an imperial overseer (vilicus) at the Atrantum station, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14600172.text_edition: us Quintianus beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae templum vetustate conlabsum sic et in ruina conversum sumptu suo restituit
EDCS-14600172.publication: CIL 03, 11676 = RINMS 00107 = CIMRM-02, 01485 = ILLPRON 01947 = ROPR p 92 = Legio-II-Ital-Nsl, 00011 = CBI 00266 = RICeleia 00440
EDCS-14600172.translation: [---] Quintianus, beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italic Legion, restored this temple, which had collapsed due to age and was reduced to a ruin, at his own expense.
EDCS-14600172.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an ancient temple, which had fallen into ruin, by Quintianus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio II Italica, at his own cost, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-14600173.text_edition: A Aurelius Asclepiodotus  voto soluto donum dedit
EDCS-14600173.translation: A. Aurelius Asclepiodotus gave this gift in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-14600173.summary: This inscription is a dedicatory offering made by A. Aurelius Asclepiodotus to a deity in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14600175.text_edition: Adsallutae et Savo Augustis sacrum Caius Cassius Quietus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600175.translation: Sacred to Adsalluta and Savus Augusti. Gaius Cassius Quietus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600175.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication made by Gaius Cassius Quietus to the local deities Adsalluta and Savus, who are honored with the epithet Augusti, indicating an imperial connection or reverence, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14600176.translation: To Adsaluta Augusta. Gaius C. A. [---].
EDCS-14600176.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Adsaluta Augusta by Gaius C. A., likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14600177.text_edition: Quartus Diastulli annorum Titua Quarti annorum XXX
EDCS-14600177.translation: Quartus, son of Diastullus, aged [---] years. Titua, daughter of Quartus, aged 30 years.
EDCS-14600177.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Quartus, son of Diastullus, and Titua, daughter of Quartus, who died at age 30, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-14600178.translation: Surio made this for Invictus Mithras.
EDCS-14600178.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Surio to the god Mithras, specifically 'Invictus Mithras', dating to the imperial period and associated with Mithraic cults.

EDCS-14600179.text_edition: Nymphis Augustis sacrum Appuleius Finiti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600179.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Nymphs. Appuleius Finitus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600179.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication made by Appuleius Finitus to the Nymphs, here given the epithet Augustae, in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-14600180.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Barbius Finitus miles cohortis I praetoriae ob peregrinationis expeditionum regressus in urbem sacrum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600180.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Barbius Finitus, soldier of the First Praetorian Cohort, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow upon his return to the city from his travels and expeditions.
EDCS-14600180.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a praetorian soldier, Barbius Finitus, celebrating his safe return from journeys.

EDCS-14600181.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Lottius Felix et Marcia Bonita eius pro se et suis omnibus votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14600181.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lucius Lottius Felix and Marcia Bonita, his wife, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow for themselves and all their kin.
EDCS-14600181.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Lucius Lottius Felix and his wife Marcia Bonita for the well-being of themselves and their family.

EDCS-14600187.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelius Victor miles legionis II Italicae bello desideratus hoste Gutica sic annorum XXX Aurelia Lupula coniugi karissimo fecit
EDCS-14600187.publication: CIL 03, 11700 = ILLPRON 01770 = Legio-II-Ital-Nsl, 00012 = AIJ 00010 = Schober 00200 = AE 2012, +00075 = AE 2014, +00988 = RICeleia 00285
EDCS-14600187.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Victor, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion, lost in the Gothic war, aged 30. Aurelia Lupula made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-14600187.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelia Lupula for her dearest husband, Aurelius Victor, a soldier of the Second Italic Legion who died at age 30 in the Gothic War.

EDCS-14600188.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Maximo civis Surus ex regione Zeugma vico Hennia annorum XXV Aurelius Bassus Barathae vivus fecit fratri et Aurelio Sabino civis Surus ex regione Zeugma vico
EDCS-14600188.publication: CIL 03, 11701 = ILLPRON 01742 = AIJ 00054 = D 07207 = Schober 00237 = RICeleia 00090
EDCS-14600188.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Maximus, a Syrian citizen from the region of Zeugma, village of Hennia, aged 25. Aurelius Bassus Barathae made this for his brother while living, and for Aurelius Sabinus, a Syrian citizen from the region of Zeugma, village [---].
EDCS-14600188.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Aurelius Bassus Barathae while living for his brother Aurelius Maximus, a 25-year-old Syrian citizen from Zeugma, and also for Aurelius Sabinus, another Syrian citizen from Zeugma.

EDCS-14600189.text_edition: Caio Iunio Venusto patri sanctissimo Trosia Successa uxor pientissimo et Caio Iunio Potenti nnorum III Marcus Pompeius Po de suo faciendum curavit
EDCS-14600189.translation: To Gaius Iunius Venustus, most holy father, Trosia Successa, his most devoted wife, [made this], and Marcus Pompeius Po [---] took care of making this from his own funds for Gaius Iunius Potens, aged 3.
EDCS-14600189.summary: This monument records the dedication by Trosia Successa to her most holy husband, Gaius Iunius Venustus, and Marcus Pompeius Po arranged its construction for Gaius Iunius Potens, aged 3, suggesting a family funerary monument.

EDCS-14600191.summary: This short inscription commemorates Osicu, daughter of Ecetio, who lived to be 35 years old.

EDCS-14600192.text_edition: Vindu Comatillae viva fecit sibi et Satullae filiae annorum XXX
EDCS-14600192.translation: Vindu, daughter of Comatilla, made this for herself while living, and for her daughter Satulla, aged 30.
EDCS-14600192.summary: This funerary inscription was prepared by Vindu Comatilla for herself during her lifetime and for her daughter Satulla, who died at age 30.

EDCS-14600194.text_edition: Dis Manibus Baebius Speratinus obitus annorum LXX Speratus filius et Candidae uxori
EDCS-14600194.translation: To the Divine Manes. Baebius Speratinus, who died at age 70. Speratus, his son, [made this] and for Candida, his wife.
EDCS-14600194.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Speratus, the son, to his father Baebius Speratinus, who died at 70, and also to his mother Candida.

EDCS-14600195.text_edition: Maximus Masculi annorum LX et Amatia Kalandina coniux  Excingomaro Valentis filio annorum C et Tertiae Comati filiae uxori annorum LX Masculus Excingomari filius annorum LXXX Ecouta Sexti annorum LXX Maximilla filia Adiutus Excingomari annorum LXXX  Marcianus Masculi annorum L  Excingomari et Tertia et Maxumilla
EDCS-14600195.translation: Maximus, son of Masculi, aged 60, and Amatia Kalandina, his wife. [To] Excingomarus, son of Valens, aged 100, and Tertia, daughter of Comatus, his wife, aged 60. Masculus, son of Excingomarus, aged 80. Ecouta, daughter of Sextus, aged 70. Maximilla, daughter [---]. Adiutus, son of Excingomarus, aged 80. Marcianus, son of Masculi, aged 50. [To] Excingomarus, and Tertia, and Maxumilla.
EDCS-14600195.summary: This extensive funerary inscription commemorates multiple family members, listing their names, patronymics, and ages, indicating a collective tomb for several generations of a family.

EDCS-14600197.text_edition: Ausca Adiutoris viva fecit sibi et Ianuario filio annorum XXV Calandina Calandini filia annorum LX obita
EDCS-14600197.translation: Ausca, daughter of Adiutor, made this for herself while living, and for her son Ianuarius, aged 25. Calandina, daughter of Calandinus, aged 60, who died.
EDCS-14600197.summary: This funerary inscription was prepared by Ausca Adiutoris for herself during her lifetime and for her son Ianuarius, who died at 25, also commemorating Calandina Calandini filia, who died at 60.

EDCS-14600198.text_edition: et Primus filius vivi fecerunt sibi Iulius Iustus annorum XV
EDCS-14600198.translation: and Primus, his living son, made this for themselves. Julius Justus, 15 years old.
EDCS-14600198.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a family tomb, set up by Primus for himself and his father, including a mention of Julius Justus, aged 15.

EDCS-14600199.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valentinus libertus vivus fecit sibi et suis Ursulae coniugi carissimae annorum XXV et Ursi filio annorum V
EDCS-14600199.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valentinus, a freedman, made this during his lifetime for himself and his family, for his dearest wife Ursula, 25 years old, and for his son Ursus, 5 years old.
EDCS-14600199.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by the freedman Valentinus during his lifetime for himself, his wife Ursula, and his son Ursus.

EDCS-14600201.text_edition: Ausscus Saturnini et Verecundae coniugi optimae vivus fecit sibi et Avito filio annorum XXV
EDCS-14600201.translation: Ausscus, son of Saturninus, made this during his lifetime for himself and for his excellent wife Verecunda, and for his son Avitus, 25 years old.
EDCS-14600201.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Ausscus during his lifetime for himself, his wife Verecunda, and his son Avitus, aged 25.

EDCS-14600202.text_edition: Dis deabusque omnibus Aurelius Surus et Aelius Atticianus votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14600202.translation: To all the gods and goddesses, Aurelius Surus and Aelius Atticianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14600202.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Aurelius Surus and Aelius Atticianus fulfilled a vow to all gods and goddesses.

EDCS-14600205.text_edition: Quinto Curiatio Cai filio Festo anni I Appia Brigia mater fecit
EDCS-14600205.translation: To Quintus Curiatius Festus, son of Gaius, one year old. His mother Appia Brigia made this.
EDCS-14600205.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Curiatius Festus, who died at the age of one, set up by his mother Appia Brigia.

EDCS-14600206.text_edition: Lucio Memmio Luci liberto Andriae annorum LXXXX et Acauconi coniugi et Lucio Memmio Scorpo annorum LX et Musae coniugi annorum LV Cosmus filius faciendum curavit
EDCS-14600206.translation: Cosmus, the son, took care of having this made for Lucius Memmius Andrias, freedman of Lucius, 90 years old, and for his wife Acauco, and for Lucius Memmius Scorpus, 60 years old, and for his wife Musa, 55 years old.
EDCS-14600206.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Memmius Andrias and his wife Acauco, and for Lucius Memmius Scorpus and his wife Musa, commissioned by their son Cosmus.

EDCS-14600207.text_edition: Noiibio sic Docnimari annorum XXXV hic situs est
EDCS-14600207.translation: Noiibio, thus Docnimar, 35 years old, lies here.
EDCS-14600207.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Noiibio, also known as Docnimar, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-14600208.text_edition: Quartus Cattaes filius vivus fecit sibi et Sammuni Castionis filiae coniugi pientissimae et fidelissimae et Sexto filio obito annorum XX
EDCS-14600208.translation: Quartus, son of Catta, made this during his lifetime for himself and for Sammunis, daughter of Castio, his most devoted and faithful wife, and for his deceased son Sextus, 20 years old.
EDCS-14600208.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quartus during his lifetime for himself, his wife Sammunis, and his deceased son Sextus, aged 20.

EDCS-14600209.text_edition: Vitalis Secundi libertus et Annia Botta vivi fecerunt sibi et Secundinae filiae annorum VII defunctae et Ingenuo Sabini et Vitalini
EDCS-14600209.translation: Vitalis, freedman of Secundus, and Annia Botta, made this during their lifetimes for themselves and for their deceased daughter Secundina, 7 years old, and for Ingenuus, son of Sabinus, and for Vitalinus.
EDCS-14600209.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Vitalis and Annia Botta during their lifetimes for themselves, their deceased daughter Secundina, and also mentioning Ingenuus and Vitalinus.

EDCS-14600210.summary: This fragment appears to be a part of an inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, mentioning a son of Quintus.

EDCS-14600212.text_edition: Triccus Marini filius et Spectata coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Aelio Valerino annorum XX
EDCS-14600212.translation: Triccus, son of Marinus, and his wife Spectata, made this in their lifetimes for themselves and for Aelius Valerinus, aged 20.
EDCS-14600212.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a burial plot made by Triccus and Spectata for themselves and Aelius Valerinus.

EDCS-14600213.text_edition: Quintus Veratius Honoratus decurio Iuli Carnici sibi et Paganiae Piae uxori optimae et Quintus Veratius Iuventianus sibi et Caeliae Cai filiae Ritullae uxori pudentissimae
EDCS-14600213.translation: Quintus Veratius Honoratus, decurion of Iulium Carnicum, for himself and Pagania Pia, his excellent wife, and Quintus Veratius Iuventianus for himself and Caelia, daughter of Gaius, Ritulla, his most modest wife.
EDCS-14600213.summary: This is likely a funerary monument dedicated by two men, Quintus Veratius Honoratus and Quintus Veratius Iuventianus, for themselves and their respective wives, with Honoratus holding the civic rank of decurion in Iulium Carnicum.

EDCS-14600214.text_edition: Cabalio Sauri filio et Dresiae Busturi filiae coniugi
EDCS-14600214.translation: To Cabalius, son of Saurus, and to Dresia, daughter of Busturus, his wife.
EDCS-14600214.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cabalius, son of Saurus, and his wife Dresia, daughter of Busturus.

EDCS-14600217.text_edition: Herculi et Victoriae Augustae sacrum pro salute et adventu Marci Munati Sullae Cerialis clarissimi viri optimi praesidis et integerrimi Vibena Vibeni et Finitus Corbi maritus libentes votum solverunt
EDCS-14600217.translation: Sacred to Hercules and Victoria Augusta, for the health and arrival of Marcus Munatius Sulla Cerialis, most distinguished man, excellent and most upright governor. Vibena, daughter of Vibeni, and Finitus, husband of Corbus, willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14600217.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules and Victoria Augusta for the well-being and arrival of Marcus Munatius Sulla Cerialis, a high-ranking governor, by Vibena and Finitus.

EDCS-14600218.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae Publius Aelius S et Vindia Vera votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14600218.translation: To Victoria Augusta, Publius Aelius S[---] and Vindia Vera willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-14600218.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Victoria Augusta by Publius Aelius and Vindia Vera.

EDCS-14600219.text_edition: Attus Oiconis filius Victoriae Augustae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600219.translation: Attus, son of Oico, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Victoria Augusta.
EDCS-14600219.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Victoria Augusta by Attus, son of Oico.

EDCS-14600220.text_edition: nio Marco Valerio Cottae militi legionis XX Valeriae victricis
EDCS-14600220.translation: [---]nio Marco Valerio Cottae, soldier of the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix.
EDCS-14600220.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating a soldier named Marcus Valerius Cotta, who served in the Twentieth Legion Valeria Victrix.

EDCS-14600222.text_edition: Baro Masculi annorum LXX et Antoniae Respecti libertae annorum et Baro filio Respect[us &
EDCS-14600222.translation: Baro, son of Masculi, aged 70, and to Antonia, freedwoman of Respectus, aged [---], and to Baro, son of Respectus [---].
EDCS-14600222.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baro, son of Masculi, Antonia, a freedwoman, and a son also named Baro, likely related to Respectus.

EDCS-14600223.text_edition: Candidus Vibeni filius vivus fecit sibi et Mentitae Amoeni filiae coniugi optimae et Candidiano filio militi alae Aurianae annorum XX
EDCS-14600223.translation: Candidus, son of Vibeni, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Mentita, daughter of Amoenus, his excellent wife, and for Candidianus, his son, a soldier of the Ala Auriana, aged 20.
EDCS-14600223.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Candidus during his lifetime for himself, his wife Mentita, and their son Candidianus, a soldier.

EDCS-14600224.text_edition: Tertio Quadrati Tertulla Optati viva fecit sibi et marito
EDCS-14600224.translation: Tertulla, daughter of Optatus, made this in her lifetime for herself and for Tertius, son of Quadratus, her husband.
EDCS-14600224.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Tertulla during her lifetime for herself and her husband, Tertius.

EDCS-14600226.text_edition: Gratus et Ursicinus et Paternus filii fecerunt sibi et parentibus Urso et Optatae coniugi
EDCS-14600226.translation: Gratus, Ursicinus, and Paternus, the sons, made this for themselves and for their parents, Ursus and Optata, his wife.
EDCS-14600226.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by three sons for themselves and their parents, Ursus and Optata.

EDCS-14600227.text_edition: Asclepio Augusto Quintus Sabinius Onesimus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600227.translation: To Asclepius Augustus, Quintus Sabinius Onesimus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600227.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Quintus Sabinius Onesimus to the god Asclepius Augustus.

EDCS-14600228.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et omnibus dibus Marcus Ulpius Philippus beneficiarius Egnati Prisci procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600228.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all the gods, Marcus Ulpius Philippus, a beneficiarius of Egnatius Priscus, the imperial procurator, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600228.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Marcus Ulpius Philippus, a soldier serving an imperial procurator, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all the gods.

EDCS-14600229.text_edition: Victoriae Augustae sacrum Fortunatus VRVL IFORI votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600229.translation: Sacred to Victory Augusta, Fortunatus VRVL IFORI willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600229.summary: This inscription is a sacred votive offering dedicated by Fortunatus to Victory Augusta.

EDCS-14600230.text_edition: tinus Marci Artori Iactualis libertus III coniiugi fecit
EDCS-14600230.translation: [---]tinus, freedman of Marcus Artorius Iactualis, made this for his wife.
EDCS-14600230.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by a freedman named [---]tinus for his wife.

EDCS-14600232.text_edition: Faustus Passeris obitus annorum LX Ategenta Iuvenalis filia sibi et marito viva fecit
EDCS-14600232.translation: Faustus Passeris, who died at 60 years of age. Ategenta Iuvenalis, his daughter, made this for herself and her living husband.
EDCS-14600232.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Ategenta Iuvenalis for her deceased father Faustus Passeris, and for herself and her living husband.

EDCS-14600233.text_edition: Ianuarius Lolli Prisci libertus obitus annorum LXXV Candida marito carissimo viva fecit
EDCS-14600233.translation: Ianuarius, freedman of Lollius Priscus, who died at 75 years of age. Candida, while living, made this for her dearest husband.
EDCS-14600233.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Candida for her deceased husband Ianuarius, a freedman of Lollius Priscus.

EDCS-14600234.text_edition: Lollius Martius et Lollius Antolianus sibi  et Lolliae Martiae obitae annorum XXXVII
EDCS-14600234.translation: Lollius Martius and Lollius Antolianus made this for themselves and for Lollia Martia, who died at 37 years of age.
EDCS-14600234.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Lollius Martius and Lollius Antolianus for themselves and for the deceased Lollia Martia.

EDCS-14600236.text_edition: Peregrino Iuli Moderati servo nnorum XXI Speratus et Peregrina parentes vivi fecerunt
EDCS-14600236.translation: To Peregrinus, slave of Iulius Moderatus, who lived 21 years. Speratus and Peregrina, his parents, made this while living.
EDCS-14600236.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Speratus and Peregrina for their deceased son Peregrinus, a slave of Iulius Moderatus.

EDCS-14600241.text_edition: Bedaio Augusto Publius Domitius Flaccus et Quintus Lucretius Optatus IIviri Pompeiano et Avito consulibus
EDCS-14600241.publication: CIL 03, 11777 = ILLPRON 01504 = D 04854 = IBR 00014 = NIBayern 00046 = EQNoricum-I, 00016
EDCS-14600241.translation: To Bedaius Augustus, Publius Domitius Flaccus and Quintus Lucretius Optatus, duoviri, in the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.
EDCS-14600241.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deity Bedaius Augustus, made by two magistrates (duoviri) and dated to the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus (likely 209 CE).

EDCS-14600242.text_edition: Bedaio Augusto sacrum Alonarum Lucius Cassius Lucullus et Caius Vindius Constans IIviri Fusco II et Dextro consulibus
EDCS-14600242.publication: CIL 03, 11778 = ILLPRON 01500 = D 04855 = IBR 00015 = NIBayern 00047 = EQNoricum-I, 00017
EDCS-14600242.translation: Sacred to Bedaius Augustus. Lucius Cassius Lucullus and Gaius Vindius Constans, duoviri, [dedicated this] to the Alounae, in the consulship of Fuscus II and Dexter.
EDCS-14600242.summary: This inscription records a dedication by two duoviri, Lucius Cassius Lucullus and Gaius Vindius Constans, to the deity Bedaius Augustus and the Alounae, dated to the year 225 AD by the specified consuls.

EDCS-14600243.text_edition: Sacro Alounarum sic Augustarum Nonius Iuvenalis et Lucius Vigrius Victorinus Gordiano et Pompeiano consulibus votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-14600243.publication: CIL 03, 11779 = ILLPRON 01501 = D 04856 = IBR 00016 = NIBayern 00048 = EQNoricum-I, 00018
EDCS-14600243.translation: Sacred to the Alounae, thus to the Augustae, Nonius Iuvenalis and Lucius Vigrius Victorinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow in the consulship of Gordianus and Pompeianus.
EDCS-14600243.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow made by Nonius Iuvenalis and Lucius Vigrius Victorinus to the Alounae and Augustae, dated to the year 241 AD by the specified consuls.

EDCS-14600244.text_edition: XII IIviri Glabrione et Homullo consulibus
EDCS-14600244.translation: The XII duoviri [dedicated this] in the consulship of Glabrio and Homullus.
EDCS-14600244.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication by twelve duoviri, dated to the year 152 AD by the specified consuls.

EDCS-14600245.text_edition: Mariae Cai filiae Avitae Publius Septimius Ursus coniugi testamento poni iussit Annia Avita mater posuit   Aeliis Ovilavis Annia Avita posuit
EDCS-14600245.translation: For Maria Avita, daughter of Gaius, Publius Septimius Ursus ordered this to be placed in his will for his wife. Annia Avita, her mother, placed it. For the Aelii Ovilaves, Annia Avita placed this.
EDCS-14600245.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Maria Avita, initially commissioned by her husband Publius Septimius Ursus, and later placed by her mother Annia Avita, who also dedicated a separate monument for the Aelii Ovilaves.

EDCS-14600247.text_edition: Caius Betuus Communis vivus fecit sibi et Flaviae Crescentinae coniugi @(obitae) annorum XXXV
EDCS-14600247.translation: Gaius Betuus Communis made this for himself while alive, and for Flavia Crescentina, his wife, who died at 35 years of age.
EDCS-14600247.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Betuus Communis for himself and his deceased wife, Flavia Crescentina.

EDCS-14600248.text_edition: Lucio Papio Silvestri patri Papio Hilarianoanoeterano? ex? b(ene)f(iciario) consularis  anno--- LI heres ex  testamento faciendum curavit
EDCS-14600248.translation: To Lucius Papius Silvester, father, to Papius Hilarianus, veteran, beneficiary of the consular. In the year [---] LI. His heir, according to his will, ensured this was made.
EDCS-14600248.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Papius Silvester and Papius Hilarianus, a veteran and consular beneficiary, erected by an heir according to a will.

EDCS-14600249.text_edition: Sagittiae Cai filiae Florentinae annorum XX Titus Iulius Verecundus coniugi vivus fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-14600249.translation: For Sagittia Florentina, daughter of Gaius, 20 years old, Titus Iulius Verecundus made this for his wife while alive, for himself and his family.
EDCS-14600249.summary: This is a funerary inscription created by Titus Iulius Verecundus for his deceased wife, Sagittia Florentina, and also for himself and his family.

EDCS-14600254.text_edition: ymphi Pri Titus Flavius Magister T F
EDCS-14600254.translation: Nymphs, Pri[mus/mus?]. Titus Flavius Magister. T.F.
EDCS-14600254.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly dedicated to nymphs, with the names Titus Flavius Magister and an abbreviation T.F. also present.

EDCS-14600255.text_edition: sacrum Caius Orgetius Quartus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600255.translation: Sacred. Gaius Orgetius Quartus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14600255.summary: This inscription records Gaius Orgetius Quartus fulfilling a vow to an unspecified deity or deities.

EDCS-14600256.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque omnibus Publi Aeli Saturninus Naevianus Cupitus
EDCS-14600256.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to all gods and goddesses. Publius Aelius Saturninus Naevianus Cupitus [dedicated this].
EDCS-14600256.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Publius Aelius Saturninus Naevianus Cupitus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all other gods and goddesses.

EDCS-14600259.text_edition: Valeriae  matri et Sois filiae annorum et V Crispi filio annorum  et Vir
EDCS-14600259.translation: To Valeria, mother, and to Sois, daughter, of [missing] years, and to Crispus, son, of [missing] years, and Vir[---].
EDCS-14600259.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely dedicated to a mother, daughter, and two sons, possibly by a surviving family member.

EDCS-14600260.text_edition: Aurel=Iiae#Aureliae#AURIIAE Ianuariae filiae obitae annorum X AureI(lii)#Aure(lii)#RI Ianuarius eLt#et#LT  parentes  fecerunt
EDCS-14600260.translation: To Aurelia Ianuaria, daughter, who died at 10 years of age. Aurelius Ianuarius and his parents made this.
EDCS-14600260.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aurelius Ianuarius and his parents for their daughter, Aurelia Ianuaria, who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-14600261.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudio Cupito annorum XIII Novellae sorori annorum XII Claudius Messor et Musso parentes filiis et sibi vivi fecerunt hic siti sunt
EDCS-14600261.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Claudius Cupitus, of 13 years, and to Novella, sister, of 12 years. Claudius Messor and Musso, their parents, made this for their children and for themselves while living. They are buried here.
EDCS-14600261.summary: This is a funerary inscription and monument dedicated by Claudius Messor and Musso to their deceased children, Claudius Cupitus and Novella, and for themselves while still living, indicating their shared burial place.

EDCS-14600262.text_edition: Verino Verionis filio beneficiario Useni Secundi annorum XL Dubitata Cupiti filia marito fecit et sibi et Carminio Restituto
EDCS-14600262.translation: To Verinus, son of Verio, a beneficiarius of Usenus Secundus, 40 years old. Dubitata, daughter of Cupitus, made this for her husband and for herself and Carminius Restitutus.
EDCS-14600262.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Dubitata, daughter of Cupitus, to her husband Verinus, a beneficiarius, and also for herself and Carminius Restitutus, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-14600264.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II pater patriae consul proconsul et Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus nobilissimus Caesar princeps iuventutis providentissimi Augusti fecerunt a Teurnia milia passuum
EDCS-14600264.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, in his second tribunician power, Father of the Country, Consul, Proconsul, and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, Princeps Iuventutis, the most provident Augusti, made [this] from Teurnia, X thousand paces.
EDCS-14600264.summary: This is a milestone or public monument inscription recording construction or repair work undertaken by the emperors Macrinus and Diadumenianus, likely dating to their joint reign (circa 217-218 AD).
