EDCS-14600326.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque omnibus et Genio huiusce loci Cai filius
EDCS-14600326.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to all gods and goddesses, and to the Genius of this place. Gaius, son of [missing].
EDCS-14600326.summary: This is a religious dedication made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, all gods and goddesses, and the Genius of the place, by a person named Gaius.

EDCS-14600327.text_edition: Nibonia ILIANO filia Conina votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-14600327.translation: Nibonia, daughter of Ilianus Conina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-14600327.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Nibonia, daughter of Ilianus Conina, fulfilled a vow she had made.

EDCS-14600328.text_edition: Cocusioni Luteivaes Monnus nepos vivus fecit
EDCS-14600328.translation: Monnus, grandson, made this while living for Cocusio, son of Luteiva.
EDCS-14600328.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Monnus for his grandfather Cocusio, son of Luteiva, while Monnus himself was still alive, indicating the creation of a family tomb.

EDCS-14600329.text_edition: Caius Priscius Vegetus vivus fecit sibi et N
EDCS-14600329.translation: Gaius Priscius Vegetus made this while living for himself and N[---].
EDCS-14600329.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Priscius Vegetus for himself and another person (likely a family member whose name is abbreviated or missing) during his lifetime.

EDCS-14600330.text_edition: Tattus Sexti filius annorum L fecit munimentum sibi et suis Tatto annorum L Mocio anni I Instituto annorum XV
EDCS-14600330.translation: Tattus, son of Sextus, 50 years old, made this monument for himself and his family: for Tatto, 50 years old, Mocio, 1 year old, and Institutus, 15 years old.
EDCS-14600330.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription made by Tattus, son of Sextus, for himself and his family members: Tatto, Mocio, and Institutus.

EDCS-14600331.text_edition: Divae Iuliae Lucius Cassius Claudia Maximus |(centurio) legionis VI ferratae testamento fieri iussit  Domitiae Augustae Lucius Cassius Claudia Maximus |(centurio) legionis VI ferratae testamento fieri iussit Lucius Cassius Eudaemon faciendas curavit
EDCS-14600331.translation: To Diva Iulia, Lucius Cassius Claudia Maximus, centurion of the Sixth Legion Ferrata, ordered this to be made by his will. To Domitia Augusta, Lucius Cassius Claudia Maximus, centurion of the Sixth Legion Ferrata, ordered this to be made by his will. Lucius Cassius Eudaemon oversaw the construction.
EDCS-14600331.summary: This inscription records the testamentary wishes of Lucius Cassius Claudia Maximus, a centurion, for dedications to Diva Iulia and Domitia Augusta, with Lucius Cassius Eudaemon supervising the work.

EDCS-14600333.text_edition: Publius Vedius Publi filius Potitus fecit sibi et Vediae Titi filiae Paullae coniugi et Publio Vedio Avito filio nnorum XXX
EDCS-14600333.translation: Publius Vedius, son of Publius, Potitus, made this for himself and for Vedia Paulla, daughter of Titus, his wife, and for Publius Vedius Avitus, his son, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-14600333.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Publius Vedius Potitus for himself, his wife Vedia Paulla, and their son Publius Vedius Avitus, who died at age 30.

EDCS-14600335.text_edition: Flavius Ianuarius miles vivus fecit condita sepulcro hic pausat Ursa Chrestiana sic fidelis annorum XXXVIII per partum subito ducente inpio fato est tradita Tartaris imis et me subito linquit sibi coniugem pro tempore iunctum quem sic ambulo et quaero miser quem sic ipse aeterna condidi terra o quit sic tribuat genesis qui sic separat convirginios dulcis sic ut non licuit nobis iugiter supernam frunisci caritatem hoc dico legentibus et lacrimis prosequor verba coniuncti amantis sic semper se bene dicere debent quia nihil erit dulcius quam prima iuventus
EDCS-14600335.translation: Flavius Ianuarius, a soldier, made this while living. Here rests Ursa Chrestiana, so faithful, 38 years old, suddenly given to the lowest Tartarus by impious fate during childbirth, and she suddenly leaves me, her husband, joined to her for a time, whom I thus walk and seek, miserable, whom I myself thus laid in eternal earth. Oh, may genesis grant to him who thus separates sweet virgins that it was not permitted for us to continuously enjoy supreme love. I say this to those reading, and I accompany with tears the words of a loving spouse: thus they should always speak well of themselves, because nothing will be sweeter than early youth.
EDCS-14600335.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription made by the soldier Flavius Ianuarius for himself while living, and for his wife Ursa Chrestiana, who died at age 38 during childbirth, expressing profound grief and a reflection on love and youth.

EDCS-14600336.text_edition: Candido Tassarni filio annorum LXX et Noveniae Camuli filiae parentibus filii fecerunt
EDCS-14600336.translation: To Candidus, son of Tassarnus, 70 years old, and to Novenia, daughter of Camulus, their parents, their sons made this.
EDCS-14600336.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by sons in honor of their parents, Candidus, who lived 70 years, and Novenia.

EDCS-14600337.text_edition: VI Nonas Maias depositCio#depositio#DEPOSICIO Valeriae viduae
EDCS-14600337.translation: On the Sixth Day before the Kalends of May, the burial of Valeria, a widow.
EDCS-14600337.summary: This inscription records the date of burial for a widow named Valeria, which occurred on April 26th.

EDCS-14600351.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et dis deabusque et Genio dominorum nnostrorum Auggustorum Caius Aemilius Respectus beneficiarius consularis legionis II Italicae piae fidelis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito Pompeiano et Avito consulibus Idibus Maiis
EDCS-14600351.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the gods and goddesses, and to the Genius of our lords the Augusti, Gaius Aemilius Respectus, beneficiarius consularis of the Second Italic Legion, Pious and Faithful, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his kin on the Ides of May, during the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.
EDCS-14600351.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, other deities, and the Genius of the emperors by Gaius Aemilius Respectus, a beneficiarius consularis, in May of 220 AD.

EDCS-14600353.text_edition: Aiuni Castici filiae Caius Iulius Cai libertus Clymeno vivus fecit de suo
EDCS-14600353.translation: To Aiunia, daughter of Casticus, Gaius Iulius Clymenus, freedman of Gaius, made this from his own funds while living.
EDCS-14600353.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Iulius Clymenus, a freedman, for Aiunia, daughter of Casticus, using his own money during his lifetime.

EDCS-14600355.text_edition: Primo Calandina filia viva fecit et sibi
EDCS-14600355.translation: Prima, daughter of Calandina, made this while living, also for herself.
EDCS-14600355.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Prima, daughter of Calandina, for herself while she was still alive.

EDCS-14600356.text_edition: Rufius Musicus et Vibena Ingenui filia uxor vivi fecerunt sibi et Masculo filio annorum VII
EDCS-14600356.translation: Rufius Musicus and Vibena, daughter of Ingenuus, his wife, made this while living for themselves and for Masculinus, their son, who lived 7 years.
EDCS-14600356.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Rufius Musicus and his wife Vibena for themselves while living, and for their son Masculinus, who died at the age of 7.

EDCS-14600359.text_edition: Dis Manibus Priminus vivus fecit sibi et Veneriae coniugi karissimae
EDCS-14600359.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Priminus made this while living for himself and for Veneria, his dearest wife.
EDCS-14600359.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed, made by Priminus for himself while still alive, and for his beloved wife Veneria.

EDCS-14600362.text_edition: Clemenoni sic Poti servo et Sporae Pametae libertae uxori filii fecerunt Fin   et Messori
EDCS-14600362.translation: To Clemenon, slave of Poti, and to Spora Pameta, freedwoman, his wife, their sons Fin[---] and Messor made this.
EDCS-14600362.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Fin[---] and Messor for their parents, Clemenon (a slave) and Spora Pameta (a freedwoman).

EDCS-14600363.text_edition: Borissus  sibi et Secundinae Vercai filiae uxori et Secundino filio et Mussae uxori eius  N
EDCS-14600363.translation: Borissus, for himself and for Secundina, daughter of Vercai, his wife, and for Secundinus, his son, and for Mussa, his wife. N[---].
EDCS-14600363.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a monument prepared by Borissus for himself, his wife Secundina, his son Secundinus, and Secundinus's wife Mussa.

EDCS-14600364.text_edition: Maro Tertulli annorum LXL Bononia Secundiini annorum XXXIII Marcianus annorum LXXXV Verekundus sic annorum L
EDCS-14600364.translation: Maro, son of Tertullus, 60 years old. Bononia, daughter of Secundinus, 33 years old. Marcianus, 85 years old. Verecundus, 50 years old.
EDCS-14600364.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing the names and ages of four individuals: Maro, Bononia, Marcianus, and Verecundus, likely sharing a tomb.

EDCS-14600365.text_edition: Dibus sic Manibus Valerius Tertuliliani vibus sic fecit coniugii Secundinae obitae  an=NA(norum)#an(norum)#NA XXX  Valentinus annorum XXX Valerius annorum XL Tertulla
EDCS-14600365.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius, son of Tertullianus, made this for himself while living, for his wife Secundina, who died at 30 years old, for Valentinus, 30 years old, Valerius, 40 years old, and Tertulla.
EDCS-14600365.summary: This is a funerary inscription prepared by Valerius Tertullianus for himself, his deceased wife Secundina, and three other family members: Valentinus, Valerius, and Tertulla.

EDCS-14600366.text_edition: Tertius Rigonis filius nnorum C Tertia Belatulli annorum C Latinus filius fecit Specetatus Terti filius annorum X
EDCS-14600366.translation: Tertius, son of Rigo, 100 years old. Tertia, daughter of Belatullus, 100 years old. Latinus, their son, made this. Spectatus, son of Tertius, 10 years old.
EDCS-14600366.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Latinus for his parents, Tertius and Tertia, and also mentioning Spectatus, son of Tertius.

EDCS-14600367.translation: Acutus, son of Maximus, years old [---].
EDCS-14600367.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Acutus, son of Maximus, with his age missing.

EDCS-14600368.text_edition: Dis Manibus Adiutor MPIIS  coniugi cundi  fecit
EDCS-14600368.translation: To the Divine Manes. Adiutor, most pious, made this for his wife [Se]cunda.
EDCS-14600368.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, recording a monument made by Adiutor for his wife, Secunda.

EDCS-14600369.text_edition: Dis Manibus di bae sibi et Seli marito karissimo nnorum XXXV
EDCS-14600369.translation: To the Divine Manes. [---]ba made this for herself and for Selius, her dearest husband, 35 years old.
EDCS-14600369.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, recording a monument made by an unnamed woman for herself and her beloved husband Selius.

EDCS-14600370.text_edition: Cassius Primianus vivus fecit sibi et Terentiae Iuliae coniugi iucundissimae
EDCS-14600370.translation: Cassius Primianus, while living, made this for himself and for Terentia Iulia, his most pleasant wife.
EDCS-14600370.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument prepared by Cassius Primianus during his lifetime for himself and his wife, Terentia Iulia.

EDCS-14600371.text_edition: ASIIari fili-- viv-- fecit Otu Senonis filia hic sita est annorum XX
EDCS-14600371.translation: [---]sius, son of [---], made this while living. Ota, daughter of Seno, is buried here, 20 years old.
EDCS-14600371.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by an unnamed individual for himself and for Ota, daughter of Seno, who is buried there.

EDCS-14600373.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimus Titi Vari Surionis servus vivus fecit sibi et Quintae coniugi pientissimae annorum XXXV
EDCS-14600373.translation: To the Divine Manes. Septimus, slave of Titus Varius Surio, made this while living for himself and for Quinta, his most devoted wife, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-14600373.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Septimus, a slave of Titus Varius Surio, for himself and his wife Quinta, indicating it is likely a tombstone or a monument for burial.

EDCS-14600388.text_edition: Urso Ursuli filio annorum XXX et Suadra uxor Calventi filia viva fecit et Ursulus et Restuta uxor
EDCS-14600388.translation: Suadra, daughter of Calventus, made this while living for Urso, son of Ursulus, who lived 30 years, and for herself, and Ursulus and his wife Restuta.
EDCS-14600388.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Suadra for her husband Urso, her father-in-law Ursulus, and his wife Restuta, suggesting a family tomb or memorial.

EDCS-14600390.text_edition: Caius Florentinius Censorinus vivus et Aelia Brenturia coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Florentinio Floro filio carissimo equiti alae Commagenorum primae singulari stupendiorum sic XX annorum XL et Caio Aelio C et Florentinia Florentina annorum LXX
EDCS-14600390.translation: Gaius Florentinius Censorinus, while living, and Aelia Brenturia, his wife, while living, made this for themselves and for their dearest son Florentinius Florus, a cavalryman of the First Ala of Commageni, a singular soldier with twenty years of service, who lived 40 years, and for Gaius Aelius C. and Florentina Florentina, who lived 70 years.
EDCS-14600390.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Florentinius Censorinus and Aelia Brenturia for themselves, their son Florentinius Florus, a decorated soldier, and for Gaius Aelius C. and Florentina Florentina, likely indicating a family burial monument.

EDCS-14600391.text_edition: Satico Censoris annorum LXXX Candida Celvi annorum LX
EDCS-14600391.translation: Satico, son of Censor, 80 years old. Candida, daughter of Celvus, 60 years old.
EDCS-14600391.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names and ages of Satico and Candida, likely a shared tombstone for two individuals.

EDCS-14600392.translation: Calventius, freedman of Mauso, and Calandina.
EDCS-14600392.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calventius, a freedman of Mauso, and Calandina, probably indicating a shared memorial for a couple or family members.

EDCS-14600393.text_edition: Calventius Mercatoris libertus sibi vivus fecit et Candidae coniugi obitae
EDCS-14600393.translation: Calventius, freedman of Mercator, made this while living for himself and for Candida, his deceased wife.
EDCS-14600393.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Calventius, a freedman of Mercator, for himself and his deceased wife Candida, suggesting a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-14600394.text_edition: Cavecio Loi filio obito annorum L Uragiso Tessilli filia cGoniugi#coniugi#GONIUGI et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-14600394.translation: Uragiso, daughter of Tessillus, made this while living for her deceased husband Cavecius, son of Loi, who lived 50 years, and for herself.
EDCS-14600394.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Uragiso for her deceased husband Cavecius and for herself, indicating a joint burial monument.

EDCS-14600395.text_edition: Dis Manibus Amanda Quinti Tessi liberta obita annorum L Firmus Litugenae maritus ex proprio coniugi carissimae fecit et sibi
EDCS-14600395.translation: To the Divine Manes. Firmus Litugena, husband, made this from his own resources for his dearest wife Amanda, freedwoman of Quintus Tessius, who died at 50 years old, and for himself.
EDCS-14600395.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Firmus Litugena for his deceased wife Amanda and for himself, indicating a family tombstone or monument.

EDCS-14600396.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Maximinus Constantis et Annilio Litugenae vivi sibi fecerunt et posterisque suis
EDCS-14600396.translation: To the Divine Manes. Maximinus, son of Constans, and Annilio Litugena, made this while living for themselves and for their descendants.
EDCS-14600396.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Maximinus and Annilio Litugena for themselves and their future descendants, likely indicating a family tomb or mausoleum.

EDCS-14600398.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Paterna viva fecit sibi
EDCS-14600398.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Paterna made this while living for herself.
EDCS-14600398.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aurelia Paterna for herself, indicating a personal tombstone or monument prepared during her lifetime.

EDCS-14600406.text_edition: Sextus Iuliae Litugenae libertus vivus fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-14600406.translation: Sextus, freedman of Iulia Litugena, made this for himself and his family while still alive.
EDCS-14600406.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Sextus, a freedman, for himself and his family, indicating a common practice of preparing tombs during one's lifetime.

EDCS-14600407.text_edition: Primus Cai Veponi Praesentis servus vivus fecit sibi et Primulae coniugi et Firmae filiae annorum XIIII et Insecuventi sic filio annorum XVIII
EDCS-14600407.translation: Primus, slave of Gaius Veponius Praesens, made this while alive for himself, and for his wife Primula, and for his daughter Firma, aged 14 years, and for his son Insecuventus, aged 18 years.
EDCS-14600407.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Primus, a slave, for himself and his family (wife and two children) during his lifetime, highlighting the social dynamics of slave families in Roman society.

EDCS-14600408.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription naming individuals like 'Atti[---]' with parts of other names possibly beginning with 'Cla' and 'A', likely part of a monument or dedication, but too incomplete to determine its full purpose.

EDCS-14600411.text_edition: Silvano Tertii filio et Togiae Vercombogi filiae vivus fecit sibi et coniugi
EDCS-14600411.translation: For Silvanus, son of Tertius, and for Togia, daughter of Vercombogus, he made this while alive for himself and his wife.
EDCS-14600411.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by an unnamed individual for himself and his wife, Togia, who is identified as the daughter of Vercombogus, and also mentions Silvanus, son of Tertius, possibly as the dedicator himself or another family member.

EDCS-14600418.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Ulpius Primus annorum LX testamento poni iussit Ulpiae Sisiuni uxori et Ulpiae
EDCS-14600418.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Ulpius Primus, 60 years old, ordered this to be placed by his will for his wife Ulpia Sisiunis and for Ulpia.
EDCS-14600418.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Primus, aged 60, who, through his will, arranged for this monument to be erected for his wife, Ulpia Sisiunis, and another Ulpia, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-14600420.text_edition: Genio legionis II Italicae piae Marcus Gavius Firmus primus pilus Vellina sic Firmo Piceno dedicante Caio Memmio Fido Iulio Albio consule designato legato Augusti pro praetore XIIII Kalendas Octobres Aproniano et Bradua consulibus
EDCS-14600420.translation: To the Genius of the Second Italic Legion, Pious. Marcus Gavius Firmus, first centurion, with Firmus Picenus of the Vellina tribe dedicating, on the 14th Kalends of October, in the consulship of Apronianus and Bradua, with Gaius Memmius Fidus Iulius Albius as consul-designate and imperial legate pro praetore.
EDCS-14600420.summary: This is a military dedication to the Genius of the Legio II Italica Pia by Marcus Gavius Firmus, a centurion, with a specific date of September 18th, 185 CE, identifiable by the consuls and consul-designate, detailing the patron god of the legion and key military officials.

EDCS-14800149.text_edition: Masculus Ianuari libertus vivus fecit sibi et Daglandrunae coniugi pientissimae
EDCS-14800149.translation: Masculus, freedman of Ianuarius, made this while alive for himself and for Daglandruna, his most devoted wife.
EDCS-14800149.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Masculus, a freedman of Ianuarius, for himself and his deeply beloved wife, Daglandruna, during their lifetime.

EDCS-14800150.translation: Celsus, son of Camio. OAE IIC
EDCS-14800150.summary: This is a very brief and fragmentary inscription identifying Celsus, son of Camio, accompanied by an abbreviated formula, possibly indicating a military unit or a personal descriptor.

EDCS-14800151.text_edition: tonius i libertus Itius EMA I
EDCS-14800151.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Antonius, a freedman, and Itius, possibly another individual or a place name, followed by an abbreviated formula, likely a funerary or dedicatory context.

EDCS-14800153.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, likely part of a larger monument, with insufficient information to determine its full content or purpose beyond the partial name 'Luc[---]'.

EDCS-14800161.text_edition: SA SIRA PirenAEIAS garrulae munus A puer--- Asclepiad in pectore consens A dedit lapidem inscripsit Verone---
EDCS-14800161.translation: SA SIRA, the gift of the garrulous PirenAEIAS from a boy--- Asclepiad in his heart, a consensus he gave--- he inscribed the stone at Verona---
EDCS-14800161.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to be a funerary or dedicatory text, possibly by Asclepiad, recording a gift and an inscription on stone in Verona.

EDCS-14800162.translation: Sentia Secunda makes glass at Aquileia.
EDCS-14800162.summary: This inscription is a simple notice indicating Sentia Secunda's profession and location, likely a workshop mark or a public record of her trade in Aquileia.

EDCS-14800163.summary: This inscription, similar to the previous, records Sentia Secunda's activity at Aquileia, possibly a workshop mark, although the specific product is omitted here.

EDCS-15000089.text_edition: Pluton sivem sic Iovem infernum dici opornotet Aeracura Iuno inferna acciete iam celerius infra scribtum sic et tradite I Manibus Aurelium Sinnianum Caeserianum  Sic Silvia inversum maritum ceernis sic quommodi sic nomen illius scribtum sic est
EDCS-15000089.translation: Pluto, or as he should be called, infernal Jupiter; Aeracura, infernal Juno; receive now swiftly what is written below, and deliver it to the Manes of Aurelius Sinnianus Caeserianus. So, Silvia, you see your husband, thus his name is written.
EDCS-15000089.summary: This is a defixio (curse tablet) invoking chthonic deities Pluto (infernal Jupiter) and Aeracura (infernal Juno) to deliver a message to the Manes of Aurelius Sinnianus Caeserianus, possibly related to a marital issue involving Silvia.

EDCS-15100060.text_edition: Domino fartori Victoriano salutem mox litteras meas perceperis ut statuemus demes litteras meas felicissime excipas sic cum Livia fuimus
EDCS-15100060.translation: Greetings to our master baker Victoriano. As soon as you receive my letters, so we shall decide. You will most happily accept my letters, thus we were with Livia.
EDCS-15100060.summary: This inscription is a personal letter or message from an individual to 'master baker' Victoriano, discussing future plans contingent on the receipt of letters and mentioning a past encounter with Livia.

EDCS-15800386.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Marr(ius) Ursinus veteranus ex iussu posuit libens laetus merito
EDCS-15800386.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Marrius Ursinus, a veteran, placed this willingly, joyfully, and deservedly, by order.
EDCS-15800386.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, made by the veteran Marrius Ursinus in fulfillment of a divine command.

EDCS-15800387.text_edition: Castori et Polluci sigillum ex iusso sic Iovis Optimi Maximi Dolicheni Vindicii Florentinus et Moderatus votum solverunt libentes laeti merito
EDCS-15800387.translation: To Castor and Pollux, a seal by command, thus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Vindicius Florentinus and Moderatus willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15800387.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Castor and Pollux, likely accompanying a seal, and also acknowledges a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Vindicius Florentinus and Moderatus.

EDCS-15800388.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno  Tiberius Vibius Messinus pro salute votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800388.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Tiberius Vibius Messinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his health.
EDCS-15800388.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Tiberius Vibius Messinus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus for his well-being.

EDCS-15800389.text_edition: Iovi Dolicheno Postumius Celer decurio ex imperio
EDCS-15800389.translation: To Jupiter Dolichenus, Postumius Celer, a decurion, by divine command.
EDCS-15800389.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Postumius Celer, a decurion, acting upon a divine command.

EDCS-15800390.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Maria ex voto posuit
EDCS-15800390.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Maria placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-15800390.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, made by Maria in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800391.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Ulpia Iuliana pro salute Gai Vibi Honorati fili votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800391.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, Ulpia Iuliana willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for the health of her son, Gaius Vibius Honoratus.
EDCS-15800391.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus made by Ulpia Iuliana for the well-being of her son, Gaius Vibius Honoratus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800392.text_edition: Dulicheno sic Marrius Ursinus pro se suisque ex iussu votum solvit
EDCS-15800392.translation: To Dolichenus, Marius Ursinus, by command, willingly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-15800392.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Marius Ursinus to Dolichenus, fulfilling a vow for himself and his family as commanded.

EDCS-15800393.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Dolicheno Iusta Pastoris votum solvit libens laeta
EDCS-15800393.translation: To Jupiter Best, Dolichenus, Iusta, daughter of Pastor, willingly and happily fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-15800393.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus made by Iusta, daughter of Pastor, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800394.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Claudius Maternus ex voto pro se et suis votum  solvit libens  laetus merito
EDCS-15800394.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, Claudius Maternus, from a vow, willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-15800394.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus made by Claudius Maternus, fulfilling a vow for himself and his family.

EDCS-15800395.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Victura votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800395.translation: To Queen Juno, Victura willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-15800395.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Queen Juno made by Victura, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800396.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Vera votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-15800396.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, Vera willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-15800396.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus made by Vera, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800397.text_edition: Iovi Dulicheno sic Probus et Marina pro se et pro suis
EDCS-15800397.translation: To Jupiter Dolichenus, Probus and Marina for themselves and for their family.
EDCS-15800397.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus made by Probus and Marina on behalf of themselves and their family.

EDCS-15800398.text_edition: Lucius Cassius Ambrosius fecit in circo Flaminio  Lucius Cassius Ambrosius
EDCS-15800398.translation: Lucius Cassius Ambrosius made this in the Circus Flaminius, Lucius Cassius Ambrosius.
EDCS-15800398.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Cassius Ambrosius made this object in the Circus Flaminius.

EDCS-16100281.text_edition: Cai Tituri Festi |(centurionis) cohortis XIIII praetoriae
EDCS-16100281.translation: Of Gaius Titurius Festus, centurion of the 14th Praetorian cohort.
EDCS-16100281.summary: This inscription identifies the individual Gaius Titurius Festus as a centurion of the 14th Praetorian cohort.

EDCS-16201562.text_edition: Teurniae sanctissimae Augustae Lucius Herennius Epictetus
EDCS-16201562.translation: To Teurnia, most holy Augusta, Lucius Herennius Epictetus [dedicates this].
EDCS-16201562.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Herennius Epictetus to the most holy Augusta Teurnia.

EDCS-16201563.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae iussu dei Dolicheni Claudius Rufinus et Octavia Compsilla  pro se dono dederunt
EDCS-16201563.translation: To Juno Regina, by order of the god Dolichenus, Claudius Rufinus and Octavia Compsilla gave this as a gift for themselves.
EDCS-16201563.summary: This is a votive inscription to Juno Regina and Dolichenus, dedicated by Claudius Rufinus and Octavia Compsilla, likely from the late 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-16500266.translation: To the Helvetian Waters, Gemellianus made this.
EDCS-16500266.summary: This short inscription is a dedication by Gemellianus to the local Helvetian Waters, probably at a healing spring or sanctuary.

EDCS-20601152.summary: This is a brief label, likely indicating a quantity of rings belonging to or made by Antiochus.

EDCS-20601153.summary: This concise inscription, likely a merchant's mark or tally, records Antiochus and the number 18, possibly a quantity or price.

EDCS-20601155.text_edition: Θ(εοῖς) Χ(θονίοις) scifos vini XXV  Antiochus Kalendis Maiis
EDCS-20601155.translation: To the Chthonic Gods, 25 cups of wine, Antiochus, on the Kalends of May.
EDCS-20601155.summary: This inscription records an offering of 25 cups of wine made by Antiochus to the Chthonic Gods on May 1st, likely for a funerary or underworld ritual.

EDCS-20601158.summary: This is a product label identifying Falernian wine as belonging to Herennius Priscus, likely from a wine jar or amphora.

EDCS-20601160.translation: Coan wine, Aemilianus, (property) of Marcus, (property) of Marcus.
EDCS-20601160.summary: This inscription identifies a type of wine (Coan) and notes its association with Aemilianus and two individuals named Marcus, possibly owners or producers.

EDCS-20601161.text_edition: ntin  plena pondo CXV Cai Iuli Communis
EDCS-20601161.translation: [Con]tain[er] full, 115 pounds, of Gaius Iulius Communis.
EDCS-20601161.summary: This is a product label, likely from a container, indicating its full weight of 115 pounds and identifying its owner or producer as Gaius Iulius Communis.

EDCS-20601162.text_edition: Lumphae sic pondo CX Luci L
EDCS-20601162.translation: Lumphae, thus, 110 pounds, of Lucius L[---].
EDCS-20601162.summary: This inscription is a label, likely from a container, indicating a quantity of 110 pounds of 'Lumphae' (possibly a commodity or place name) and associating it with Lucius L[---].

EDCS-20601166.translation: To Vernio Pomp[---], the finest olive oil.
EDCS-20601166.summary: This is a label or dedication, likely indicating that the contained product is the 'finest olive oil' associated with or dedicated to Vernio Pomp[---].

EDCS-20601167.translation: Olive oil, table grade, by weight... To be offered...
EDCS-20601167.summary: This is a fragment likely detailing a quantity of olive oil, possibly for an offering or inventory, from an uncertain date.

EDCS-20601170.text_edition: X   excellens Auli B S Cai Iuli Pisonis
EDCS-20601170.translation: 10. Excellent. Of Aulus B. S. and Gaius Iulius Piso.
EDCS-20601170.summary: This fragment mentions a quantity (10) and names Aulus B. S. and Gaius Iulius Piso, likely indicating ownership or an associated quality, from an uncertain date.

EDCS-20601171.text_edition: Garum Hispanicum QQuintorum Caeciliorum Publius M C
EDCS-20601171.translation: Spanish garum of Quintus and Quintus Caecilius. Publius M. C.
EDCS-20601171.summary: This inscription records a type of fish sauce, Spanish garum, associated with two individuals named Quintus Caecilius and Publius M. C., from an uncertain date.

EDCS-24200330.text_edition: Tiberius Cassius Tiberi filius  Cri T
EDCS-24200330.translation: Tiberius Cassius, son of Tiberius, of the tribe Cri... T...
EDCS-24200330.summary: This fragment is a funerary or civic inscription for Tiberius Cassius, son of Tiberius, specifying his tribe Cri, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24200331.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Telesphorus Cai Antoni Rufi servus publici portorii vilicus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-24200331.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, Telesphorus, slave of Gaius Antonius Rufus, overseer of the public customs, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24200331.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Mithras by Telesphorus, a slave and customs official of Gaius Antonius Rufus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24200336.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli possessionis, tituli sacri
EDCS-24200336.text_edition: Exactum ad XX Castoris  Exactum ad X Castoris  Exactum V Castoris  Exactum III Castoris  Exactum II Castoris  Exactum I Castoris  Exactum |(semis) Castoris  |(triens)  |(quadrans)  Exactum |(sextans) Castoris  |(uncia)
EDCS-24200336.translation: Collected at 20 (units of) Castor. Collected at 10 (units of) Castor. Collected 5 (units of) Castor. Collected 3 (units of) Castor. Collected 2 (units of) Castor. Collected 1 (unit of) Castor. Collected 1/2 (unit of) Castor. 1/3. 1/4. Collected 1/6 (unit of) Castor. 1/12.
EDCS-24200336.summary: This inscription lists various amounts collected, possibly taxes or payments, measured in units called 'Castor' and fractions thereof, from an uncertain date.

EDCS-24200341.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Augusti Cnaeus Firmininus Publius Antonius Avitus Publius Firminius Veruicius Maria Vindilla Aurelia Vivia Domitia Iusta qui pro se et suis omnib(us) votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-24200341.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Augustus, Gnaeus Firmininus, Publius Antonius Avitus, Publius Firminius Veruicius, Maria Vindilla, Aurelia Vivia, Domitia Iusta, who for themselves and all their kin willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-24200341.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Augustus by multiple individuals, likely a family group, fulfilling a vow for themselves and their relatives, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24200342.summary: This short inscription likely indicates an individual named Lucilius associated with the emperor (Princeps), possibly a freedman or official, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24200344.summary: This is a fragmented name inscription, likely for an individual named Theomnestus, from an uncertain date.

EDCS-24200348.text_edition: Adiectus Aduci libertus vivus sibi fecit et Botuni coniugi et Iantullae annorum XX
EDCS-24200348.translation: Adiectus, freedman of Aducius, made this for himself while alive, and for his wife Botunis and Iantulla, aged 20.
EDCS-24200348.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Adiectus, a freedman of Aducius, for himself and his wife Botunis and a young woman named Iantulla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-24900098.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying Acastus as a slave, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26400012.text_edition: Lucius Stlaccius Luci filius Secundus  C
EDCS-26400012.translation: Lucius Stlaccius, son of Lucius, Secundus, C.
EDCS-26400012.summary: This is a fragment likely from a funerary or dedicatory inscription, providing the name and filiation of Lucius Stlaccius Secundus.

EDCS-27200237.text_edition: Adginnos Vercombogi Aeso votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27200237.translation: Adginnos, son of Vercombogus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to Aesos.
EDCS-27200237.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Gaul, where Adginnos, son of Vercombogus, makes a vow to the deity Aesos, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27200243.translation: Weight: 20 pounds. To his wife. Finitianus. E.
EDCS-27200243.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary monument, mentioning a weight, a wife, and the name Finitianus.

EDCS-27300214.text_edition: Dextro Bottonis filio Nemeta Iuni filia uxor viva fecit et Silvestro Iuni filio et sibi
EDCS-27300214.translation: Nemeta, daughter of Iunus, made this while alive for her husband Dextrus, son of Botto, and for Silvester, son of Iunus, and for herself.
EDCS-27300214.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, erected by Nemeta, daughter of Iunus, for her husband Dextrus, her relative Silvester, and for herself, indicating a pre-need burial plot.

EDCS-27300223.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument for Tiberius Claudius Saecularis, possibly including an expression of endearment or a wish for peaceful rest.

EDCS-27300243.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, containing parts of the names Pollia and Pollio.

EDCS-27300245.translation: To Rusticus, son of Secundus, 60 years old, and to Gaixu, his spouse. His son.
EDCS-27300245.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Rusticus, son of Secundus, and his spouse Gaixu, by their son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27300251.text_edition: Finitus Silvani Solliae Satullae coniugi annorum XXI
EDCS-27300251.translation: Finitus Silvanus for Solli Satulla, his wife, 21 years old.
EDCS-27300251.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Finitus Silvanus to his wife, Solli Satulla, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-27300253.text_edition: Soli deo Invicto Publius Tapp(ius) Pollinus votum solvit
EDCS-27300253.translation: To the Invincible Sun God, Publius Tappius Pollinus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27300253.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Sol Invictus, made by Publius Tappius Pollinus, likely dating from the late Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27800669.text_edition: Cre nus A  Delicia vivi fecerunt sibi et Valentinae coniugi annorum XVII Variila mater viva fecit
EDCS-27800669.translation: Crenus A. Delicia, while alive, made this for themselves and for their wife Valentina, seventeen years old. Her mother, Variila, while alive, made this.
EDCS-27800669.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Crenus A. Delicia, while alive, erected a tomb for themselves and their deceased wife Valentina, seventeen years old, and that Valentina's mother, Variila, also made a contribution while alive.

EDCS-27900213.text_edition: Imperatoribus Caesaribus Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto tribunicia potestate XV consuli III et Lucio Aurelio Vero Augusto tribunicia potestate consuli II I
EDCS-27900213.translation: To the Emperors Caesars Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, with tribunician power for the 15th time, consul for the 3rd time, and Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, with tribunician power for the [X] time, consul for the 2nd time.
EDCS-27900213.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-emperors Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, likely dating to the mid-2nd century CE based on their consular and tribunician powers.

EDCS-27900214.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XII pater patriae consul et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus Germanicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVII imperator III consul IIII miliaria vetustate collapsa restitui iusserunt I
EDCS-27900214.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 9th time, hailed imperator for the 12th time, father of his country, consul, and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Maximus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 17th time, hailed imperator for the 3rd time, consul for the 4th time, ordered milestones, collapsed from age, to be restored.
EDCS-27900214.summary: This inscription records that the emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus) ordered the restoration of milestones that had fallen into disrepair, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27900215.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II pater patriae consul et Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus nobilissimus Caesar princebs sic iuventutis providentissimi Augusti fecerunt a Celeia mille passus
EDCS-27900215.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, father of his country, consul, and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, the most provident Augusti, made this a thousand paces from Celeia.
EDCS-27900215.summary: This inscription records the construction or restoration of a monument by the emperors Macrinus and his son Diadumenianus, located one thousand paces from Celeia, likely dating to 217-218 CE.

EDCS-27900328.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augusto tribunicia potestate II consul II pater patriae proconsul et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar princeps iuventutis domini indulgentissimo sic pontes refecerunt et vias munierunt et miliaria restituerunt mille passus I
EDCS-27900328.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of his country, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, our most indulgent lords, thus rebuilt bridges, fortified roads, and restored milestones one thousand paces.
EDCS-27900328.summary: This inscription commemorates the emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, who rebuilt bridges, fortified roads, and restored milestones, likely dating to around 237-238 CE.

EDCS-27900337.text_edition: Imp(eratori) d(omino) n(ostro) Gal(erio) Val(erio) Maximino P(io) F(elici) Invic(to) Augusto   milia passuum XII
EDCS-27900337.translation: To the Emperor, our lord Galerius Valerius Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus. Twelve miles.
EDCS-27900337.summary: This is a milestone or dedication to the emperor Galerius Valerius Maximinus Augustus, indicating a distance of twelve miles, likely dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-27900341.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico Britannico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III imperatori VII consuli II patri patriae proconsuli et Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico Brittannico maximo Germanico maximo tribunicia potestate XIII imperatori IIII consuli IIII patri patriae proconsuli fortissimo principi domino indulgentissimo XXXVI  Ob communem dominum et Imperatorem nostri sic Flavium Claudium Iulianum victorem ac triumphatorem semper Augustum    XXXVI   ECR NDI   ab Iuvavo milia passuum XXXVI   felicissimo principi
EDCS-27900341.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Britannicus, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, hailed imperator for the 7th time, consul for the 2nd time, father of his country, proconsul, and to the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus Britannicus Maximus Germanicus Maximus, greatest pontiff, with tribunician power for the 13th time, hailed imperator for the 4th time, consul for the 4th time, most valiant prince, most indulgent lord, 36. For our common lord and Emperor, thus Flavius Claudius Julianus, victor and triumphator, ever Augustus, 36. From Iuvavum, 36 miles. To the most fortunate prince.
EDCS-27900341.summary: This complex inscription is a milestone or dedication, initially honoring the emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus), and later re-dedicated to the emperor Julian (Flavius Claudius Julianus), indicating a distance of 36 miles from Iuvavum and reflecting different imperial periods.

EDCS-27900372.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Augusto XXXXIII XXL[---]
EDCS-27900372.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Augustus, 43 [---]
EDCS-27900372.summary: This appears to be a dedication or milestone to the emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus Augustus, likely Gordian III, dating to the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-27900373.text_edition: Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimi sic Ceseri sic Augusto
EDCS-27900373.translation: To Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, thus Augustus.
EDCS-27900373.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Marcus Julius Philippus, honoring him first as 'most noble Caesar' and then as 'Augustus', likely referring to Philip the Arab and his son, Philip II, during their co-rule or succession in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-27900385.text_edition: Marcus Iulius Philippus nobilissimus Caesar Augustus
EDCS-27900385.translation: Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-27900385.summary: This inscription identifies Marcus Julius Philippus as 'most noble Caesar Augustus', likely referring to Philip the Arab's son, Philip II, after he was elevated to co-Augustus in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-27900386.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Messius Quintus Troianus sic Deccius sic Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate consul pater patriae proconsul
EDCS-27900386.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul.
EDCS-27900386.summary: This inscription records the full titulature of Emperor Trajan Decius, likely from a public monument erected during his reign (249-251 CE).

EDCS-28500896.summary: This inscription records that Primitivus made a monument for Pastorina, likely a tombstone given its brevity and dedicatory nature.

EDCS-28701025.text_edition: Lucius Boniati filius et Iulia Prisca coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Bussugnatae filiae nnum XV
EDCS-28701025.translation: Lucius, son of Boniat, and Iulia Prisca, his wife, made this in their lifetime for themselves and for their daughter Bussugnata, who lived for 15 years.
EDCS-28701025.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Lucius and Iulia Prisca during their lifetime for themselves and their deceased daughter Bussugnata.

EDCS-28701026.text_edition: Speratus Silvani annorum L et Verecunda Suri et Iusto filio annorum XXXX
EDCS-28701026.translation: Speratus, son of Silvanus, aged 50, and Verecunda, daughter of Surus, and their son Iustus, aged 40.
EDCS-28701026.summary: This inscription likely commemorates Speratus, Verecunda, and their son Iustus, specifying their ages, suggesting a funerary context.

EDCS-28800822.text_edition: LIO Rufi quae co Accia  V
EDCS-28800822.translation: Lio, daughter of Rufus, who (---) Accia (---) V.
EDCS-28800822.summary: This fragmented inscription mentions Lio, daughter of Rufus, and Accia, possibly a relative, suggesting a funerary context with partially lost information.

EDCS-28800823.text_edition: Dis Manibus ius Successi filius mus annorum LX testamento fieri iussit sibi? et? Ulpiae Suadrae bene meritae  faciendum curav---
EDCS-28800823.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ius, son of Successus, (---) aged 60, ordered this to be made by his will for himself and for Ulpia Suadra, who deserved it well. (---) saw to the making.
EDCS-28800823.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Ius, son of Successus, made according to his will, and for Ulpia Suadra, a well-deserving individual, indicating a posthumous dedication.

EDCS-28800825.text_edition: vinti fili-- CON EREX anno--- XXV
EDCS-28800825.translation: --- of Vintus, son of ---, with --- erected in the year --- 25.
EDCS-28800825.summary: This fragmented inscription records an erection by someone related to Vintus, with an indication of age or year, likely from a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-29900145.text_edition: Ceudo Ariomani filius et Tocia Veriugi filia Admatae filiae sibi et suis vivi fecerunt
EDCS-29900145.translation: Ceudo, son of Ariomanus, and Tocia, daughter of Veriugus, made this in their lifetime for their daughter Admata, for themselves, and for their family.
EDCS-29900145.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Ceudo and Tocia during their lifetime for their daughter Admata, themselves, and their family.

EDCS-29900146.text_edition: et Philomuso socero nnorum LX et Litanunicae
EDCS-29900146.translation: and for Philomus, his father-in-law, aged 60, and for Litanunica.
EDCS-29900146.summary: This inscription is likely a fragment of a larger funerary monument, commemorating Philomus (aged 60) and Litanunica, with an implied dedicant.

EDCS-29900148.text_edition: Albinio Lucretio et Mansueto Aviti servo Albinia Couso viva fecit
EDCS-29900148.translation: Albinia Couso, while alive, made this for Albinius Lucretius and for Mansuetus, slave of Avitus.
EDCS-29900148.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Albinia Couso during her lifetime for Albinius Lucretius and for Mansuetus, a slave belonging to Avitus.

EDCS-29900149.text_edition: Tito Flavio Diocaito et Claudiae Finitae coniugi vivi fecerunt sibi et Ingenuo filio annorum XX
EDCS-29900149.translation: Titus Flavius Diocaitus and Claudia Finita, his wife, made this in their lifetime for themselves and for their son Ingenuus, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-29900149.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Titus Flavius Diocaitus and his wife Claudia Finita during their lifetime for themselves and their deceased son Ingenuus.

EDCS-29900150.text_edition: Dis Manibus Surio Verecundi filius vivus sibi et AIciae coniugi C filiae et TII-
EDCS-29900150.translation: To the Divine Manes. Surius, son of Verecundus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for his wife Aicia and for his daughter C[---] and TII[-].
EDCS-29900150.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes by Surius, son of Verecundus, for himself, his wife Aicia, and his daughter C[---], with additional illegible text.

EDCS-29900151.translation: Ius Maro of Ovilava and his wife.
EDCS-29900151.summary: This appears to be a fragment of an inscription mentioning Ius Maro, a resident of Ovilava, and his wife, likely from a funerary or dedicatory context.

EDCS-30101179.translation: Iucundus, by the hand of Ruffus.
EDCS-30101179.summary: This short inscription likely indicates that Iucundus was the subject or recipient, with Ruffus being the artisan or scribe who made it.

EDCS-30101193.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vitalis Finiti et Ingenua Crispi coniunx vivi sibi fecerunt et Verino annorum XXV et Fuscae annorum VII et Valentino annorum annorum II filiis
EDCS-30101193.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vitalis Finitus and Ingenua Crispi, his wife, made this in their lifetime for themselves and for their sons Verinus, who lived 25 years, Fusca, who lived 7 years, and Valentinus, who lived 2 years.
EDCS-30101193.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, made by Vitalis Finitus and his wife Ingenua Crispi during their lifetime for themselves and their three deceased sons, Verinus, Fusca, and Valentinus.

EDCS-30101194.translation: To Belenus. Lucius Sentius Forensis [dedicated this].
EDCS-30101194.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deity Belenus by Lucius Sentius Forensis.

EDCS-30101195.translation: [---] most noble Caesar, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-30101195.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription repeatedly acclaiming a 'most noble Caesar,' likely from an honorific monument or public dedication.

EDCS-30101201.text_edition: Lucius Varius  Claudia Pr  PRO PRO
EDCS-30101201.translation: Lucius Varius, Claudia Pr[---], PRO PRO.
EDCS-30101201.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Lucius Varius and Claudia Pr[---], with the repeated 'PRO' possibly indicating a title, office, or part of a formula.

EDCS-30101202.text_edition: cius V Secundinae Primigeni filiae coniugi optimae annorum XXX
EDCS-30101202.translation: cius V to Secundina Primigeni, his most excellent wife, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-30101202.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by [---]cius V to his most excellent wife, Secundina Primigeni, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-30101204.summary: This short inscription simply records the name of Publius Alfius Priamus, likely from a tombstone, dedication, or property marker.

EDCS-30301340.text_edition: Titus Iulius Bellicus decurio alae Asturum II decurio municipii Claudiae Celeiae testamento fieri iussit Tiberius Iulius Frontonis filius Civis frater et heres fecit
EDCS-30301340.publication: CIL 03, 15205,3 = ILLPRON 01887 = Schober 00316 = AGN-2, 00383 = AIJ 00082 = EQNoricum-C, 00027 = AE 1903, 00071 = RICeleia 00284
EDCS-30301340.translation: Titus Julius Bellicus, decurion of the Second Asturian cavalry wing, decurion of the municipality of Claudia Celeia, ordered this to be made by testament. Tiberius Julius Civis, son of Fronto, his brother and heir, made it.
EDCS-30301340.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the last will of Titus Julius Bellicus, a veteran and municipal dignitary, whose monument was erected by his brother and heir.

EDCS-30400932.text_edition: Caio Cabalionio Masculi TIONEIN et Claudiae filiae AITAVXKI et Tim Mas
EDCS-30400932.translation: To Gaius Cabalionio, son of Masculus, TIONEIN, and to Claudia, his daughter, AITAVXKI, and to Tim Mas.
EDCS-30400932.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Cabalionio, his daughter Claudia, and others, likely erected by family members.

EDCS-31500094.text_edition: Titus Ambisavi filius hannorum sic LXXX hic situs Uxela Griponi filia annorum L
EDCS-31500094.translation: Titus, son of Ambisavus, aged 80, lies here. Uxela, daughter of Gripo, aged 50.
EDCS-31500094.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus, son of Ambisavus, and Uxela, daughter of Gripo, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-32200531.text_edition: Pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti  collegiati quorum nomina infra scripta sunt  ius Severinus onius Secundinus ius Optatus ius Mandatus ius Quinctus Annius Respectus ius Extricatus ius Ferrox Titius Ianuarius Iulius Candidus  s Exsuperans s Iuncinus s Secundinus ius Candidus s Homuncio s Restitutus ius Primitivus s Fuscus us Crescens s Messor   Ulpius Rufius [ &
EDCS-32200531.publication: ILLPRON 00611 = ILLPRON 00618 = ILLPRON 00619 = Leber 00076 = EQNoricum-V, 00039 = AEA 1999/00, +00019
EDCS-32200531.translation: For the well-being of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, the members of the college whose names are written below: Severinus, Secundinus, Optatus, Mandatus, Quintus Annius Respectus, Extricatus, Ferrox, Titius Januarius, Julius Candidus, Exsuperans, Juncinus, Secundinus, Candidus, Homuncio, Restitutus, Primitivus, Fuscus, Crescens, Messor, Ulpius Rufius.
EDCS-32200531.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a collegium (association) for the health and well-being of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, likely Caracalla (reigned 198-217 AD), listing its members.

EDCS-32300401.text_edition: Caio Vitorio Iucundo et Primiae Privatae Caius Vitorius Ingenuus parentibus optimis vivus fecit et sibi et suis
EDCS-32300401.translation: To Gaius Vitorius Jucundus and Primia Privata, his most excellent parents, Gaius Vitorius Ingenuus made this while living, for them, himself, and his family.
EDCS-32300401.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Gaius Vitorius Ingenuus for his parents, and also for himself and his family, while he was still alive.

EDCS-32300437.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Pacilius Privatus et Ursa Ursuli coniunx et Ianuaria filia vivi fecerunt
EDCS-32300437.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pacilius Privatus and Ursa, wife of Ursulus, and Januaria, their daughter, made this while living.
EDCS-32300437.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Manes, prepared by Pacilius Privatus, his wife Ursa, and their daughter Januaria, during their lifetime.

EDCS-32300479.translation: [...] for the municipality of Claudius Aguntum.
EDCS-32300479.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a dedication or construction related to the municipality of Claudius Aguntum.

EDCS-32300635.text_edition: Publio Cassio Materniano filio annnorum III
EDCS-32300635.translation: To Publius Cassius Maternianus, his son, aged 3 years.
EDCS-32300635.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Cassius Maternianus, who died at the young age of three.

EDCS-32300650.text_edition: Dis Manibus Saprius Secundus Ulpiae Domtillae coniugi @(obitae) annorum L
EDCS-32300650.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Saprius Secundus (made this) for Ulpia Domtilla, his deceased wife, aged 50 years.
EDCS-32300650.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Saprius Secundus for his wife, Ulpia Domtilla, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-32300651.text_edition: Dis Manibus Restuti Quarti equitis alae Commagenorum singularis nnorum XXXV heres Respectus Dubitati faciendum curavit
EDCS-32300651.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Restutus Quartus, a horseman of the Commagenian cavalry wing, a personal bodyguard, aged 35 years. His heir, Respectus, son of Dubitatus, had this made.
EDCS-32300651.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Restutus Quartus, a cavalryman and bodyguard, erected by his heir, Respectus, son of Dubitatus.

EDCS-32300685.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Caio Iulio Ursino decurioni alae I Commagenorum annorum XL Titia Procula marito optimo fecit et sibi
EDCS-32300685.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Iulius Ursinus, decurion of the First Commagenian Wing, 40 years old. Titia Procula made this for her excellent husband and for herself.
EDCS-32300685.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Titia Procula for her husband, Gaius Iulius Ursinus, a decurion in a cavalry unit, and for herself.

EDCS-32300695.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Aemilio veterano annorum LXV Aelia Amuca coniux marito faciendum curavit
EDCS-32300695.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelia Amuca, his wife, had this made for her husband Aelius Aemilius, a veteran 65 years old.
EDCS-32300695.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Aelia Amuca for her husband, Aelius Aemilius, a veteran.

EDCS-32400005.text_edition: Potens Savariensis vivus fecit sibi et Maeciae    coniugi optimae et Maecio Primitivo filio et Aug Suadrae coniugi
EDCS-32400005.translation: Potens Savariensis made this while living for himself and for Maecia, his excellent wife, and for Maecius Primitivus, his son, and for Aug. Suadra, his wife.
EDCS-32400005.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Potens Savariensis during his lifetime for himself, his first wife Maecia, his son Maecius Primitivus, and his second wife Aug. Suadra.

EDCS-32400010.text_edition: Tertio Valentis et Samuconi Cupiti et Valentino filio annorum L et Suadrae coniugi
EDCS-32400010.translation: To Tertius, son of Valens, and to Samucon, son of Cupitus, and to Valentinus, his son, 50 years old, and to Suadra, his wife.
EDCS-32400010.summary: This appears to be a funerary dedication, possibly incomplete or poorly preserved, mentioning Tertius, Samucon, Valentinus, and Suadra.

EDCS-32400014.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae Aelius Donatus pro salute sua et Octavies sic coniugis
EDCS-32400014.translation: To Nemesis Augusta. Aelius Donatus [made this] for his own well-being and that of his wife, Octavies.
EDCS-32400014.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Nemesis Augusta by Aelius Donatus for the health and well-being of himself and his wife, Octavies.

EDCS-32400015.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae sacrum Martialis venator votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-32400015.translation: Sacred to Nemesis Augusta. Martialis, a hunter, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32400015.summary: This is a votive inscription to Nemesis Augusta, dedicated by Martialis, a hunter, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32400016.text_edition: Campestribus sacrum Verecundus eques alae I Thracum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32400016.translation: Sacred to the Campestres. Verecundus, a cavalryman of the First Thracian Wing, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32400016.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Campestres (field deities) by Verecundus, a cavalryman from the First Thracian Wing, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32400018.text_edition: Nemesi Reginae  Augustae Cassius Sextus quod voberat sic libenti sic animo posuit
EDCS-32400018.translation: To Nemesis Regina Augusta. Cassius Sextus placed this, having vowed it, with a willing spirit.
EDCS-32400018.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Nemesis Regina Augusta by Cassius Sextus, fulfilling a vow he made.

EDCS-32400019.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae sacrum Marcus venator ex voto libens merito fecit
EDCS-32400019.translation: Sacred to Nemesis Augusta. Marcus, a hunter, willingly and deservedly made this out of a vow.
EDCS-32400019.summary: This is a votive inscription to Nemesis Augusta, dedicated by Marcus, a hunter, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32400025.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Aurelio Valerio Diocletiano invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate iterum consuli patri patriae proconsuli ordo coloniae Ovilavensium devoti sic numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-32400025.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletianus, the unconquered Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, Consul, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul. The order of the colony of Ovilava, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-32400025.summary: This is a public dedication, likely on a monument or statue base, by the municipal council of Ovilava, honoring Emperor Diocletian and listing his titles, likely dating to c. 287 AD based on his iteration of tribunician power.

EDCS-32400026.text_edition: Gentilis Finiti filius et Crispa Atti vivi fecerunt sibi et Censorinae filiae annorum X
EDCS-32400026.translation: Gentilis, son of Finitus, and Crispa, daughter of Attus, made this in their lifetime for themselves and for their daughter Censorina, who lived 10 years.
EDCS-32400026.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the creation of a tomb by Gentilis and Crispa for themselves and their deceased daughter Censorina, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32400240.translation: To Septumus, son of Lucius, and to his wife Quincta.
EDCS-32400240.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication made for Septumus, son of Lucius, and his wife Quincta.

EDCS-32400268.text_edition: Herculi Aug(usto) sacrum Quintus Luccius Maximus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32400268.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Quintus Luccius Maximus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32400268.summary: This is a religious dedication on an altar or votive stone, made by Quintus Luccius Maximus to Hercules Augustus as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32400271.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Postumius [made this].
EDCS-32400271.summary: This is a short religious dedication to Hercules Augustus, made by an individual named Postumius.

EDCS-32400273.text_edition: Flaviae Tertullae coniugi pientissimae Metilius Ianuarius vir vivus fecit
EDCS-32400273.translation: To Flavia Tertulla, his most devoted wife, Metilius Ianuarius, her husband, made this in his lifetime.
EDCS-32400273.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Tertulla, erected by her husband Metilius Ianuarius while still alive, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32400274.text_edition: Cai Iuni Luci filii Paeti   ab Carthaginis  LXXXXVIII
EDCS-32400274.translation: To Gaius Iunius Paetus, son of Lucius, from Carthage, [who lived] 98 years.
EDCS-32400274.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iunius Paetus, who was from Carthage and lived to be 98 years old.

EDCS-32400324.text_edition: Marcus Iunius -- Sextus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32400324.translation: Marcus Iunius [and] Sextus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-32400324.summary: This is a religious dedication recording that Marcus Iunius and Sextus fulfilled a vow, likely on a votive altar.

EDCS-32500204.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Aurelius Aurelianus [made this].
EDCS-32500204.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, made by or for Quintus Aurelius Aurelianus.

EDCS-32500210.text_edition: Butta Septimi filia sibi et Felicioni Primigeni filio viro optimo
EDCS-32500210.translation: Butta, daughter of Septimus, [made this] for herself and for Felicio, son of Primigenius, her most excellent husband.
EDCS-32500210.summary: This is a funerary inscription created by Butta for herself and her husband Felicio, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32500217.text_edition: Dis Manibus onio ino  IIviro  nno-- L
EDCS-32500217.translation: To the Divine Manes. [---]nius [---]nus, duumvir, [who lived] 50 years.
EDCS-32500217.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, for an individual who was a duumvir and lived 50 years, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32500293.text_edition: Publio Lepidio Publi filio Prisco domo Vicetia Prisca et Sabina filiae fecerunt
EDCS-32500293.translation: To Publius Lepidius Priscus, son of Publius, from Vicetia; Prisca and Sabina, his daughters, made this.
EDCS-32500293.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Prisca and Sabina for their father, Publius Lepidius Priscus, who was from Vicetia.

EDCS-32600001.text_edition: Belino Augusto sacrum Marcus dius Iunus aedilicia potestate Claudi Viruni et Restitutia Restuta pro se et suis voto posuerunt laeti libentes merito Praesente et Extricato consulibus
EDCS-32600001.translation: Sacred to Belinus Augustus. Marcus Dius Iunus, with the aedilician power of Claudius Virunus and Restitutia Restuta, gladly, willingly, and deservedly placed this vow for themselves and their kin, in the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus.
EDCS-32600001.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory vow to the deity Belinus Augustus, made by Marcus Dius Iunus, Restitutia Restuta, and possibly Claudius Virunus, dated to the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus (likely 217 AD).

EDCS-32601995.text_edition: Aureli-- Plu Aureli-- Dem Aureli-- Ch Aureli-- Fer Aureli--  Aureli--  A[ureli-- &
EDCS-32601995.translation: Aurelius Plu[---], Aurelius Dem[---], Aurelius Ch[---], Aurelius Fer[---], Aurelius [---], Aurelius [---], A[urelius ---].
EDCS-32601995.summary: This fragment likely records a list of individuals, possibly a group of Aurelii, though the specific purpose or context is largely lost due to its fragmentary nature.

EDCS-32602005.text_edition: Pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Imperatorum Maximini et Maximi Caesarum Auggustorum Caius Cassius Honoratus murum longitudinis pedum XXXX ruina conlapsum a solo restituit et podium amphitheatri opere tectorio cum pictura muneris sui exornavit et portam novam fecit Idibus Maiis Perpetuo et Corneliano consulibus
EDCS-32602005.translation: For the well-being of our Lords, the Emperors Maximinus and Maximus Caesars Augusti, Gaius Cassius Honoratus restored from its foundation a wall forty feet in length, which had collapsed in ruins, and adorned the amphitheater podium with plasterwork, including paintings of his own show, and built a new gate, on the Ides of May, in the consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelianus.
EDCS-32602005.summary: This inscription records the extensive restoration work on an amphitheater, including a wall and podium decoration, as well as the construction of a new gate by Gaius Cassius Honoratus, dedicated for the well-being of Emperors Maximinus and Maximus Caesars Augusti, and dated to May 15th during the consulship of Perpetuus and Cornelianus (237 AD).

EDCS-32602006.text_edition: Lucio Sestio Sexto et Valeriae Quinctionis filiae Lutumarae Moderatus filius vivus fecit
EDCS-32602006.translation: Moderatus, his son, made this for Lucius Sestius Sextus and Valeria Quinctionis Lutumara, while still alive.
EDCS-32602006.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by Moderatus for his parents, Lucius Sestius Sextus and Valeria Quinctionis Lutumara, while he was still living.

EDCS-32602011.text_edition: Tiberius? Iulius Ti(beri) libertus Quintallus vivus fecit s(ibi) et Iul(iae) Severae coniug(i) optimae et Quintiano filio
EDCS-32602011.translation: Tiberius (?) Iulius Quintallus, freedman of Tiberius, made this while alive for himself and for Iulia Severa, his excellent wife, and for Quintianus, his son.
EDCS-32602011.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Tiberius (?) Iulius Quintallus for himself, his wife Iulia Severa, and his son Quintianus, while he was still living.

EDCS-32602012.translation: And Suadr[---], his daughter (or son), made [this monument] for his wife [---].
EDCS-32602012.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely pertaining to a funerary monument, possibly indicating a dedication made by a child, Suadr[---], for their parent or a spouse.

EDCS-32602032.text_edition: M GE vivus fecit  et Vi coniugi
EDCS-32602032.translation: M. GE[---] made this while alive, and for Vi[---] his wife.
EDCS-32602032.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription for a funerary monument made by M. GE[---] for himself and his wife, Vi[---], during his lifetime.

EDCS-32700002.summary: This is an extremely fragmentary inscription, with only a few letters preserved, making its content and purpose entirely obscure.

EDCS-32700147.text_edition: Caio Iulio Cai filio Iant Tiberi-  Ia
EDCS-32700147.translation: To Gaius Iulius Iant[---], son of Gaius, and Tiberius [---], Ia[---].
EDCS-32700147.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely dedicating a monument to Gaius Iulius Iant[---] and possibly another individual, Tiberius [---].

EDCS-35100008.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication, likely on a monument, made by Vecconius to Sarapio and at least one other unnamed individual.

EDCS-38000292.text_edition: Luci AtI m IV pondo III |(unciis duabus  Philimuso sic
EDCS-38000292.translation: Of Lucius Atius, weight four pounds, two ounces. To Philomusus, thus.
EDCS-38000292.summary: This inscription appears to be an ownership or inventory mark, possibly on an object, detailing its weight and dedication to Philomusus, likely dating from the early imperial period.

EDCS-38000298.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication to the gods, possibly associated with Menelaus, suggesting a religious context.

EDCS-44200174.translation: Iunianus, freedman of the Augusti, foster-child.
EDCS-44200174.summary: This inscription identifies Iunianus as a foster-child and a freedman of the emperors, likely dating to the imperial period and indicating his status.

EDCS-44200178.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory fragment naming Pompeius and another individual, likely the son of Titus, suggesting a Roman family context.

EDCS-44200180.text_edition: Iunianus vivus fecit sibi et Quinto Cen filio quaestori Claudiae Celeiae annorum XXXV et nae filiae annorum XXVIII
EDCS-44200180.translation: Iunianus, while living, made this for himself and for Quintus Censo, his son, quaestor of Claudia Celeia, 35 years old, and for his daughter [---]na, 28 years old.
EDCS-44200180.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Iunianus built a tomb for himself while alive, and for his son Quintus Censo, a quaestor, and his daughter, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-44200181.summary: This is a fragment recording a transaction where Verus purchased something from a person whose name began with 'Res-', likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-44300014.text_edition: Alba Cisioni viva fecit sibi et Rata Alba   annorum XXX X
EDCS-44300014.translation: Alba Cisioni, while living, made this for herself and for Rata Alba, 30 years old.
EDCS-44300014.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Alba Cisioni built a tomb for herself and for Rata Alba while she was still alive, indicating a pre-mortem commemoration.

EDCS-44300015.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication or funerary inscription addressed to Gaius Julius Visonius, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-44300017.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication, possibly to a deity or group known as the Ambisontes, suggesting a religious or tribal context.

EDCS-44300018.text_edition: Dis Manibus ius Potens  signifer legionis INtutia na uxor Titus Sentus filii vivi  SENTIS
EDCS-44300018.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iustus Potens, standard-bearer of the [...] legion. His wife [---]ia, his wife Titus Sentius, his children, all living, to Sentius.
EDCS-44300018.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a standard-bearer, erected by his wife and children while still living.

EDCS-44300019.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Lucius Martius Verecundus, notable for the unusual age recorded.

EDCS-44300020.text_edition: Ulpio Ronodeo veterano alae I Augustae annorum LV Memmia Sibulla coniugi pientissimo viva fecit
EDCS-44300020.translation: To Ulpius Ronodeus, veteran of the First Augustan Wing, aged 55. Memmia Sibulla made this while living for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-44300020.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Memmia Sibulla to her husband, Ulpius Ronodeus, a veteran, during her lifetime.

EDCS-44300021.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Iulio Straboni sesquiplicario alae I Augustae annorum XXXV stipendiorum XII Marcus Attonius Seranus heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-44300021.translation: To the Divine Manes of Quintus Iulius Strabo, a sesquiplicarius (one-and-a-half-pay soldier) of the First Augustan Wing, aged 35, with 12 years of service. Marcus Attonius Seranus, his heir, saw to its erection.
EDCS-44300021.summary: This funerary inscription honors Quintus Iulius Strabo, a soldier, and was erected by his heir, Marcus Attonius Seranus.

EDCS-44300022.text_edition: Haldius Atti filius eques alae I Thracum annorum XXXVII stipendiorum XVIII heres fE(aciendum)#f(aciendum)#E curavit Macer signifer
EDCS-44300022.translation: Haldius, son of Attius, a cavalryman of the First Thracian Wing, aged 37, with 18 years of service. Macer, the standard-bearer, his heir, saw to its erection.
EDCS-44300022.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Haldius, a cavalryman, set up by his heir, Macer, who was a standard-bearer.

EDCS-44300023.summary: This inscription appears to be a commercial or inventory tag for a quantity of olives.

EDCS-44300025.text_edition: Insequens Apti vivus fecit sibi et Mansuetae Tironis filiae coniugi optimae annorum XXX
EDCS-44300025.translation: Insequens, son of Aptius, made this while living for himself and for Mansueta, daughter of Tiro, his most excellent wife, aged 30.
EDCS-44300025.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Insequens for himself and his wife, Mansueta, during his lifetime.

EDCS-44300029.summary: This brief inscription identifies Secundinus as the freedman of Senecio, likely part of a larger funerary or ownership context.

EDCS-46600012.summary: This short inscription indicates ownership or dedication by Flavius Fontanus.

EDCS-46600014.text_edition: Aulus Iunius Car Cereri donum dedit
EDCS-46600014.translation: Aulus Iunius Car gave this gift to Ceres.
EDCS-46600014.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording a gift from Aulus Iunius Car to the goddess Ceres.

EDCS-46600018.summary: This inscription records that Catulus was the maker of the object it adorns, likely a crafted item or small monument.

EDCS-48700106.summary: This inscription identifies Amoenus as a slave belonging to Priamus, likely part of a funerary monument or ownership mark.

EDCS-48800035.translation: Of Novellus, the standard-bearer. Of Singularis, from the Astures.
EDCS-48800035.summary: This inscription likely records the names of two individuals, Novellus, a standard-bearer, and Singularis, from the Astures, possibly on a shared monument or two separate but related dedications.

EDCS-48900538.text_edition: Rufinus L Serus NINIIINIII Ingenui IISILIIIICIIIVV
EDCS-48900538.summary: This fragmentary inscription lists several names, Rufinus, L. Serus, and Ingenuus, with additional illegible characters, likely from a dedicatory or funerary monument.

EDCS-48900539.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions Adiutor and Ingenuus, with the rest missing, probably from a funerary or votive context.

EDCS-48900543.text_edition: Panna Verecundaes sic empta vigessi  XXV
EDCS-48900543.translation: Panna of Verecunda, thus bought for twenty-five.
EDCS-48900543.summary: This inscription records the purchase of a 'panna' (possibly a piece of land, a garment, or another item) belonging to Verecunda for 25 units.

EDCS-48900553.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iuvavo pro salute Mari Aniceti et negotiationis eiius sic
EDCS-48900553.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to Iuvavus, for the health of Marius Anicetus and for his business, thus.
EDCS-48900553.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Iuvavus, made for the well-being and successful business of Marius Anicetus.

EDCS-48900561.translation: Liccaius, son of Pomponius (or belonging to Pomponius).
EDCS-48900561.summary: This inscription identifies Liccaius as either the son or a dependent of Pomponius, likely from a personal marker or funerary inscription.

EDCS-48900565.summary: This inscription identifies Adgonetus by his patronymic, stating he is the son of Vindunis, typical of a funerary or personal record.

EDCS-48900567.summary: This fragmentary inscription names Primanus and identifies him as the son of Elvissus, with the rest of the text missing, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-48900568.text_edition: Censorino Terti filio et Secundinae ionis filiae Acceptus? f(ilius)
EDCS-48900568.translation: To Censorinus, son of Tertius, and to Secundina, daughter of Io. Acceptus, their son.
EDCS-48900568.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Acceptus for his parents Censorinus and Secundina, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-48900595.text_edition: Iulius Iulianus miles cohortis I Aureliae Brittonum magister figulinae Loco Felicis  SA
