EDCS-48900595.translation: Iulius Iulianus, soldier of the First Aurelian Cohort of Britons, master of the brickworks, at Felix's place.
EDCS-48900595.summary: This inscription records Iulius Iulianus, a soldier and master craftsman in a brickworks, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-48900597.text_edition: Cotunno Tiberi Iuli Respecti servo per Septumum sic fratrem impensa dominica
EDCS-48900597.translation: To Cotunnus, slave of Tiberius Iulius Respectus, through Septumus, his brother, at the master's expense.
EDCS-48900597.summary: This inscription commemorates Cotunnus, a slave, with a monument provided by his master, Tiberius Iulius Respectus, and erected by his brother Septumus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-48900598.text_edition: IAIA f(ecit) sibi et Sao coniugi et Masculo filio
EDCS-48900598.translation: IAIA made this for herself and for her husband Sao, and for her son Masculinus.
EDCS-48900598.summary: This is a funerary inscription for IAIA, her husband Sao, and her son Masculinus, made by IAIA herself during her lifetime, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-48900599.text_edition: Bussumaro Atevali filio  Aelius  filius Secundus vivus fecit
EDCS-48900599.translation: To Bussumarus, son of Atevalus. Aelius Secundus, his son, made this while living.
EDCS-48900599.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aelius Secundus for his father Bussumarus, son of Atevalus, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-48900600.text_edition: Primo Septumi sic Marci liberto Restuta Restuti filia coniugi viva fecit et CaeruloE#Caerulo#CERULE coniugi et Solito filio
EDCS-48900600.translation: To Primus, freedman of Septumus. Restuta, daughter of Restutus, made this while living for her husband and for Caerulo, her husband, and for Solitus, her son.
EDCS-48900600.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Restuta for her husband Primus, a freedman, and for her other husband Caerulo, and her son Solitus, indicating multiple marriages or a complex family structure, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-48900605.translation: Ursus, a gift vowed to Jupiter.
EDCS-48900605.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a gift dedicated to Jupiter by Ursus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-48900606.text_edition: Marcus Iunius Iustinus IGN  Sen Ursinus
EDCS-48900606.summary: This is likely a funerary or ownership inscription mentioning Marcus Iunius Iustinus and Sen Ursinus, probably from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-48900607.summary: This is a short inscription that seems to record a gift from Caius and a dinner provided by Iustinus, possibly related to a banquet or dedication, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-48900609.text_edition: Sura Suri  Sura Masculi CIONI Suri
EDCS-48900609.translation: Sura, son of Sura. Sura, son of Masculinus, son of Cioni, son of Sura.
EDCS-48900609.summary: This inscription lists a series of names, possibly indicating a lineage or family relationship, likely from a funerary context, dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-49100360.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Pius Augustus et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Pollieno Sebenno legato beneficia quae amplissimo ordine vel aliquo principe iubente collegiis centonariorum concessa sunt temere convelli non oportet sed quod legibus eorum sanxum sic est custodiatur et ii quos dicis divitiis suis sine onere uti publica subire munera compellantur neque enim collegiorum privilegium prosit aut iis qui artem non exercent aut iis qui maiores facultates praefinito modo possident adversus divites dum legis adhibendum est remedium non propter hos minuendus numerus alioquin tenuiores perfruantur vacatione quae non competit beneficiis collegiorum derogari   s  Tertulli Severi Priscus  Quarti nis Ursulus  Propincus sic  Calvisi Victoriani  Vibeni itae Sexti  Atilius Iunianus Castrucius Castruci Crispius Quartus Respectinus Restituti Severinus Severi Quintus Castruci Congius Cosalus Ursus Publi Dubitanus Masculi Secundinus Adnamati Valerius Commodus Maternus Crispi  Maleio Maturi Primianus Primitivi Secundinus Quarti Sertorius Karus Claudius Leo Ulpius Vitalis Atilianus Attici Emeritus Serotini Iulius Saturninus Vitalis Ruccessi Kanius Dignus Veranus Iuniani  Classicianus Canionis Rettius Heracla Nonius Tertullinus Insequens Taciti Vibius Catussa Secundus Tertulli Finitus Valentionis Sertorius Karus iunior Calendinus Angulati Spectatianus Spectati Valentinus Vitalis Iulius Ianuarius Iulius Marinianus Ulpius Quietus  Crispinus Vibeni Pompeius Vitalis Masculinus Surionis Tertullinus Tutoris Potentinus Potentinae Sextius Atilis Flavius Annianus Kanius Valentinus Aurelius Sabinianus Sabinianus Sabini Sacretius Sextus Secundianus Secundi Iulius Secundinus Iulius Tacitianus  Vitalis Ingenui Sextius Maximus Rutilius Rutilianus Severus Senilis Primigenianus Primigeni Longinius Paterio Domitius Adnamatus Titius Restutus Valerius Valerianus Tertius Vitalis Iunius Tertullus Ingenuus Adnami Rufius Optatus Marcianus Ingenui  Flavius Genialis Terentinus Marini Iustus Iustini Iunius Paterio Aurelius Maximus Crispius Honoratus Ingenuus Maleionis Surianus Secundini Iulius Valentinus LIicinius#Licinius#IIC Iunius Quintianus Quinti Iunius Secundinus Macedo Optati  ordo et populus in honorem Marci Secundi Secundini patris tabulam posuit ex pecunia collegii centonariorum decreto decurionum rei publicae Solvensium pridie Idus Octobres Imperatore Antonino II et Geta consulibus curam agente Ursino
EDCS-49100360.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Pius Augustus and the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus [declare to] Pollianus Sebenius, legate: the privileges granted to the collegia of centonarii (firefighters/felt-makers) by order of the most distinguished council or by any prince should not be rashly revoked, but what has been sanctioned by their laws should thus be maintained. And those whom you mention, who use their wealth without burden, should be compelled to undertake public duties. For the privilege of the collegia should not benefit either those who do not practice the craft or those who possess greater means than the defined limit. Against the wealthy, while a legal remedy must be applied, the number of otherwise poorer members enjoying the exemption (which is not due to them) should not be diminished by derogating the benefits of the collegia. S. Tertullus, Severus, Priscus, Quartinius, Ursulus, Propincus, Calvisius Victorianus, Vibenius Itta, Sextus, Atilius Iunianus, Castrucius, Castrucius Crispius, Quartus Respectinus, Restitutus, Severinus, Severus, Quintus Castrucius, Congius, Cosalus, Ursus, Publius Dubitanus, Masculinus, Secundinus, Adnamatus, Valerius Commodus, Maternus Crispius, Maelius, Maturus, Primianus, Primitivus, Secundinus, Quartus, Sertorius Carus, Claudius Leo, Ulpius Vitalis, Atilianus, Atticus, Emeritus, Serotinus, Iulius Saturninus, Vitalis, Rucessus, Canius, Dignus, Veranus, Iunianus, Classicianus, Canionus, Rettius Heracla, Nonius Tertullinus, Insequens Taciti, Vibius Catussa, Secundus Tertullus, Finitus Valentio, Sertorius Carus iunior, Calendinus Angulatus, Spectatianus, Spectatus, Valentinus Vitalis, Iulius Ianuarius, Iulius Marinianus, Ulpius Quietus, Crispinus Vibenius, Pompeius Vitalis, Masculinus Surio, Tertullinus Tutor, Potentinus Potentina, Sextius Atilius, Flavius Annianus, Canius Valentinus, Aurelius Sabinianus, Sabinianus Sabinus, Sacretius Sextus, Secundianus Secundus, Iulius Secundinus, Iulius Tacitianus, Vitalis Ingenuus, Sextius Maximus, Rutilius Rutilianus, Severus Senilis, Primigenianus Primigenius, Longinius Paterio, Domitius Adnamatus, Titius Restitutus, Valerius Valerianus, Tertius Vitalis, Iunius Tertullus, Ingenuus Adnamatus, Rufius Optatus, Marcianus Ingenuus, Flavius Genialis, Terentinus Marinus, Iustus Iustinus, Iunius Paterio, Aurelius Maximus, Crispius Honoratus, Ingenuus Maleio, Surianus Secundinus, Iulius Valentinus, Licinius, Iunius Quintianus, Quintus, Iunius Secundinus, Macedo Optatus – the order and people erected this tablet in honor of Marcus Secundus Secundinus, his father, from the collegium of centonarii's funds, by decree of the decurions of the community of Solva, on the day before the Ides of October, with Emperors Antoninus II and Geta as consuls, under the care of Ursinus.
EDCS-49100360.summary: This lengthy inscription is an imperial rescript from Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla (Antoninus) to the legate Pollianus Sebenius, confirming and clarifying the privileges of the collegia of centonarii in the community of Solva, and lists numerous individuals likely members of this collegium, dated to October 14, 205 CE.

EDCS-49100366.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum Flavius Covnertus et Nonius in honorem collegii Manliensium titulum donum dederunt   Hilarus Secundi talis s   Varius Ursus Naevius Fortunatus Domitius Primus Iulius Vibianus Iulius Secundinus dius Calendini Ingenuus unus   Aulus A Iulius Iulius I P      s s us s Ingenuus  Rediti  Virunensium Mi Ursus ato Maximus Vitalius Successianus Claudius Hilarus Romanius Ligur Speratius Maximi Baebius Fortunatus Cornelius Dubitatus Crescentius Tertullus  erc E Atunus Butti Vettius Secundus Ursus Itti Martius Covnerti Publicus Aviti Ursus Lucconis Genetivus Marini Quintius Quintilianus Aurelius Ingenuus Flavius Ingenuus Lucanius Secundus Flavius Ursinus Septimius Successi Ulpius Valerius Speratus Hilari Maternius Acutio Tappius Firmus Aurelius  Iulius Fortunatus Septimius Claudianus   Do Iulius  Candidus Murcianus Aurelius  Antonius  Catronius Aurelius  Ulpianus Campanius  Securianus
EDCS-49100366.publication: CIL 03, 04778 = CIL 03, 04785 (p 2328,44) = ILLPRON 00522 = ILLPRON 00616 = ILLPRON 00617 = ILLPRON 00625 = ILLPRON 00628 = ILLPRON 00629 = ILLPRON 00630 = Leber 00075 = EQNoricum-V, 00002 = AEA 2008, +00040
EDCS-49100366.translation: Sacred to Augusta Fortuna. Flavius Covnertus and Nonius gave this inscription as a gift in honor of the collegium of Manlienses. Hilarus Secundus, and so on... Varius Ursus, Naevius Fortunatus, Domitius Primus, Iulius Vibianus, Iulius Secundinus, a god Calendinus, Ingenuus, one Aulus A. Iulius, Iulius I.P., Ingenuus, Reditus, of the Virunenses, M. Ursus, Maxius Vitalius, Successianus, Claudius Hilarus, Romanius, Ligur, Speratius Maximus, Baebius Fortunatus, Cornelius Dubitatus, Crescentius Tertullus, Erc E. Atunus, Butti, Vettius Secundus, Ursus Itti, Martius Covnerti, Publicus Aviti, Ursus Lucco, Genetivus Marinus, Quintius Quintilianus, Aurelius Ingenuus, Flavius Ingenuus, Lucanius Secundus, Flavius Ursinus, Septimius Successus, Ulpius Valerius, Speratus Hilarus, Maternius Acutio, Tappius Firmus, Aurelius, Iulius Fortunatus, Septimius Claudianus, Do Iulius, Candidus Murcianus, Aurelius, Antonius, Catronius Aurelius, Ulpianus Campanius, Securianus.
EDCS-49100366.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Fortuna Augusta by Flavius Covnertus and Nonius, on behalf of the collegium of Manlienses, and lists a large number of individuals, likely members or benefactors of the collegium, suggesting a communal religious act or commemorative event.

EDCS-49100368.text_edition: Nomina collegiatorum subaedianorum  Aterius Calendinus Curionius Cautus Gaius Alfius Granius Satrius Tertianus Secundinus Obilonis Titius Quartus Tertullus Calvonis Octavius Vitalis Postumius Ripanus Successus Iuliani Aelius Axio Primigenius Secundini Crispius Tertianus Ulpius Petronianus Corido Ingenui Ingenuus Cupiti Iulius Vicarius Respectus Maximi Lucanus Secundini  Pompeius Verinus Liberalis Axionis Viatorius Castus Barbius Primigenius Britannius Candianus Crescentinus Maximi Attonius Pictor [] Mogetimarus Mogeti Successus Crescentis Messorinus Rufini [] Pompeius Antenor Acceptus Cassiani Ursulus Cauti Claudius Iucundus  [] Flora Cauti Iucunda Grani Priscia Tertiani Quinta Surionis Tertulla Secundini Benigna Quarti Sucelu Calvonis Veponia Quintilla Gemina Successi Rantia Ianuaria Secundina Secundini [] Primitiva Petroniani Secundina Ingenui  [] Calida Casti Restituta Primigeni Ingenua Mogetimari Sabinilla Cassiani Sabina Iucundi Dubitata Crescentis
EDCS-49100368.translation: Names of the collegiates of the subaediani (subordinates of the temple aediles): Aterius Calendinus, Curionius Cautus, Gaius Alfius, Granius Satrius, Tertianus Secundinus, Obilo, Titius Quartus, Tertullus Calvonis, Octavius Vitalis, Postumius Ripanus, Successus Iulianus, Aelius Axio, Primigenius Secundinus, Crispius Tertianus, Ulpius Petronianus, Corido Ingenuus, Ingenuus Cupiti, Iulius Vicarius, Respectus Maximus, Lucanus Secundinus, Pompeius Verinus, Liberalis Axio, Viatorius Castus, Barbius Primigenius, Britannius Candianus, Crescentinus Maximus, Attonius Pictor, Mogetimarus Mogeti, Successus Crescens, Messorinus Rufinus, Pompeius Antenor, Acceptus Cassianus, Ursulus Cautus, Claudius Iucundus, Flora Cautus, Iucunda Grani, Priscia Tertiana, Quinta Surio, Tertulla Secundina, Benigna Quartus, Sucelus Calvonis, Veponia Quintilla, Gemina Successus, Rantia Ianuaria, Secundina Secundina, Primitiva Petroniana, Secundina Ingenua, Calida Castus, Restituta Primigenia, Ingenua Mogetimarus, Sabinilla Cassiana, Sabina Iucundus, Dubitata Crescens.
EDCS-49100368.summary: This inscription is a list of members, both male and female, belonging to a collegium of 'subaediani,' likely an association connected with the administration or service of a temple, possibly a guild of temple functionaries or attendants.

EDCS-49300002.text_edition: Olei Histrici flos pondo V  Quinti Lusidi Dextri
EDCS-49300002.translation: Flower of Histric oil, five pounds. Of Quintus Lusidius Dexter.
EDCS-49300002.summary: This short inscription appears to be a label or record of a specific quantity (five pounds) of a high-quality 'Histric oil,' possibly noting its owner or vendor, Quintus Lusidius Dexter.

EDCS-49300008.summary: This brief inscription records the name 'Curenus' and specifies his military unit or administrative division as the 'centuria of Magus,' likely referring to a soldier and his centurion.

EDCS-49300010.summary: This very fragmentary inscription provides the name 'Silo' and part of another name, 'Gaius M[---]', possibly indicating two individuals or a patronymic.

EDCS-49300011.summary: This inscription is a simple record of two names, 'Tillius' and 'Alfenus,' possibly indicating ownership, dedication, or a simple list of individuals.

EDCS-49300012.summary: This inscription lists two names, 'Publius Postumius Frequens' and 'Atpomarus,' potentially identifying individuals in a formal context such as a public record or a dedication.

EDCS-49300015.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be part of a dedication or record involving 'Caesar' and includes the number 'XV,' possibly referring to a quantity, legion, or a fifteenth year/installment.

EDCS-49300017.summary: This inscription records the name 'Claudianus' twice, each time followed by a Roman numeral (VI and X), which could denote different individuals, a sequence of events, or quantities related to Claudianus.

EDCS-49300019.text_edition: Opsta sic feliciter leo patiens is
EDCS-49300019.translation: Opsta, thus happily, patient lion. This is it.
EDCS-49300019.summary: This inscription seems to be a personal or funerary lament, possibly for someone named Opsta, perhaps metaphorically referred to as a patient lion.

EDCS-49300020.summary: This inscription simply records the name 'Caesar,' possibly as a signature, ownership mark, or a very brief dedication.

EDCS-49300022.summary: This inscription records the travel of a person named Potens to Rome, likely a personal note or a funerary epitaph mentioning a significant event in their life.

EDCS-49300023.summary: This inscription likely marks the presence or ownership of Anxur Carpoforus, possibly at a specific location or on an object.

EDCS-49300025.text_edition: De fide Ombrionis secures CCCLV pondo II semis
EDCS-49300025.translation: Regarding Ombrion's trustworthiness, 355 axes weighing 2.5 pounds.
EDCS-49300025.summary: This inscription records a transaction or inventory related to Ombrion's reliability concerning 355 axes weighing 2.5 pounds each, indicating a commercial context.

EDCS-49300027.text_edition: Emit CX cumbas pondo XV Sineros Aquileia
EDCS-49300027.translation: Sineros from Aquileia bought 110 tubs weighing 15 pounds each.
EDCS-49300027.summary: This inscription records a commercial transaction where Sineros from Aquileia purchased 110 tubs, each weighing 15 pounds.

EDCS-49300030.text_edition: Aurum bonum secures pondo XI CCCXCIII Filenus sic Roma
EDCS-49300030.translation: Good gold axes, weighing 11 pounds, 393. Filenus from Rome, thus.
EDCS-49300030.summary: This inscription records a commercial or inventory note about 393 'good gold axes' weighing 11 pounds each, attributed to Filenus from Rome.

EDCS-49300032.text_edition: Aulus Pontius Vetulonia Publius Pontius Vitalio filius Firminus Vergilianus Cilo
EDCS-49300032.translation: Aulus Pontius from Vetulonia, Publius Pontius Vitalio, son Firminus Vergilianus Cilo.
EDCS-49300032.summary: This inscription lists the names of several individuals, likely indicating a family group or a succession, with Aulus Pontius from Vetulonia, Publius Pontius Vitalio, and his son Firminus Vergilianus Cilo.

EDCS-49300034.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the name Ruscus associated with the number 15 and the city of Bononia, likely indicating a person, quantity, or origin.

EDCS-49300035.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the names Septimianus and Taulus, likely referring to two individuals or indicating a familial relationship.

EDCS-49300036.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a dedication or a funerary marker, recording the name Volsenus Vindelicus, possibly from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-49300037.text_edition: exIuliis pecunia locata in Idus Octobres
EDCS-49300037.translation: From the Iulii family, money invested until the Ides of October.
EDCS-49300037.summary: This inscription records a financial transaction where money from the Iulii family was invested for a specific period, indicating a private or public financial record, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49300038.text_edition: s cados CL uncos DLX anulos DLXX s anulos DL uncos DX  anulos DLX uncos DLXV ius Albanus DX   Orobius d[iscos &
EDCS-49300038.translation: 150 jars, 560 hooks, 570 rings; 550 rings, 510 hooks; 560 rings, 565 hooks. Albanus 510. Orobius [discs]...
EDCS-49300038.summary: This is an inventory or list of various items (jars, hooks, rings, discs) with quantities, possibly related to trade, supply, or a treasury record, featuring names like Albanus and Orobius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49300039.translation: To Mercury. Proculus the father, Proclus M.
EDCS-49300039.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mercury by Proculus, the father, and Proclus M., likely a votive offering or an altar inscription from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49300040.translation: Mercurius, having undertaken a vow, to Mercury.
EDCS-49300040.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Mercury, made by an individual named Mercurius who fulfilled a vow, likely an altar or plaque from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49300043.summary: This short inscription records the name Abudius alongside a quantity of 160 discs, likely part of an inventory, a list of contributions, or a record of ownership, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49300044.summary: This inscription lists Orosius and a quantity of 110 discs, suggesting an inventory, a record of goods, or a similar administrative document, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49300045.summary: This inscription records Audonius with a quantity of 110 boats, likely an inventory or a record of naval assets or trade, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49300046.text_edition: Pecunia locata ex II Kalendis Februariis in Kalendas Iulias nus Sexti filius Lanivias
EDCS-49300046.translation: Money invested from the day before the Kalends of February until the Kalends of July. The son of Sextus, Lanivias.
EDCS-49300046.summary: This inscription details a financial investment made for a specific period, naming the son of Sextus, Lanivias, and likely served as a public or private financial record from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49300048.summary: This inscription records the name Rullus Proculus Verulanus followed by the number 110, likely indicating a quantity, a sum, or a sequence in a list, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49300049.summary: This is a brief record, possibly an inventory or ledger entry, noting Antipalus and a quantity of anvils and jars, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-49300056.text_edition: Lucius Opaius Verrucosus sacrum Mercurio fecit sua manu
EDCS-49300056.translation: Lucius Opaius Verrucosus made this sacred offering to Mercury with his own hand.
EDCS-49300056.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Opaius Verrucosus to the god Mercury, likely on a small altar or votive object, dating from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-49300061.text_edition: Recognitum? Astigis arca pondo LXX VIII Kalendas Martias  Pansa
EDCS-49300061.translation: Inspected? A chest from Astigi weighing 70 pounds, on the 8th Kalends of March. Pansa.
EDCS-49300061.summary: This inscription is an administrative record, possibly a shipping label or inventory note, detailing an inspected chest from Astigi, its weight, date, and a person named Pansa, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-49400001.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a funerary or dedicatory text, mentioning Genuinus Pisus, with the rest of the text missing or illegible, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-51100470.text_edition: Herculi Augusto Gemellus Biraconis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-51100470.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Gemellus, son of Biraco, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-51100470.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Gemellus, son of Biraco, fulfilling a vow to Hercules Augustus, likely on an altar, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-51100472.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription dedicated to Mars Ultor by Titus Kanius (or a similar name), likely on an altar, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-51100473.text_edition: Marti Augusto r Covnertus |(centurio) legionis VII geminae felicis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-51100473.translation: To Mars Augustus, Gaius Covnertus, centurion of the Seventh Gemina Felix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-51100473.summary: This is a votive inscription where Gaius Covnertus, a centurion, fulfills a vow to Mars Augustus, likely on an altar, dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-51100474.text_edition: Apulo Croutae filio et Boniatae Attonis filiae filii fecerunt
EDCS-51100474.translation: The sons made this for Apulus, son of Crouta, and Boniata, daughter of Atto.
EDCS-51100474.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the construction of a monument by sons in honor of their parents, Apulus son of Crouta and Boniata daughter of Atto, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-51100475.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque omnibus et Genio I  legionis II Italicae piae fidelis cum suis votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-51100475.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to all gods and goddesses, and to the Genius of the II Italic Legion, pious and faithful, with his companions, he willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-51100475.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, all gods and goddesses, and the Genius of the Legio II Italica Pia Fidelis, made by an individual and his companions, likely on a large altar or monumental base, dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-51100478.text_edition: Capito Satulli filio et Belatullae Covnerti filiae Belatullus filius fecit parentibus
EDCS-51100478.translation: Belatullus, son of Capito, made this for his parents, Capito, son of Satullus, and Belatulla, daughter of Covnertus.
EDCS-51100478.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Belatullus, son of Capito, dedicating a monument to his parents, Capito son of Satullus and Belatulla daughter of Covnertus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-51100479.text_edition: Icuinus et Claudia Restuta parentibus filii fecerunt
EDCS-51100479.translation: Icuinus and Claudia Restuta, children, made this for their parents.
EDCS-51100479.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Icuinus and Claudia Restuta for their parents.

EDCS-51100480.text_edition: Cupito et Tertiae coniugi filii fecerunt et Restutae
EDCS-51100480.translation: The children made this for Cupitus and Tertia, their spouse, and for Restuta.
EDCS-51100480.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by children for Cupitus, Tertia (his spouse), and Restuta.

EDCS-51100481.text_edition: Secundinus  et Tertullavivi? fecerunt? et Ingenuo Secundini filio? et Crescenti Secundini filio? et  Secundini f(ili--)? ---
EDCS-51100481.translation: Secundinus and Tertulla (living?) made this, and for Ingenuus, son of Secundinus, and for Crescens, son of Secundinus, and for the son(s) of Secundinus [---].
EDCS-51100481.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument made by Secundinus and Tertulla for themselves and their sons Ingenuus and Crescens, and other unnamed sons of Secundinus.

EDCS-51100484.text_edition: Aureliae Verecundae sorori Marci Aureli Surii
EDCS-51100484.translation: To Aurelia Verecunda, sister of Marcus Aurelius Surius.
EDCS-51100484.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Verecunda by her brother, Marcus Aurelius Surius.

EDCS-51400033.summary: This is an inscription dedicated to Publius Aelius Adiutor, likely part of a funerary monument or statue base.

EDCS-51400043.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Martinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-51400043.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Martinus, indicating a religious offering or dedication.

EDCS-51600011.text_edition: Iussu dei Mithrae F ROSAVIINI Aug Aurelius Aquila et Aurelius Flavianus sacerdotes posuerunt
EDCS-51600011.translation: By order of the god Mithras, F. Rosavinus Augustalis, Aurelius Aquila and Aurelius Flavianus, priests, erected this.
EDCS-51600011.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the erection of a monument by Aurelius Aquila and Aurelius Flavianus, who were priests, and F. Rosavinus Augustalis, acting under divine command from Mithras.

EDCS-54300072.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum  nus templum restituerunt
EDCS-54300072.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. [---] restored the temple.
EDCS-54300072.summary: This is a religious inscription dedicating a restored temple to Silvanus Augustus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-54300073.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Lucius VV
EDCS-54300073.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Lucius V.V. [dedicated this].
EDCS-54300073.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Lucius V.V., indicating a religious offering.

EDCS-58000092.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely indicating ownership or dedication relating to Petronius Severus.

EDCS-59200185.text_edition: SENOIVNOS  coniugi dulcissimae  finivit vitam annorum XXVIII IEus Adiuti filius  SEI carissimae
EDCS-59200185.translation: To Senoiunus, his sweetest wife. She finished her life at 28 years. Ieius, son of Adiutus, to his dearest.
EDCS-59200185.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a husband, Ieius, son of Adiutus, to his beloved wife, Senoiunus, who died at 28 years old.

EDCS-59200188.text_edition: Attius Vitalis atinius IuAvenis#Iuvenis#AVENIS FA posuit OL LM VS
EDCS-59200188.translation: Attius Vitalis, a young Atinian, placed this very willingly, having fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-59200188.summary: This is a votive inscription by Attius Vitalis, a young man from Atina, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-59900156.text_edition: Deo I(nvicto) Mithrae Martialis Augustorum pro salute Augustorum nostrorum vilici stationis Atrantinae
EDCS-59900156.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, Martialis, imperial slave, on behalf of the health of our emperors, the manager of the Atrantine station.
EDCS-59900156.summary: This is a dedication to Mithras by Martialis, an imperial slave and manager of a station, for the well-being of the emperors, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-62900040.text_edition: IIII Nonas Augustas Isauricus  Legio II
EDCS-62900040.translation: On the fourth day before the Nones of August (August 2nd), Isauricus, Legion II.
EDCS-62900040.summary: This inscription records a date, August 2nd, and possibly identifies a soldier named Isauricus from the Second Legion, likely marking a military event or activity.

EDCS-66300064.text_edition: Lucana Lucani filia dat ex numin-- monitu
EDCS-66300064.translation: Lucana, daughter of Lucanus, gives this by the command of the deities.
EDCS-66300064.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Lucana, daughter of Lucanus, made in response to a divine command.

EDCS-67300027.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Abuius [or Abuvio].
EDCS-67300027.summary: This is a funerary inscription, dedicating the monument to the Manes and bearing the partial name Abuius or Abuvio.

EDCS-67600233.summary: This is a short inscription, possibly a label or record, indicating a person named Hilarus, likely from Ate(---), and a quantity of 11 modii.

EDCS-67600234.translation: To Coxiva, from Essivus. Byssus, 10 pounds.
EDCS-67600234.summary: This inscription appears to be an inventory label or a record of a gift, specifying 'byssus' (fine linen or silk) weighing 10 pounds, for Coxiva from Essivus.

EDCS-67600235.translation: A 'paenula' (cloak) weighing 8 pounds. Lovan(---), from Ate(---).
EDCS-67600235.summary: This inscription is likely an inventory label, noting a cloak ('paenula') weighing 8 pounds, associated with a person named Lovan(---) from Ate(---).

EDCS-67600236.translation: Monica, wife of Saccus. Red exuviae (spoils/skins), 10. 1 denarius to the excisor (cutter/tax collector) for one.
EDCS-67600236.summary: This inscription appears to be an inventory or tax record, possibly related to animal skins or spoils, detailing a transaction for Monica, wife of Saccus, including payment to an excisor.

EDCS-67600237.translation: Liccaia and Trauseus, [---]anaeus. Gausapa, 8, a cloak for 10 denarii, 1 sesterce, 5 libellae. One for the shoemaker.
EDCS-67600237.summary: This inscription is an economic record, likely detailing transactions or inventory, involving Liccaia, Trauseus, and a shoemaker, listing items like gausapa and a cloak with their prices in denarii, sesterces, and libellae.

EDCS-67600238.translation: Laetus, slave of Lucius Peccius. CI. Agilis, slave.
EDCS-67600238.summary: This inscription records the names of two slaves, Laetus and Agilis, with Laetus being identified as belonging to Lucius Peccius.

EDCS-67600239.text_edition: TOTV NA  ISPA VII XVII pondo VIII semis
EDCS-67600239.translation: TOTV NA ISPA, 7, 17 pounds, 8 and a half.
EDCS-67600239.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of weight or quantity, possibly related to goods, specifying 17 pounds and 8.5 units for something identified as TOTV NA ISPA.

EDCS-67600240.translation: For Lascivus, the fuller. Spanish, received, 3 pounds.
EDCS-67600240.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lascivus, a fuller, noting the receipt of 3 pounds of a 'Spanish' item, likely wool or cloth for processing.

EDCS-68300256.translation: Thallus restored a temple of a god, which had collapsed from old age, for the well-being of our Emperor.
EDCS-68300256.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a dilapidated temple by Thallus for the health and prosperity of the reigning Emperor, indicating a votive offering or public work.

EDCS-68300257.translation: Thallus, bailiff of our Emperor, restored a temple that had collapsed from old age for his own safety and that of his household.
EDCS-68300257.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an ancient, collapsed temple by Thallus, the Emperor's bailiff, as a votive offering for his own well-being and that of his family.

EDCS-68300258.translation: [---] for the well-being of [---] Eusebes, our Caesar, [---] Eutyches willingly and deservedly [made this].
EDCS-68300258.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Eutyches, likely for the health of a Caesar named Eusebes, indicating a personal act of devotion or a public demonstration of loyalty.

EDCS-68300259.translation: ---linus, slave and bailiff of [---] the procurator.
EDCS-68300259.summary: This inscription identifies an individual named Linus (partially preserved) as a slave and bailiff belonging to a procurator, highlighting his administrative role within a Roman household or estate.

EDCS-68300260.translation: To the Unconquered God. Amandus, a clerk of the public portorium of Gaius Antonius Rufus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-68300260.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Amandus, a clerk responsible for the public customs (portorium) under Gaius Antonius Rufus, to an 'Unconquered God,' fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-73200027.text_edition: Aquoni Lucius Trosius Abascantus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-73200027.translation: Lucius Trosius Abascantus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Aquo.
EDCS-73200027.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Lucius Trosius Abascantus to the deity Aquo, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-73200030.translation: [...] his wife made this for [...] silver.
EDCS-73200030.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely erected by a wife, mentioning a name ending in -mant and possibly referring to a silver object or offering, though precise dating is impossible.

EDCS-73200034.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, with too little text to determine its original purpose or context, beyond the mention of names starting with 'IER' and 'Iu'.

EDCS-73200036.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a daughter, but insufficient context remains to identify the dedicant or recipient.

EDCS-73200040.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, mentioning a male individual named Quintus, but the full context is lost.

EDCS-73200064.translation: Albina, daughter of Masclus, 80 years old.
EDCS-73200064.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Albina, the daughter of Masclus, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-73200065.translation: [...] Saurus, son of Dunosedus, 80 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-73200065.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saurus, son of Dunosedus, who lived to be 80 years old and is interred at this location.

EDCS-73200067.translation: [...] for himself and for his most fortunate son, who died at the age of 20.
EDCS-73200067.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, erected by an unnamed individual for himself and his son who passed away at twenty years old.

EDCS-73200071.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, mentioning a daughter and a freedman of Marcus, but lacking sufficient detail to establish its full purpose.

EDCS-73200072.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, mentioning a son and suggesting the person was still alive when the monument was made, but the context is largely lost.

EDCS-75300005.translation: Quiemon made this while alive for himself and for Venixema his wife, who is alive, and for his daughter Mai[...], who died at the age of 20.
EDCS-75300005.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Quiemon while still living, for himself, his living wife Venixema, and his deceased daughter Mai[...] who passed away at the age of 20.

EDCS-75300228.translation: To the Holy God He[rcules(?) ...], whose names [...], Gaius Julius Ingenuus [...], Marcus Aurelius F[...], Aurelius [...], Att[...], Julius Victor [...], Aurelius F[...].
EDCS-75300228.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to the Holy God Hercules, made by several individuals including Gaius Julius Ingenuus and Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-75600110.translation: Secundinus, purple-dyer, 1,000 sesterces [...]. Bugia [...]. One denarius for one person, two denarii for two people.
EDCS-75600110.summary: This inscription appears to be an economic record or ledger entry, possibly related to a purple-dyer named Secundinus and mentioning monetary transactions.

EDCS-75700038.translation: Licinius, priest of Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, by order of the Divinity, for himself and his family, on the 6th day before the Kalends of July, when Sabinus and Venustus were consuls. Licinius Tertinus, Marcus Aurelius Ursus. P.V.N.V.S. Longinus made this.
EDCS-75700038.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, made by Licinius, a priest, for himself and his family, dating to the consulate of Sabinus and Venustus (likely 240 AD), with other individuals also named.

EDCS-75700039.translation: Publius Aelius Sextinus, decurion and duumvir, for Aelia Ovila and Flavia Nativa, his wife, who died at 20 years of age, and for Septimia Honorata, his mother-in-law, and Aelia Firmina, his daughter, and Aelius Sabinus, his son, 10 years old.
EDCS-75700039.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Publius Aelius Sextinus, a decurion and duumvir, commemorating his wife Flavia Nativa, his mother-in-law Septimia Honorata, and his children Aelia Firmina and Aelius Sabinus.

EDCS-75700041.translation: [...] Secundus, son of Festus, made this while alive [...].
EDCS-75700041.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription indicating that Secundus, son of Festus, had the monument made for himself during his lifetime.
